name: cim-graph
documentation:
# CIMantic Graphs Library

![GitHub Tag](https://img.shields.io/github/v/tag/PNNL-CIM-Tools/CIM-Graph)
![GitHub Release Date](https://img.shields.io/github/release-date-pre/PNNL-CIM-Tools/CIM-Graph)
![GitHub Actions Workflow Status](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/PNNL-CIM-Tools/CIM-Graph/dev-pre-release.yml)
![Libraries.io dependency status for GitHub repo](https://img.shields.io/librariesio/github/PNNL-CIM-Tools/CIM-Graph)


![PyPI - Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/cim-graph)
![PyPI - Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/cim-graph?label=pypi%20downloads)
![PyPI - Format](https://img.shields.io/pypi/format/cim-graph)


![GitHub Issues or Pull Requests](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/PNNL-CIM-Tools/CIM-Graph)
![GitHub Issues or Pull Requests](https://img.shields.io/github/issues-pr/PNNL-CIM-Tools/CIM-Graph)
![GitHub commit activity](https://img.shields.io/github/commit-activity/t/PNNL-CIM-Tools/CIM-Graph)

![GitHub Downloads (all assets, all releases)](https://img.shields.io/github/downloads/PNNL-CIM-Tools/CIM-Graph/total?label=git%20downloads)
![GitHub License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/PNNL-CIM-Tools/CIM-Graph)
![https://doi.org/10.11578/dc.20240507.3](https://img.shields.io/badge/doi-10.11578/dc.20240507.3-blue)


CIMantic Graphs is an open-source library for for creating, parsing, and editing CIM power system models using in-memory knowledge graphs to reduce the burden and learning curve associated with using the Common Information Model.

Key features:

* Single API method to obtain data for any CIM class. No more custom database queries.
* Single API method to obtain data for EMS node-breaker transmission models, bus-branch planning models, and distribution feeder models.
* Singe API method for both centralized and distributed architectures.
* Support for multiple databases and query languages with no changes to upper-level graph data or API calls. Only need to change host/port specified in ConnectionParameters data object.
* Ability to create CIM models "from scratch".
* Open-source data engineering tool for management of CIM models.
* Knowledge graph approach based on semantic understanding of CIM.
* Object-oriented data structure with enforcement of CIM Schema.
* Data profiles generated directly from Enterprise Architect UML.
* Support for custom profiles using CIMTool or Schema Composer.
* Support for direct creation / editing / parsing of CIM XML, JSON-LD.
* API support for centralized/distributed transmission + distribution models.

![summary-image](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PNNL-CIM-Tools/CIM-Graph/develop/cim_graph_structure.png)

## Requirements

CIM-Graph requires a python version >=3.8 and <4. No testing has been done with other versions.

Support is currently offered for opening XML, JSON-LD, and CSV files

Support is currently offered for GridAPPS-D, Blazegraph, Neo4J, GraphDB, and json-formatted MySQL databases. More databases will be added in the future.

## Installation

```bash
pip install cim-graph
```

## Specifying the CIM Profile

The CIM-Graph library supports multiple CIM profiles, which can be exported using CIMtool or Enterprise Architect Schema Composer as a .xsd data profile. The data profiles are ingested using the xsdata python library and saved in the cimgraph/data_profile directory.

When importing the library, the CIM profile must be specified using the gridappsd-python constructor or directly as

```python
import cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021 as cim
```

or by using `importlib`:

```python
import importlib
cim_profile = 'rc4_2021'
cim = importlib.import_module('cimgraph.data_profile.' + cim_profile)
```

## Specifying the Connection Parameters

The CIM-Graph library supports multiple databases. The ConnectionParameters specify how to read the CIM model:

__Required arguments:__

* `cim_profile`: This specifies the specific version of CIM to be used, based on the available python data profiles loaded into the library

__Optional arguments:__

* `namespace`: CIM namespace, default is `"http://iec.ch/TC57/CIM100#"`
* `iec61970-301`: Serialization version. Versions 7(default) and below use `rdf:ID=`. Version 8 uses `rdf:about=urn:uuid:`
* `url`: URL at which the database can be reached via TCP/IP or other connection
* `host`: Database host address
* `port`: Database host port
* `database`: Database name
* `username`: Database username
* `password`: Database password
* `filename`: Filename for importing CIM models from an XML file

Note that not all parameters are required. Each database connection uses a subset of these arguments depending on the requirements of the database connection driver.

## Graph Model Classes

### Model Initialization

The CIM-Graph library creates object instances populated with the attributes of `mRID` for all nodes, terminals, and conducting equipment in the centralized netork or each distributed area. All other attributes are `None` or `[]` by default until queried for using `.get_all_edges(cim.ClassName)`.

### Transmission Node-Breaker Models

```python
from cimgraph.models import NodeBreakerModel

geo_region_id = "_EE4C60AE-550D-4599-92F4-022DF3118B3C" #Maple 10 bus
geo_region = cim.GeographicalRegion(mRID = geo_region_id)
network = NodeBreakerModel(connection=database, container=geo_region, distributed=True)
```

### Transmission Bus-Branch Models

```python
from cimgraph.models import BusBranchModel

model_mrid = "1783D2A8-1204-4781-A0B4-7A73A2FA6038" #IEEE 118 Bus"
container = cim.ConnectivityNodeContainer(mRID = model_mrid)
network = BusBranchModel(connection=database, container=container, distributed=False)
```

### Distribution Feeder Models

```python
from cimgraph.models import FeederModel

feeder_mrid = "49AD8E07-3BF9-A4E2-CB8F-C3722F837B62"
feeder = cim.Feeder(mRID = feeder_mrid)
network = FeederModel(connection=database, container=feeder, distributed=False)
```

### Centralized vs Distributed Models

If the `distributed` flag is set to `False`, then all equipment are contained within a single knowledge graph. If set to `True`, a `DistributedArea` graph model is created for each topological area inside the power system model.

```python
network = FeederModel(connection=rdf, container=feeder, distributed=True)
for switch_area in network.distributed_areas:
    switch_area.get_all_edges(cim.ACLineSegement)

    for secondary_area in switch_area.distributed_areas:
         secondary_area.get_all_edges(cim.ACLineSegement)
```

### Usage with GridAPPS-D Context Manager

If an application is built using the GridAPPS-D Context Manager and Field Interface in gridappsd-python, initialization of the `FeederModel` and `DistributedArea` graphs is performed automatically.

Internally, the GridAPPS-D Context Manager initializes distributed areas as `dist_network =  FeederModel(connection=gapps, container=feeder, distributed=True, topology_message=topo_msg)`.

## Core Library Methods

### .get_all_edges(cim.ClassName)

The CIM power system model can then be parsed by invoking the `.get_all_edges(cim.ClassName)` method. The method populates all available attributes of the given class and creates default instances of all associated class object instances that are one association away in the CIM UML. Associated default instances are only populated with `mRID` attribute. The `.get_all_edges` method must be invoked in sequential order following the inheritance hierarchy in the CIM UML, starting with the particular equiment class (e.g. ACLineSegment) and then each child class inheriting from the previous class.

### .graph[cim.ClassName]

The Python object instances can be accessed using the `graph` dictionary of each distributed area class instance. The typed catalog is organized by the class type and then mRID of each object. The attributes of each class can be accessed directly or through any associated class. These two calls are equivalent:

```python
bus_name = network.graph[cim.ConnectivityNode][node_mrid].name
```

```python
bus_name = network.graph[cim.ACLineSegment][line_mrid].Terminals[0].ConnectivityNode.name
```

Note that all classes and attributes are case sensitive and follow the CIM UML conventions for each class.

### pprint

Printing of individual CIM objects using default `print()` is now supported. Individual objects can be pretty-printed using the

```python
line = cim.ACLineSegment(name = 'new_line', r=0.002, x=0.050, b=0.003)
line.pprint()
```

All instances of all given CIM class within the network graph can be printed `.pprint(cim.ClassName)` method:

```python
network.pprint(cim.ACLineSegment)
```

Additional examples of usage are available on ReadTheDocs.

classes.name: cimgraph.models.node_breaker_model.NodeBreakerModel
documentation:
Knowledge graph class for transmission node-breaker models. This should class
should be used for all models with detailed substation representations.
Args:
    container: a CIM object inheriting from EquipmentContainer with specified mRID
    connection: a ConnectionInterface object, such as BlazegraphConnection
    distributed: a boolean to indicate if the graph is distributed
Optional Args:
    distributed_hierarchy: Custom inheritance structure for defining distributed areas
    topology_message: JSON message from GridAPPS-D Topology Proccessor Service
Returns:
    none
Methods:
    graph[cim.ClassName]: access to graph dictionary sorted by class and mRID
    add_to_graph(object): adds a new CIM object to the knowledge graph
    get_all_edges(cim.ClassName): universal database query to expand graph by one edge
    get_all_attributes(cim.ClassName): universal query to get attributes without expanding graph
    get_edges_query(cim.ClassName): returns query text for debugging
    pprint(cim.ClassName): pretty-print method for showing graph of a class type

class_variables.name: cimgraph.models.graph_model.GraphModel.graph
documentation:
Underlying root class for all knowledge graph models, inlcuding
FeederModel, BusBranchModel, and NodeBreakerModel
Required Args:
    container: a CIM container object inheriting from ConnectivityNodeContainer
    connection: a ConnectionInterface object, such as BlazegraphConnection
    distributed: a boolean to indicate if the graph is distributed
Returns:
    none
Methods:
    add_to_graph(object): adds a new CIM object to the knowledge graph
    get_all_edges(cim.ClassName): universal database query to expand graph by one edge
    graph[cim.ClassName]: access to graph dictionary sorted by class and mRID
    pprint(cim.ClassName): pretty-print method for showing graph of a class type
    get_edges_query(cim.ClassName): returns query text for debugging

functions.name: cimgraph.queries.cypher.get_all_edges.get_all_edges_cypher
documentation:
Generates SPARQL query string for a given catalog of objects and feeder id
Args:
    graph (dict[type, dict[UUID, object]]): The graph of CIM objects organized by
        class type and UUID object identifier
    cim_class (type): The CIM class type to query
    uuid_list (list[UUID]): List of UUIDs to query for
    connection_params (ConnectionParameters): Database connection parameters

Returns:
    query_message: query string that can be used in Neo4J database

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitMultiplier.none
documentation: No multiplier or equivalently multiply by 1.

The unit multipliers defined for the CIM. When applied to unit symbols,
the unit symbol is treated as a derived unit. Regardless of the contents
of the unit symbol text, the unit symbol shall be treated as if it were
a single-character unit symbol. Unit symbols should not contain multipliers,
and it should be left to the multiplier to define the multiple for an entire
data type.
For example, if a unit symbol is 'm2Pers' and the multiplier is 'k', then
the value is k(m**2/s), and the multiplier applies to the entire final
value, not to any individual part of the value. This can be conceptualized
by substituting a derived unit symbol for the unit type. If one imagines
that the symbol 'Þ' represents the derived unit 'm2Pers', then applying
the multiplier 'k' can be conceptualized simply as 'kÞ'.
For example, the SI unit for mass is 'kg' and not 'g'. If the unit symbol
is defined as 'kg', then the multiplier is applied to 'kg' as a whole and
does not replace the 'k' in front of the 'g'. In this case, the multiplier
of 'm' would be used with the unit symbol of 'kg' to represent one gram.
As a text string, this violates the instructions in IEC 80000-1. However,
because the unit symbol in CIM is treated as a derived unit instead of
as an SI unit, it makes more sense to conceptualize the 'kg' as if it were
replaced by one of the proposed replacements for the SI mass symbol. If
one imagines that the 'kg' were replaced by a symbol 'Þ', then it is easier
to conceptualize the multiplier 'm' as creating the proper unit 'mÞ', and
not the forbidden unit 'mkg'.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.K

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.KPers

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.VAr

Reactive power in volt amperes reactive. The “reactive” or “imaginary”

Note: Different meter designs use different methods to arrive at their
results. Some meters may compute reactive power as an arithmetic value,
while others compute the value vectorially. The data consumer should determine
the method in use and the suitability of the measurement for the intended
purpose.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.VPerVA
documentation:
Power factor, PF, the ratio of the active power to the apparent power.
Note: The sign convention used for power factor will differ between IEC
meters and EEI (ANSI) meters. It is assumed that the data consumers understand
the type of meter being used and agree on the sign convention in use at
any given utility.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.VPerVAr
documentation:
Power factor, PF, the ratio of the active power to the apparent power.
Note: The sign convention used for power factor will differ between IEC
meters and EEI (ANSI) meters. It is assumed that the data consumers understand
the type of meter being used and agree on the sign convention in use at
any given utility.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.Vh

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.charPers

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.character

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.cosPhi
documentation:
Power factor, dimensionless.
Note 1: This definition of power factor only holds for balanced systems.
See the alternative definition under code 153.
Note 2 : Beware of differing sign conventions in use between the IEC and
EEI. It is assumed that the data consumer understands the type of meter
in use and the sign convention in use by the utility.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.d

Sound pressure level in decibels. Note: multiplier “d” is included in this

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.dBm

Power level (logarithmic ratio of signal strength , Bel-mW), normalized

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.deg

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.degC
documentation:
Relative temperature in degrees Celsius.
In the SI unit system the symbol is °C. Electric charge is measured in
coulomb that has the unit symbol C. To distinguish degree Celsius from
coulomb the symbol used in the UML is degC. The reason for not using °C
is that the special character ° is difficult to manage in software.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.ha

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.katPerm3

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.kg

Mass in kilograms. Note: multiplier “k” is included in this unit symbol

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.kgPerJ

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.l

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.lPerh

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.lPerl
documentation:
Concentration, The ratio of the volume of a solute divided by the volume

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.lPers

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.lm
documentation: Luminous flux in lumens (cd·sr).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.lx

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.m

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.m2

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.m2Pers

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.m3

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.m3Compensated

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.m3Perh

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.m3Perkg

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.m3Uncompensated

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.mPerm3

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.mPers
documentation: Velocity in metres per second (m/s).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.mPers2

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.molPermol

Concentration, Molar fraction, the ratio of the molar amount of a solute

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.none

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.ohm
documentation: Electric resistance in ohms (V/A).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.ohmPerm
documentation: Electric resistance per length in ohms per metre ((V/A)/m).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.ohmm
documentation: Resistivity, ohm metres, (rho).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.onePerHz
documentation: Reciprocal of frequency (1/Hz).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.onePerm
documentation: Wavenumber, reciprocal metres, (1/m).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.ppm
documentation: Concentration in parts per million.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.rad

Time, Ratio of time. Note: Users may need to supply a prefix such as ‘µ’
to show rates such as ‘µs/s’.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.sr

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.therm
documentation: Energy, therms.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.UnitSymbol.tonne
documentation: Mass in tons, “tonne” or “metric ton” (1000 kg = 1 Mg).

The derived units defined for usage in the CIM. In some cases, the derived
unit is equal to an SI unit. Whenever possible, the standard derived symbol
is used instead of the formula for the derived unit. For example, the unit
symbol Farad is defined as 'F' instead of 'CPerV'. In cases where a standard
symbol does not exist for a derived unit, the formula for the unit is used
as the unit symbol. For example, density does not have a standard symbol
and so it is represented as 'kgPerm3'. With the exception of the 'kg',
which is an SI unit, the unit symbols do not contain multipliers and therefore
represent the base derived unit to which a multiplier can be applied as
a whole.
Every unit symbol is treated as an unparseable text as if it were a single-letter
symbol. The meaning of each unit symbol is defined by the accompanying
descriptive text and not by the text contents of the unit symbol.
To allow the widest possible range of serializations without requiring
special character handling, several substitutions are made which deviate
from the format described in IEC 80000-1. The division symbol '/' is replaced
by the letters 'Per'. Exponents are written in plain text after the unit
as 'm3' instead of being formatted as 'm' with a superscript of 3 or introducing
a symbol as in 'm^3'. The degree symbol '°' is replaced with the letters
'deg'. Any clarification of the meaning for a substitution is included
in the description for the unit symbol.
Non-SI units are included in list of unit symbols to allow sources of data
to be correctly labelled with their non-SI units (for example, a GPS sensor
that is reporting numbers that represent feet instead of meters). This
allows software to use the unit symbol information correctly convert and
scale the raw data of those sources into SI-based units.
The integer values are used for harmonization with IEC 61850.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.ActivePower
documentation:
Product of RMS value of the voltage and the RMS value of the in-phase
component of the current.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.ActivePowerChangeRate
documentation: Rate of change of active power per time.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.ActivePowerPerCurrentFlow
documentation: Active power variation with current flow.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.ActivePowerPerFrequency
documentation: Active power variation with frequency.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Admittance
documentation: Ratio of current to voltage.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.AngleDegrees
documentation: Ratio of current to voltage.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.AngleRadians
documentation: Ratio of current to voltage.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.ApparentPower
documentation: Product of the RMS value of the voltage and the RMS value of the current.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Area
documentation: Area.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Capacitance
documentation: Capacitive part of reactance (imaginary part of impedance), at rated frequency.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.CapacitancePerLength
documentation: Capacitance per unit length.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Classification
documentation:
Classification of level.  Specify as 1..n, with 1 being the most detailed,
highest priority, etc as described on the attribute using this data type.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Conductance
documentation:
Factor by which voltage must be multiplied to give corresponding power lost
from a circuit. Real part of admittance.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.ConductancePerLength
documentation: Real part of admittance per unit of length.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.CurrentFlow
documentation:
Electrical current with sign convention: positive flow is out of the
conducting equipment into the connectivity node. Can be both AC and DC.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Damping
documentation:
Per-unit active power variation with frequency referenced on the system
apparent power base. Typical values are in the range 1,0 - 2,0.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Date
documentation:
Date as "yyyy-mm-dd", which conforms with ISO 8601. UTC time zone is
specified as "yyyy-mm-ddZ". A local timezone relative UTC is specified as
"yyyy-mm-dd(+/-)hh:mm".

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.DateInterval
documentation: Interval between two dates.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Displacement
documentation:
Unit of displacement relative to a reference position,
hence can be negative.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Duration
documentation:
Duration as "PnYnMnDTnHnMnS" which conforms to ISO 8601, where nY expresses
a number of years, nM a number of months, nD a number of days. The letter
T separates the date expression from the time expression and, after it,
nH identifies a number of hours, nM a number of minutes and nS a number
of seconds. The number of seconds could be expressed as a decimal number,
but all other numbers are integers.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Emission
documentation: Quantity of emission per fuel heat content.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Frequency
documentation: Cycles per second.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.HeatRate
documentation: Heat generated, in energy per time unit of elapsed time.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Hours
documentation: Time specified in hours

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Impedance
documentation: Ratio of voltage to current.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Inductance
documentation: Inductive part of reactance (imaginary part of impedance), at rated frequency.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.InductancePerLength
documentation: Inductance per unit of length.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.KiloActivePower
documentation: Inductance per unit of length.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Length
documentation: Unit of length. It shall be a positive value or zero.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Mass
documentation: Heat generated, in energy per time unit of elapsed time.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Minutes
documentation: Time in minutes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.MonthDay
documentation: MonthDay format as "--mm-dd", which conforms with XSD data type gMonthDay.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.MonthDayInterval
documentation: Interval between two times specified as month and day.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.PU
documentation: Per Unit - a positive or negative value referred to a defined base. Values typically range from -10 to +10.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.PerCent
documentation: Percentage on a defined base. For example, specify as 100 to indicate at the defined base.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Pressure
documentation: Time in minutes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Reactance
documentation: Reactance (imaginary part of impedance), at rated frequency.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.ReactancePerLength
documentation: Reactance (imaginary part of impedance) per unit of length, at rated frequency.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.ReactivePower
documentation:
Product of RMS value of the voltage and the RMS value of the quadrature
component of the current.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Resistance
documentation: Resistance (real part of impedance).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.ResistancePerLength
documentation: Resistance (real part of impedance) per unit of length.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.RotationSpeed
documentation: Number of revolutions per second.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Seconds
documentation: Time specified in seconds

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Speed
documentation: Distance per unit of time

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Susceptance
documentation: Imaginary part of admittance.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.SusceptancePerLength
documentation: Imaginary part of admittance per unit of length.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Temperature
documentation: Distance per unit of time

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Time
documentation:
Time as "hh:mm:ss.sss", which conforms with ISO 8601. UTC time zone is
specified as "hh:mm:ss.sssZ". A local timezone relative UTC is specified
as "hh:mm:ss.sss±hh:mm". The second component (shown here as "ss.sss")
could have any number of digits in its fractional part to allow any
kind of precision beyond seconds.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.TimeInterval
documentation: Interval between two times.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Voltage
documentation: Electrical voltage, can be both AC and DC.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.VoltagePerReactivePower
documentation: Voltage variation with reactive power.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.Volume
documentation: Volume.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.VolumeFlowRate
documentation: Volume per time

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.units.units.WaterLevel
documentation: Reservoir water level referred to a given datum such as mean sea level.

This is the new root class from CIM 18 to provide common identification
for all classes needing identification and naming attributes.
IdentifiedObject is now a child class of Identity.
mRID is superseded by Identity.identifier, which is typed to be a UUID.

variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls._log
documentation:
Annotated CIMantic Graphs data profile for ufls
Generated by CIMTool http://cimtool.org

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.IdentifiedObject

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.IdentifiedObject.mRID
documentation:
Master resource identifier issued by a model authority. The mRID is unique
within an exchange context. Global uniqueness is easily achieved by using
a UUID, as specified in RFC 4122, for the mRID. The use of UUID is strongly
recommended.
For CIMXML data files in RDF syntax conforming to IEC 61970-552 Edition
1, the mRID is mapped to rdf:ID or rdf:about attributes that identify CIM
object elements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.IdentifiedObject.aliasName
documentation:
The aliasName is free text human readable name of the object alternative
to IdentifiedObject.name. It may be non unique and may not correlate to
a naming hierarchy.
The attribute aliasName is retained because of backwards compatibility
between CIM relases. It is however recommended to replace aliasName with
the Name class as aliasName is planned for retirement at a future time.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.IdentifiedObject.description
documentation:
The description is a free human readable text describing or naming the
object. It may be non unique and may not correlate to a naming hierarchy.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.IdentifiedObject.name
documentation:
The name is any free human readable and possibly non unique text naming
the object.

This is a root class to provide common identification for all classes needing
identification and naming attributes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ACDCTerminal

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ACDCTerminal.connected
documentation:
The connected status is related to a bus-branch model and the topological
node to terminal relation. True implies the terminal is connected to the
related topological node and false implies it is not.
In a bus-branch model, the connected status is used to tell if equipment
is disconnected without having to change the connectivity described by
the topological node to terminal relation. A valid case is that conducting
equipment can be connected in one end and open in the other. In particular
for an AC line segment, where the reactive line charging can be significant,
this is a relevant case.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ACDCTerminal.sequenceNumber
documentation:
The orientation of the terminal connections for a multiple terminal conducting
equipment. The sequence numbering starts with 1 and additional terminals
should follow in increasing order. The first terminal is the "starting
point" for a two terminal branch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ACDCTerminal.Measurements
documentation:
Measurements associated with this terminal defining where the measurement
is placed in the network topology. It may be used, for instance, to capture
the sensor position, such as a voltage transformer (PT) at a busbar or
a current transformer (CT) at the bar between a breaker and an isolator.

An electrical connection point (AC or DC) to a piece of conducting equipment.
Terminals are connected at physical connection points called connectivity
nodes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Terminal

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Terminal.BoundedSchedulingArea
documentation: The SubSchedulingArea bounded by the specific Terminal

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Terminal.ConductingEquipment
documentation:
The conducting equipment of the terminal. Conducting equipment have terminals
that may be connected to other conducting equipment terminals via connectivity
nodes or topological nodes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Terminal.ConnectivityNode
documentation: The connectivity node to which this terminal connects with zero impedance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Terminal.NormalHeadFeeder
documentation:
The feeder that this terminal normally feeds. Only specifed for the terminals
at head of feeders.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Terminal.TopologicalNode
documentation:
The topological node associated with the terminal. This can be used as
an alternative to the connectivity node path to topological node, thus
making it unneccesary to model connectivity nodes in some cases. Note that
the if connectivity nodes are in the model, this association would probably
not be used as an input specification.

An AC electrical connection point to a piece of conducting equipment. Terminals
are connected at physical connection points called connectivity nodes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.BaseVoltage

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.BaseVoltage.nominalVoltage
documentation: The power system resource's base voltage.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.BaseVoltage.ConductingEquipment
documentation:
All conducting equipment with this base voltage. Use only when there is
no voltage level container used and only one base voltage applies. For
example, not used for transformers.
Defines a system base voltage which is referenced.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ConnectivityNode

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ConnectivityNode.ConnectivityNodeContainer
documentation: Container of this connectivity node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ConnectivityNode.Terminals
documentation: Terminals interconnected with zero impedance at a this connectivity node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ConnectivityNode.TopologicalNode
documentation:
The topological node to which this connectivity node is assigned. May depend
on the current state of switches in the network.

Connectivity nodes are points where terminals of AC conducting equipment
are connected together with zero impedance.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.FunctionBlock

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.FunctionBlock.enabled
documentation: True, if the function block is enabled (active). Otherwise false.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.FunctionBlock.priority
documentation:
Value 0 means ignore priority. 1 means the highest priority, 2 is the second
highest priority.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.FunctionBlock.FunctionOutputVariable
documentation:
Function output variable describe the output or codomain to the function
block.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.FunctionBlock.Input
documentation: Function input variable describe the input or domain to the function block.

Function block is a function described as a set of elementary blocks. The
blocks describe the function between input variables and output variables.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.FunctionInputVariable

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.FunctionInputVariable.Function
documentation:
Function block describe the function that function input variable provides
the domain for.
Functional input variable defines the domain of the function.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.FunctionOutputVariable

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.FunctionOutputVariable.FunctionBlock
documentation:
Function block describe the function that function output variable provides
the codomain for.
Functional output variable defines the codomain of the function.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.GeographicalRegion

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.GeographicalRegion.Regions
documentation: All sub-geograhpical regions within this geographical region.
A geographical region of a power system network model.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Measurement

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Measurement.measurementType
documentation:
Specifies the type of measurement. For example, this specifies if the measurement
represents an indoor temperature, outdoor temperature, bus voltage, line
flow, etc.
When the measurementType is set to "Specialization", the type of Measurement
is defined in more detail by the specialized class which inherits from
Measurement.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Measurement.PowerSystemResource
documentation: The power system resource that contains the measurement.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Measurement.Terminal
documentation: One or more measurements may be associated with a terminal in the network.

A Measurement represents any measured, calculated or non-measured non-calculated
quantity. Any piece of equipment may contain Measurements, e.g. a substation
may have temperature measurements and door open indications, a transformer
may have oil temperature and tank pressure measurements, a bay may contain
a number of power flow measurements and a Breaker may contain a switch
status measurement.
The PSR - Measurement association is intended to capture this use of Measurement
and is included in the naming hierarchy based on EquipmentContainer. The
naming hierarchy typically has Measurements as leafs, e.g. Substation-VoltageLevel-Bay-Switch-Measurement.
Some Measurements represent quantities related to a particular sensor location
in the network, e.g. a voltage transformer (PT) at a busbar or a current
transformer (CT) at the bar between a breaker and an isolator. The sensing
position is not captured in the PSR - Measurement association. Instead
it is captured by the Measurement - Terminal association that is used to
define the sensing location in the network topology. The location is defined
by the connection of the Terminal to ConductingEquipment.
If both a Terminal and PSR are associated, and the PSR is of type ConductingEquipment,
the associated Terminal should belong to that ConductingEquipment instance.
When the sensor location is needed both Measurement-PSR and Measurement-Terminal
are used. The Measurement-Terminal association is never used alone.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Analog

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Analog.maxValue
documentation:
Normal value range maximum for any of the MeasurementValue.values. Used
for scaling, e.g. in bar graphs or of telemetered raw values.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Analog.minValue
documentation:
Normal value range minimum for any of the MeasurementValue.values. Used
for scaling, e.g. in bar graphs or of telemetered raw values.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Analog.normalValue
documentation: Normal measurement value, e.g., used for percentage calculations.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Analog.positiveFlowIn
documentation:
If true then this measurement is an active power, reactive power or current
with the convention that a positive value measured at the Terminal means
power is flowing into the related PowerSystemResource.
Analog represents an analog Measurement.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Discrete

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Discrete.maxValue
documentation:
Normal value range maximum for any of the MeasurementValue.values. Used
for scaling, e.g. in bar graphs or of telemetered raw values.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Discrete.minValue
documentation:
Normal value range minimum for any of the MeasurementValue.values. Used
for scaling, e.g. in bar graphs or of telemetered raw values.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Discrete.normalValue
documentation: Normal measurement value, e.g., used for percentage calculations.

Discrete represents a discrete Measurement, i.e. a Measurement representing
discrete values, e.g. a Breaker position.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PowerSystemResource

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PowerSystemResource.Measurements
documentation: The measurements associated with this power system resource.

A power system resource can be an item of equipment such as a switch, an
equipment container containing many individual items of equipment such
as a substation, or an organisational entity such as sub-control area.
Power system resources can have measurements associated.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ConnectivityNodeContainer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ConnectivityNodeContainer.ConnectivityNodes
documentation: Connectivity nodes which belong to this connectivity node container.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ConnectivityNodeContainer.TopologicalNode
documentation: The topological nodes which belong to this connectivity node container.

A base class for all objects that may contain connectivity nodes or topological
nodes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.EquipmentContainer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.EquipmentContainer.AdditionalGroupedEquipment
documentation:
The additonal contained equipment. The equipment belong to the equipment
container. The equipment is contained in another equipment container, but
also grouped with this equipment container. Examples include when a switch
contained in a substation is also desired to be grouped with a line contianer
or when a switch is included in a secondary substation and also grouped
in a feeder.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.EquipmentContainer.Equipments
documentation: Contained equipment.
A modeling construct to provide a root class for containing equipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Feeder

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Feeder.DistributionArea
documentation: The DistributionArea to which the feeder belongs

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Feeder.FeederArea
documentation: The FeederArea (which contains Equipment not contained in

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Feeder.NamingSecondarySubstation
documentation:
The secondary substations that are normally energized from the feeder.
Used for naming purposes. Should be consistent with the other associations
for energizing terminal specification and the feeder energization specification.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Feeder.NormalEnergizedSubstation
documentation: The substations that are normally energized by the feeder.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Feeder.NormalEnergizingSubstation
documentation:
The substation that nominally energizes the feeder. Also used for naming
purposes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Feeder.NormalHeadTerminal
documentation: The normal head terminal or terminals of the feeder.

A collection of equipment for organizational purposes, used for grouping
distribution resources.
The organization a feeder does not necessarily reflect connectivity or
current operation state.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Substation

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Substation.NamingFeeder
documentation:
The primary feeder that normally energizes the secondary substation. Used
for naming purposes. Either this association or the substation to subgeographical
region should be used for hiearchical containment specification.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Substation.NormalEnergizedFeeder
documentation: The normal energized feeders of the substation. Also used for naming purposes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Substation.NormalEnergizingFeeder
documentation:
The feeders that potentially energize the downstream substation. Should
be consistent with the associations that describe the naming hiearchy.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Substation.Region
documentation: The SubGeographicalRegion containing the substation.

A collection of equipment for purposes other than generation or utilization,
through which electric energy in bulk is passed for the purposes of switching
or modifying its characteristics.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Equipment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Equipment.aggregate
documentation:
The single instance of equipment represents multiple pieces of equipment
that have been modeled together as an aggregate. Examples would be power
transformers or synchronous machines operating in parallel modeled as a
single aggregate power transformer or aggregate synchronous machine. This
is not to be used to indicate equipment that is part of a group of interdependent
equipment produced by a network production program.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Equipment.inService
documentation: If true, the equipment is in service.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Equipment.networkAnalysisEnabled
documentation:
The equipment is enabled to participate in network analysis. If unspecified,
the value is assumed to be true.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Equipment.normallyInService
documentation: If true, the equipment is normally in service.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Equipment.AdditionalEquipmentContainer
documentation:
Additional equipment container beyond the primary equipment container.
The equipment is contained in another equipment container, but also grouped
with this equipment container.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Equipment.EquipmentContainer
documentation: Container of this equipment.
The parts of a power system that are physical devices, electronic or mechanical.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ConductingEquipment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ConductingEquipment.BaseVoltage
documentation:
Base voltage of this conducting equipment. Use only when there is no voltage
level container used and only one base voltage applies. For example, not
used for transformers.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ConductingEquipment.Terminals
documentation:
Conducting equipment have terminals that may be connected to other conducting
equipment terminals via connectivity nodes or topological nodes.

The parts of the AC power system that are designed to carry current or
that are conductively connected through terminals.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.EnergyConsumer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.EnergyConsumer.customerCount
documentation: Number of individual customers represented by this demand.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.EnergyConsumer.grounded
documentation: Used for Yn and Zn connections. True if the neutral is solidly grounded.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.EnergyConsumer.p
documentation:
Active power of the load. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
For voltage dependent loads the value is at rated voltage.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.EnergyConsumer.q
documentation:
Reactive power of the load. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive
sign means flow out from a node.
For voltage dependent loads the value is at rated voltage.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.EnergyConsumer.PowerCutZone
documentation: The energy consumer is assigned to this power cut zone.
Generic user of energy - a point of consumption on the power system model.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.RegulatingCondEq

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.RegulatingCondEq.controlEnabled
documentation:
Specifies the regulation status of the equipment. True is regulating, false
is not regulating.

A type of conducting equipment that can regulate a quantity (i.e. voltage
or flow) at a specific point in the network.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PowerElectronicsConnection

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PowerElectronicsConnection.maxIFault
documentation:
Maximum fault current this device will contribute, in per-unit of rated
current, before the converter protection will trip or bypass.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PowerElectronicsConnection.maxQ
documentation:
Maximum reactive power limit. This is the maximum (nameplate) limit for
the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PowerElectronicsConnection.minQ
documentation:
Minimum reactive power limit for the unit. This is the minimum (nameplate)
limit for the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PowerElectronicsConnection.p
documentation:
Active power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PowerElectronicsConnection.q
documentation:
Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PowerElectronicsConnection.ratedS
documentation:
Nameplate apparent power rating for the unit.
The attribute shall have a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PowerElectronicsConnection.ratedU
documentation:
Rated voltage (nameplate data, Ur in IEC 60909-0). It is primarily used
for short circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909.

A connection to the AC network for energy production or consumption that
uses power electronics rather than rotating machines.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Switch

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Switch.normalOpen
documentation:
The attribute is used in cases when no Measurement for the status value
is present. If the Switch has a status measurement the Discrete.normalValue
is expected to match with the Switch.normalOpen.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Switch.open
documentation:
The attribute tells if the switch is considered open when used as input
to topology processing.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Switch.retained
documentation:
Branch is retained in a bus branch model. The flow through retained switches
will normally be calculated in power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Switch.ratedCurrent
documentation:
The maximum continuous current carrying capacity in amps governed by the
device material and construction.

A generic device designed to close, or open, or both, one or more electric
circuits. All switches are two terminal devices including grounding switches.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ProtectedSwitch

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ProtectedSwitch.breakingCapacity
documentation:
The maximum fault current a breaking device can break safely under prescribed
conditions of use.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ProtectedSwitch.OperatedByProtectionEquipment
documentation: Protection equipments that operate this ProtectedSwitch.
A ProtectedSwitch is a switching device that can be operated by ProtectionEquipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Breaker

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Breaker.inTransitTime
documentation: The transition time from open to close.

A mechanical switching device capable of making, carrying, and breaking
currents under normal circuit conditions and also making, carrying for
a specified time, and breaking currents under specified abnormal circuit
conditions e.g. those of short circuit.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PowerElectronicsUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PowerElectronicsUnit.maxP
documentation:
Maximum active power limit. This is the maximum (nameplate) limit for the
unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PowerElectronicsUnit.minP
documentation:
Minimum active power limit. This is the minimum (nameplate) limit for the
unit.

A generating unit or battery or aggregation that connects to the AC network
using power electronics rather than rotating machines.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.BatteryUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.BatteryUnit.batteryState
documentation: indicates whether the battery is charging, discharging or idle

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.BatteryUnit.ratedE
documentation: full energy storage capacity of the battery

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.BatteryUnit.storedE
documentation: amount of energy currently stored; no more than ratedE
An electrochemical energy storage device

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PhotovoltaicUnit
documentation: A photovoltaic device or an aggregation of such devices

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ProtectionEquipment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ProtectionEquipment.highLimit
documentation: The maximum allowable value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ProtectionEquipment.lowLimit
documentation: The minimum allowable value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ProtectionEquipment.powerDirectionFlag
documentation: Direction same as positive active power flow value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ProtectionEquipment.relayDelayTime
documentation: The time delay from detection of abnormal conditions to relay operation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ProtectionEquipment.unitMultiplier
documentation: The unit multiplier of the value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ProtectionEquipment.unitSymbol
documentation: The unit of measure of the value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ProtectionEquipment.ProtectedSwitches
documentation: Protected switches operated by this ProtectionEquipment.

An electrical device designed to respond to input conditions in a prescribed
manner and after specified conditions are met to cause contact operation
or similar abrupt change in associated electric control circuits, or simply
to display the detected condition. Protection equipment is associated with
conducting equipment and usually operate circuit breakers.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PowerCutZone

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PowerCutZone.cutLevel1
documentation: First level (amount) of load to cut as a percentage of total zone load.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PowerCutZone.cutLevel2
documentation: Second level (amount) of load to cut as a percentage of total zone load.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PowerCutZone.EnergyConsumers
documentation: Energy consumer is assigned to the power cut zone.
An area or zone of the power system which is used for load shedding purposes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.SubSchedulingArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.SubSchedulingArea.BoundaryTerminals
documentation: The set of terminals that define the persistent boundaries of the SubSchedulingArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.SubSchedulingArea.ContainedEquipment
documentation: The Equipment contained within the SubSchedulingArea.

A persistent connectivity-based containment of ConductingEquipment objects
with clearly-defined electrical boundaries forming a local power system
with one or more points of common coupling. Each piece of ConductingEquipment
can be associated with one ResourceContainer. The boundaries of the ResourceContainer
are specified through the Terminals of equipment forming the boundary (such
as a Recloser or PowerTransformer)

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.DistributionArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.DistributionArea.Feeders
documentation:
Feeders within the service territory of the DistributionArea of a particular
Distribution System Operator (DSO)

A persistent connectivity-based containment of medium-voltage and high-voltage
distribution ConductingEquipment with clearly defined electrical boundaries
based on electrical connectivity of a distribution substation or multiple
substations.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.FeederArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.FeederArea.Feeder
documentation:
The Feeder (which contains the ConnectivityNode and all Equipment) associated
with the FeeederArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.FeederArea.SwitchAreas
documentation: The set of SwitchArea normally energized by the FeederArea

A persistent connectivity-based containment of medium-voltage distribution
ConductingEquipment with clearly defined electrical boundaries based on
electrical connectivity of a distribution feeder.
The FeederArea contains all medium voltage equipment not contained in a
SwitchArea or Substation / Bay. It also includes all Sectionalisers, Reclosers,
and all other poletop and pad-mounted switchgear that form the boundary
of a SwitchArea.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.SecondaryArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.SecondaryArea.primaryPhase
documentation:
Used to represent the ABC phase to which the secondary split-phase transformer
is connected in North American systems. For secondary areas served by a
center-tap transformer, the phase connection of equipment will generally
be SinglePhaseKind.s1 or SinglePhaseKind.s2, and it is not readily apparent
what phase serves the loads at the medium voltage level.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.SecondaryArea.SwitchArea
documentation: The SwitchArea that normally energizes the SecondaryArea

A persistent connectivity-based containment of low-voltage distribution
ConductingEquipment with clearly defined electrical boundaries formed by
one or more PowerTransformer objects.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.SwitchArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.SwitchArea.FeederArea
documentation: The FeederArea that normally energizes the SwitchArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.SwitchArea.SecondaryAreas
documentation: The set of SecondAreas normally energized by the SwitchArea

A persistent connectivity-based containment of medium-voltage distribution
ConductingEquipment with clearly defined electrical boundaries formed by
one or more Switch objects.
The SwitchArea contains all conductors, fuses, poletop equipment, and vault
equipment. It also contains all secondary service transformers not contained
in a SecondarySubstation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Microgrid
documentation:
A persistent connectivity-based containment of distribution ConductingEquipment
that 1) has clearly-defined electrical boundaries formed by one or more
point of common coupling Switch objects and 2) that acts as a single controllable
entity which can be operated in grid-connected or islanded mode.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.SubGeographicalRegion

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.SubGeographicalRegion.Region
documentation: The geographical region to which this sub-geographical region is within.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.SubGeographicalRegion.Substations
documentation: The substations in this sub-geographical region.
A subset of a geographical region of a power system network model.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.TopologicalNode

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.TopologicalNode.pInjection
documentation:
The active power injected into the bus at this location in addition to
injections from equipment. Positive sign means injection into the TopologicalNode
(bus).
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.TopologicalNode.qInjection
documentation:
The reactive power injected into the bus at this location in addition to
injections from equipment. Positive sign means injection into the TopologicalNode
(bus).
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.TopologicalNode.BaseVoltage
documentation: The base voltage of the topologocial node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.TopologicalNode.ConnectivityNodeContainer
documentation: The connectivity node container to which the toplogical node belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.TopologicalNode.ConnectivityNodes
documentation:
The connectivity nodes combine together to form this topological node.
May depend on the current state of switches in the network.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.TopologicalNode.Terminal
documentation:
The terminals associated with the topological node. This can be used as
an alternative to the connectivity node path to terminal, thus making it
unneccesary to model connectivity nodes in some cases. Note that if connectivity
nodes are in the model, this association would probably not be used as
an input specification.

For a detailed substation model a topological node is a set of connectivity
nodes that, in the current network state, are connected together through
any type of closed switches, including jumpers. Topological nodes change
as the current network state changes (i.e., switches, breakers, etc. change
state).
For a planning model, switch statuses are not used to form topological
nodes. Instead they are manually created or deleted in a model builder
tool. Topological nodes maintained this way are also called "busses".

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PhaseCode.s12N

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PhaseCode.s1N

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PhaseCode.s2
documentation: Secondary phase 2.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PhaseCode.s2N
documentation: Secondary phase 2 and neutral.

An unordered enumeration of phase identifiers. Allows designation of phases
for both transmission and distribution equipment, circuits and loads. The
enumeration, by itself, does not describe how the phases are connected
together or connected to ground. Ground is not explicitly denoted as a
phase.
Residential and small commercial loads are often served from single-phase,
or split-phase, secondary circuits. For example of s12N, phases 1 and 2
refer to hot wires that are 180 degrees out of phase, while N refers to
the neutral wire. Through single-phase transformer connections, these secondary
circuits may be served from one or two of the primary phases A, B, and
C. For three-phase loads, use the A, B, C phase codes instead of s12N.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitMultiplier.G

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitMultiplier.none
documentation: No multiplier or equivalently multiply by 1.

The unit multipliers defined for the CIM. When applied to unit symbols,
the unit symbol is treated as a derived unit. Regardless of the contents
of the unit symbol text, the unit symbol shall be treated as if it were
a single-character unit symbol. Unit symbols should not contain multipliers,
and it should be left to the multiplier to define the multiple for an entire
data type.
For example, if a unit symbol is "A2Perh" and the multiplier is "k", then
the value is k(A^2/h), and the multiplier applies to the entire final value,
not to any individual part of the value. This can be conceptualized by
substituting a derived unit symbol for the unit type. If one imagines that
the symbol "Þ" represents the derived unit "A2Perh", then applying the
multiplier "k" can be conceptualized simply as "kÞ".
For example, the SI unit for mass is "kg" and not "g". If the unit symbol
is defined as "kg", then the multiplier is applied to "kg" as a whole and
does not replace the "k" in front of the "g". In this case, the multiplier
of "m" would be used with the unit symbol of "kg" to represent one gram.
As a text string, this violates the instructions in IEC 80000-1. However,
because the unit symbol in CIM is treated as a derived unit instead of
as an SI unit, it makes more sense to conceptualize the "kg" as if it were
replaced by one of the proposed replacements for the SI mass symbol. If
one imagines that the "kg" were replaced by a symbol "Þ", then it is easier
to conceptualize the multiplier "m" as creating the proper unit "mÞ", and
not the forbidden unit "mkg".

to 1mW. Note: multiplier “d” is included in this unit symbol for compatibility
with IEC 61850-7-3.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.deg

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.degC
documentation:
Relative temperature in degrees Celsius.
In the SI unit system the symbol is ºC. Electric charge is measured in
coulomb that has the unit symbol C. To distinguish degree Celsius form
coulomb the symbol used in the UML is degC. Reason for not using ºC is
the special character º is difficult to manage in software.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.gPerg

Concentration, The ratio of the mass of a solute divided by the mass of
the solution. Note: Users may need use a prefix such a ‘µ’ to express a
quantity such as ‘µg/g’.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.h

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.kgm2

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.kn

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.l

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.lPerh

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.lPerl
documentation:
Concentration, The ratio of the volume of a solute divided by the volume

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.lPers

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.lm

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.lx

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.m
documentation: Length in meter.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.m2
documentation: Area in square metre (m²).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.m2Pers
documentation: Viscosity in metre square / second (m²/s).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.m3
documentation: Volume in cubic metre (m³).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.m3Compensated

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.m3Perh

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.m3Perkg
documentation: Specific volume, cubic metre per kilogram, v.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.m3Pers

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.m3Uncompensated

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.mPerm3

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.mPers
documentation: Velocity in metre per second (m/s).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.mPers2

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.min
documentation: Time, minute = 60 s.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.mmHg
documentation: Pressure, millimeter of mercury (1 mmHg is approximately 133.3 Pa).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.mol
documentation: Amount of substance in mole.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.molPerkg
documentation:
Concentration, Molality, the amount of solute in moles and the amount of
solvent in kilograms.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.molPerm3
documentation:
Concentration, The amount of substance concentration, (c), the amount of

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.molPermol
documentation:
Concentration, Molar fraction (?), the ratio of the molar amount of a solute
divided by the molar amount of the solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.none
documentation: Dimension less quantity, e.g. count, per unit, etc.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.ohm
documentation: Electric resistance in ohm (V/A).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.ohmPerm
documentation: Electric resistance per length in ohm per metre ((V/A)/m).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.ohmm
documentation: resistivity, Ohm metre, (rho).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.onePerHz
documentation: Reciprocal of frequency (1/Hz).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.onePerm
documentation: Wavenumber, reciprocal metre, (1/m).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.ppm
documentation: Concentration in parts per million.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.rad
documentation: Plane angle in radian (m/m).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.radPers
documentation: Angular velocity in radians per second (rad/s).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.radPers2

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.rev
documentation: Amount of rotation, Revolutions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.rotPers
documentation: Rotations per second (1/s). See also Hz (1/s).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.s
documentation: Time in seconds.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.sPers

Time, Ratio of time Note: Users may need to supply a prefix such as ‘µ’
to show rates such as ‘µs/s’

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.sr
documentation: Solid angle in steradian (m2/m2).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.therm
documentation: Energy, Therm.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.UnitSymbol.tonne
documentation: mass, “tonne” or “metric ton” (1000 kg = 1 Mg).

The derived units defined for usage in the CIM. In some cases, the derived
unit is equal to an SI unit. Whenever possible, the standard derived symbol
is used instead of the formula for the derived unit. For example, the unit
symbol Farad is defined as "F" instead of "CPerV". In cases where a standard
symbol does not exist for a derived unit, the formula for the unit is used
as the unit symbol. For example, density does not have a standard symbol
and so it is represented as "kgPerm3". With the exception of the "kg",
which is an SI unit, the unit symbols do not contain multipliers and therefore
represent the base derived unit to which a multiplier can be applied as
a whole.
Every unit symbol is treated as an unparseable text as if it were a single-letter
symbol. The meaning of each unit symbol is defined by the accompanying
descriptive text and not by the text contents of the unit symbol.
To allow the widest possible range of serializations without requiring
special character handling, several substitutions are made which deviate
from the format described in IEC 80000-1. The division symbol "/" is replaced
by the letters"Per". Exponents are written in plain text after the unit
as "m3" instead of being formatted as in "m<sup>3</sup>" or introducing
a symbol as in "m^3". The degree symbol "°" is replaced with the letters
"deg". Any clarification of the meaning for a substitution is included
in the description for the unit symbol.
Non-SI units are included in list of unit symbols to allow sources of data
to be correctly labeled with their non-SI units (for example, a GPS sensor
that is reporting numbers that represent feet instead of meters). This
allows software to use the unit symbol information correctly convert and
scale the raw data of those sources into SI-based units.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ReactivePower.value
documentation:
Product of RMS value of the voltage and the RMS value of the quadrature
component of the current.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Seconds.value
documentation: Time, in seconds.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Voltage.value
documentation: Electrical voltage, can be both AC and DC.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PerCent.value
documentation:
Percentage on a defined base. For example, specify as 100 to indicate at
the defined base.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ApparentPower.value
documentation: Product of the RMS value of the voltage and the RMS value of the current.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.RealEnergy.value
documentation: Real electrical energy.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.CurrentFlow.value
documentation:
Electrical current with sign convention: positive flow is out of the conducting
equipment into the connectivity node. Can be both AC and DC.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.PU.value
documentation:
Per Unit - a positive or negative value referred to a defined base. Values
typically range from -10 to +10.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.Frequency.value
documentation: Cycles per second.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.ufls.ufls.ActivePower.value
documentation:
Product of RMS value of the voltage and the RMS value of the in-phase component
of the current.
