classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.IdentifiedObject

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.IdentifiedObject.mRID
documentation:
Master resource identifier issued by a model authority. The mRID is unique
within an exchange context. Global uniqueness is easily achieved by using
a UUID, as specified in RFC 4122, for the mRID. The use of UUID is strongly
recommended.
For CIMXML data files in RDF syntax conforming to IEC 61970-552 Edition
1, the mRID is mapped to rdf:ID or rdf:about attributes that identify CIM
object elements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.IdentifiedObject.aliasName
documentation:
The aliasName is free text human readable name of the object alternative
to IdentifiedObject.name. It may be non unique and may not correlate to
a naming hierarchy.
The attribute aliasName is retained because of backwards compatibility
between CIM relases. It is however recommended to replace aliasName with
the Name class as aliasName is planned for retirement at a future time.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.IdentifiedObject.description
documentation:
The description is a free human readable text describing or naming the
object. It may be non unique and may not correlate to a naming hierarchy.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.IdentifiedObject.name
documentation:
The name is any free human readable and possibly non unique text naming
the object.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.IdentifiedObject.Names
documentation: All names of this identified object.

This is a root class to provide common identification for all classes needing
identification and naming attributes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACDCTerminal

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACDCTerminal.connected
documentation:
The connected status is related to a bus-branch model and the topological
node to terminal relation. True implies the terminal is connected to the
related topological node and false implies it is not.
In a bus-branch model, the connected status is used to tell if equipment
is disconnected without having to change the connectivity described by
the topological node to terminal relation. A valid case is that conducting
equipment can be connected in one end and open in the other. In particular
for an AC line segment, where the reactive line charging can be significant,
this is a relevant case.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACDCTerminal.sequenceNumber
documentation:
The orientation of the terminal connections for a multiple terminal conducting
equipment. The sequence numbering starts with 1 and additional terminals
should follow in increasing order. The first terminal is the "starting
point" for a two terminal branch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACDCTerminal.BusNameMarker
documentation: The bus name marker used to name the bus (topological node).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACDCTerminal.Measurements
documentation:
Measurements associated with this terminal defining where the measurement
is placed in the network topology. It may be used, for instance, to capture
the sensor position, such as a voltage transformer (PT) at a busbar or
a current transformer (CT) at the bar between a breaker and an isolator.

An electrical connection point (AC or DC) to a piece of conducting equipment.
Terminals are connected at physical connection points called connectivity
nodes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Terminal

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Terminal.BoundedSchedulingArea
documentation: The SubSchedulingArea bounded by the specific Terminal

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Terminal.ConductingEquipment
documentation:
The conducting equipment of the terminal. Conducting equipment have terminals
that may be connected to other conducting equipment terminals via connectivity
nodes or topological nodes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Terminal.ConnectivityNode
documentation: The connectivity node to which this terminal connects with zero impedance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Terminal.HasFirstMutualCoupling
documentation: Mutual couplings associated with the branch as the first branch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Terminal.HasSecondMutualCoupling
documentation: Mutual couplings with the branch associated as the first branch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Terminal.NormalHeadFeeder
documentation:
The feeder that this terminal normally feeds. Only specifed for the terminals
at head of feeders.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Terminal.RegulatingControl
documentation: The controls regulating this terminal.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Terminal.TopologicalNode
documentation:
The topological node associated with the terminal. This can be used as
an alternative to the connectivity node path to topological node, thus
making it unneccesary to model connectivity nodes in some cases. Note that
the if connectivity nodes are in the model, this association would probably
not be used as an input specification.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Terminal.TransformerEnd
documentation: All transformer ends connected at this terminal.

An AC electrical connection point to a piece of conducting equipment. Terminals
are connected at physical connection points called connectivity nodes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Asset

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Asset.AssetContainer
documentation: Container of this asset.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Asset.AssetInfo
documentation: Data applicable to this asset.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Asset.Location
documentation: Location of this asset.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Asset.OrganisationRoles
documentation: All roles an organisation plays for this asset.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Asset.PowerSystemResources
documentation:
All power system resources used to electrically model this asset. For example,
transformer asset is electrically modelled with a transformer and its windings
and tap changer.

Tangible resource of the utility, including power system equipment, various
end devices, cabinets, buildings, etc. For electrical network equipment,
the role of the asset is defined through PowerSystemResource and its subclasses,
defined mainly in the Wires model (refer to IEC61970-301 and model package
IEC61970::Wires). Asset description places emphasis on the physical characteristics
of the equipment fulfilling that role.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AssetContainer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AssetContainer.Assets
documentation: All assets within this container asset.

Asset that is aggregation of other assets such as conductors, transformers,
switchgear, land, fences, buildings, equipment, vehicles, etc.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DuctBank

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DuctBank.circuitCount
documentation:
Number of circuits in duct bank. Refer to associations between a duct (ConductorAsset)
and an ACLineSegment to understand which circuits are in which ducts.

A duct contains individual wires in the layout as specified with associated
wire spacing instances; number of them gives the number of conductors in
this duct.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EndDevice

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EndDevice.amrSystem
documentation:
Automated meter reading (AMR) or other communication system responsible
for communications to this end device.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EndDevice.installCode
documentation: Installation code.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EndDevice.isPan
documentation: If true, this is a premises area network (PAN) device.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EndDevice.isVirtual
documentation:
If true, there is no physical device. As an example, a virtual meter can
be defined to aggregate the consumption for two or more physical meters.
Otherwise, this is a physical hardware device.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EndDevice.timeZoneOffset
documentation: Time zone offset relative to GMT for the location of this end device.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EndDevice.Customer
documentation: Customer owning this end device.

Asset container that performs one or more end device functions. One type
of end device is a meter which can perform metering, load management, connect/disconnect,
accounting functions, etc. Some end devices, such as ones monitoring and
controlling air conditioners, refrigerators, pool pumps may be connected
to a meter. All end devices may have communication capability defined by
the associated communication function(s). An end device may be owned by
a consumer, a service provider, utility or otherwise.
There may be a related end device function that identifies a sensor or
control point within a metering application or communications systems (e.g.,
water, gas, electricity).
Some devices may use an optical port that conforms to the ANSI C12.18 standard
for communications.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Structure
documentation:
Construction holding assets such as conductors, transformers, switchgear,
etc. Where applicable, number of conductors can be derived from the number
of associated wire spacing instances.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Bushing
documentation: Bushing asset.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AssetInfo

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AssetInfo.Assets
documentation: All assets described by this data.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AssetInfo.PowerSystemResources
documentation: All power system resources with this datasheet information.

Set of attributes of an asset, representing typical datasheet information
of a physical device that can be instantiated and shared in different data
exchange contexts:
- as attributes of an asset instance (installed or in stock)
- as attributes of an asset model (product by a manufacturer)
- as attributes of a type asset (generic type of an asset as used in designs/extension
planning).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BusbarSectionInfo

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BusbarSectionInfo.ratedVoltage

Busbar section data.

Factory measured capacitance, measured between the power factor tap and
the bushing conductor.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BushingInfo.c1PowerFactor

Factory measured insulation power factor, measured between the power factor

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BushingInfo.c2Capacitance

Factory measured capacitance measured between the power factor tap and
ground.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BushingInfo.c2PowerFactor
documentation:
Factory measured insulation power factor, measured between the power factor
tap and ground.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BushingInfo.ratedCurrent
documentation: Rated current for bushing as installed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BushingInfo.ratedImpulseWithstandVoltage
documentation: Rated impulse withstand voltage, also known as BIL (Basic Impulse Level).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BushingInfo.ratedLineToGroundVoltage
documentation:
Rated line-to-ground voltage. Also referred to as U<sub>y</sub> on bushing
nameplate.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BushingInfo.ratedVoltage
documentation:
Rated voltage. Can be referred to as U<sub>m</sub>, system voltage or class
on bushing nameplate.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BushingInfo.insulationKind
documentation: Kind of insulation.
Bushing datasheet information.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.InterrupterUnitInfo.interruptingMedium
documentation: Interrupting medium.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OperatingMechanismInfo

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OperatingMechanismInfo.closeAmps
documentation: Close current (nominal).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEndInfo

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEndInfo.endNumber
documentation:
Number for this transformer end, corresponding to the end's order in the
PowerTransformer.vectorGroup attribute. Highest voltage winding should
be 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEndInfo.phaseAngleClock
documentation:
Winding phase angle where 360 degrees are represented with clock hours,
so the valid values are {0, ..., 11}. For example, to express the second
winding in code 'Dyn11', set attributes as follows: 'endNumber'=2, 'connectionKind'
= Yn and 'phaseAngleClock' = 11.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEndInfo.emergencyS
documentation:
Apparent power that the winding can carry under emergency conditions (also
called long-term emergency power).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEndInfo.insulationU
documentation: Basic insulation level voltage rating.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEndInfo.r
documentation: DC resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEndInfo.ratedS
documentation: Normal apparent power rating.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEndInfo.ratedU
documentation:
Rated voltage: phase-phase for three-phase windings, and either phase-phase
or phase-neutral for single-phase windings.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEndInfo.shortTermS
documentation:
Apparent power that this winding can carry for a short period of time (in
emergency).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEndInfo.connectionKind
documentation: Kind of connection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEndInfo.CoreAdmittance
documentation:
Core admittance calculated from this transformer end datasheet, representing
magnetising current and core losses. The full values of the transformer
should be supplied for one transformer end info only.

documentation: All no-load test measurements in which this transformer end was energised.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEndInfo.EnergisedEndOpenCircuitTests
documentation: All open-circuit test measurements in which this transformer end was excited.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEndInfo.EnergisedEndShortCircuitTests
documentation: All short-circuit test measurements in which this transformer end was energised.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEndInfo.FromMeshImpedances
documentation: All mesh impedances between this 'to' and other 'from' transformer ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEndInfo.GroundedEndShortCircuitTests
documentation: All short-circuit test measurements in which this transformer end was short-circuited.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEndInfo.OpenEndOpenCircuitTests
documentation:
All open-circuit test measurements in which this transformer end was not
excited.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEndInfo.ToMeshImpedances
documentation: All mesh impedances between this 'from' and other 'to' transformer ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEndInfo.TransformerStarImpedance
documentation: Transformer star impedance calculated from this transformer end datasheet.

Geometric mean radius. If we replace the conductor by a thin walled tube
of radius GMR, then its reactance is identical to the reactance of the
actual conductor.

documentation: (if insulated conductor) Material used for insulation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WireInfo.insulationThickness
documentation: (if insulated conductor) Thickness of the insulation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WireInfo.material
documentation: Conductor material.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WireInfo.rAC25

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WireInfo.rAC50
documentation: AC resistance per unit length of the conductor at 50 °C.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WireInfo.rAC75
documentation: AC resistance per unit length of the conductor at 75 °C.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WireInfo.radius
documentation: Outside radius of the wire.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WireInfo.ratedCurrent
documentation: Current carrying capacity of the wire under stated thermal conditions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WireInfo.rDC20
documentation: DC resistance per unit length of the conductor at 20 °C.

Wire data that can be specified per line segment phase, or for the line

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CableInfo.shieldMaterial

Percentage of the tape shield width that overlaps in each wrap, typically

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WireSpacingInfo.isCable
documentation: If true, this spacing data describes a cable.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WireSpacingInfo.phaseWireCount
documentation:
Number of wire sub-conductors in the symmetrical bundle (typically between
1 and 4).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WireSpacingInfo.phaseWireSpacing
documentation: Distance between wire sub-conductors in a symmetrical bundle.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WireSpacingInfo.usage
documentation: Usage of the associated wires.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WireSpacingInfo.WirePositions
documentation: All positions of single wires (phase or neutral) making the conductor.

Wire spacing data that associates multiple wire positions with the line
segment, and allows to calculate line segment impedances. Number of phases
can be derived from the number of associated wire positions whose phase
is not neutral.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BaseFrequency

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BaseFrequency.frequency
documentation: The base frequency.

The class describe a base frequency for a power system network. In case
of multiple power networks with different frequencies, e.g. 50 or 60 Hertz
each network will have it's own base frequency class. Hence it is assumed
that power system objects having different base frequencies appear in separate
documents where each document has a single base frequency instance.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BasePower

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BasePower.basePower
documentation: Value used as base power.
The BasePower class defines the base power used in the per unit calculations.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BaseVoltage

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BaseVoltage.nominalVoltage
documentation: The power system resource's base voltage.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BaseVoltage.ConductingEquipment
documentation:
All conducting equipment with this base voltage. Use only when there is
no voltage level container used and only one base voltage applies. For
example, not used for transformers.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BaseVoltage.TopologicalNode
documentation: The topological nodes at the base voltage.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BaseVoltage.TransformerEnds

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BaseVoltage.VoltageLevel
documentation: The voltage levels having this base voltage.
Defines a system base voltage which is referenced.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BasicIntervalSchedule.value1Multiplier

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BasicIntervalSchedule.value1Unit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BasicIntervalSchedule.value2Multiplier

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BasicIntervalSchedule.value2Unit

Schedule of values at points in time.

The schedule has time points where the time between them varies.

specific date.

documentation:
The time between each pair of subsequent regular time points in sequence
order.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegularIntervalSchedule.TimePoints
documentation: The regular interval time point data values that define this schedule.
The schedule has time points where the time between them is constant.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.GenUnitOpSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.GenUnitOpSchedule.GeneratingUnit
documentation:
A generating unit may have an operating schedule, indicating the planned
operation of the unit.

The generating unit's Operator-approved current operating schedule (or
plan), typically produced with the aid of unit commitment type analyses.
The X-axis represents absolute time. The Y1-axis represents the status
(0=off-line and unavailable: 1=available: 2=must run: 3=must run at fixed
power value: etc.). The Y2-axis represents the must run fixed power value
where required.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SeasonDayTypeSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SeasonDayTypeSchedule.DayType
documentation: DayType for the Schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SeasonDayTypeSchedule.Season
documentation: Season for the Schedule.

A time schedule covering a 24 hour period, with curve data for a specific
type of season and day.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ConformLoadSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ConformLoadSchedule.ConformLoadGroup
documentation: The ConformLoadGroup where the ConformLoadSchedule belongs.

A curve of load versus time (X-axis) showing the active power values (Y1-axis)
and reactive power (Y2-axis) for each unit of the period covered. This
curve represents a typical pattern of load over the time period for a given
day type and season.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonConformLoadSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonConformLoadSchedule.NonConformLoadGroup
documentation: The NonConformLoadGroup where the NonConformLoadSchedule belongs.

An active power (Y1-axis) and reactive power (Y2-axis) schedule (curves)
versus time (X-axis) for non-conforming loads, e.g., large industrial load
or power station service (where modeled).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegulationSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegulationSchedule.RegulatingControl
documentation: Regulating controls that have this Schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegulationSchedule.VoltageControlZones
documentation: A VoltageControlZone may have a voltage regulation schedule.

A pre-established pattern over time for a controlled variable, e.g., busbar
voltage.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SwitchSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SwitchSchedule.Switch
documentation: A SwitchSchedule is associated with a Switch.

A schedule of switch positions. If RegularTimePoint.value1 is 0, the switch
is open. If 1, the switch is closed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BranchGroup.maximumReactivePower
documentation: The maximum reactive power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BranchGroup.minimumActivePower
documentation: The minimum active power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BranchGroup.minimumReactivePower
documentation: The minimum reactive power flow.

A group of branch terminals whose directed flow summation is to be monitored.
A branch group need not form a cutset of the network.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BusNameMarker

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BusNameMarker.priority
documentation:
Priority of bus name marker for use as topology bus name. Use 0 for don
t care. Use 1 for highest priority. Use 2 as priority is less than 1 and
so on.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BusNameMarker.ReportingGroup
documentation: The reporting group to which this bus name marker belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BusNameMarker.Terminal
documentation: The terminals associated with this bus name marker.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BusNameMarker.TopologicalNode
documentation:
A user defined topological node that was originally defined in a planning
model not yet having topology described by ConnectivityNodes. Once ConnectivityNodes
has been created they may linked to user defined ToplogicalNdes using BusNameMarkers.

Used to apply user standard names to topology buses. Typically used for
"bus/branch" case generation. Associated with one or more terminals that
are normally connected with the bus name. The associated terminals are
normally connected by non-retained switches. For a ring bus station configuration,
all busbar terminals in the ring are typically associated. For a breaker
and a half scheme, both busbars would normally be associated. For a ring
bus, all busbars would normally be associated. For a "straight" busbar
configuration, normally only the main terminal at the busbar would be associated.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CatalogAssetType

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CatalogAssetType.stockItem
documentation: True if item is a stock item (default).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CatalogAssetType.estimatedUnitCost
documentation:
Estimated unit cost (or cost per unit length) of this type of asset. It
does not include labor to install, construct or configure it.
assets that may be used for planning, work or design purposes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ConnectivityNode

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ConnectivityNode.ConnectivityNodeContainer
documentation: Container of this connectivity node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ConnectivityNode.Terminals
documentation: Terminals interconnected with zero impedance at a this connectivity node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ConnectivityNode.TopologicalNode
documentation:
The topological node to which this connectivity node is assigned. May depend
on the current state of switches in the network.

Connectivity nodes are points where terminals of AC conducting equipment
are connected together with zero impedance.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CoordinateSystem

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CoordinateSystem.crsUrn
documentation:
A Uniform Resource Name (URN) for the coordinate reference system (crs)
used to define 'Location.PositionPoints'.
An example would be the European Petroleum Survey Group (EPSG) code for
a coordinate reference system, defined in URN under the Open Geospatial
Consortium (OGC) namespace as: urn:ogc:def:uom:EPSG::XXXX, where XXXX is
an EPSG code (a full list of codes can be found at the EPSG Registry web
site http://www.epsg-registry.org/). To define the coordinate system as
being WGS84 (latitude, longitude) using an EPSG OGC, this attribute would
be urn:ogc:def:uom:EPSG::4236.
A profile should limit this code to a set of allowed URNs agreed to by
all sending and receiving parties.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CoordinateSystem.Locations
documentation: All locations described with position points in this coordinate system.
Coordinate reference system.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Curve

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Curve.curveStyle
documentation: The style or shape of the curve.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Curve.xMultiplier
documentation: Multiplier for X-axis.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Curve.xUnit
documentation: The X-axis units of measure.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Curve.y1Multiplier
documentation: Multiplier for Y1-axis.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Curve.y1Unit
documentation: The Y1-axis units of measure.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Curve.y2Multiplier
documentation: Multiplier for Y2-axis.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Curve.y2Unit
documentation: The Y2-axis units of measure.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Curve.y3Multiplier
documentation: Multiplier for Y3-axis.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Curve.y3Unit
documentation: The Y3-axis units of measure.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Curve.CurveDatas
documentation: The point data values that define this curve.

A multi-purpose curve or functional relationship between an independent
variable (X-axis) and dependent (Y-axis) variables.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.GrossToNetActivePowerCurve

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.GrossToNetActivePowerCurve.GeneratingUnit
documentation:
A generating unit may have a gross active power to net active power curve,
describing the losses and auxiliary power requirements of the unit.

Relationship between the generating unit's gross active power output on
the X-axis (measured at the terminals of the machine(s)) and the generating
unit's net active power output on the Y-axis (based on utility-defined
measurements at the power station). Station service loads, when modeled,
should be treated as non-conforming bus loads. There may be more than one
curve, depending on the auxiliary equipment that is in service.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ReactiveCapabilityCurve

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ReactiveCapabilityCurve.coolantTemperature
documentation:
The machine's coolant temperature (e.g., ambient air or stator circulating
water).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ReactiveCapabilityCurve.hydrogenPressure
documentation: The hydrogen coolant pressure

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ReactiveCapabilityCurve.SynchronousMachines
documentation: Synchronous machines using this curve.

Reactive power rating envelope versus the synchronous machine's active
power, in both the generating and motoring modes. For each active power
value there is a corresponding high and low reactive power limit value.
Typically there will be a separate curve for each coolant condition, such
as hydrogen pressure. The Y1 axis values represent reactive minimum and
the Y2 axis values represent reactive maximum.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Customer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Customer.locale
documentation: Locale designating language to use in communications with this customer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Customer.pucNumber
documentation: (if applicable) Public utilities commission (PUC) identification number.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Customer.specialNeed
documentation:
True if customer organisation has special service needs such as life support,
hospitals, etc.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Customer.vip
documentation:
(use 'priority' instead) True if this is an important customer. Importance
is for matters different than those in 'specialNeed' attribute.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Customer.kind
documentation: Kind of customer.
Organisation receiving services from service supplier.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CustomerAgreement

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CustomerAgreement.isPrePay
documentation: If true, the customer is a pre-pay customer for the specified service.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CustomerAgreement.loadMgmt
documentation: Load management code.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CustomerAgreement.shutOffDateTime
documentation: Final date and time the service will be billed to the previous customer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CustomerAgreement.Customer
documentation: Customer for this agreement.

Agreement between the customer and the service supplier to pay for service
at a specific service location. It records certain billing information
about the type of service provided at the service location and is used
during charge creation to determine the type of service.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DayType
documentation:
Group of similar days. For example it could be used to represent weekdays,
weekend, or holidays.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DemandResponseProgram

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DemandResponseProgram.type
documentation:
Type of demand response program; examples are CPP (critical-peak pricing),
RTP (real-time pricing), DLC (direct load control), DBP (demand bidding
program), BIP (base interruptible program). Note that possible types change
a lot and it would be impossible to enumerate them all.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DemandResponseProgram.CustomerAgreements
documentation:
All customer agreements through which the customer is enrolled in this
demand response program.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DemandResponseProgram.EndDeviceGroups
documentation: All groups of end devices enrolled in this demand response program.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DemandResponseProgram.UsagePointGroups
documentation: All usage point groups enrolled in this demand response program.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DemandResponseProgram.validityInterval
documentation: Interval within which the program is valid.
Demand response program.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EndDeviceControl
documentation:
Instructs an end device (or an end device group) to perform a specified
action.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EndDeviceGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EndDeviceGroup.type
documentation: Type of this group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EndDeviceGroup.DemandResponsePrograms
documentation: All demand response programs this group of end devices is enrolled in.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EndDeviceGroup.EndDeviceControls
documentation: All end device controls sending commands to this end device group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EndDeviceGroup.EndDevices
documentation: All end devices this end device group refers to.

Abstraction for management of group communications within a two-way AMR
system or the data for a group of related end devices. Commands can be
issued to all of the end devices that belong to the group using a defined
group address and the underlying AMR communication infrastructure. A DERGroup
and a PANDeviceGroup is an EndDeviceGroup.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyArea
documentation:
Describes an area having energy production or consumption. Specializations
are intended to support the load allocation function as typically required
in energy management systems or planning studies to allocate hypothesized
load levels to individual load points for power flow analysis. Often the
energy area can be linked to both measured and forecast load levels.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LoadArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LoadArea.SubLoadAreas
documentation: The SubLoadAreas in the LoadArea.

The class is the root or first level in a hierarchical structure for grouping
of loads for the purpose of load flow load scaling.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SubLoadArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SubLoadArea.LoadArea
documentation: The LoadArea where the SubLoadArea belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SubLoadArea.LoadGroups
documentation: The Loadgroups in the SubLoadArea.

The class is the second level in a hierarchical structure for grouping
of loads for the purpose of load flow load scaling.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConnectionProfile

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConnectionProfile.dssDaily
documentation:
Reference to OpenDSS Daily curve, for Load, Storage, PVSystem, Generator,
and WindGen power

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConnectionProfile.dssDuty
documentation:
Reference to OpenDSS Duty Cycle curve, for Load, Storage, PVSystem, Generator,
and WindGen power

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConnectionProfile.dssLoadCvrCurve
documentation: Reference to OpenDSS CvrCurve, for Load objects

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConnectionProfile.dssLoadGrowth
documentation: Reference to OpenDSS Growth curve, for Load objects

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConnectionProfile.dssPVTDaily
documentation: Reference to OpenDSS Daily curve, for PVSystem temperature

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConnectionProfile.dssPVTDuty
documentation: Reference to OpenDSS Duty Cycle curve, for PVSystem temperature

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConnectionProfile.dssPVTYearly
documentation: Reference to OpenDSS Yearly curve, for PVSystem temperature

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConnectionProfile.dssSpectrum
documentation:
Reference to OpenDSS harmonic current Spectrum, for Load, Storage, PVSystem,
Generator, and WindGen power

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConnectionProfile.dssYearly
documentation:
Reference to OpenDSS Yearly curve, for Load, Storage, PVSystem, Generator,
and WindGen power

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConnectionProfile.gldPlayer
documentation:
GridLAB-D Player for base_power attributes on Load and Triplex_Load objects,
and P_Out for Battery objects. Netlisted as player.value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConnectionProfile.gldSchedule
documentation:
GridLAB-D schedule for base_power attributes on Load and Triplex_Load objects,
and P_Out attributes on Battery objects.

Optional references to shapes for OpenDSS, and players or schedules for
GridLAB-D. See attribute documentation for applicability. The shapes, players,
and schedules are not maintained in CIM, i.e., they must be made available
to the simulator from an external source.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FunctionBlock

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FunctionBlock.enabled
documentation: True, if the function block is enabled (active). Otherwise false.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FunctionBlock.priority
documentation:
Value 0 means ignore priority. 1 means the highest priority, 2 is the second
highest priority.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FunctionBlock.FunctionOutputVariable
documentation:
Function output variable describe the output or codomain to the function
block.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FunctionBlock.Input
documentation: Function input variable describe the input or domain to the function block.

Function block is a function described as a set of elementary blocks. The
blocks describe the function between input variables and output variables.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FunctionInputVariable

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FunctionInputVariable.Function
documentation:
Function block describe the function that function input variable provides
the domain for.
Functional input variable defines the domain of the function.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FunctionOutputVariable

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FunctionOutputVariable.FunctionBlock
documentation:
Function block describe the function that function output variable provides
the codomain for.
Functional output variable defines the codomain of the function.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.GeographicalRegion

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.GeographicalRegion.Regions
documentation: All sub-geograhpical regions within this geographical region.
A geographical region of a power system network model.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.House
documentation:
In GridLAB-D, a single-family residence with building envelope represented
by the equivalent thermal parameter (ETP) model, heating, ventilating and
air conditioning (HVAC), other appliances, lights and plug loads. In power
flow, these house loads aggregate into ZIP loads. These house parameters
are the minimal set required to consistently initialize or repeat a GridLAB-D
simulation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.IOPoint
documentation:
The class describe a measurement or control value. The purpose is to enable
having attributes and associations common for measurement and control.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Control

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Control.controlType
documentation:
Specifies the type of Control, e.g. BreakerOn/Off, GeneratorVoltageSetPoint,
TieLineFlow etc. The ControlType.name shall be unique among all specified
types and describe the type.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Control.operationInProgress
documentation:
Indicates that a client is currently sending control commands that has
not completed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Control.timeStamp
documentation: The last time a control output was sent.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Control.unitMultiplier
documentation: The unit multiplier of the controlled quantity.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Control.unitSymbol
documentation: The unit of measure of the controlled quantity.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Control.PowerSystemResource
documentation: Regulating device governed by this control output.

Control is used for supervisory/device control. It represents control outputs
that are used to change the state in a process, e.g. close or open breaker,
a set point value or a raise lower command.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AccumulatorReset

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AccumulatorReset.AccumulatorValue
documentation: The accumulator value that is reset by the command.
This command reset the counter value to zero.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AnalogControl

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AnalogControl.maxValue
documentation:
Normal value range maximum for any of the Control.value. Used for scaling,
e.g. in bar graphs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AnalogControl.minValue
documentation:
Normal value range minimum for any of the Control.value. Used for scaling,
e.g. in bar graphs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AnalogControl.AnalogValue
documentation: The MeasurementValue that is controlled.
An analog control used for supervisory control.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RaiseLowerCommand

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RaiseLowerCommand.ValueAliasSet
documentation: The ValueAliasSet used for translation of a Control value to a name.

An analog control that increase or decrease a set point value with pulses.
Unless otherwise specified, one pulse moves the set point by one.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SetPoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SetPoint.normalValue
documentation: Normal value for Control.value e.g. used for percentage scaling.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SetPoint.value
documentation: The value representing the actuator output.
An analog control that issue a set point value.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Command

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Command.normalValue
documentation: Normal value for Control.value e.g. used for percentage scaling.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Command.value
documentation: The value representing the actuator output.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Command.DiscreteValue
documentation: The MeasurementValue that is controlled.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Command.ValueAliasSet
documentation: The ValueAliasSet used for translation of a Control value to a name.
A Command is a discrete control used for supervisory control.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MeasurementValue

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MeasurementValue.timeStamp
documentation: The time when the value was last updated

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MeasurementValue.sensorAccuracy
documentation:
The limit, expressed as a percentage of the sensor maximum, that errors
will not exceed when the sensor is used under reference conditions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MeasurementValue.MeasurementValueQuality
documentation: A MeasurementValue has a MeasurementValueQuality associated with it.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MeasurementValue.MeasurementValueSource
documentation:
A reference to the type of source that updates the MeasurementValue, e.g.
SCADA, CCLink, manual, etc. User conventions for the names of sources are
contained in the introduction to IEC 61970-301.

The current state for a measurement. A state value is an instance of a
measurement from a specific source. Measurements can be associated with
many state values, each representing a different source for the measurement.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AccumulatorValue

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AccumulatorValue.value
documentation: The value to supervise. The value is positive.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AccumulatorValue.Accumulator
documentation: Measurement to which this value is connected.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AccumulatorValue.AccumulatorReset
documentation: The command that reset the accumulator value.
AccumulatorValue represents an accumulated (counted) MeasurementValue.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AnalogValue

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AnalogValue.value
documentation: The value to supervise.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AnalogValue.Analog
documentation: Measurement to which this value is connected.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AnalogValue.AnalogControl
documentation: The Control variable associated with the MeasurementValue.
AnalogValue represents an analog MeasurementValue.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DiscreteValue

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DiscreteValue.value
documentation: The value to supervise.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DiscreteValue.Command
documentation: The Control variable associated with the MeasurementValue.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DiscreteValue.Discrete
documentation: Measurement to which this value is connected.
DiscreteValue represents a discrete MeasurementValue.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.StringMeasurementValue

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.StringMeasurementValue.value
documentation: The value to supervise.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.StringMeasurementValue.StringMeasurement
documentation: Measurement to which this value is connected.
StringMeasurementValue represents a measurement value of type string.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Limit
documentation:
Specifies one limit value for a Measurement. A Measurement typically has
several limits that are kept together by the LimitSet class. The actual
meaning and use of a Limit instance (i.e., if it is an alarm or warning
limit or if it is a high or low limit) is not captured in the Limit class.
However the name of a Limit instance may indicate both meaning and use.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AccumulatorLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AccumulatorLimit.value
documentation: The value to supervise against. The value is positive.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AccumulatorLimit.LimitSet
documentation: The set of limits.
Limit values for Accumulator measurements.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AnalogLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AnalogLimit.value
documentation: The value to supervise against.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AnalogLimit.LimitSet

Limit values for Analog measurements.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LimitSet

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LimitSet.isPercentageLimits
documentation:
Tells if the limit values are in percentage of normalValue or the specified
Unit for Measurements and Controls.

Specifies a set of Limits that are associated with a Measurement. A Measurement
may have several LimitSets corresponding to seasonal or other changing
conditions. The condition is captured in the name and description attributes.
The same LimitSet may be used for several Measurements. In particular percentage
limits are used this way.
