class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AccumulatorLimitSet.Limits
documentation: The limit values used for supervision of Measurements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AccumulatorLimitSet.Measurements
documentation: The Measurements using the LimitSet.

An AccumulatorLimitSet specifies a set of Limits that are associated with
an Accumulator measurement.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AnalogLimitSet

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AnalogLimitSet.Limits
documentation: The limit values used for supervision of Measurements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AnalogLimitSet.Measurements
documentation: The Measurements using the LimitSet.

An AnalogLimitSet specifies a set of Limits that are associated with an
Analog measurement.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LoadGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LoadGroup.SubLoadArea
documentation: The SubLoadArea where the Loadgroup belongs.

The class is the third level in a hierarchical structure for grouping of
loads for the purpose of load flow load scaling.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ConformLoadGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ConformLoadGroup.ConformLoadSchedules
documentation: The ConformLoadSchedules in the ConformLoadGroup.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ConformLoadGroup.EnergyConsumers
documentation: Conform loads assigned to this ConformLoadGroup.
A group of loads conforming to an allocation pattern.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonConformLoadGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonConformLoadGroup.EnergyConsumers
documentation: Conform loads assigned to this ConformLoadGroup.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonConformLoadGroup.NonConformLoadSchedules
documentation: The NonConformLoadSchedules in the NonConformLoadGroup.
Loads that do not follow a daily and seasonal load variation pattern.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LoadResponseCharacteristic

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LoadResponseCharacteristic.exponentModel
documentation:
Indicates the exponential voltage dependency model is to be used. If false,
the coefficient model is to be used.
The exponential voltage dependency model consist of the attributes
- pVoltageExponent
- qVoltageExponent.
The coefficient model consist of the attributes
- pConstantImpedance
- pConstantCurrent
- pConstantPower
- qConstantImpedance
- qConstantCurrent
- qConstantPower.
The sum of pConstantImpedance, pConstantCurrent and pConstantPower shall
equal 1.
The sum of qConstantImpedance, qConstantCurrent and qConstantPower shall
equal 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LoadResponseCharacteristic.pConstantCurrent
documentation: Portion of active power load modeled as constant current.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LoadResponseCharacteristic.pConstantImpedance
documentation: Portion of active power load modeled as constant impedance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LoadResponseCharacteristic.pConstantPower
documentation: Portion of active power load modeled as constant power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LoadResponseCharacteristic.pVoltageExponent
documentation: Exponent of per unit voltage effecting real power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LoadResponseCharacteristic.qConstantCurrent
documentation: Portion of reactive power load modeled as constant current.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LoadResponseCharacteristic.qConstantImpedance
documentation: Portion of reactive power load modeled as constant impedance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LoadResponseCharacteristic.qConstantPower
documentation: Portion of reactive power load modeled as constant power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LoadResponseCharacteristic.qVoltageExponent
documentation: Exponent of per unit voltage effecting reactive power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LoadResponseCharacteristic.EnergyConsumer
documentation: The set of loads that have the response characteristics.

Models the characteristic response of the load demand due to changes in
system conditions such as voltage and frequency. This is not related to
demand response.
If LoadResponseCharacteristic.exponentModel is True, the voltage exponents
are specified and used as to calculate:
Active power component = Pnominal * (Voltage/cim:BaseVoltage.nominalVoltage)
** cim:LoadResponseCharacteristic.pVoltageExponent
Reactive power component = Qnominal * (Voltage/cim:BaseVoltage.nominalVoltage)**
cim:LoadResponseCharacteristic.qVoltageExponent
Where * means "multiply" and ** is "raised to power of".

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Location

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Location.direction
documentation:
(if applicable) Direction that allows field crews to quickly find a given
asset. For a given location, such as a street address, this is the relative
direction in which to find the asset. For example, a streetlight may be
located at the 'NW' (northwest) corner of the customer's site, or a usage
point may be located on the second floor of an apartment building.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Location.geoInfoReference
documentation:
(if applicable) Reference to geographical information source, often external
to the utility.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Location.type
documentation:
Classification by utility's corporate standards and practices, relative
to the location itself (e.g., geographical, functional accounting, etc.,
not a given property that happens to exist at that location).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Location.Assets
documentation: All assets at this location.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Location.CoordinateSystem
documentation: Coordinate system used to describe position points of this location.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Location.PositionPoints
documentation:
Sequence of position points describing this location, expressed in coordinate
system 'Location.CoordinateSystem'.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Location.PowerSystemResources
documentation: All power system resources at this location.

The place, scene, or point of something where someone or something has
been, is, and/or will be at a given moment in time. It can be defined with
one or more postition points (coordinates) in a given coordinate system.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Measurement

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Measurement.measurementType
documentation:
Specifies the type of measurement. For example, this specifies if the measurement
represents an indoor temperature, outdoor temperature, bus voltage, line
flow, etc.
When the measurementType is set to "Specialization", the type of Measurement
is defined in more detail by the specialized class which inherits from
Measurement.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Measurement.phases
documentation:
Indicates to which phases the measurement applies and avoids the need to
use 'measurementType' to also encode phase information (which would explode
the types). The phase information in Measurement, along with 'measurementType'
and 'phases' uniquely defines a Measurement for a device, based on normal
network phase. Their meaning will not change when the computed energizing
phasing is changed due to jumpers or other reasons.
If the attribute is missing three phases (ABC) shall be assumed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Measurement.PowerSystemResource
documentation: The power system resource that contains the measurement.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Measurement.Terminal
documentation: One or more measurements may be associated with a terminal in the network.

A Measurement represents any measured, calculated or non-measured non-calculated
quantity. Any piece of equipment may contain Measurements, e.g. a substation
may have temperature measurements and door open indications, a transformer
may have oil temperature and tank pressure measurements, a bay may contain
a number of power flow measurements and a Breaker may contain a switch
status measurement.
The PSR - Measurement association is intended to capture this use of Measurement
and is included in the naming hierarchy based on EquipmentContainer. The
naming hierarchy typically has Measurements as leafs, e.g. Substation-VoltageLevel-Bay-Switch-Measurement.
Some Measurements represent quantities related to a particular sensor location
in the network, e.g. a voltage transformer (PT) at a busbar or a current
transformer (CT) at the bar between a breaker and an isolator. The sensing
position is not captured in the PSR - Measurement association. Instead
it is captured by the Measurement - Terminal association that is used to
define the sensing location in the network topology. The location is defined
by the connection of the Terminal to ConductingEquipment.
If both a Terminal and PSR are associated, and the PSR is of type ConductingEquipment,
the associated Terminal should belong to that ConductingEquipment instance.
When the sensor location is needed both Measurement-PSR and Measurement-Terminal
are used. The Measurement-Terminal association is never used alone.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Accumulator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Accumulator.maxValue
documentation:
Normal value range maximum for any of the MeasurementValue.values. Used
for scaling, e.g. in bar graphs or of telemetered raw values.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Accumulator.AccumulatorValues
documentation: The values connected to this measurement.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Accumulator.LimitSets
documentation: A measurement may have zero or more limit ranges defined for it.

Accumulator represents an accumulated (counted) Measurement, e.g. an energy
value.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Analog

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Analog.maxValue
documentation:
Normal value range maximum for any of the MeasurementValue.values. Used
for scaling, e.g. in bar graphs or of telemetered raw values.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Analog.minValue
documentation:
Normal value range minimum for any of the MeasurementValue.values. Used
for scaling, e.g. in bar graphs or of telemetered raw values.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Analog.normalValue
documentation: Normal measurement value, e.g., used for percentage calculations.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Analog.positiveFlowIn
documentation:
If true then this measurement is an active power, reactive power or current
with the convention that a positive value measured at the Terminal means
power is flowing into the related PowerSystemResource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Analog.AnalogValues
documentation: The values connected to this measurement.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Analog.LimitSets
documentation: A measurement may have zero or more limit ranges defined for it.
Analog represents an analog Measurement.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Discrete

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Discrete.maxValue
documentation:
Normal value range maximum for any of the MeasurementValue.values. Used
for scaling, e.g. in bar graphs or of telemetered raw values.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Discrete.minValue
documentation:
Normal value range minimum for any of the MeasurementValue.values. Used
for scaling, e.g. in bar graphs or of telemetered raw values.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Discrete.normalValue
documentation: Normal measurement value, e.g., used for percentage calculations.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Discrete.DiscreteValues
documentation: The values connected to this measurement.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Discrete.ValueAliasSet
documentation:
The ValueAliasSet used for translation of a MeasurementValue.value to a
name.

Discrete represents a discrete Measurement, i.e. a Measurement representing
discrete values, e.g. a Breaker position.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.StringMeasurement

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.StringMeasurement.StringMeasurementValues
documentation: The values connected to this measurement.
StringMeasurement represents a measurement with values of type string.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MeasurementValueSource

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MeasurementValueSource.MeasurementValues
documentation: The MeasurementValues updated by the source.

MeasurementValueSource describes the alternative sources updating a MeasurementValue.
User conventions for how to use the MeasurementValueSource attributes are
described in the introduction to IEC 61970-301.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MutualCoupling

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MutualCoupling.b0ch
documentation:
Zero sequence mutual coupling shunt (charging) susceptance, uniformly distributed,
of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MutualCoupling.distance11
documentation:
Distance to the start of the coupled region from the first line's terminal
having sequence number equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MutualCoupling.distance12
documentation:
Distance to the end of the coupled region from the first line's terminal
with sequence number equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MutualCoupling.distance21
documentation:
Distance to the start of coupled region from the second line's terminal
with sequence number equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MutualCoupling.distance22
documentation:
Distance to the end of coupled region from the second line's terminal with
sequence number equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MutualCoupling.g0ch
documentation:
Zero sequence mutual coupling shunt (charging) conductance, uniformly distributed,
of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MutualCoupling.r0
documentation: Zero sequence branch-to-branch mutual impedance coupling, resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MutualCoupling.x0
documentation: Zero sequence branch-to-branch mutual impedance coupling, reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MutualCoupling.First_Terminal
documentation:
The starting terminal for the calculation of distances along the first
branch of the mutual coupling. Normally MutualCoupling would only be used
for terminals of AC line segments. The first and second terminals of a
mutual coupling should point to different AC line segments.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MutualCoupling.Second_Terminal
documentation:
The starting terminal for the calculation of distances along the second
branch of the mutual coupling.
This class represents the zero sequence line mutual coupling.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OperatingParticipant

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OperatingParticipant.OperatingShare
documentation:
The operating shares of this operating participant. An operating participant
can be resused for any number of power system resources.

An operator of multiple power system resource objects. Note multple operating
participants may operate the same power system resource object. This can
be used for modeling jointly owned units where each owner operates as a
contractual share.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OperationalLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OperationalLimit.OperationalLimitSet
documentation: The limit set to which the limit values belong.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OperationalLimit.OperationalLimitType
documentation: The limit type associated with this limit.

A value associated with a specific kind of limit.
The sub class value attribute shall be positive.
The sub class value attribute is inversely proportional to OperationalLimitType.acceptableDuration
(acceptableDuration for short). A pair of value_x and acceptableDuration_x
are related to each other as follows:
if value_1 > value_2 > value_3 >... then
acceptableDuration_1 < acceptableDuration_2 < acceptableDuration_3
< ...
A value_x with direction="high" shall be greater than a value_y with direction="low".

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ActivePowerLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ActivePowerLimit.normalValue
documentation: The normal value of active power limit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ActivePowerLimit.value
documentation: Value of active power limit.
Limit on active power flow.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ApparentPowerLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ApparentPowerLimit.normalValue
documentation: The normal apparent power limit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ApparentPowerLimit.value
documentation: The apparent power limit.
Apparent power limit.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CurrentLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CurrentLimit.normalValue
documentation: The normal value for limit on current flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CurrentLimit.value
documentation: Limit on current flow.
Operational limit on current.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.VoltageLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.VoltageLimit.normalValue
documentation:
The normal limit on voltage. High or low limit nature of the limit depends
upon the properties of the operational limit type.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.VoltageLimit.value
documentation:
Limit on voltage. High or low limit nature of the limit depends upon the
properties of the operational limit type.
Operational limit applied to voltage.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OperationalLimitSet

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OperationalLimitSet.Equipment
documentation: The equipment to which the limit set applies.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OperationalLimitSet.OperationalLimitValue
documentation: Values of equipment limits.

A set of limits associated with equipment. Sets of limits might apply to
a specific temperature, or season for example. A set of limits may contain
different severities of limit levels that would apply to the same equipment.
The set may contain limits of different types such as apparent power and
current limits or high and low voltage limits that are logically applied
together as a set.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OperationalLimitType

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OperationalLimitType.acceptableDuration
documentation:
The nominal acceptable duration of the limit. Limits are commonly expressed
in terms of the a time limit for which the limit is normally acceptable.
The actual acceptable duration of a specific limit may depend on other
local factors such as temperature or wind speed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OperationalLimitType.direction
documentation: The direction of the limit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OperationalLimitType.OperationalLimit
documentation: The operational limits associated with this type of limit.
The operational meaning of a category of limits.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PSRType

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PSRType.PowerSystemResources
documentation: Power system resources classified with this power system resource type.

Classifying instances of the same class, e.g. overhead and underground
ACLineSegments. This classification mechanism is intended to provide flexibility
outside the scope of this standard, i.e. provide customisation that is
non standard.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PerLengthLineParameter

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PerLengthLineParameter.WireAssemblyInfo
documentation:
A WireAssemblyInfo is used to compute the PerLengthParameter data in the
Wires package
Common type for per-length electrical catalogues describing line parameters.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PerLengthImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PerLengthImpedance.ACLineSegments
documentation: All line segments described by this per-length impedance.
Common type for per-length impedance electrical catalogues.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PerLengthPhaseImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PerLengthPhaseImpedance.conductorCount
documentation:
Number of phase, neutral, and other wires retained. Constrains the number
of matrix elements and the phase codes that can be used with this matrix.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PerLengthPhaseImpedance.PhaseImpedanceData
documentation: All data that belong to this conductor phase impedance.

Impedance and admittance parameters per unit length for n-wire unbalanced
lines, in matrix form.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PerLengthSequenceImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.b0ch
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) susceptance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.bch
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) susceptance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.g0ch
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) conductance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.gch
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.r
documentation: Positive sequence series resistance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.r0
documentation: Zero sequence series resistance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.x
documentation: Positive sequence series reactance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.x0
documentation: Zero sequence series reactance, per unit of length.

Sequence impedance and admittance parameters per unit length, for transposed
lines of 1, 2, or 3 phases. For 1-phase lines, define x=x0=xself. For 2-phase
lines, define x=xs-xm and x0=xs+xm.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChangerTable

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChangerTable.PhaseTapChangerTablePoint
documentation: The points of this table.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChangerTable.PhaseTapChangerTabular
documentation: The phase tap changers to which this phase tap table applies.

Describes a tabular curve for how the phase angle difference and impedance
varies with the tap step.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerSystemResource

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerSystemResource.AssetDatasheet
documentation: Datasheet information for this power system resource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerSystemResource.Assets
documentation:
All assets represented by this power system resource. For example, multiple
conductor assets are electrically modelled as a single AC line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerSystemResource.Controls
documentation:
The controller outputs used to actually govern a regulating device, e.g.
the magnetization of a synchronous machine or capacitor bank breaker actuator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerSystemResource.Location
documentation: Location of this power system resource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerSystemResource.Measurements
documentation: The measurements associated with this power system resource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerSystemResource.PSRType
documentation: Custom classification for this power system resource.

A power system resource can be an item of equipment such as a switch, an
equipment container containing many individual items of equipment such
as a substation, or an organisational entity such as sub-control area.
Power system resources can have measurements associated.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACLineSegmentPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACLineSegmentPhase.sequenceNumber
documentation:
Number designation for this line segment phase. Each line segment phase
within a line segment should have a unique sequence number. This is useful
for unbalanced modeling to bind the mathematical model (PhaseImpedanceData
of PerLengthPhaseImpedance) with the connectivity model (this class) and
the physical model (WirePosition, WirePhaseInfo) without tight coupling.
Multiple circuits on the same pole, tower or right-of-way can be included
with unique sequence numbers for the phases, and identical sequence numbers
for any shared neutrals.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACLineSegmentPhase.phase
documentation: The phase connection of the wire at both ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACLineSegmentPhase.ACLineSegment
documentation: The line segment to which the phase belongs.
Represents a single wire of an alternating current line segment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ConnectivityNodeContainer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ConnectivityNodeContainer.ConnectivityNodes
documentation: Connectivity nodes which belong to this connectivity node container.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ConnectivityNodeContainer.TopologicalNode
documentation: The topological nodes which belong to this connectivity node container.

A base class for all objects that may contain connectivity nodes or topological
nodes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EquipmentContainer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EquipmentContainer.AdditionalGroupedEquipment
documentation:
The additonal contained equipment. The equipment belong to the equipment
container. The equipment is contained in another equipment container, but
also grouped with this equipment container. Examples include when a switch
contained in a substation is also desired to be grouped with a line contianer
or when a switch is included in a secondary substation and also grouped
in a feeder.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EquipmentContainer.Equipments
documentation: Contained equipment.
A modeling construct to provide a root class for containing equipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Bay

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Bay.bayEnergyMeasFlag
documentation: Indicates the presence/absence of energy measurements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Bay.bayPowerMeasFlag
documentation: Indicates the presence/absence of active/reactive power measurements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Bay.breakerConfiguration
documentation: Breaker configuration.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Bay.busBarConfiguration
documentation: Bus bar configuration.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Bay.Substation
documentation: Substation containing the bay.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Bay.VoltageLevel
documentation: The voltage level containing this bay.

A collection of power system resources (within a given substation) including
conducting equipment, protection relays, measurements, and telemetry. A
bay typically represents a physical grouping related to modularization
of equipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Feeder

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Feeder.DistributionArea
documentation: The DistributionArea to which the feeder belongs

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Feeder.FeederArea
documentation: The FeederArea (which contains Equipment not contained in

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Feeder.NamingSecondarySubstation
documentation:
The secondary substations that are normally energized from the feeder.
Used for naming purposes. Should be consistent with the other associations
for energizing terminal specification and the feeder energization specification.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Feeder.NormalEnergizedSubstation
documentation: The substations that are normally energized by the feeder.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Feeder.NormalEnergizingSubstation
documentation:
The substation that nominally energizes the feeder. Also used for naming
purposes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Feeder.NormalHeadTerminal
documentation: The normal head terminal or terminals of the feeder.

A collection of equipment for organizational purposes, used for grouping
distribution resources.
The organization a feeder does not necessarily reflect connectivity or
current operation state.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Line

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Line.Region
documentation: The sub-geographical region of the line.

Contains equipment beyond a substation belonging to a power transmission
line.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Plant
documentation: A Plant is a collection of equipment for purposes of generation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Substation

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Substation.Bays
documentation: Bays contained in the substation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Substation.NamingFeeder
documentation:
The primary feeder that normally energizes the secondary substation. Used
for naming purposes. Either this association or the substation to subgeographical
region should be used for hiearchical containment specification.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Substation.NormalEnergizedFeeder
documentation: The normal energized feeders of the substation. Also used for naming purposes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Substation.NormalEnergizingFeeder
documentation:
The feeders that potentially energize the downstream substation. Should
be consistent with the associations that describe the naming hiearchy.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Substation.Region
documentation: The SubGeographicalRegion containing the substation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Substation.VoltageLevels
documentation: The voltage levels within this substation.

A collection of equipment for purposes other than generation or utilization,
through which electric energy in bulk is passed for the purposes of switching
or modifying its characteristics.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.VoltageLevel

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.VoltageLevel.highVoltageLimit
documentation: The bus bar's high voltage limit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.VoltageLevel.lowVoltageLimit
documentation: The bus bar's low voltage limit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.VoltageLevel.BaseVoltage
documentation: The base voltage used for all equipment within the voltage level.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.VoltageLevel.Bays
documentation: The bays within this voltage level.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.VoltageLevel.Substation
documentation: The substation of the voltage level.

A collection of equipment at one common system voltage forming a switchgear.
The equipment typically consist of breakers, busbars, instrumentation,
control, regulation and protection devices as well as assemblies of all
these.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConsumerPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConsumerPhase.p
documentation:
Active power of the load. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
For voltage dependent loads the value is at rated voltage.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConsumerPhase.phase
documentation:
Phase of this energy consumer component. If the energy consumer is wye
connected, the connection is from the indicated phase to the central ground
or neutral point. If the energy consumer is delta connected, the phase
indicates an energy consumer connected from the indicated phase to the
next logical non-neutral phase.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConsumerPhase.q
documentation:
Reactive power of the load. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive
sign means flow out from a node.
For voltage dependent loads the value is at rated voltage.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConsumerPhase.EnergyConsumer
documentation: The energy consumer to which this phase belongs.
A single phase of an energy consumer.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergySourcePhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergySourcePhase.phase
documentation:
Phase of this energy source component. If the energy source wye connected,
the connection is from the indicated phase to the central ground or neutral
point. If the energy source is delta connected, the phase indicates an
energy source connected from the indicated phase to the next logical non-neutral
phase.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergySourcePhase.EnergySource
documentation: The energy sourceto which the phase belongs.
Represents the single phase information of an unbalanced energy source.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Equipment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Equipment.aggregate
documentation:
The single instance of equipment represents multiple pieces of equipment
that have been modeled together as an aggregate. Examples would be power
transformers or synchronous machines operating in parallel modeled as a
single aggregate power transformer or aggregate synchronous machine. This
is not to be used to indicate equipment that is part of a group of interdependent
equipment produced by a network production program.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Equipment.inService
documentation: If true, the equipment is in service.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Equipment.networkAnalysisEnabled
documentation:
The equipment is enabled to participate in network analysis. If unspecified,
the value is assumed to be true.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Equipment.normallyInService
documentation: If true, the equipment is normally in service.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Equipment.AdditionalEquipmentContainer
documentation:
Additional equipment container beyond the primary equipment container.
The equipment is contained in another equipment container, but also grouped
with this equipment container.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Equipment.EquipmentContainer
documentation: Container of this equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Equipment.OperationalLimitSet
documentation: The operational limit sets associated with this equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Equipment.SubSchedulingArea
documentation: The SubSchedulingArea in which the equipment is contained and controlled.
The parts of a power system that are physical devices, electronic or mechanical.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CompositeSwitch

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CompositeSwitch.compositeSwitchType
documentation:
An alphanumeric code that can be used as a reference to extra information
such as the description of the interlocking scheme if any.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CompositeSwitch.Switches
documentation: Switches contained in this Composite switch.

A model of a set of individual Switches normally enclosed within the same
cabinet and possibly with interlocks that restrict the combination of switch
positions. These are typically found in medium voltage distribution networks.
A CompositeSwitch could represent a Ring-Main-Unit (RMU), or pad-mounted
switchgear, with primitive internal devices such as an internal bus-bar
plus 3 or 4 internal switches each of which may individually be open or
closed. A CompositeSwitch and a set of contained Switches can also be used
to represent a multi-position switch e.g. a switch that can connect a circuit
to Ground, Open or Busbar.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ConductingEquipment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ConductingEquipment.BaseVoltage
documentation:
Base voltage of this conducting equipment. Use only when there is no voltage
level container used and only one base voltage applies. For example, not
used for transformers.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ConductingEquipment.Terminals
documentation:
Conducting equipment have terminals that may be connected to other conducting
equipment terminals via connectivity nodes or topological nodes.

The parts of the AC power system that are designed to carry current or
that are conductively connected through terminals.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Clamp

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Clamp.lengthFromTerminal1
documentation:
The length to the place where the clamp is located starting from side one
of the line segment, i.e. the line segment terminal with sequence number
equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Clamp.ACLineSegment
documentation: The line segment to which the clamp is connected.

A Clamp is a galvanic connection at a line segment where other equipment
is connected. A Clamp does not cut the line segment.
A Clamp is ConductingEquipment and has one Terminal with an associated
ConnectivityNode. Any other ConductingEquipment can be connected to the
Clamp ConnectivityNode.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Conductor

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Conductor.length
documentation: Segment length for calculating line section capabilities

Combination of conducting material with consistent electrical characteristics,
building a single electrical system, used to carry current between points
in the power system.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACLineSegment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACLineSegment.b0ch
documentation:
Zero sequence shunt (charging) susceptance, uniformly distributed, of the
entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACLineSegment.bch
documentation:
Positive sequence shunt (charging) susceptance, uniformly distributed,
of the entire line section. This value represents the full charging over
the full length of the line.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACLineSegment.g0ch
documentation:
Zero sequence shunt (charging) conductance, uniformly distributed, of the
entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACLineSegment.gch
documentation:
Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance, uniformly distributed,
of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACLineSegment.r
documentation: Positive sequence series resistance of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACLineSegment.r0
documentation: Zero sequence series resistance of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACLineSegment.shortCircuitEndTemperature
documentation:
Maximum permitted temperature at the end of SC for the calculation of minimum
short-circuit currents. Used for short circuit data exchange according
to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACLineSegment.x
documentation: Positive sequence series reactance of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACLineSegment.x0
documentation: Zero sequence series reactance of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACLineSegment.ACLineSegmentPhases
documentation: The line segment phases which belong to the line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ACLineSegment.PerLengthImpedance
documentation: Per-length impedance of this line segment.

A wire or combination of wires, with consistent electrical characteristics,
building a single electrical system, used to carry alternating current
between points in the power system.
For symmetrical, transposed 3ph lines, it is sufficient to use attributes
of the line segment, which describe impedances and admittances for the
entire length of the segment. Additionally impedances can be computed by
using length and associated per length impedances.
The BaseVoltage at the two ends of ACLineSegments in a Line shall have
the same BaseVoltage.nominalVoltage. However, boundary lines may have slightly
different BaseVoltage.nominalVoltages and variation is allowed. Larger
voltage difference in general requires use of an equivalent branch.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Connector
documentation:
A conductor, or group of conductors, with negligible impedance, that serve
to connect other conducting equipment within a single substation and are
modelled with a single logical terminal.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BusbarSection

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BusbarSection.ipMax
documentation:
Maximum allowable peak short-circuit current of busbar (Ipmax in the IEC
60909-0).
Mechanical limit of the busbar in the substation itself. Used for short
circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BusbarSection.VoltageControlZone
documentation: A VoltageControlZone is controlled by a designated BusbarSection.

A conductor, or group of conductors, with negligible impedance, that serve
to connect other conducting equipment within a single substation.
Voltage measurements are typically obtained from VoltageTransformers that
are connected to busbar sections. A bus bar section may have many physical
terminals but for analysis is modelled with exactly one logical terminal.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Junction
documentation:
A point where one or more conducting equipments are connected with zero
resistance.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EarthFaultCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EarthFaultCompensator.r
documentation: Nominal resistance of device.

A conducting equipment used to represent a connection to ground which is
typically used to compensate earth faults.. An earth fault compensator
device modeled with a single terminal implies a second terminal solidly
connected to ground. If two terminals are modeled, the ground is not assumed
and normal connection rules apply.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.GroundingImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.GroundingImpedance.x
documentation: Reactance of device.
A fixed impedance device used for grounding.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PetersenCoil

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PetersenCoil.nominalU
documentation: The nominal voltage for which the coil is designed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PetersenCoil.offsetCurrent
documentation:
The offset current that the Petersen coil controller is operating from
the resonant point. This is normally a fixed amount for which the controller
is configured and could be positive or negative. Typically 0 to 60 Amperes
depending on voltage and resonance conditions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PetersenCoil.positionCurrent
documentation:
The control current used to control the Petersen coil also known as the
position current. Typically in the range of 20-200mA.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PetersenCoil.xGroundMax
documentation: The maximum reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PetersenCoil.xGroundMin
documentation: The minimum reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PetersenCoil.xGroundNominal
documentation:
The nominal reactance. This is the operating point (normally over compensation)
that is defined based on the resonance point in the healthy network condition.
The impedance is calculated based on nominal voltage divided by position
current.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PetersenCoil.mode
documentation: The mode of operation of the Petersen coil.

A tunable impedance device normally used to offset line charging during
single line faults in an ungrounded section of network.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConsumer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConsumer.customerCount
documentation: Number of individual customers represented by this demand.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConsumer.grounded
documentation: Used for Yn and Zn connections. True if the neutral is solidly grounded.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConsumer.p
documentation:
Active power of the load. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
For voltage dependent loads the value is at rated voltage.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConsumer.phaseConnection
documentation: The type of phase connection, such as wye or delta.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConsumer.q
documentation:
Reactive power of the load. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive
sign means flow out from a node.
For voltage dependent loads the value is at rated voltage.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConsumer.EnergyConsumerPhase
documentation: The individual phase models for this energy consumer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConsumer.LoadResponse
documentation:
The load response characteristic of this load. If missing, this load is
assumed to be constant power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.EnergyConsumer.PowerCutZone
documentation: The energy consumer is assigned to this power cut zone.
Generic user of energy - a point of consumption on the power system model.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ConformLoad

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ConformLoad.LoadGroup
documentation: Group of this ConformLoad.

ConformLoad represent loads that follow a daily load change pattern where
the pattern can be used to scale the load with a system load.

documentation: Group of this ConformLoad.

NonConformLoad represent loads that do not follow a daily load change pattern
and changes are not correlated with the daily load change pattern.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.StationSupply
documentation: Station supply with load derived from the station output.

Maximum ratio of zero sequence resistance of Network Feeder to its zero

documentation:
Maximum ratio of zero sequence impedance to its positive sequence impedance
(Z(0)/Z(1) max). Used for short circuit data exchange according to IEC
60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ExternalNetworkInjection.minR0ToX0Ratio
documentation:
Indicates whether initial symmetrical short-circuit current and power have
been calculated according to IEC (Ik"). Used for short circuit data exchange

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ExternalNetworkInjection.minR1ToX1Ratio
documentation:
Minimum ratio of positive sequence resistance of Network Feeder to its
positive sequence reactance (R(1)/X(1) min). Used for short circuit data
exchange according to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ExternalNetworkInjection.minZ0ToZ1Ratio
documentation:
Minimum ratio of zero sequence impedance to its positive sequence impedance
(Z(0)/Z(1) min). Used for short circuit data exchange according to IEC
60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ExternalNetworkInjection.referencePriority
documentation:
Priority of unit for use as powerflow voltage phase angle reference bus
selection. 0 = don t care (default) 1 = highest priority. 2 is less than
1 and so on.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ExternalNetworkInjection.governorSCD
documentation:
Power Frequency Bias. This is the change in power injection divided by
the change in frequency and negated. A positive value of the power frequency
bias provides additional power injection upon a drop in frequency.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxInitialSymShCCurrent
documentation:
Maximum initial symmetrical short-circuit currents (Ik" max) in A (Ik"
= Sk"/(SQRT(3) Un)). Used for short circuit data exchange according to
IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxP
documentation: Maximum active power of the injection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxQ
documentation:
Not for short circuit modelling; It is used for modelling of infeed for
load flow exchange. If maxQ and minQ are not used ReactiveCapabilityCurve
can be used

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ExternalNetworkInjection.minInitialSymShCCurrent
documentation:
Minimum initial symmetrical short-circuit currents (Ik" min) in A (Ik"
= Sk"/(SQRT(3) Un)). Used for short circuit data exchange according to
IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ExternalNetworkInjection.minP
documentation: Minimum active power of the injection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ExternalNetworkInjection.minQ
documentation:
Not for short circuit modelling; It is used for modelling of infeed for
load flow exchange. If maxQ and minQ are not used ReactiveCapabilityCurve

Active power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign

Starting value for steady state solutions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ExternalNetworkInjection.q

Starting value for steady state solutions.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShuntCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShuntCompensator.grounded
documentation: Used for Yn and Zn connections. True if the neutral is solidly grounded.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShuntCompensator.maximumSections

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShuntCompensator.normalSections

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShuntCompensator.sections
documentation:
Shunt compensator sections in use.
Starting value for steady state solution. Non integer values are allowed
to support continuous variables. The reasons for continuous value are to
support study cases where no discrete shunt compensators has yet been designed,
a solutions where a narrow voltage band force the sections to oscillate
or accommodate for a continuous solution as input.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShuntCompensator.aVRDelay
documentation:

voltage regulation (AVR).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShuntCompensator.nomU
documentation:
The voltage at which the nominal reactive power may be calculated. This
should normally be within 10% of the voltage at which the capacitor is
connected to the network.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShuntCompensator.phaseConnection
documentation: The type of phase connection, such as wye or delta.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShuntCompensator.ShuntCompensatorPhase
documentation: The individual phases models for the shunt compensator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShuntCompensator.SvShuntCompensatorSections
documentation: The state for the number of shunt compensator sections in service.

A shunt capacitor or reactor or switchable bank of shunt capacitors or
reactors. A section of a shunt compensator is an individual capacitor or
reactor. A negative value for reactivePerSection indicates that the compensator
is a reactor. ShuntCompensator is a single terminal device. Ground is implied.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LinearShuntCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LinearShuntCompensator.b0PerSection
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) susceptance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LinearShuntCompensator.bPerSection
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) susceptance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LinearShuntCompensator.g0PerSection
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) conductance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LinearShuntCompensator.gPerSection
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance per section

A linear shunt compensator has banks or sections with equal admittance
values.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonlinearShuntCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonlinearShuntCompensator.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoints
documentation: All points of the non-linear shunt compensator.

A non linear shunt compensator has bank or section admittance values that
differs.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.StaticVarCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.StaticVarCompensator.capacitiveRating
documentation: Maximum available capacitive reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.StaticVarCompensator.inductiveRating
documentation: Maximum available inductive reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.StaticVarCompensator.q
documentation:
Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.StaticVarCompensator.slope
documentation:
The characteristics slope of an SVC defines how the reactive power output
changes in proportion to the difference between the regulated bus voltage
and the voltage setpoint.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.StaticVarCompensator.sVCControlMode
documentation: SVC control mode.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.StaticVarCompensator.voltageSetPoint
documentation:
The reactive power output of the SVC is proportional to the difference
between the voltage at the regulated bus and the voltage setpoint. When
the regulated bus voltage is equal to the voltage setpoint, the reactive
power output is zero.

A facility for providing variable and controllable shunt reactive power.
The SVC typically consists of a stepdown transformer, filter, thyristor-controlled
reactor, and thyristor-switched capacitor arms.
The SVC may operate in fixed MVar output mode or in voltage control mode.
When in voltage control mode, the output of the SVC will be proportional
to the deviation of voltage at the controlled bus from the voltage setpoint.
The SVC characteristic slope defines the proportion. If the voltage at
the controlled bus is equal to the voltage setpoint, the SVC MVar output
is zero.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Ground
documentation:
A point where the system is grounded used for connecting conducting equipment
to ground. The power system model can have any number of grounds.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerTransformer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerTransformer.vectorGroup
documentation:
Vector group of the transformer for protective relaying, e.g., Dyn1. For
unbalanced transformers, this may not be simply determined from the constituent
winding connections and phase angle dispacements.
The vectorGroup string consists of the following components in the order
listed: high voltage winding connection, mid voltage winding connection
(for three winding transformers), phase displacement clock number from
0 to 11, low voltage winding connection
phase displacement clock number from 0 to 11. The winding connections are
D (delta), Y (wye), YN (wye with neutral), Z (zigzag), ZN (zigzag with
neutral), A (auto transformer). Upper case means the high voltage, lower
case mid or low. The high voltage winding always has clock postion 0 and
is not included in the vector group string. Some examples: YNy0 (two winding
wye to wye with no phase displacement), YNd11 (two winding wye to delta
with 330 degrees phase displacement), YNyn0d5 (three winding transformer
wye with neutral high voltgage, wye with neutral mid voltgage and no phase
displacement, delta low voltage with 150 degrees displacement).
Phase displacement is defined as the angular difference between the phasors
representing the voltages between the neutral point (real or imaginary)
and the corresponding terminals of two windings, a positive sequence voltage
system being applied to the high-voltage terminals, following each other
in alphabetical sequence if they are lettered, or in numerical sequence
if they are numbered: the phasors are assumed to rotate in a counter-clockwise
sense.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerTransformer.PowerTransformerEnd
documentation: The ends of this power transformer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerTransformer.TransformerTanks
documentation: All transformers that belong to this bank.

An electrical device consisting of two or more coupled windings, with or
without a magnetic core, for introducing mutual coupling between electric
circuits. Transformers can be used to control voltage and phase shift (active
power flow).
A power transformer may be composed of separate transformer tanks that
need not be identical.
A power transformer can be modeled with or without tanks and is intended
for use in both balanced and unbalanced representations. A power transformer
typically has two terminals, but may have one (grounding), three or more
terminals.
The inherited association ConductingEquipment.BaseVoltage should not be
used. The association from TransformerEnd to BaseVoltage should be used
instead.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SeriesCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SeriesCompensator.varistorPresent
documentation:
Describe if a metal oxide varistor (mov) for over voltage protection is
configured at the series compensator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SeriesCompensator.r
documentation: Positive sequence resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SeriesCompensator.r0
documentation: Zero sequence resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SeriesCompensator.varistorRatedCurrent
documentation: The maximum current the varistor is designed to handle at specified duration.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SeriesCompensator.varistorVoltageThreshold
documentation: The dc voltage at which the varistor start conducting.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SeriesCompensator.x
documentation: Positive sequence reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SeriesCompensator.x0
documentation: Zero sequence reactance.

A Series Compensator is a series capacitor or reactor or an AC transmission
line without charging susceptance. It is a two terminal device.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Switch

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Switch.normalOpen
documentation:
The attribute is used in cases when no Measurement for the status value
is present. If the Switch has a status measurement the Discrete.normalValue
is expected to match with the Switch.normalOpen.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Switch.open
documentation:
The attribute tells if the switch is considered open when used as input
to topology processing.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Switch.retained
documentation:
Branch is retained in a bus branch model. The flow through retained switches
will normally be calculated in power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Switch.ratedCurrent
documentation:
The maximum continuous current carrying capacity in amps governed by the
device material and construction.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Switch.CompositeSwitch
documentation: Composite switch to which this Switch belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Switch.SwitchPhase
documentation: The individual switch phases for the switch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Switch.SwitchSchedules
documentation: A Switch can be associated with SwitchSchedules.

A generic device designed to close, or open, or both, one or more electric
circuits. All switches are two terminal devices including grounding switches.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Cut

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Cut.lengthFromTerminal1
documentation:
The length to the place where the cut is located starting from side one
of the cut line segment, i.e. the line segment Terminal with sequenceNumber
equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Cut.ACLineSegment
documentation: The line segment to which the cut is applied.

A cut separates a line segment into two parts. The cut appears as a switch
inserted between these two parts and connects them together. As the cut
is normally open there is no galvanic connection between the two line segment
parts. But it is possible to close the cut to get galvanic connection.
The cut terminals are oriented towards the line segment terminals with
the same sequence number. Hence the cut terminal with sequence number equal
to 1 is oriented to the line segment's terminal with sequence number equal
to 1.
The cut terminals also act as connection points for jumpers and other equipment,
e.g. a mobile generator. To enable this, connectivity nodes are placed
at the cut terminals. Once the connectivity nodes are in place any conducting
equipment can be connected at them.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Disconnector
documentation:
A manually operated or motor operated mechanical switching device used
for changing the connections in a circuit, or for isolating a circuit or
equipment from a source of power. It is required to open or close circuits
when negligible current is broken or made.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Fuse
documentation:
An overcurrent protective device with a circuit opening fusible part that
is heated and severed by the passage of overcurrent through it. A fuse
is considered a switching device because it breaks current.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.GroundDisconnector
documentation:
A manually operated or motor operated mechanical switching device used
for isolating a circuit or equipment from ground.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Jumper
documentation:
A short section of conductor with negligible impedance which can be manually
removed and replaced if the circuit is de-energized. Note that zero-impedance
branches can potentially be modeled by other equipment types.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ProtectedSwitch

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ProtectedSwitch.breakingCapacity
documentation:
The maximum fault current a breaking device can break safely under prescribed
conditions of use.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ProtectedSwitch.OperatedByProtectionEquipment
documentation: Protection equipments that operate this ProtectedSwitch.
A ProtectedSwitch is a switching device that can be operated by ProtectionEquipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Breaker

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Breaker.inTransitTime
documentation: The transition time from open to close.

A mechanical switching device capable of making, carrying, and breaking
currents under normal circuit conditions and also making, carrying for
a specified time, and breaking currents under specified abnormal circuit
conditions e.g. those of short circuit.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LoadBreakSwitch
documentation:
A mechanical switching device capable of making, carrying, and breaking
currents under normal operating conditions.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Recloser
documentation:
Pole-mounted fault interrupter with built-in phase and ground relays, current
transformer (CT), and supplemental controls.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Sectionaliser
documentation:
Automatic switch that will lock open to isolate a faulted section. It may,
or may not, have load breaking capability. Its primary purpose is to provide
fault sectionalising at locations where the fault current is either too
high, or too low, for proper coordination of fuses.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.GeneratingUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.GeneratingUnit.maxOperatingP
documentation:
This is the maximum operating active power limit the dispatcher can enter
for this unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.GeneratingUnit.minOperatingP
documentation:
This is the minimum operating active power limit the dispatcher can enter
for this unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.GeneratingUnit.GrossToNetActivePowerCurves
documentation:
A generating unit may have a gross active power to net active power curve,
describing the losses and auxiliary power requirements of the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.GeneratingUnit.RotatingMachine
documentation:
A synchronous machine may operate as a generator and as such becomes a
member of a generating unit.

A single or set of synchronous machines for converting mechanical power
into alternating-current power. For example, individual machines within
a set may be defined for scheduling purposes while a single control signal
is derived for the set. In this case there would be a GeneratingUnit for
each member of the set and an additional GeneratingUnit corresponding to
the set.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SolarGeneratingUnit
documentation:
A solar thermal generating unit, connected to the grid by means of a rotating
machine. This class does not represent photovoltaic (PV) generation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WindGeneratingUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WindGeneratingUnit.windGenUnitType
documentation: The kind of wind generating unit

A wind driven generating unit, connected to the grid by means of a rotating
machine. May be used to represent a single turbine or an aggregation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.HydroPump

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.HydroPump.RotatingMachine
documentation:
The synchronous machine drives the turbine which moves the water from a
low elevation to a higher elevation. The direction of machine rotation
for pumping may or may not be the same as for generating.

A synchronous motor-driven pump, typically associated with a pumped storage
plant.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerElectronicsUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerElectronicsUnit.maxP
documentation:
Maximum active power limit. This is the maximum (nameplate) limit for the
unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerElectronicsUnit.minP
documentation:
Minimum active power limit. This is the minimum (nameplate) limit for the
unit.

A generating unit or battery or aggregation that connects to the AC network
using power electronics rather than rotating machines.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BatteryUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BatteryUnit.ratedE
documentation: full energy storage capacity of the battery

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BatteryUnit.storedE
documentation: amount of energy currently stored; no more than ratedE

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BatteryUnit.batteryState
documentation: indicates whether the battery is charging, discharging or idle
An electrochemical energy storage device

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhotovoltaicUnit
documentation: A photovoltaic device or an aggregation of such devices

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerElectronicsWindUnit
documentation:
A wind generating unit that connects to the AC network with power electronics
rather than rotating machines or an aggregation of such units.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ProtectionEquipment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ProtectionEquipment.highLimit
documentation: The maximum allowable value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ProtectionEquipment.lowLimit
documentation: The minimum allowable value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ProtectionEquipment.powerDirectionFlag
documentation: Direction same as positive active power flow value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ProtectionEquipment.relayDelayTime
documentation: The time delay from detection of abnormal conditions to relay operation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ProtectionEquipment.unitMultiplier
documentation: The unit multiplier of the value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ProtectionEquipment.unitSymbol
documentation: The unit of measure of the value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ProtectionEquipment.ProtectedSwitches
documentation: Protected switches operated by this ProtectionEquipment.

An electrical device designed to respond to input conditions in a prescribed
manner and after specified conditions are met to cause contact operation
or similar abrupt change in associated electric control circuits, or simply
to display the detected condition. Protection equipment is associated with
conducting equipment and usually operate circuit breakers.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerTank

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerTank.PowerTransformer
documentation: Bank this transformer belongs to.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerTank.TransformerTankEnds
documentation: All windings of this transformer.

An assembly of two or more coupled windings that transform electrical power
between voltage levels. These windings are bound on a common core and place
in the same tank. Transformer tank can be used to model both single-phase
and 3-phase transformers.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerCutZone

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerCutZone.cutLevel1
documentation: First level (amount) of load to cut as a percentage of total zone load.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerCutZone.cutLevel2
documentation: Second level (amount) of load to cut as a percentage of total zone load.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerCutZone.EnergyConsumers
documentation: Energy consumer is assigned to the power cut zone.
An area or zone of the power system which is used for load shedding purposes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerElectronicsConnectionPhase.p
documentation:
Active power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow into the equipment from the network.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerElectronicsConnectionPhase.q
documentation:
Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow into the equipment from the network.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerElectronicsConnectionPhase.phase
documentation:
Phase of this energy producer component. If the energy producer is wye
connected, the connection is from the indicated phase to the central ground
or neutral point. If the energy producer is delta connected, the phase
indicates an energy producer connected from the indicated phase to the
next logical non-neutral phase.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegulatingControl

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegulatingControl.discrete
documentation:
The regulation is performed in a discrete mode. This applies to equipment
with discrete controls, e.g. tap changers and shunt compensators.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegulatingControl.enabled
documentation: The flag tells if regulation is enabled.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegulatingControl.targetDeadband
documentation:
This is a deadband used with discrete control to avoid excessive update
of controls like tap changers and shunt compensator banks while regulating.
The units of those appropriate for the mode.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegulatingControl.targetValue
documentation:
The target value specified for case input. This value can be used for the
target value without the use of schedules. The value has the units appropriate
to the mode attribute.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegulatingControl.mode
documentation:
The regulating control mode presently available. This specification allows
for determining the kind of regulation without need for obtaining the units
from a schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegulatingControl.monitoredPhase
