documentation: Phase voltage controlling this regulator, measured at regulator location.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegulatingControl.RegulatingCondEq
documentation: The equipment that participates in this regulating control scheme.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegulatingControl.RegulationSchedule
documentation: Schedule for this Regulating regulating control.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegulatingControl.Terminal
documentation:
The terminal associated with this regulating control. The terminal is associated
instead of a node, since the terminal could connect into either a topological
node (bus in bus-branch model) or a connectivity node (detailed switch
model). Sometimes it is useful to model regulation at a terminal of a bus
bar object since the bus bar can be present in both a bus-branch model
or a model with switch detail.

Specifies a set of equipment that works together to control a power system
quantity such as voltage or flow.
Remote bus voltage control is possible by specifying the controlled terminal
located at some place remote from the controlling equipment.
In case multiple equipment, possibly of different types, control same terminal
there must be only one RegulatingControl at that terminal. The most specific
subtype of RegulatingControl shall be used in case such equipment participate
in the control, e.g. TapChangerControl for tap changers.
For flow control load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign means
flow out from a TopologicalNode (bus) into the conducting equipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChangerControl

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChangerControl.lineDropCompensation
documentation: If true, the line drop compensation is to be applied.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChangerControl.lineDropR
documentation: Line drop compensator resistance setting for normal (forward) power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChangerControl.lineDropX
documentation: Line drop compensator reactance setting for normal (forward) power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChangerControl.maxLimitVoltage
documentation:
Maximum allowed regulated voltage on the PT secondary, regardless of line
drop compensation. Sometimes referred to as first-house protection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChangerControl.reverseLineDropR
documentation: Line drop compensator resistance setting for reverse power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChangerControl.reverseLineDropX
documentation: Line drop compensator reactance setting for reverse power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChangerControl.TapChanger
documentation: The tap changers that participates in this regulating tap control scheme.

Describes behavior specific to tap changers, e.g. how the voltage at the
end of a line varies with the load level and compensation of the voltage
drop by tap adjustment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SubSchedulingArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SubSchedulingArea.BoundaryTerminals
documentation: The set of terminals that define the persistent boundaries of the SubSchedulingArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SubSchedulingArea.ContainedEquipment
documentation: The Equipment contained within the SubSchedulingArea.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SubSchedulingArea.SinkConfiguration
documentation: Set of possible configurations for the sink area.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SubSchedulingArea.SourceConfiguration
documentation: The set of possible configurations for the source area

A persistent connectivity-based containment of ConductingEquipment objects
with clearly-defined electrical boundaries forming a local power system
with one or more points of common coupling. Each piece of ConductingEquipment
can be associated with one ResourceContainer. The boundaries of the ResourceContainer
are specified through the Terminals of equipment forming the boundary (such
as a Recloser or PowerTransformer)

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DistributionArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DistributionArea.Feeders
documentation:
Feeders within the service territory of the DistributionArea of a particular
Distribution System Operator (DSO)

A persistent connectivity-based containment of medium-voltage and high-voltage
distribution ConductingEquipment with clearly defined electrical boundaries
based on electrical connectivity of a distribution substation or multiple
substations.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FeederArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FeederArea.Feeder
documentation:
The Feeder (which contains the ConnectivityNode and all Equipment) associated
with the FeeederArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FeederArea.SwitchAreas
documentation: The set of SwitchArea normally energized by the FeederArea

A persistent connectivity-based containment of medium-voltage distribution
ConductingEquipment with clearly defined electrical boundaries based on
electrical connectivity of a distribution feeder.
The FeederArea contains all medium voltage equipment not contained in a
SwitchArea or Substation / Bay. It also includes all Sectionalisers, Reclosers,
and all other poletop and pad-mounted switchgear that form the boundary
of a SwitchArea.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SecondaryArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SecondaryArea.primaryPhase
documentation:
Used to represent the ABC phase to which the secondary split-phase transformer
is connected in North American systems. For secondary areas served by a
center-tap transformer, the phase connection of equipment will generally
be SinglePhaseKind.s1 or SinglePhaseKind.s2, and it is not readily apparent
what phase serves the loads at the medium voltage level.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SecondaryArea.SwitchArea
documentation: The SwitchArea that normally energizes the SecondaryArea

A persistent connectivity-based containment of low-voltage distribution
ConductingEquipment with clearly defined electrical boundaries formed by
one or more PowerTransformer objects.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SwitchArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SwitchArea.FeederArea
documentation: The FeederArea that normally energizes the SwitchArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SwitchArea.SecondaryAreas
documentation: The set of SecondAreas normally energized by the SwitchArea

A persistent connectivity-based containment of medium-voltage distribution
ConductingEquipment with clearly defined electrical boundaries formed by
one or more Switch objects.
The SwitchArea contains all conductors, fuses, poletop equipment, and vault
equipment. It also contains all secondary service transformers not contained
in a SecondarySubstation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Microgrid
documentation:
A persistent connectivity-based containment of distribution ConductingEquipment
that 1) has clearly-defined electrical boundaries formed by one or more
point of common coupling Switch objects and 2) that acts as a single controllable
entity which can be operated in grid-connected or islanded mode.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShuntCompensatorPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShuntCompensatorPhase.maximumSections
documentation: The maximum number of sections that may be switched in for this phase.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShuntCompensatorPhase.normalSections
documentation: For the capacitor phase, the normal number of sections switched in.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShuntCompensatorPhase.sections
documentation:
Number of sections in use for this phase, when controlled independently
from the other phases. If not provided, may default to the parent ShuntCompensator.sections
value (see ShuntCompensator documentation for more details).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShuntCompensatorPhase.phase
documentation:
Phase of this shunt compensator component. If the shunt compensator is
wye connected, the connection is from the indicated phase to the central
ground or neutral point. If the shunt compensator is delta connected, the
phase indicates a shunt compensator connected from the indicated phase
to the next logical non-neutral phase.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShuntCompensatorPhase.ShuntCompensator
documentation: Shunt compensator of this shunt compensator phase.

Single phase of a multi-phase shunt compensator when its attributes might
be different per phase.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LinearShuntCompensatorPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LinearShuntCompensatorPhase.bPerSection
documentation:
Susceptance per section of the phase if shunt compensator is wye connected.
Susceptance per section phase to phase if shunt compensator is delta connected.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.LinearShuntCompensatorPhase.gPerSection
documentation:
Conductance per section for this phase if shunt compensator is wye connected.
Conductance per section phase to phase if shunt compensator is delta connected.

A per phase linear shunt compensator has banks or sections with equal admittance
values.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhase.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoints
documentation: All points of the non-linear shunt compensator phase.

A per phase non linear shunt compensator has bank or section admittance
values that differs.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SwitchPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SwitchPhase.closed
documentation:
The attribute tells if the switch is considered closed when used as input
to topology processing.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SwitchPhase.normalOpen
documentation:
Used in cases when no Measurement for the status value is present. If the
SwitchPhase has a status measurement the Discrete.normalValue is expected
to match with this value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SwitchPhase.ratedCurrent
documentation: Rated current through this phase, if different from the others.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SwitchPhase.phaseSide1
documentation:
Phase of this SwitchPhase on the side with terminal sequence number equal
1. Should be a phase contained in that terminal’s phases attribute.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SwitchPhase.phaseSide2
documentation:
Phase of this SwitchPhase on the side with terminal sequence number equal
2. Should be a phase contained in that terminal’s Terminal.phases
attribute.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SwitchPhase.Switch
documentation: The switch of the switch phase.

Single phase of a multi-phase switch when its attributes might be different
per phase.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChanger

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChanger.controlEnabled
documentation:
Specifies the regulation status of the equipment. True is regulating, false
is not regulating.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChanger.highStep
documentation:
Highest possible tap step position, advance from neutral.
The attribute shall be greater than lowStep.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChanger.lowStep
documentation: Lowest possible tap step position, retard from neutral

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChanger.ltcFlag
documentation: Specifies whether or not a TapChanger has load tap changing capabilities.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChanger.neutralStep
documentation:
The neutral tap step position for this winding.
The attribute shall be equal or greater than lowStep and equal or less
than highStep.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChanger.normalStep
documentation:
The tap step position used in "normal" network operation for this winding.
For a "Fixed" tap changer indicates the current physical tap setting.
The attribute shall be equal or greater than lowStep and equal or less
than highStep.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChanger.step
documentation:
Tap changer position.
Starting step for a steady state solution. Non integer values are allowed
to support continuous tap variables. The reasons for continuous value are
to support study cases where no discrete tap changers has yet been designed,
a solutions where a narrow voltage band force the tap step to oscillate
or accommodate for a continuous solution as input.
The attribute shall be equal or greater than lowStep and equal or less
than highStep.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChanger.initialDelay
documentation: For an LTC, the delay for initial tap changer operation (first step change)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChanger.neutralU
documentation: Voltage at which the winding operates at the neutral tap setting.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChanger.subsequentDelay
documentation:
For an LTC, the delay for subsequent tap changer operation (second and
later step changes)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChanger.SvTapStep
documentation: The tap step state associated with the tap changer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChanger.TapChangerControl
documentation: The regulating control scheme in which this tap changer participates.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChanger.TapSchedules
documentation: A TapChanger can have TapSchedules.
Mechanism for changing transformer winding tap positions.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChanger

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChanger.TransformerEnd
documentation: Transformer end to which this phase tap changer belongs.

A transformer phase shifting tap model that controls the phase angle difference
across the power transformer and potentially the active power flow through
the power transformer. This phase tap model may also impact the voltage
magnitude.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChangerLinear

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChangerLinear.stepPhaseShiftIncrement
documentation:
Phase shift per step position. A positive value indicates a positive phase
shift from the winding where the tap is located to the other winding (for
a two-winding transformer).
The actual phase shift increment might be more accurately computed from
the symmetrical or asymmetrical models or a tap step table lookup if those
are available.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChangerLinear.xMax
documentation:
The reactance depend on the tap position according to a "u" shaped curve.
The maximum reactance (xMax) appear at the low and high tap positions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChangerLinear.xMin
documentation:
The reactance depend on the tap position according to a "u" shaped curve.
The minimum reactance (xMin) appear at the mid tap position.

Describes a tap changer with a linear relation between the tap step and
the phase angle difference across the transformer. This is a mathematical
model that is an approximation of a real phase tap changer.
The phase angle is computed as stepPhaseShitfIncrement times the tap position.
The secondary side voltage magnitude is the same as at the primary side.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChangerNonLinear

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChangerNonLinear.voltageStepIncrement
documentation:
The voltage step increment on the out of phase winding specified in percent
of neutral voltage of the tap changer.
When the increment is negative, the voltage decreases when the tap step
increases.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChangerNonLinear.xMax
documentation:
The reactance depend on the tap position according to a "u" shaped curve.
The maximum reactance (xMax) appear at the low and high tap positions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChangerNonLinear.xMin
documentation:
The reactance depend on the tap position according to a "u" shaped curve.
The minimum reactance (xMin) appear at the mid tap position.

The non-linear phase tap changer describes the non-linear behavior of a
phase tap changer. This is a base class for the symmetrical and asymmetrical
phase tap changer models. The details of these models can be found in the
IEC 61970-301 document.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChangerAsymmetrical

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChangerAsymmetrical.windingConnectionAngle
documentation:
The phase angle between the in-phase winding and the out-of -phase winding
used for creating phase shift. The out-of-phase winding produces what is
known as the difference voltage. Setting this angle to 90 degrees is not
the same as a symmetrical transformer.

Describes the tap model for an asymmetrical phase shifting transformer
in which the difference voltage vector adds to the primary side voltage.
The angle between the primary side voltage and the difference voltage is
named the winding connection angle. The phase shift depends on both the
difference voltage magnitude and the winding connection angle.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChangerSymmetrical
documentation:
Describes a symmetrical phase shifting transformer tap model in which the
secondary side voltage magnitude is the same as at the primary side. The
difference voltage magnitude is the base in an equal-sided triangle where
the sides corresponds to the primary and secondary voltages. The phase
angle difference corresponds to the top angle and can be expressed as twice
the arctangent of half the total difference voltage.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChangerTabular.PhaseTapChangerTable
documentation: The phase tap changer table for this phase tap changer.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RatioTapChanger

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RatioTapChanger.stepVoltageIncrement
documentation:
Tap step increment, in per cent of neutral voltage, per step position.
When the increment is negative, the voltage decreases when the tap step
increases.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RatioTapChanger.RatioTapChangerTable
documentation: The tap ratio table for this ratio tap changer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RatioTapChanger.TransformerEnd
documentation: Transformer end to which this ratio tap changer belongs.

A tap changer that changes the voltage ratio impacting the voltage magnitude
but not the phase angle across the transformer.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.VoltageControlZone

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.VoltageControlZone.BusbarSection
documentation: A VoltageControlZone is controlled by a designated BusbarSection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.VoltageControlZone.RegulationSchedule
documentation: A VoltageControlZone may have a voltage regulation schedule.

An area of the power system network which is defined for secondary voltage
control purposes. A voltage control zone consists of a collection of substations
with a designated bus bar section whose voltage will be controlled.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ProductAssetModel

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ProductAssetModel.Asset
documentation: An asset of this model.
Asset model by a specific manufacturer.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RatioTapChangerTable

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RatioTapChangerTable.RatioTapChanger
documentation: The ratio tap changer of this tap ratio table.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RatioTapChangerTable.RatioTapChangerTablePoint
documentation: Points of this table.

Describes a curve for how the voltage magnitude and impedance varies with
the tap step.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ReportingGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ReportingGroup.BusNameMarker
documentation: The bus name markers that belong to this reporting group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ReportingGroup.PowerSystemResource
documentation: Power system resources which belong to this reporting group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ReportingGroup.ReportingSuperGroup
documentation: Reporting super group to which this reporting group belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ReportingGroup.TopologicalNode
documentation: The topological nodes that belong to the reporting group.
A reporting group is used for various ad-hoc groupings used for reporting.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ReportingSuperGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ReportingSuperGroup.ReportingGroup
documentation: Reporting groups that are grouped under this super group.
A reporting super group, groups reporting groups for a higher level report.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Season

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Season.endDate
documentation: Date season ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Season.startDate
documentation: Date season starts.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Season.SeasonDayTypeSchedules
documentation: Schedules that use this Season.
A specified time period of the year.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SubGeographicalRegion

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SubGeographicalRegion.Lines
documentation: The lines within the sub-geographical region.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SubGeographicalRegion.Region
documentation: The geographical region to which this sub-geographical region is within.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SubGeographicalRegion.Substations
documentation: The substations in this sub-geographical region.
A subset of a geographical region of a power system network model.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ThermostatController

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ThermostatController.aggregatorName
documentation:
name of a market aggregator that collects bid curves for a higher-level
market

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ThermostatController.rampHigh
documentation: slope of high-temperature bidding curve, $/degreeC

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ThermostatController.rampLow
documentation: slope of low-temperature bidding curve, $/degreeC

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ThermostatController.baseSetpoint
documentation:
user's desired thermostat setpoint, including the effects of pre-programmed
schedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ThermostatController.priceCap
documentation:
maximum price per kwh that the controller will bid, regardless of the market's
price cap

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ThermostatController.rangeHigh
documentation: maximum postive offset to the thermostat setpoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ThermostatController.rangeLow
documentation: maximum negative offset to the thermostat setpoint
a price-responsive or bidding smart thermostat

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TopologicalIsland

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TopologicalIsland.AngleRefTopologicalNode
documentation:
The angle reference for the island. Normally there is one TopologicalNode
that is selected as the angle reference for each island. Other reference
schemes exist, so the association is typically optional.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TopologicalIsland.TopologicalNodes
documentation: A topological node belongs to a topological island.

An electrically connected subset of the network. Topological islands can
change as the current network state changes: e.g. due to
- disconnect switches or breakers change state in a SCADA/EMS
- manual creation, change or deletion of topological nodes in a planning
tool.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TopologicalNode

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TopologicalNode.pInjection
documentation:
The active power injected into the bus at this location in addition to
injections from equipment. Positive sign means injection into the TopologicalNode
(bus).
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TopologicalNode.qInjection
documentation:
The reactive power injected into the bus at this location in addition to
injections from equipment. Positive sign means injection into the TopologicalNode
(bus).
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TopologicalNode.AngleRefTopologicalIsland
documentation:
The island for which the node is an angle reference. Normally there is
one angle reference node for each island.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TopologicalNode.BaseVoltage
documentation: The base voltage of the topologocial node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TopologicalNode.BusNameMarker
documentation: BusnameMarkers that may refer to a pre defined TopologicalNode.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TopologicalNode.ConnectivityNodeContainer
documentation: The connectivity node container to which the toplogical node belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TopologicalNode.ConnectivityNodes
documentation:
The connectivity nodes combine together to form this topological node.
May depend on the current state of switches in the network.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TopologicalNode.ReportingGroup
documentation: The reporting group to which the topological node belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TopologicalNode.Terminal
documentation:
The terminals associated with the topological node. This can be used as
an alternative to the connectivity node path to terminal, thus making it
unneccesary to model connectivity nodes in some cases. Note that if connectivity
nodes are in the model, this association would probably not be used as
an input specification.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TopologicalNode.TopologicalIsland
documentation: A topological node belongs to a topological island.

For a detailed substation model a topological node is a set of connectivity
nodes that, in the current network state, are connected together through
any type of closed switches, including jumpers. Topological nodes change
as the current network state changes (i.e., switches, breakers, etc. change
state).
For a planning model, switch statuses are not used to form topological
nodes. Instead they are manually created or deleted in a model builder
tool. Topological nodes maintained this way are also called "busses".

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerCoreAdmittance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerCoreAdmittance.b
documentation: Magnetizing branch susceptance (B mag). The value can be positive or negative.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerCoreAdmittance.b0
documentation: Zero sequence magnetizing branch susceptance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerCoreAdmittance.g
documentation: Magnetizing branch conductance (G mag).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerCoreAdmittance.g0
documentation: Zero sequence magnetizing branch conductance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerCoreAdmittance.TransformerEnd
documentation: All transformer ends having this core admittance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerCoreAdmittance.TransformerEndInfo
documentation: Transformer end datasheet used to calculate this core admittance.

The transformer core admittance. Used to specify the core admittance of
a transformer in a manner that can be shared among power transformers.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEnd

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEnd.endNumber
documentation:
Number for this transformer end, corresponding to the end's order in the
power transformer vector group or phase angle clock number. Highest voltage
winding should be 1. Each end within a power transformer should have a
unique subsequent end number. Note the transformer end number need not
match the terminal sequence number.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEnd.grounded
documentation: (for Yn and Zn connections) True if the neutral is solidly grounded.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEnd.rground
documentation:
(for Yn and Zn connections) Resistance part of neutral impedance where
'grounded' is true.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEnd.xground
documentation:
(for Yn and Zn connections) Reactive part of neutral impedance where 'grounded'
is true.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEnd.BaseVoltage
documentation: Base voltage of the transformer end. This is essential for PU calculation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEnd.CoreAdmittance
documentation:
Core admittance of this transformer end, representing magnetising current
and core losses. The full values of the transformer should be supplied
for one transformer end only.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEnd.FromMeshImpedance
documentation: All mesh impedances between this 'to' and other 'from' transformer ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEnd.PhaseTapChanger
documentation: Phase tap changer associated with this transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEnd.RatioTapChanger
documentation: Ratio tap changer associated with this transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEnd.StarImpedance
documentation:
(accurate for 2- or 3-winding transformers only) Pi-model impedances of
this transformer end. By convention, for a two winding transformer, the
full values of the transformer should be entered on the high voltage end
(endNumber=1).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEnd.Terminal
documentation: Terminal of the power transformer to which this transformer end belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerEnd.ToMeshImpedance
documentation: All mesh impedances between this 'from' and other 'to' transformer ends.

A conducting connection point of a power transformer. It corresponds to
a physical transformer winding terminal. In earlier CIM versions, the TransformerWinding
class served a similar purpose, but this class is more flexible because
it associates to terminal but is not a specialization of ConductingEquipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerTransformerEnd

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerTransformerEnd.phaseAngleClock
documentation:
Terminal voltage phase angle displacement where 360 degrees are represented
with clock hours. The valid values are 0 to 11. For example, for the secondary
side end of a transformer with vector group code of 'Dyn11', specify the
connection kind as wye with neutral and specify the phase angle of the
clock as 11. The clock value of the transformer end number specified as
1, is assumed to be zero. Note the transformer end number is not assumed
to be the same as the terminal sequence number.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerTransformerEnd.connectionKind
documentation: Kind of connection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerTransformerEnd.r
documentation:
Resistance (star-model) of the transformer end.
The attribute shall be equal or greater than zero for non-equivalent transformers.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerTransformerEnd.ratedS
documentation:
Normal apparent power rating.
The attribute shall be a positive value. For a two-winding transformer
the values for the high and low voltage sides shall be identical.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerTransformerEnd.ratedU
documentation:
Rated voltage: phase-phase for three-phase windings, and either phase-phase
or phase-neutral for single-phase windings.
A high voltage side, as given by TransformerEnd.endNumber, shall have a
ratedU that is greater or equal than ratedU for the lower voltage sides.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PowerTransformerEnd.PowerTransformer
documentation: The power transformer of this power transformer end.

A PowerTransformerEnd is associated with each Terminal of a PowerTransformer.
The impedance values r, r0, x, and x0 of a PowerTransformerEnd represents
a star equivalent as follows
1) for a two Terminal PowerTransformer the high voltage (TransformerEnd.endNumber=1)
PowerTransformerEnd has non zero values on r, r0, x, and x0 while the low
voltage (TransformerEnd.endNumber=0) PowerTransformerEnd has zero values
for r, r0, x, and x0.
2) for a three Terminal PowerTransformer the three PowerTransformerEnds
represents a star equivalent with each leg in the star represented by r,
r0, x, and x0 values.
3) For a three Terminal transformer each PowerTransformerEnd shall have
g, g0, b and b0 values corresponding the no load losses distributed on
the three PowerTransformerEnds. The total no load loss shunt impedances
may also be placed at one of the PowerTransformerEnds, preferably the end
numbered 1, having the shunt values on end 1 is the preferred way.
4) for a PowerTransformer with more than three Terminals the PowerTransformerEnd
impedance values cannot be used. Instead use the TransformerMeshImpedance
or split the transformer into multiple PowerTransformers.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerTankEnd

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerTankEnd.orderedPhases
documentation:
Identifies the phases present and the order of their connection on this
winding (end) of the transformer. In some use cases, such as open-wye,
open-delta transformers and single-phase, center-tap secondary transformers,
the order of phase connection is important, so the OrderedPhaseCodeKind
enumeration is used instead of PhaseCode.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerTankEnd.TransformerTank
documentation: Transformer this winding belongs to.

Transformer tank end represents an individual winding for unbalanced models
or for transformer tanks connected into a bank (and bank is modelled with
the PowerTransformer).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerMeshImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerMeshImpedance.r
documentation: Resistance between the 'from' and the 'to' end, seen from the 'from' end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerMeshImpedance.r0
documentation:
Zero-sequence resistance between the 'from' and the 'to' end, seen from
the 'from' end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerMeshImpedance.x
documentation: Reactance between the 'from' and the 'to' end, seen from the 'from' end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerMeshImpedance.x0
documentation:
Zero-sequence reactance between the 'from' and the 'to' end, seen from
the 'from' end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerMeshImpedance.FromTransformerEnd
documentation:
From end this mesh impedance is connected to. It determines the voltage
reference.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerMeshImpedance.FromTransformerEndInfo
documentation:
'from' transformer end datasheet this mesh impedance is calculated from.
It determines the voltage reference.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerMeshImpedance.ToTransformerEnd
documentation: All transformer ends this mesh impedance is connected to.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerMeshImpedance.ToTransformerEndInfos
documentation:
All 'to' transformer end datasheets this mesh impedance for 'from' transformer
end is calculated from.

Transformer mesh impedance (Delta-model) between transformer ends.
The typical case is that this class describes the impedance between two
transformer ends pair-wise, i.e. the cardinalities at both tranformer end
associations are 1. But in cases where two or more transformer ends are
modeled the cardinalities are larger than 1.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerStarImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerStarImpedance.r
documentation: Resistance of the transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerStarImpedance.x
documentation: Positive sequence series reactance of the transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerStarImpedance.x0
documentation: Zero sequence series reactance of the transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerStarImpedance.TransformerEnd
documentation: All transformer ends having this star impedance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerStarImpedance.TransformerEndInfo
documentation: Transformer end datasheet used to calculate this transformer star impedance.

Transformer star impedance (Pi-model) that accurately reflects impedance
for transformers with 2 or 3 windings. For transformers with 4 or more
windings, you must use TransformerMeshImpedance class.
For transmission networks use PowerTransformerEnd impedances (r, r0, x,
x0, b, b0, g and g0).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerTest

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerTest.basePower
documentation:
Base power at which the tests are conducted, usually equal to the rateds
of one of the involved transformer ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TransformerTest.temperature
documentation: Temperature at which the test is conducted.

Test result for transformer ends, such as short-circuit, open-circuit (excitation)
or no-load test.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NoLoadTest

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NoLoadTest.energisedEndVoltage
documentation: Voltage applied to the winding (end) during test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NoLoadTest.excitingCurrent
documentation:
Exciting current measured from a positive-sequence or single-phase excitation
test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NoLoadTest.excitingCurrentZero
documentation:
Exciting current measured from a zero-sequence open-circuit excitation
test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NoLoadTest.loss
documentation: Losses measured from a positive-sequence or single-phase excitation test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NoLoadTest.lossZero
documentation: Losses measured from a zero-sequence excitation test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NoLoadTest.EnergisedEnd
documentation: Transformer end that current is applied to in this no-load test.

No-load test results determine core admittance parameters. They include
exciting current and core loss measurements from applying voltage to one
winding. The excitation may be positive sequence or zero sequence. The
test may be repeated at different voltages to measure saturation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OpenCircuitTest

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OpenCircuitTest.energisedEndStep
documentation: Tap step number for the energised end of the test pair.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OpenCircuitTest.openEndStep
documentation: Tap step number for the open end of the test pair.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OpenCircuitTest.energisedEndVoltage
documentation: Voltage applied to the winding (end) during test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OpenCircuitTest.openEndVoltage
documentation:
Voltage measured at the open-circuited end, with the energised end set
to rated voltage and all other ends open.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OpenCircuitTest.phaseShift
documentation:
Phase shift measured at the open end with the energised end set to rated
voltage and all other ends open.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OpenCircuitTest.EnergisedEnd
documentation: Transformer end that current is applied to in this open-circuit test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OpenCircuitTest.OpenEnd
documentation:
Transformer end measured for induced voltage and angle in this open-circuit
test.

Open-circuit test results verify winding turn ratios and phase shifts.
They include induced voltage and phase shift measurements on open-circuit
windings, with voltage applied to the energised end. For three-phase windings,
the excitation can be a positive sequence (the default) or a zero sequence.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShortCircuitTest

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShortCircuitTest.energisedEndStep
documentation: Tap step number for the energised end of the test pair.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShortCircuitTest.groundedEndStep
documentation: Tap step number for the grounded end of the test pair.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShortCircuitTest.leakageImpedance
documentation:
Leakage impedance measured from a positive-sequence or single-phase short-circuit
test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShortCircuitTest.leakageImpedanceZero
documentation: Leakage impedance measured from a zero-sequence short-circuit test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShortCircuitTest.loss
documentation: Load losses from a positive-sequence or single-phase short-circuit test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShortCircuitTest.lossZero
documentation: Load losses from a zero-sequence short-circuit test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShortCircuitTest.EnergisedEnd
documentation:
Transformer end that voltage is applied to in this short-circuit test.
The test voltage is chosen to induce rated current in the energised end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ShortCircuitTest.GroundedEnds
documentation: All ends short-circuited in this short-circuit test.

Short-circuit test results determine mesh impedance parameters. They include
load losses and leakage impedances. For three-phase windings, the excitation
can be a positive sequence (the default) or a zero sequence. There shall
be at least one grounded winding.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.UsagePointGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.UsagePointGroup.type
documentation: Type of this group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.UsagePointGroup.DemandResponsePrograms
documentation: All demand response programs this usage point group is enrolled in.

Abstraction for management of group communications within a two-way AMR
system or the data for a group of related usage points. Commands can be
issued to all of the usage points that belong to a usage point group using
a defined group address and the underlying AMR communication infrastructure.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ValueAliasSet
documentation:
Describes the translation of a set of values into a name and is intendend
to facilitate cusom translations. Each ValueAliasSet has a name, description
etc. A specific Measurement may represent a discrete state like Open, Closed,
Intermediate etc. This requires a translation from the MeasurementValue.value
number to a string, e.g. 0->"Invalid", 1->"Open", 2->"Closed",
3->"Intermediate". Each ValueToAlias member in ValueAliasSet.Value describe
a mapping for one particular value to a name.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ValueToAlias

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ValueToAlias.value
documentation: The value that is mapped.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.ValueToAlias.ValueAliasSet
documentation: The ValueAliasSet having the ValueToAlias mappings.

Describes the translation of one particular value into a name, e.g. 1 as
"Open".

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WirePosition

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WirePosition.sequenceNumber
documentation:
Numbering for wires on a WireSpacingInfo. Neutrals should be numbered last.
Multiple circuits on the same pole, tower or right-of-way can be included
with unique sequence numbers for the phases, and identical sequence numbers
for any shared neutrals.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WirePosition.xCoord
documentation:
Signed horizontal distance from the wire at this position to a common reference
point.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WirePosition.yCoord
documentation:
Signed vertical distance from the wire at this position: above ground (positive
value) or burial depth below ground (negative value).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.WirePosition.WireSpacingInfo
documentation: Wire spacing data this wire position belongs to.

Identification, spacing and configuration of the wires of a conductor with
respect to a structure.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AreaConfiguration

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AreaConfiguration.priority
documentation:
Value 0 means ignore priority. 1 means the highest priority, 2 is the second
highest priority.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AreaConfiguration.EnergizedArea
documentation: The sink area being energized by the source area.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AreaConfiguration.EnergizingArea
documentation: The source area which is energizing the sink area

Alternate configurations for abnormal feeder switching conditions. The
distribution feeder can be segmented into source and sink SubSchedulingArea
to represent upstream and downstream sections relative to the head terminal.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AssetOrganisationRole

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.AssetOrganisationRole.Assets
documentation: All assets for this organisation role.
Role an organisation plays with respect to asset.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BranchGroupTerminal

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BranchGroupTerminal.positiveFlowIn
documentation:
The flow into the terminal is summed if set true. The flow out of the terminanl
is summed if set false.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BranchGroupTerminal.BranchGroup
documentation: The branch group to which the directed branch group terminals belong.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.BranchGroupTerminal.Terminal
documentation: The terminal to be summed.
A specific directed terminal flow for a branch group.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CurveData

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CurveData.xvalue
documentation: The data value of the X-axis variable, depending on the X-axis units.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CurveData.y1value
documentation: The data value of the first Y-axis variable, depending on the Y-axis units.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CurveData.y2value
documentation:
The data value of the second Y-axis variable (if present), depending on
the Y-axis units.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CurveData.y3value
documentation:
The data value of the third Y-axis variable (if present), depending on
the Y-axis units.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.CurveData.Curve
documentation: The curve of this curve data point.

Multi-purpose data points for defining a curve. The use of this generic
class is discouraged if a more specific class can be used to specify the
x and y axis values along with their specific data types.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERDynamics

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERDynamics.AsynchronousMachine
documentation: Asynchronous machine model with which this DER dynamics model is associated.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERDynamics.PowerElectronicsConnection
documentation: Power electronics connection with which this DER dynamics model is associated.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERDynamics.SynchronousMachine
documentation: Synchronous machine model with which this DER dynamics model is associated.
Parent class supporting relationships to DER dynamics models.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DispatchSchedule.numberOfIntervals
documentation:
Used to specify the number of intervals when requesting a forecast or a
dispatch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DispatchSchedule.startTime
documentation: The start time of the first interval in the dispatch schedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DispatchSchedule.timeIntervalDuration
documentation: The length of time for each interval in the dispatch schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DispatchSchedule.curveStyleKind
documentation:
Used to specify whether the values over an interval are constant (constantYValue)
or linearly interpolated (straightLineYValues)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DispatchSchedule.timeIntervalUnit
documentation: The unit of measure for the time axis of the dispatch schedule.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.IEC61970CIMVersion

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.IEC61970CIMVersion.date
documentation: Form is YYYY-MM-DD for example for January 5, 2009 it is 2009-01-05.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.IEC61970CIMVersion.version
documentation:
Form is IEC61970CIMXXvYY where XX is the major CIM package version and
the YY is the minor version. For example IEC61970CIM13v18.
This is the IEC 61970 CIM version number assigned to this UML model.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.IrregularTimePoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.IrregularTimePoint.value1
documentation:
The first value at the time. The meaning of the value is defined by the
derived type of the associated schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.IrregularTimePoint.value2
documentation:
The second value at the time. The meaning of the value is defined by the
derived type of the associated schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.IrregularTimePoint.time
documentation: The time is relative to the schedule starting time.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.IrregularTimePoint.IntervalSchedule
documentation: An IrregularTimePoint belongs to an IrregularIntervalSchedule.
TimePoints for a schedule where the time between the points varies.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Name

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Name.name
documentation: Any free text that name the object.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Name.IdentifiedObject
documentation: Identified object that this name designates.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Name.NameType
documentation: Type of this name.

The Name class provides the means to define any number of human readable names for an object. A name is not to be used for defining inter-object relationships. For inter-object relationships instead use the object identification 'mRID'.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NameType

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NameType.description
documentation: Description of the name type.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NameType.name
documentation: Name of the name type.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NameType.Names
documentation: All names of this type.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NameType.NameTypeAuthority
documentation: Authority responsible for managing names of this type.

Type of name. Possible values for attribute 'name' are implementation dependent
but standard profiles may specify types. An enterprise may have multiple
IT systems each having its own local name for the same object, e.g. a planning
system may have different names from an EMS. An object may also have different
names within the same IT system, e.g. localName as defined in CIM version
14. The definition from CIM14 is:
The localName is a human readable name of the object. It is a free text
name local to a node in a naming hierarchy similar to a file directory
structure. A power system related naming hierarchy may be: Substation,
VoltageLevel, Equipment etc. Children of the same parent in such a hierarchy
have names that typically are unique among them.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NameTypeAuthority

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NameTypeAuthority.description
documentation: Description of the name type authority.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NameTypeAuthority.name
documentation: Name of the name type authority.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NameTypeAuthority.NameTypes
documentation: All name types managed by this authority.

Authority responsible for creation and management of names of a given type;
typically an organization or an enterprise system.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoint.sectionNumber
documentation: The number of the section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoint.b
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) susceptance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoint.g
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoint.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhase
documentation: Non-linear shunt compensator phase owning this point.
A per phase non linear shunt compensator bank or section admittance value.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.sectionNumber
documentation: The number of the section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.b
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) susceptance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.b0
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) susceptance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.g
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.g0
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) conductance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.NonlinearShuntCompensator
documentation: Non-linear shunt compensator owning this point.
A non linear shunt compensator bank or section admittance value.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OperatingShare

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OperatingShare.percentage
documentation:
Percentage operational ownership between the pair (power system resource
and operatging participant) associated with this share. The total percentage
ownership for a power system resource should add to 100%.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OperatingShare.OperatingParticipant
documentation:
The operating participant having this share with the associated power system
resource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OperatingShare.PowerSystemResource
documentation: The power system resource to which the share applies.

Specifies the operations contract relationship between a power system resource
and a contract participant.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseImpedanceData

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseImpedanceData.column
documentation:
This matrix element's column number, in the range 1 to row. Only the lower
triangle needs to be stored. Neutrals should be numbered last. Multiple
circuits on the same pole, tower or right-of-way can be included with unique
sequence numbers for the phases, and identical sequence numbers for any
shared neutrals. This solumn number matches ACLineSegmentPhase.sequenceNumber,
WirePosition.sequenceNumber and WirePhaseInfo.sequenceNumber as applicable..

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseImpedanceData.row
documentation:
This matrix element's row number, in the range 1 to PerLengthPhaseImpedance.conductorCount.
Only the lower triangle needs to be stored. Neutrals should be numbered
last. Multiple circuits on the same pole, tower or right-of-way can be
included with unique sequence numbers for the phases, and identical sequence
numbers for any shared neutrals. This row number matches ACLineSegmentPhase.sequenceNumber,
WirePosition.sequenceNumber and WirePhaseInfo.sequenceNumber as applicable..

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseImpedanceData.b
documentation: Susceptance matrix element value, per length of unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseImpedanceData.g
documentation: Conductance matrix element value, per length of unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseImpedanceData.r
documentation: Resistance matrix element value, per length of unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseImpedanceData.x
documentation: Reactance matrix element value, per length of unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseImpedanceData.PhaseImpedance
documentation: Conductor phase impedance to which this data belongs.

Impedance and conductance matrix element values.
The diagonal elements are described by the elements having the same toPhase
and fromPhase value and the off diagonal elements have different toPhase
and fromPhase values. The matrix can also be stored in symmetric lower
triangular format using the row and column attributes, which map to ACLineSegmentPhase.sequenceNumber.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PositionPoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PositionPoint.groupNumber
cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PositionPoint.sequenceNumber
documentation: Zero-relative sequence number of this point within a series of points.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PositionPoint.xPosition
documentation: X axis position.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PositionPoint.yPosition
documentation: Y axis position.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PositionPoint.zPosition
documentation: (if applicable) Z axis position.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PositionPoint.Location
documentation: Location described by this position point.

Set of spatial coordinates that determine a point, defined in the coordinate
system specified in 'Location.CoordinateSystem'. Use a single position
point instance to desribe a point-oriented location. Use a sequence of
position points to describe a line-oriented object (physical location of
non-point oriented objects like cables or lines), or area of an object
(like a substation or a geographical zone - in this case, have first and
last position point with the same values).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Quality61850

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Quality61850.badReference
documentation: Measurement value may be incorrect due to a reference being out of calibration.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Quality61850.estimatorReplaced
documentation:
Value has been replaced by State Estimator. estimatorReplaced is not an
IEC61850 quality bit but has been put in this class for convenience.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Quality61850.failure
documentation:
This identifier indicates that a supervision function has detected an internal
or external failure, e.g. communication failure.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Quality61850.oldData
documentation:
Measurement value is old and possibly invalid, as it has not been successfully
updated during a specified time interval.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Quality61850.operatorBlocked
documentation: Measurement value is blocked and hence unavailable for transmission.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Quality61850.oscillatory
documentation:
To prevent some overload of the communication it is sensible to detect
and suppress oscillating (fast changing) binary inputs. If a signal changes
in a defined time (tosc) twice in the same direction (from 0 to 1 or from
1 to 0) then oscillation is detected and the detail quality identifier
"oscillatory" is set. If it is detected a configured numbers of transient
changes could be passed by. In this time the validity status "questionable"
is set. If after this defined numbers of changes the signal is still in
the oscillating state the value shall be set either to the opposite state
of the previous stable value or to a defined default value. In this case
the validity status "questionable" is reset and "invalid" is set as long
as the signal is oscillating. If it is configured such that no transient
changes should be passed by then the validity status "invalid" is set immediately
in addition to the detail quality identifier "oscillatory" (used for status
information only).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Quality61850.outOfRange
documentation: Measurement value is beyond a predefined range of value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Quality61850.overFlow
documentation:
Measurement value is beyond the capability of being represented properly.
For example, a counter value overflows from maximum count back to a value
of zero.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Quality61850.suspect
documentation:
A correlation function has detected that the value is not consitent with
other values. Typically set by a network State Estimator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Quality61850.test
documentation: Measurement value is transmitted for test purposes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Quality61850.source
documentation:
Source gives information related to the origin of a value. The value may
be acquired from the process, defaulted or substituted.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Quality61850.validity
documentation: Validity of the measurement value.

Quality flags in this class are as defined in IEC 61850, except for estimatorReplaced,
which has been included in this class for convenience.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MeasurementValueQuality

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.MeasurementValueQuality.MeasurementValue
documentation: A MeasurementValue has a MeasurementValueQuality associated with it.

Measurement quality flags. Bits 0-10 are defined for substation automation
in draft IEC 61850 part 7-3. Bits 11-15 are reserved for future expansion
by that document. Bits 16-31 are reserved for EMS applications.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegularTimePoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegularTimePoint.sequenceNumber
documentation:
The position of the regular time point in the sequence. Note that time
points don't have to be sequential, i.e. time points may be omitted. The
actual time for a RegularTimePoint is computed by multiplying the associated
regular interval schedule's time step with the regular time point sequence
number and adding the associated schedules start time.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegularTimePoint.value1
documentation:
The first value at the time. The meaning of the value is defined by the
derived type of the associated schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegularTimePoint.value2
documentation:
The second value at the time. The meaning of the value is defined by the
derived type of the associated schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RegularTimePoint.IntervalSchedule
documentation: Regular interval schedule containing this time point.

Time point for a schedule where the time between the consecutive points
is constant.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SvShuntCompensatorSections

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SvShuntCompensatorSections.sections
documentation:
The number of sections in service as a continous variable. To get integer
value scale with ShuntCompensator.bPerSection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SvShuntCompensatorSections.phase
documentation:
The terminal phase at which the connection is applied. If missing, the
injection is assumed to be balanced among non-neutral phases.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SvShuntCompensatorSections.ShuntCompensator
documentation: The shunt compensator for which the state applies.
State variable for the number of sections in service for a shunt compensator.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SvTapStep

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SvTapStep.position
documentation:
The floating point tap position. This is not the tap ratio, but rather
the tap step position as defined by the related tap changer model and normally
is constrained to be within the range of minimum and maximum tap positions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.SvTapStep.TapChanger
documentation: The tap changer associated with the tap step state.
State variable for transformer tap step.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChangerTablePoint.ratio
documentation:
The voltage at the tap step divided by rated voltage of the transformer
end having the tap changer. Hence this is a value close to one.
For example, if the ratio at step 1 is 1.01, and the rated voltage of the
transformer end is 110kV, then the voltage obtained by setting the tap
changer to step 1 to is 111.1kV.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChangerTablePoint.step
documentation: The tap step.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChangerTablePoint.b
documentation:
The magnetizing branch susceptance deviation in percent of nominal value.
The actual susceptance is calculated as follows:
calculated magnetizing susceptance = b(nominal) * (1 + b(from this class)/100).
The b(nominal) is defined as the static magnetizing susceptance on the
associated power transformer end or ends. This model assumes the star impedance
(pi model) form.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChangerTablePoint.g
documentation:
The magnetizing branch conductance deviation in percent of nominal value.
The actual conductance is calculated as follows:
calculated magnetizing conductance = g(nominal) * (1 + g(from this class)/100).
The g(nominal) is defined as the static magnetizing conductance on the
associated power transformer end or ends. This model assumes the star impedance
(pi model) form.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChangerTablePoint.r
documentation:
The resistance deviation in percent of nominal value. The actual reactance
is calculated as follows:
calculated resistance = r(nominal) * (1 + r(from this class)/100). The
r(nominal) is defined as the static resistance on the associated power
transformer end or ends. This model assumes the star impedance (pi model)
form.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.TapChangerTablePoint.x
documentation:
The series reactance deviation in percent of nominal value. The actual
reactance is calculated as follows:
calculated reactance = x(nominal) * (1 + x(from this class)/100). The x(nominal)
is defined as the static series reactance on the associated power transformer
end or ends. This model assumes the star impedance (pi model) form.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChangerTablePoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChangerTablePoint.angle
documentation:
The angle difference in degrees. A positive value indicates a positive
phase shift from the winding where the tap is located to the other winding
(for a two-winding transformer).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.PhaseTapChangerTablePoint.PhaseTapChangerTable
documentation: The table of this point.
Describes each tap step in the phase tap changer tabular curve.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.RatioTapChangerTablePoint.RatioTapChangerTable

documentation: Current in Ampere.
