documentation: Ampere-hours, Ampere-hours.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.As
documentation: Ampere seconds (A·s).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.Btu
documentation: Energy, British Thermal Unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.Hz
documentation: Frequency in Hertz (1/s).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.Q
documentation: Quantity power, Q.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.Qh
documentation: Quantity energy, Qh.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.V
documentation: Electric potential in Volt (W/A).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.VA
documentation: Apparent power in Volt Ampere (See also real power and reactive power.)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.VAh
documentation: Apparent energy in Volt Ampere hours.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.VAr
documentation:
Reactive power in Volt Ampere reactive. The “reactive” or “imaginary” component
of electrical power (VIsin(phi)). (See also real power and apparent power).
Note: Different meter designs use different methods to arrive at their
results. Some meters may compute reactive power as an arithmetic value,
while others compute the value vectorially. The data consumer should determine
the method in use and the suitability of the measurement for the intended
purpose.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.VArh
documentation: Reactive energy in Volt Ampere reactive hours.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.VPerVA
documentation:
Power factor, PF, the ratio of the active power to the apparent power.
Note: The sign convention used for power factor will differ between IEC
meters and EEI (ANSI) meters. It is assumed that the data consumers understand
the type of meter being used and agree on the sign convention in use at
any given utility.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.VPerVAr
documentation:
Power factor, PF, the ratio of the active power to the apparent power.
Note: The sign convention used for power factor will differ between IEC
meters and EEI (ANSI) meters. It is assumed that the data consumers understand
the type of meter being used and agree on the sign convention in use at
any given utility.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.Vh
documentation: Volt-hour, Volt hours.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.Vs
documentation: Volt second (Ws/A).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.W
documentation:
Real power in Watt (J/s). Electrical power may have real and reactive components.
The real portion of electrical power (I²R or VIcos(phi)), is expressed
in Watts. (See also apparent power and reactive power.)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.WPerA
documentation: Active power per current flow, watt per Ampere.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.WPers
documentation: Ramp rate in Watt per second.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.Wh
documentation: Real energy in Watt hours.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.deg
documentation: Plane angle in degrees.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.degC
documentation:
Relative temperature in degrees Celsius.
In the SI unit system the symbol is ºC. Electric charge is measured in
coulomb that has the unit symbol C. To distinguish degree Celsius form
coulomb the symbol used in the UML is degC. Reason for not using ºC is
the special character º is difficult to manage in software.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.h
documentation: Time, hour = 60 min = 3600 s.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.min
documentation: Time, minute = 60 s.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.ohm
documentation: Electric resistance in ohm (V/A).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.ohmPerm
documentation: Electric resistance per length in ohm per metre ((V/A)/m).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.ohmm
documentation: resistivity, Ohm metre, (rho).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.onePerHz
documentation: Reciprocal of frequency (1/Hz).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.s
documentation: Time in seconds.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.DERUnitSymbol.therm
documentation: Energy, Therm.
The units defined for usage in the CIM.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FlowDirectionKind

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FlowDirectionKind.forward
documentation:
"Delivered," or "Imported" as defined 61968-2.
Forward Active Energy is a positive kWh value as one would naturally expect
to find as energy is supplied by the utility and consumed at the service.
Forward Reactive Energy is a positive VArh value as one would naturally
expect to find in the presence of inductive loading.
In polyphase metering, the forward energy register is incremented when
the sum of the phase energies is greater than zero:
<img src="HTS_1.PNG" width="209" height="16" border="0" alt="graphic"/>

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FlowDirectionKind.lagging
documentation:
Typically used to describe that a power factor is lagging the reference
value.
Note 1: When used to describe VA, “lagging” describes a form of measurement
where reactive power is considered in all four quadrants, but real power
is considered only in quadrants I and IV.
Note 2: When used to describe power factor, the term “Lagging” implies
that the PF is negative. The term “lagging” in this case takes the place
of the negative sign. If a signed PF value is to be passed by the data
producer, then the direction of flow enumeration zero (none) should be
used in order to avoid the possibility of creating an expression that employs
a double negative. The data consumer should be able to tell from the sign
of the data if the PF is leading or lagging. This principle is analogous
to the concept that “Reverse” energy is an implied negative value, and
to publish a negative reverse value would be ambiguous.
Note 3: Lagging power factors typically indicate inductive loading.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FlowDirectionKind.leading
documentation:

In other systems the value passed as a “net” value is always a positive

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FlowDirectionKind.reverse
documentation:
Reverse Active Energy is equivalent to "Received," or "Exported" as defined
in 61968-2.
Reverse Active Energy is a positive kWh value as one would expect to find
when energy is backfed by the service onto the utility network.
Reverse Reactive Energy is a positive VArh value as one would expect to
find in the presence of capacitive loading and a leading Power Factor.
In polyphase metering, the reverse energy register is incremented when
the sum of the phase energies is less than zero:
<img src="HTS_1.PNG" width="209" height="16" border="0" alt="graphic"/>
Note: The value passed as a reverse value is always a positive value. It
is understood by the label “reverse” that it represents negative flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FlowDirectionKind.total
documentation:
|Forward| + |Reverse|, See 61968-2.
The sum of the commodity in all quadrants Q1+Q2+Q3+Q4.
In polyphase metering, the total energy register is incremented when the
absolute value of the sum of the phase energies is greater than zero:
<img src="HTS_1.PNG" width="217" height="16" border="0" alt="graphic"/>

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.FlowDirectionKind.totalByPhase
documentation:
In polyphase metering, the total by phase energy register is incremented
when the sum of the absolute values of the phase energies is greater than
zero:
<img src="HTS_1.PNG" width="234" height="16" border="0" alt="graphic"/>
In single phase metering, the formulas for “Total” and “Total by phase”
collapse to the same expression. For communication purposes however, the
“Total” enumeration should be used with single phase meter data.

Kind of flow direction for reading/measured values proper to some commodities
such as, for example, energy, power, demand.

documentation:
High means that a monitored value above the limit value is a violation.
If applied to a terminal flow, the positive direction is into the terminal.

Low means a monitored value below the limit is a violation. If applied

The direction attribute describes the side of a limit that is a violation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.OrderedPhaseCodeKind
documentation:
In some use cases, the ordering of phases is important. The PhaseCode class
does not represent order, but this class addresses such use cases. When
two or more phases are present, the individual phases may occur in any
order, but the neutral must always occur last. When only one phase and
the neutral is present, that phase and the neutral may be re-ordered.

documentation: Secondary phase 2 and neutral.

An unordered enumeration of phase identifiers. Allows designation of phases
for both transmission and distribution equipment, circuits and loads. The
enumeration, by itself, does not describe how the phases are connected
together or connected to ground. Ground is not explicitly denoted as a
phase.
Residential and small commercial loads are often served from single-phase,
or split-phase, secondary circuits. For example of s12N, phases 1 and 2
refer to hot wires that are 180 degrees out of phase, while N refers to
the neutral wire. Through single-phase transformer connections, these secondary
circuits may be served from one or two of the primary phases A, B, and
C. For three-phase loads, use the A, B, C phase codes instead of s12N.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.UnitMultiplier.none
documentation: No multiplier or equivalently multiply by 1.

The unit multipliers defined for the CIM. When applied to unit symbols,
the unit symbol is treated as a derived unit. Regardless of the contents
of the unit symbol text, the unit symbol shall be treated as if it were
a single-character unit symbol. Unit symbols should not contain multipliers,
and it should be left to the multiplier to define the multiple for an entire
data type.
For example, if a unit symbol is "A2Perh" and the multiplier is "k", then
the value is k(A^2/h), and the multiplier applies to the entire final value,
not to any individual part of the value. This can be conceptualized by
substituting a derived unit symbol for the unit type. If one imagines that
the symbol "Þ" represents the derived unit "A2Perh", then applying the
multiplier "k" can be conceptualized simply as "kÞ".
For example, the SI unit for mass is "kg" and not "g". If the unit symbol
is defined as "kg", then the multiplier is applied to "kg" as a whole and
does not replace the "k" in front of the "g". In this case, the multiplier
of "m" would be used with the unit symbol of "kg" to represent one gram.
As a text string, this violates the instructions in IEC 80000-1. However,
because the unit symbol in CIM is treated as a derived unit instead of
as an SI unit, it makes more sense to conceptualize the "kg" as if it were
replaced by one of the proposed replacements for the SI mass symbol. If
one imagines that the "kg" were replaced by a symbol "Þ", then it is easier
to conceptualize the multiplier "m" as creating the proper unit "mÞ", and
not the forbidden unit "mkg".

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.UnitSymbol.tonne
documentation: mass, “tonne” or “metric ton” (1000 kg = 1 Mg).

The derived units defined for usage in the CIM. In some cases, the derived
unit is equal to an SI unit. Whenever possible, the standard derived symbol
is used instead of the formula for the derived unit. For example, the unit
symbol Farad is defined as "F" instead of "CPerV". In cases where a standard
symbol does not exist for a derived unit, the formula for the unit is used
as the unit symbol. For example, density does not have a standard symbol
and so it is represented as "kgPerm3". With the exception of the "kg",
which is an SI unit, the unit symbols do not contain multipliers and therefore
represent the base derived unit to which a multiplier can be applied as
a whole.
Every unit symbol is treated as an unparseable text as if it were a single-letter
symbol. The meaning of each unit symbol is defined by the accompanying
descriptive text and not by the text contents of the unit symbol.
To allow the widest possible range of serializations without requiring
special character handling, several substitutions are made which deviate
from the format described in IEC 80000-1. The division symbol "/" is replaced
by the letters"Per". Exponents are written in plain text after the unit
as "m3" instead of being formatted as in "m<sup>3</sup>" or introducing
a symbol as in "m^3". The degree symbol "°" is replaced with the letters
"deg". Any clarification of the meaning for a substitution is included
in the description for the unit symbol.
Non-SI units are included in list of unit symbols to allow sources of data
to be correctly labeled with their non-SI units (for example, a GPS sensor
that is reporting numbers that represent feet instead of meters). This
allows software to use the unit symbol information correctly convert and
scale the raw data of those sources into SI-based units.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Validity

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Validity.GOOD
documentation:
The value is marked good if no abnormal condition of the acquisition function
or the information source is detected.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Validity.INVALID
documentation:
The value is marked invalid when a supervision function recognises abnormal
conditions of the acquisition function or the information source (missing
or non-operating updating devices). The value is not defined under this
condition. The mark invalid is used to indicate to the client that the
value may be incorrect and shall not be used.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_ufls.cimhub_ufls.Validity.QUESTIONABLE
documentation:
The value is marked questionable if a supervision function detects an abnormal
behaviour, however the value could still be valid. The client is responsible
for determining whether or not values marked "questionable" should be used.
Validity for MeasurementValue.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.IdentifiedObject

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.IdentifiedObject.mRID
documentation:
Master resource identifier issued by a model authority. The mRID is unique
within an exchange context. Global uniqueness is easily achieved by using
a UUID, as specified in RFC 4122, for the mRID. The use of UUID is strongly
recommended.
For CIMXML data files in RDF syntax conforming to IEC 61970-552, the mRID
is mapped to rdf:ID or rdf:about attributes that identify CIM object elements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.IdentifiedObject.aliasName
documentation:
The aliasName is free text human readable name of the object alternative
to IdentifiedObject.name. It may be non unique and may not correlate to
a naming hierarchy.
The attribute aliasName is retained because of backwards compatibility
between CIM relases. It is however recommended to replace aliasName with
the Name class as aliasName is planned for retirement at a future time.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.IdentifiedObject.description
documentation:
The description is a free human readable text describing or naming the
object. It may be non unique and may not correlate to a naming hierarchy.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.IdentifiedObject.name
documentation:
The name is any free human readable and possibly non unique text naming
the object.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.IdentifiedObject.InstanceSet
documentation: Dataset containing the data objects.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.IdentifiedObject.Names
documentation: All names of this identified object.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.IdentifiedObject.PropertiesCIMDataObject
documentation: The single CIM data object in the appropriate dataset context.

This is a root class to provide common identification for all classes needing
identification and naming attributes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCTerminal

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCTerminal.connected
documentation:
The connected status is related to a bus-branch model and the topological
node to terminal relation. True implies the terminal is connected to the
related topological node and false implies it is not.
In a bus-branch model, the connected status is used to tell if equipment
is disconnected without having to change the connectivity described by
the topological node to terminal relation. A valid case is that conducting
equipment can be connected in one end and open in the other. In particular
for an AC line segment, where the reactive line charging can be significant,
this is a relevant case.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCTerminal.sequenceNumber
documentation:
The orientation of the terminal connections for a multiple terminal conducting
equipment. The sequence numbering starts with 1 and additional terminals
should follow in increasing order. The first terminal is the "starting
point" for a two terminal branch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCTerminal.BusNameMarker
documentation: The bus name marker used to name the bus (topological node).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCTerminal.Measurements
documentation:
Measurements associated with this terminal defining where the measurement
is placed in the network topology. It may be used, for instance, to capture
the sensor position, such as a voltage transformer (PT) at a busbar or
a current transformer (CT) at the bar between a breaker and an isolator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCTerminal.OperationalLimitSet
documentation: The operational limit sets at the terminal.

An electrical connection point (AC or DC) to a piece of conducting equipment.
Terminals are connected at physical connection points called connectivity
nodes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCBaseTerminal

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCBaseTerminal.DCNode
documentation:
The DC connectivity node to which this DC base terminal connects with zero
impedance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCBaseTerminal.DCTopologicalNode
documentation: See association end Terminal.TopologicalNode.

An electrical connection point at a piece of DC conducting equipment. DC
terminals are connected at one physical DC node that may have multiple
DC terminals connected. A DC node is similar to an AC connectivity node.
The model requires that DC connections are distinct from AC connections.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverterDCTerminal

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverterDCTerminal.polarity
documentation:
Represents the normal network polarity condition. Depending on the converter
configuration the value shall be set as follows:
- For a monopole with two converter terminals use DCPolarityKind “positive”
and “negative”.
- For a bi-pole or symmetric monopole with three converter terminals use
DCPolarityKind “positive”, “middle” and “negative”.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverterDCTerminal.DCConductingEquipment
documentation: A DC converter terminal belong to an DC converter.

A DC electrical connection point at the AC/DC converter. The AC/DC converter
is electrically connected also to the AC side. The AC connection is inherited
from the AC conducting equipment in the same way as any other AC equipment.
The AC/DC converter DC terminal is separate from generic DC terminal to
restrict the connection with the AC side to AC/DC converter and so that
no other DC conducting equipment can be connected to the AC side.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCTerminal

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCTerminal.DCConductingEquipment
documentation: An DC terminal belong to a DC conducting equipment.
An electrical connection point to generic DC conducting equipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Terminal

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Terminal.phases
documentation:
Represents the normal network phasing condition. If the attribute is missing,
three phases (ABC) shall be assumed, except for terminals of grounding
classes (specializations of EarthFaultCompensator, GroundDisconnector,
and Ground) which will be assumed to be N. Therefore, phase code ABCN is
explicitly declared when needed, e.g. for star point grounding equipment.
The phase code on terminals connecting same ConnectivityNode or same TopologicalNode
as well as for equipment between two terminals shall be consistent.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Terminal.ConductingEquipment
documentation:
The conducting equipment of the terminal. Conducting equipment have terminals
that may be connected to other conducting equipment terminals via connectivity
nodes or topological nodes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Terminal.ConnectivityNode
documentation: The connectivity node to which this terminal connects with zero impedance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Terminal.NormalHeadFeeder
documentation:
The feeder that this terminal normally feeds. Only specified for the terminals
at head of feeders.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Terminal.RegulatingControl
documentation: The controls regulating this terminal.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Terminal.TopologicalNode
documentation:
The topological node associated with the terminal. This can be used as
an alternative to the connectivity node path to topological node, thus
making it unnecessary to model connectivity nodes in some cases. Note that
the if connectivity nodes are in the model, this association would probably
not be used as an input specification.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Terminal.TransformerEnd
documentation: All transformer ends connected at this terminal.

An AC electrical connection point to a piece of conducting equipment. Terminals
are connected at physical connection points called connectivity nodes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Asset

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Asset.critical
documentation:
True if asset is considered critical for some reason (for example, a pole
with critical attachments).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Asset.initialCondition
documentation:
Condition of asset at last baseline. Examples include new, rebuilt, overhaul
required, other. Refer to inspection data for information on the most current
condition of the asset.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Asset.lotNumber
documentation:
Lot number for this asset. Even for the same model and version number,
many assets are manufactured in lots.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Asset.position
documentation:
Position of asset or asset component. May often be in relation to other
assets or components.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Asset.serialNumber
documentation: Serial number of this asset.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Asset.type
documentation:
Utility-specific classification of Asset and its subtypes, according to
their corporate standards, practices, and existing IT systems (e.g., for
management of assets, maintenance, work, outage, customers, etc.).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Asset.utcNumber
documentation: Uniquely tracked commodity (UTC) number.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Asset.initialLossOfLife
documentation:
Percentage of initial life expectancy that has been lost as of the last
life expectancy baseline. Represents
(initial life expectancy - current life expectancy) / initial life expectancy.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Asset.inUseState
documentation:
Indication of whether asset is currently deployed (in use), ready to be
put into use or not available for use.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Asset.kind
documentation:
Kind of asset. Used in description of asset components in asset instance
templates.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Asset.purchasePrice
documentation: Purchase price of asset.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Asset.AssetContainer
documentation: Container of this asset.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Asset.AssetInfo
documentation: Data applicable to this asset.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Asset.Location
documentation: Location of this asset.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Asset.Measurements
documentation: Measurement related to this asset.

Tangible resource of the utility, including power system equipment, various
end devices, cabinets, buildings, etc. For electrical network equipment,
the role of the asset is defined through PowerSystemResource and its subclasses,
defined mainly in the Wires model (refer to IEC61970-301 and model package
IEC61970::Wires). Asset description places emphasis on the physical characteristics
of the equipment fulfilling that role.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AssetContainer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AssetContainer.Assets
documentation: All assets within this container asset.

Asset that is aggregation of other assets such as conductors, transformers,
switchgear, land, fences, buildings, equipment, vehicles, etc.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DuctBank

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DuctBank.circuitCount
documentation:
Number of circuits in duct bank. Refer to associations between a duct (ConductorAsset)
and an ACLineSegment to understand which circuits are in which ducts.

A duct contains individual wires in the layout as specified with associated
wire spacing instances; number of them gives the number of conductors in
this duct.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDevice

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDevice.amrSystem
documentation:
Automated meter reading (AMR) or other communication system responsible
for communications to this end device.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDevice.installCode
documentation: Installation code.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDevice.isPan
documentation: If true, this is a premises area network (PAN) device.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDevice.isSmartInverter
documentation: If true, there is a communicating inverter present.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDevice.isVirtual
documentation:
If true, there is no physical device. As an example, a virtual meter can
be defined to aggregate the consumption for two or more physical meters.
Otherwise, this is a physical hardware device.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDevice.timeZoneOffset
documentation: Time zone offset relative to GMT for the location of this end device.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDevice.Customer
documentation: Customer owning this end device.

Asset container that performs one or more end device functions. One type
of end device is a meter which can perform metering, load management, connect/disconnect,
accounting functions, etc. Some end devices, such as ones monitoring and
controlling air conditioners, refrigerators, pool pumps may be connected
to a meter. All end devices may have communication capability defined by
the associated communication function(s). An end device may be owned by
a consumer, a service provider, utility or otherwise.
There may be a related end device function that identifies a sensor or
control point within a metering application or communications systems (e.g.,
water, gas, electricity).
Some devices may use an optical port that conforms to the ANSI C12.18 standard
for communications.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AssetInfo.ProductAssetModel
documentation: Product asset model which conforms to this catalog asset type.

Set of attributes of an asset, representing typical datasheet information
of a physical device that can be instantiated and shared in different data
exchange contexts:
- as attributes of an asset instance (installed or in stock)
- as attributes of an asset model (product by a manufacturer)
- as attributes of a type asset (generic type of an asset as used in designs/extension
planning).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerEndInfo.phaseAngleClock
documentation:
Winding phase angle where 360 degrees are represented with clock hours,
so the valid values are {0, ..., 11}. For example, to express the second
winding in code 'Dyn11', set attributes as follows: 'endNumber'=2, 'connectionKind'
= Yn and 'phaseAngleClock' = 11.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.WireSpacingInfo.usage
documentation: Usage of the associated wires.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.WireSpacingInfo.ACLineSegment
documentation: The AC line segment defined by the wire spacing information

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.WireSpacingInfo.WirePositions
documentation: All positions of single wires (phase or neutral) making the conductor.

Wire spacing data that associates multiple wire positions with the line
segment, and allows to calculate line segment impedances. Number of phases
can be derived from the number of associated wire positions whose phase
is not neutral.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachineDynamics

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachineDynamics.AsynchronousMachine
documentation:
Asynchronous machine to which this asynchronous machine dynamics model
applies.

Asynchronous machine whose behaviour is described by reference to a standard model expressed in either time constant reactance form or equivalent circuit form or by definition of a user-defined model. Parameter details:
  1. Asynchronous machine parameters such as Xl, Xs, etc. are actually used as inductances in the model, but are commonly referred to as reactances since, at nominal frequency, the PU values are the same. However, some references use the symbol L instead of X.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BaseFrequency

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BaseFrequency.frequency
documentation: The base frequency.

The BaseFrequency class describes a base frequency for a power system network.
In case of multiple power networks with different frequencies, e.g. 50
Hz or 60 Hz each network will have its own base frequency class. Hence
it is assumed that power system objects having different base frequencies
appear in separate documents where each document has a single base frequency
instance.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BasePower

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BasePower.basePower
documentation: Value used as base power.
The BasePower class defines the base power used in the per unit calculations.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BaseVoltage

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BaseVoltage.nominalVoltage
documentation:
The power system resource's base voltage. Shall be a positive value and
not zero.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BaseVoltage.ConductingEquipment
documentation:
All conducting equipment with this base voltage. Use only when there is
no voltage level container used and only one base voltage applies. For
example, not used for transformers.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BaseVoltage.TopologicalNode
documentation: The topological nodes at the base voltage.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BaseVoltage.TransformerEnds
documentation: Transformer ends at the base voltage. This is essential for PU calculation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BaseVoltage.VoltageLevel
documentation: The voltage levels having this base voltage.
Defines a system base voltage which is referenced.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BasicIntervalSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BasicIntervalSchedule.startTime
documentation:
The time for the first time point. The value can be a time of day, not
a specific date.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BasicIntervalSchedule.value1Multiplier
documentation: Multiplier for value1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BasicIntervalSchedule.value1Unit
documentation: Value1 units of measure.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BasicIntervalSchedule.value2Multiplier
documentation: Multiplier for value2.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BasicIntervalSchedule.value2Unit
documentation: Value2 units of measure.
Schedule of values at points in time.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.IrregularIntervalSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.IrregularIntervalSchedule.TimePoints
documentation: The point data values that define a curve.
The schedule has time points where the time between them varies.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegularIntervalSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegularIntervalSchedule.endTime
documentation:
The time for the last time point. The value can be a time of day, not a
specific date.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegularIntervalSchedule.timeStep
documentation:
The time between each pair of subsequent regular time points in sequence
order.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegularIntervalSchedule.TimePoints
documentation: The regular interval time point data values that define this schedule.
The schedule has time points where the time between them is constant.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GenUnitOpSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GenUnitOpSchedule.GeneratingUnit
documentation:
A generating unit may have an operating schedule, indicating the planned
operation of the unit.

The generating unit's Operator-approved current operating schedule (or
plan), typically produced with the aid of unit commitment type analyses.
The X-axis represents absolute time. The Y1-axis represents the status
(0=off-line and unavailable: 1=available: 2=must run: 3=must run at fixed
power value: etc.). The Y2-axis represents the must run fixed power value
where required.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SeasonDayTypeSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SeasonDayTypeSchedule.DayType
documentation: DayType for the Schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SeasonDayTypeSchedule.Season
documentation: Season for the Schedule.

A time schedule covering a 24 hour period, with curve data for a specific
type of season and day.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ConformLoadSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ConformLoadSchedule.ConformLoadGroup
documentation: The ConformLoadGroup where the ConformLoadSchedule belongs.

A curve of load versus time (X-axis) showing the active power values (Y1-axis)
and reactive power (Y2-axis) for each unit of the period covered. This
curve represents a typical pattern of load over the time period for a given
day type and season.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonConformLoadSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonConformLoadSchedule.NonConformLoadGroup
documentation: The NonConformLoadGroup where the NonConformLoadSchedule belongs.

An active power (Y1-axis) and reactive power (Y2-axis) schedule (curves)
versus time (X-axis) for non-conforming loads, e.g., large industrial load
or power station service (where modelled).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegulationSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegulationSchedule.RegulatingControl
documentation: Regulating controls that have this schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegulationSchedule.VoltageControlZones
documentation: A VoltageControlZone may have a voltage regulation schedule.

A pre-established pattern over time for a controlled variable, e.g., busbar
voltage.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SwitchSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SwitchSchedule.Switch
documentation: A SwitchSchedule is associated with a Switch.

A schedule of switch positions. If RegularTimePoint.value1 is 0, the switch
is open. If 1, the switch is closed.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapSchedule.TapChanger
documentation: A TapSchedule is associated with a TapChanger.
A pre-established pattern over time for a tap step.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BranchGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BranchGroup.monitorActivePower
documentation: Monitor the active power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BranchGroup.monitorReactivePower
documentation: Monitor the reactive power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BranchGroup.maximumActivePower
documentation: The maximum active power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BranchGroup.maximumReactivePower
documentation: The maximum reactive power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BranchGroup.minimumActivePower
documentation: The minimum active power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BranchGroup.minimumReactivePower
documentation: The minimum reactive power flow.

A group of branch terminals whose directed flow summation is to be monitored.
A branch group need not form a cutset of the network.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BusNameMarker

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BusNameMarker.priority
documentation:
Priority of bus name marker for use as topology bus name. Use 0 for do
not care. Use 1 for highest priority. Use 2 as priority is less than 1
and so on.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BusNameMarker.ReportingGroup
documentation: The reporting group to which this bus name marker belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BusNameMarker.Terminal
documentation: The terminals associated with this bus name marker.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BusNameMarker.TopologicalNode
documentation:
A user defined topological node that was originally defined in a planning
model not yet having topology described by ConnectivityNodes. Once ConnectivityNodes
has been created they may linked to user defined ToplogicalNdes using BusNameMarkers.

Used to apply user standard names to TopologicalNodes. Associated with
one or more terminals that are normally connected with the bus name. The
associated terminals are normally connected by non-retained switches. For
a ring bus station configuration, all BusbarSection terminals in the ring
are typically associated. For a breaker and a half scheme, both BusbarSections
would normally be associated. For a ring bus, all BusbarSections would
normally be associated. For a "straight" busbar configuration, normally
only the main terminal at the BusbarSection would be associated.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CSCDynamics
documentation:
CSC function block whose behaviour is described by reference to a standard
model <font color="#0f0f0f">or by definition of a user-defined model.</font>

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CalculationMethodHierarchy

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CalculationMethodHierarchy.Measurement
documentation: Measurement to which this calculation method hierarchy applies.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CalculationMethodHierarchy.MeasurementValue
documentation: Measurement value to which this calculation method hierarchy applies.

The hierarchy of calculation methods used to derive this measurement.
.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CatalogAssetType
documentation: a Assets that may be used for planning, work or design purposes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ClampAction
documentation: Action on Clamp as a switching step

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ConnectivityNode

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ConnectivityNode.ConnectivityNodeContainer
documentation: Container of this connectivity node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ConnectivityNode.Terminals
documentation: Terminals interconnected with zero impedance at a this connectivity node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ConnectivityNode.TopologicalNode
documentation:
The topological node to which this connectivity node is assigned. May depend
on the current state of switches in the network.

Connectivity nodes are points where terminals of AC conducting equipment
are connected together with zero impedance.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ControlAction

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ControlAction.analogValue
documentation:
The analog value used for the analog control, the raise/lower control and
the set point control

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ControlAction.discreteValue
documentation: The integer value used for the command or the accumulator reset.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ControlAction.Control
documentation: The control that the control action is performed on.
Control executed as a switching step.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ControlAreaGeneratingUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ControlAreaGeneratingUnit.GeneratingUnit
documentation:
The generating unit specified for this control area. Note that a control
area should include a GeneratingUnit only once.

A control area generating unit. This class is needed so that alternate
control area definitions may include the same generating unit. It should
be noted that only one instance within a control area should reference
a specific generating unit.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CoordinateSystem

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CoordinateSystem.Locations
documentation: All locations described with position points in this coordinate system.
Coordinate reference system.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Curve

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Curve.curveStyle
documentation: The style or shape of the curve.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Curve.xMultiplier
documentation: Multiplier for X-axis.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Curve.xUnit
documentation: The X-axis units of measure.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Curve.y1Multiplier
documentation: Multiplier for Y1-axis.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Curve.y1Unit
documentation: The Y1-axis units of measure.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Curve.y2Multiplier
documentation: Multiplier for Y2-axis.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Curve.y2Unit
documentation: The Y2-axis units of measure.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Curve.y3Multiplier
documentation: Multiplier for Y3-axis.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Curve.y3Unit
documentation: The Y3-axis units of measure.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Curve.CurveDatas
documentation: The point data values that define this curve.

A multi-purpose curve or functional relationship between an independent
variable (X-axis) and dependent (Y-axis) variables.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ReactiveCapabilityCurve

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ReactiveCapabilityCurve.coolantTemperature
documentation:
The machine's coolant temperature (e.g., ambient air or stator circulating
water).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ReactiveCapabilityCurve.hydrogenPressure
documentation: The hydrogen coolant pressure.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ReactiveCapabilityCurve.SynchronousMachines
documentation: Synchronous machines using this curve.

Reactive power rating envelope versus the synchronous machine's active
power, in both the generating and motoring modes. For each active power
value there is a corresponding high and low reactive power limit value.
Typically there will be a separate curve for each coolant condition, such
as hydrogen pressure. The Y1 axis values represent reactive minimum and
the Y2 axis values represent reactive maximum.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsCapabilityCurve
documentation:
The P-Q capability curve for a voltage source converter, with P on X-axis
and Qmin and Qmax on Y1-axis and Y2-axis.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Customer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Customer.locale
documentation: Locale designating language to use in communications with this customer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Customer.pucNumber
documentation: (if applicable) Public utilities commission (PUC) identification number.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Customer.specialNeed
documentation:
True if customer organisation has special service needs such as life support,
hospitals, etc.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Customer.vip
documentation:
(deprecated) (use 'priority' instead) True if this is an important customer.
Importance is for matters different than those in 'specialNeed' attribute.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Customer.kind
documentation: Kind of customer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Customer.Customer
documentation:
All customers related to the primary customer. This may support customer
hierarchies. (this can be used to support some form of customer containment)
Organisation receiving services from service supplier.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CustomerAgreement
documentation:
Agreement between the customer and the service supplier to pay for service
at a specific service location. It records certain billing information
about the type of service provided at the service location and is used
during charge creation to determine the type of service.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CutAction

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CutAction.kind
documentation: Switching action to perform.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CutAction.Cut
documentation: Cut on which this action is taken.
Action on cut as a switching step.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCNode

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCNode.DCEquipmentContainer
documentation: The DC container for the DC nodes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCNode.DCTerminals
documentation:
DC base terminals interconnected with zero impedance at a this DC connectivity
node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCNode.DCTopologicalNode
documentation:
The DC topological node to which this DC connectivity node is assigned.
May depend on the current state of switches in the network.

DC nodes are points where terminals of DC conducting equipment are connected
together with zero impedance.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCTopologicalIsland
documentation:
An electrically connected subset of the network. DC topological islands
can change as the current network state changes, e.g. due to:
- disconnect switches or breakers changing state in a SCADA/EMS.
- manual creation, change or deletion of topological nodes in a planning
tool.
Only energised TopologicalNode-s shall be part of the topological island.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCTopologicalNode

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCTopologicalNode.DCEquipmentContainer
documentation: The connectivity node container to which the topological node belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCTopologicalNode.DCNodes
documentation:
The DC connectivity nodes combined together to form this DC topological
node. May depend on the current state of switches in the network.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCTopologicalNode.DCTerminals
documentation: See association end TopologicalNode.Terminal.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCTopologicalNode.DCTopologicalIsland
documentation: A DC topological node belongs to a DC topological island.
DC bus.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERGroupDispatch
documentation:
Specifies the capabilities of the DER Group that are to be dispatched according
to a schedule. Each dispatch identifies a DER Group and the specific monitorable
parameter to be dispatched over a specific time interval.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERGroupForecast

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERGroupForecast.predictionCreationDate
documentation: The timestamp for when the DER Group forecast was created

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERGroupForecast.EndDeviceGroup
documentation: The DER Group for which forecasts are made.

Specifies the forecasted values of the DERMonitorableParameters for a DER
Group over a specific time interval.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DayType
documentation:
Group of similar days. For example it could be used to represent weekdays,
weekend, or holidays.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DemandResponseProgram

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DemandResponseProgram.type
documentation:
Type of demand response program; examples are CPP (critical-peak pricing),
RTP (real-time pricing), DLC (direct load control), DBP (demand bidding
program), BIP (base interruptible program). Note that possible types change
a lot and it would be impossible to enumerate them all.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DemandResponseProgram.CustomerAgreements
documentation:
All customer agreements through which the customer is enrolled in this
demand response program.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DemandResponseProgram.EndDeviceGroups
documentation: All groups of end devices enrolled in this demand response program.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DemandResponseProgram.UsagePointGroups
documentation: All usage point groups enrolled in this demand response program.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DemandResponseProgram.validityInterval
documentation: Interval within which the program is valid.
Demand response program.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDeviceControl
documentation:
Instructs an end device (or an end device group) to perform a specified
action.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDeviceGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDeviceGroup.type
documentation: Type of this group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDeviceGroup.DemandResponsePrograms
documentation: All demand response programs this group of end devices is enrolled in.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDeviceGroup.DERFunction
documentation: Functions supported by the DER Group

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDeviceGroup.DERGroupDispatch
documentation: An individual dispatch for a DER Group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDeviceGroup.DERGroupForecast
documentation: An individual forecast for a DER Group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDeviceGroup.DERMonitorableParameter
documentation: The DER monitorable parameters associated with a DER Group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDeviceGroup.EndDeviceControls
documentation: All end device controls sending commands to this end device group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDeviceGroup.EndDevices
documentation: All end devices this end device group refers to.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDeviceGroup.MeterReadSchedule
documentation: Meter read schedule that applies to the end device group

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDeviceGroup.status
documentation: Current status information relevant to a group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EndDeviceGroup.version
documentation: version of this group

Abstraction for management of group communications within a two-way AMR
system or the data for a group of related end devices. Commands can be
issued to all of the end devices that belong to the group using a defined
group address and the underlying AMR communication infrastructure. A DERGroup
and a PANDeviceGroup is an EndDeviceGroup.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyArea.ControlArea
documentation: The control area specification that is used for the load forecast.

Describes an area having energy production or consumption. Specializations
are intended to support the load allocation function as typically required
in energy management systems or planning studies to allocate hypothesized
load levels to individual load points for power flow analysis. Often the
energy area can be linked to both measured and forecast load levels.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LoadArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LoadArea.SubLoadAreas
documentation: The SubLoadAreas in the LoadArea.

The class is the root or first level in a hierarchical structure for grouping
of loads for the purpose of load flow load scaling.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SubLoadArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SubLoadArea.LoadArea
documentation: The LoadArea where the SubLoadArea belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SubLoadArea.LoadGroups
documentation: The Loadgroups in the SubLoadArea.

The class is the second level in a hierarchical structure for grouping
of loads for the purpose of load flow load scaling.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumerAction

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumerAction.kind
documentation: Switching action to perform

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LoadDynamics

documentation: Energy consumer to which this dynamics load model applies.

Load whose behaviour is described by reference to a standard model <font
color="#0f0f0f">or by definition of a user-defined model.</font>
A standard feature of dynamic load behaviour modelling is the ability to
associate the same behaviour to multiple energy consumers by means of a
single load definition. The load model is always applied to individual
bus loads (energy consumers).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LoadResponseCharacteristic

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LoadResponseCharacteristic.exponentModel
documentation:
Indicates the exponential voltage dependency model is to be used. If false,
the coefficient model is to be used.
The exponential voltage dependency model consist of the attributes:
- pVoltageExponent
- qVoltageExponent
- pFrequencyExponent
- qFrequencyExponent.
The coefficient model consist of the attributes:
- pConstantImpedance
- pConstantCurrent
- pConstantPower
- qConstantImpedance
- qConstantCurrent
- qConstantPower.
The sum of pConstantImpedance, pConstantCurrent and pConstantPower shall
equal 1.
The sum of qConstantImpedance, qConstantCurrent and qConstantPower shall
equal 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LoadResponseCharacteristic.pConstantCurrent
documentation: Portion of active power load modelled as constant current.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LoadResponseCharacteristic.pConstantImpedance
documentation: Portion of active power load modelled as constant impedance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LoadResponseCharacteristic.pConstantPower
documentation: Portion of active power load modelled as constant power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LoadResponseCharacteristic.pFrequencyExponent
documentation: Exponent of per unit frequency effecting active power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LoadResponseCharacteristic.pVoltageExponent
documentation: Exponent of per unit voltage effecting real power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LoadResponseCharacteristic.qConstantCurrent
documentation: Portion of reactive power load modelled as constant current.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LoadResponseCharacteristic.qConstantImpedance
documentation: Portion of reactive power load modelled as constant impedance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LoadResponseCharacteristic.qConstantPower
documentation: Portion of reactive power load modelled as constant power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LoadResponseCharacteristic.qFrequencyExponent
documentation: Exponent of per unit frequency effecting reactive power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LoadResponseCharacteristic.qVoltageExponent
documentation: Exponent of per unit voltage effecting reactive power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LoadResponseCharacteristic.EnergyConsumer
documentation: The set of loads that have the response characteristics.

Models the characteristic response of the load demand due to changes in
system conditions such as voltage and frequency. It is not related to demand
response.
If LoadResponseCharacteristic.exponentModel is True, the exponential voltage
or frequency dependent models are specified and used as to calculate active
and reactive power components of the load model.
The equations to calculate active and reactive power components of the
load model are internal to the power flow calculation, hence they use different
quantities depending on the use case of the data exchange.
The equations for exponential voltage dependent load model injected power
are:
pInjection= Pnominal* (Voltage/cim:BaseVoltage.nominalVoltage) ** cim:LoadResponseCharacteristic.pVoltageExponent
qInjection= Qnominal* (Voltage/cim:BaseVoltage.nominalVoltage) ** cim:LoadResponseCharacteristic.qVoltageExponent
Where:
1) * means "multiply" and ** is "raised to power of";
2) Pnominal and Qnominal represent the active power and reactive power
at nominal voltage as any load described by the voltage exponential model
shall be given at nominal voltage. This means that EnergyConsumer.p and
EnergyConsumer.q are at nominal voltage.
3) After power flow is solved:
-pInjection and qInjection correspond to SvPowerflow.p and SvPowerflow.q
respectively.
- Voltage corresponds to SvVoltage.v at the TopologicalNode where the load
is connected.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Location.Assets
documentation: All assets at this location.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Location.CoordinateSystem
documentation: Coordinate system used to describe position points of this location.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Location.Measurements
documentation: All measurements at this location.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Location.PositionPoints

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Location.PowerSystemResources

The place, scene, or point of something where someone or something has
been, is, and/or will be at a given moment in time. It can be defined with
one or more position points (coordinates) in a given coordinate system.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Measurement

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Measurement.measurementType
documentation:
Specifies the type of measurement. For example, this specifies if the measurement
represents an indoor temperature, outdoor temperature, bus voltage, line
flow, etc.
When the measurementType is set to "Specialization", the type of Measurement
is defined in more detail by the specialized class which inherits from
Measurement.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Measurement.phases
documentation:
Indicates to which phases the measurement applies and avoids the need to
use 'measurementType' to also encode phase information (which would explode
the types). The phase information in Measurement, along with 'measurementType'
and 'phases' uniquely defines a Measurement for a device, based on normal
network phase. Their meaning will not change when the computed energizing
phasing is changed due to jumpers or other reasons.
If the attribute is missing three phases (ABC) shall be assumed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Measurement.unitMultiplier
documentation: The unit multiplier of the measured quantity.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Measurement.unitSymbol
documentation: The unit of measure of the measured quantity.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Measurement.Asset
documentation: Asset that has a measurement

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Measurement.CalculationMethodHierarchy
documentation: Calculation method hierarchy which applies to this analog.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Measurement.Locations
documentation: Location of this measurement.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Measurement.MeasurementAction
documentation: The measurement action that is performed on the measurement

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Measurement.PowerSystemResource
documentation: The power system resource that contains the measurement.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Measurement.Terminal
documentation: One or more measurements may be associated with a terminal in the network.

A Measurement represents any measured, calculated or non-measured non-calculated
quantity. Any piece of equipment may contain Measurements, e.g. a substation
may have temperature measurements and door open indications, a transformer
may have oil temperature and tank pressure measurements, a bay may contain
a number of power flow measurements and a Breaker may contain a switch
status measurement.
The PSR - Measurement association is intended to capture this use of Measurement
and is included in the naming hierarchy based on EquipmentContainer. The
naming hierarchy typically has Measurements as leaves, e.g. Substation-VoltageLevel-Bay-Switch-Measurement.
Some Measurements represent quantities related to a particular sensor location
in the network, e.g. a voltage transformer (VT) or potential transformer
(PT) at a busbar or a current transformer (CT) at the bar between a breaker
and an isolator. The sensing position is not captured in the PSR - Measurement
association. Instead it is captured by the Measurement - Terminal association
that is used to define the sensing location in the network topology. The
location is defined by the connection of the Terminal to ConductingEquipment.
If both a Terminal and PSR are associated, and the PSR is of type ConductingEquipment,
the associated Terminal should belong to that ConductingEquipment instance.
When the sensor location is needed both Measurement-PSR and Measurement-Terminal
are used. The Measurement-Terminal association is never used alone.

Normal value range maximum for any of the MeasurementValue.values. Used
for scaling, e.g. in bar graphs or of telemetered raw values.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Discrete.minValue
documentation:
Normal value range minimum for any of the MeasurementValue.values. Used
for scaling, e.g. in bar graphs or of telemetered raw values.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Discrete.normalValue
documentation: Normal measurement value, e.g., used for percentage calculations.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Discrete.DiscreteValues
documentation: The values connected to this measurement.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Discrete.ValueAliasSet
documentation:
The ValueAliasSet used for translation of a MeasurementValue.value to a
name.

Discrete represents a discrete Measurement, i.e. a Measurement representing
discrete values, e.g. a Breaker position.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.StringMeasurement

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.StringMeasurement.StringMeasurementValues
documentation: The values connected to this measurement.
StringMeasurement represents a measurement with values of type string.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.MeasurementAction

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.MeasurementAction.Measurement
documentation: The measurement that the measurement action is performed on
Measurement taken as a switching step.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.MeasurementValueSource

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.MeasurementValueSource.MeasurementValues
documentation: The MeasurementValues updated by the source.

MeasurementValueSource describes the alternative sources updating a MeasurementValue.
User conventions for how to use the MeasurementValueSource attributes are
defined in IEC 61970-301.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.IOPointSource
documentation: Indicates the point source for an IO Point.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.MeterReadSchedule.EndDevice
documentation: All end devices for the meter read schedule.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.MutualCoupling

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.MutualCoupling.b0ch
documentation:
Zero sequence mutual coupling shunt (charging) susceptance, uniformly distributed,
of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.MutualCoupling.distance11
documentation:
Distance to the start of the coupled region from the first line's terminal
having sequence number equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.MutualCoupling.distance12
documentation:
Distance to the end of the coupled region from the first line's terminal
with sequence number equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.MutualCoupling.distance21
documentation:
Distance to the start of coupled region from the second line's terminal
with sequence number equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.MutualCoupling.distance22
documentation:
Distance to the end of coupled region from the second line's terminal with
sequence number equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.MutualCoupling.g0ch
documentation:
Zero sequence mutual coupling shunt (charging) conductance, uniformly distributed,
of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.MutualCoupling.r0
documentation: Zero sequence branch-to-branch mutual impedance coupling, resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.MutualCoupling.x0
documentation: Zero sequence branch-to-branch mutual impedance coupling, reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.MutualCoupling.First_Terminal
documentation:
The starting terminal for the calculation of distances along the first
branch of the mutual coupling. Normally MutualCoupling would only be used
for terminals of AC line segments. The first and second terminals of a
mutual coupling should point to different AC line segments.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.MutualCoupling.Second_Terminal
documentation:
The starting terminal for the calculation of distances along the second
branch of the mutual coupling.
This class represents the zero sequence line mutual coupling.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OldPerson
documentation: General purpose information for name and other information to contact people.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperatingParticipant

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperatingParticipant.OperatingShare
documentation:
The operating shares of this operating participant. An operating participant
can be resused for any number of power system resources.

An operator of multiple power system resource objects. Note multple operating
participants may operate the same power system resource object. This can
be used for modeling jointly owned units where each owner operates as a
contractual share.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperationalLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperationalLimit.OperationalLimitSet
documentation: The limit set to which the limit values belong.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperationalLimit.OperationalLimitType
documentation: The limit type associated with this limit.

A value and normal value associated with a specific kind of limit.
The sub class value and normalValue attributes vary inversely to the associated
OperationalLimitType.acceptableDuration (acceptableDuration for short).
If a particular piece of equipment has multiple operational limits of the
same kind (apparent power, current, etc.), the limit with the greatest
acceptableDuration shall have the smallest limit value and the limit with
the smallest acceptableDuration shall have the largest limit value. Note:
A large current can only be allowed to flow through a piece of equipment
for a short duration without causing damage, but a lesser current can be
allowed to flow for a longer duration.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ActivePowerLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ActivePowerLimit.normalValue
documentation:
The normal value of active power limit. The attribute shall be a positive
value or zero.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ActivePowerLimit.value
documentation:
Value of active power limit. The attribute shall be a positive value or
zero.
Limit on active power flow.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ApparentPowerLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ApparentPowerLimit.normalValue
documentation:
The normal apparent power limit. The attribute shall be a positive value
or zero.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ApparentPowerLimit.value
documentation: The apparent power limit. The attribute shall be a positive value or zero.
Apparent power limit.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CurrentLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CurrentLimit.normalValue
documentation:
The normal value for limit on current flow. The attribute shall be a positive
value or zero.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CurrentLimit.value
documentation: Limit on current flow. The attribute shall be a positive value or zero.
Operational limit on current.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VoltageLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VoltageLimit.normalValue
documentation:
The normal limit on voltage. High or low limit nature of the limit depends
upon the properties of the operational limit type. The attribute shall
be a positive value or zero.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VoltageLimit.value
documentation:
Limit on voltage. High or low limit nature of the limit depends upon the
properties of the operational limit type. The attribute shall be a positive
value or zero.

Operational limit applied to voltage.
The use of operational VoltageLimit is preferred instead of limits defined
at VoltageLevel. The operational VoltageLimits are used, if present.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperationalLimitSet

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperationalLimitSet.Equipment
documentation: The equipment to which the limit set applies.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperationalLimitSet.OperationalLimitValue
documentation: Values of equipment limits.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperationalLimitSet.Terminal
documentation: The terminal where the operational limit set apply.

A set of limits associated with equipment. Sets of limits might apply to
a specific temperature, or season for example. A set of limits may contain
different severities of limit levels that would apply to the same equipment.
The set may contain limits of different types such as apparent power and
current limits or high and low voltage limits that are logically applied
together as a set.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperationalLimitType

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperationalLimitType.isInfiniteDuration
documentation:
Defines if the operational limit type has infinite duration. If true, the
limit has infinite duration. If false, the limit has definite duration
which is defined by the attribute acceptableDuration.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperationalLimitType.acceptableDuration
documentation:
The nominal acceptable duration of the limit. Limits are commonly expressed
in terms of the time limit for which the limit is normally acceptable.
The actual acceptable duration of a specific limit may depend on other
local factors such as temperature or wind speed. The attribute has meaning
only if the flag isInfiniteDuration is set to false, hence it shall not
be exchanged when isInfiniteDuration is set to true.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperationalLimitType.direction
documentation: The direction of the limit.
The operational meaning of a category of limits.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperationalRestriction
documentation:
A document that can be associated with equipment to describe any sort of
restrictions compared with the original manufacturer's specification or
with the usual operational practice e.g. temporary maximum loadings, maximum
switching current, do not operate if bus couplers are open, etc.
In the UK, for example, if a breaker or switch ever mal-operates, this
is reported centrally and utilities use their asset systems to identify
all the installed devices of the same manufacturer's type. They then apply
operational restrictions in the operational systems to warn operators of
potential problems. After appropriate inspection and maintenance, the operational
restrictions may be removed.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Outage

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Outage.communityDescriptor
documentation:
a name to denote the community - this could be a name or a code of some
kind.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Outage.customersRestored
documentation: number of customers that have been restored in the area.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Outage.metersAffected
documentation:
The updated number of meters affected by the outage as reported by the
OMS within the utility. It is assumed this number will be updated repeatedly
until the full outage is resolved.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Outage.originalCustomersServed
documentation:
the total number of customers that are served in the area (both outaged
and not outaged).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Outage.originalMetersAffected
documentation:
The original number of meters that were affected as reported by the OMS
within the utility. That is, this is the total number of meters that were
out at the beginning of the outage.
