class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Outage.utilityDisclaimer
documentation:
This contains an disclaimers the utility would like to place on the data
provided to any stakeholder. This may be different for different stakeholders.
This should possibly be an attribute under the Organization class but it
is placed here for now.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Outage.Equipments
documentation: All equipments associated with this outage.

Document describing details of an active or planned outage in a part of
the electrical network.
A non-planned outage may be created upon:
- a breaker trip,
- a fault indicator status change,
- a meter event indicating customer outage,
- a reception of one or more customer trouble calls, or
- an operator command, reflecting information obtained from the field crew.
Outage restoration may be performed using a switching plan which complements
the outage information with detailed switching activities, including the
relationship to the crew and work.
A planned outage may be created upon:
- a request for service, maintenance or construction work in the field,
or
- an operator-defined outage for what-if/contingency network analysis.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PSRType

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PSRType.PowerSystemResources
documentation: Power system resources classified with this power system resource type.

Classifying instances of the same class, e.g. overhead and underground
ACLineSegments. This classification mechanism is intended to provide flexibility
outside the scope of this document, i.e. provide customisation that is
non standard.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthLineParameter

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthLineParameter.WireAssemblyInfo
documentation:
A WireAssemblyInfo is used to compute the PerLengthParameter data in the
Wires package
Common type for per-length electrical catalogues describing line parameters.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthDCLineParameter

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthDCLineParameter.capacitance
documentation:
Capacitance per unit of length of the DC line segment; significant for
cables only.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthDCLineParameter.inductance
documentation: Inductance per unit of length of the DC line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthDCLineParameter.resistance
documentation: Resistance per length of the DC line segment.
Common type for per-length electrical catalogues describing DC line parameters.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthImpedance.ACLineSegments
documentation: All line segments described by this per-length impedance.
Common type for per-length impedance electrical catalogues.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthPhaseImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthPhaseImpedance.conductorCount
documentation:
Number of phase, neutral, and other wires retained. Constrains the number
of matrix elements and the phase codes that can be used with this matrix.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthPhaseImpedance.PhaseImpedanceData
documentation: All data that belong to this conductor phase impedance.

Impedance and admittance parameters per unit length for n-wire unbalanced
lines, in matrix form.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthSequenceImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.b0ch
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) susceptance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.bch
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) susceptance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.g0ch
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) conductance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.gch
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.r
documentation: Positive sequence series resistance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.r0
documentation: Zero sequence series resistance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.x
documentation: Positive sequence series reactance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.x0
documentation: Zero sequence series reactance, per unit of length.

Sequence impedance and admittance parameters per unit length, for transposed
lines of 1, 2, or 3 phases. For 1-phase lines, define x=x0=xself. For 2-phase
lines, define x=xs-xm and x0=xs+xm.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerTable

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerTable.PhaseTapChangerTablePoint
documentation: The points of this table.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerTable.PhaseTapChangerTabular
documentation: The phase tap changers to which this phase tap table applies.

Describes a tabular curve for how the phase angle difference and impedance
varies with the tap step.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerSystemResource

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerSystemResource.AssetDatasheet
documentation: Datasheet information for this power system resource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerSystemResource.Assets
documentation:
All assets represented by this power system resource. For example, multiple
conductor assets are electrically modelled as a single AC line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerSystemResource.Controls
documentation:
The controller outputs used to actually govern a regulating device, e.g.
the magnetization of a synchronous machine or capacitor bank breaker actuator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerSystemResource.Location
documentation: Location of this power system resource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerSystemResource.Measurements
documentation: The measurements associated with this power system resource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerSystemResource.PSRType
documentation: Custom classification for this power system resource.

A power system resource (PSR) can be an item of equipment such as a switch,
an equipment container containing many individual items of equipment such
as a substation, or an organisational entity such as sub-control area.
Power system resources can have measurements associated.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegmentPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegmentPhase.sequenceNumber
documentation:
Number designation for this line segment phase. Each line segment phase
within a line segment should have a unique sequence number. This is useful
for unbalanced modelling to bind the mathematical model (PhaseImpedanceData
of PerLengthPhaseImpedance) with the connectivity model (this class) and
the physical model (WirePosition) without tight coupling.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegmentPhase.phase
documentation: The phase connection of the wire at both ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegmentPhase.ACLineSegment
documentation: The line segment to which the phase belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegmentPhase.WireInfo
documentation: Wire information contributing to this AC line segment phase information.
Represents a single wire of an alternating current line segment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ConnectivityNodeContainer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ConnectivityNodeContainer.ConnectivityNodes
documentation: Connectivity nodes which belong to this connectivity node container.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ConnectivityNodeContainer.TopologicalNode
documentation: The topological nodes which belong to this connectivity node container.

A base class for all objects that may contain connectivity nodes or topological
nodes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EquipmentContainer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EquipmentContainer.AdditionalGroupedEquipment
documentation:
The additonal contained equipment. The equipment belong to the equipment
container. The equipment is contained in another equipment container, but
also grouped with this equipment container. Examples include when a switch
contained in a substation is also desired to be grouped with a line contianer
or when a switch is included in a secondary substation and also grouped
in a feeder.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EquipmentContainer.Equipments
documentation: Contained equipment.
A modelling construct to provide a root class for containing equipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Bay

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Bay.bayEnergyMeasFlag
documentation: Indicates the presence/absence of energy measurements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Bay.bayPowerMeasFlag
documentation: Indicates the presence/absence of active/reactive power measurements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Bay.breakerConfiguration
documentation: Breaker configuration.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Bay.busBarConfiguration
documentation: Busbar configuration.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Bay.Substation
documentation: Substation containing the bay.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Bay.VoltageLevel
documentation: The voltage level containing this bay.

A collection of power system resources (within a given substation) including
conducting equipment, protection relays, measurements, and telemetry. A
bay typically represents a physical grouping related to modularization
of equipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCEquipmentContainer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCEquipmentContainer.DCNodes
documentation: The DC nodes contained in the DC equipment container.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCEquipmentContainer.DCTopologicalNode
documentation: The topological nodes which belong to this connectivity node container.

A modelling construct to provide a root class for containment of DC as
well as AC equipment. The class differ from the EquipmentContaner for AC
in that it may also contain DCNode-s. Hence it can contain both AC and
DC equipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCConverterUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCConverterUnit.operationMode
documentation:
The operating mode of an HVDC bipole (bipolar, monopolar metallic return,
etc).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCConverterUnit.Substation
documentation: The containing substation of the DC converter unit.

Indivisible operative unit comprising all equipment between the point of
common coupling on the AC side and the point of common coupling – DC side,
essentially one or more converters, together with one or more converter
transformers, converter control equipment, essential protective and switching
devices and auxiliaries, if any, used for conversion.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCLine

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCLine.Region
documentation: The SubGeographicalRegion containing the DC line.
Overhead lines and/or cables connecting two or more HVDC substations.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Feeder

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Feeder.NamingSecondarySubstation
documentation:
The secondary substations that are normally energized from the feeder.
Used for naming purposes. Should be consistent with the other associations
for energizing terminal specification and the feeder energization specification.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Feeder.NormalEnergizedSubstation
documentation: The substations that are normally energized by the feeder.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Feeder.NormalEnergizingSubstation
documentation:
The substation that nominally energizes the feeder. Also used for naming
purposes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Feeder.NormalHeadTerminal
documentation: The normal head terminal or terminals of the feeder.

A collection of equipment for organizational purposes, used for grouping
distribution resources.
The organization a feeder does not necessarily reflect connectivity or
current operation state.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Line

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Line.Region
documentation: The sub-geographical region of the line.

Contains equipment beyond a substation belonging to a power transmission
line.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Plant
documentation: A Plant is a collection of equipment for purposes of generation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Substation

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Substation.Bays
documentation: Bays contained in the substation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Substation.NamingFeeder
documentation:
The primary feeder that normally energizes the secondary substation. Used
for naming purposes. Either this association or the substation to subgeographical
region should be used for hierarchical containment specification.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Substation.NormalEnergizedFeeder
documentation: The normal energized feeders of the substation. Also used for naming purposes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Substation.NormalEnergizingFeeder
documentation:
The feeders that potentially energize the downstream substation. Should
be consistent with the associations that describe the naming hierarchy.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Substation.Region
documentation: The SubGeographicalRegion containing the substation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Substation.VoltageLevels
documentation: The voltage levels within this substation.

A collection of equipment for purposes other than generation or utilization,
through which electric energy in bulk is passed for the purposes of switching
or modifying its characteristics.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VoltageLevel

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VoltageLevel.highVoltageLimit
documentation:
The bus bar's high voltage limit.
The limit applies to all equipment and nodes contained in a given VoltageLevel.
It is not required that it is exchanged in pair with lowVoltageLimit. It
is preferable to use operational VoltageLimit, which prevails, if present.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VoltageLevel.lowVoltageLimit
documentation:
The bus bar's low voltage limit.
The limit applies to all equipment and nodes contained in a given VoltageLevel.
It is not required that it is exchanged in pair with highVoltageLimit.
It is preferable to use operational VoltageLimit, which prevails, if present.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VoltageLevel.BaseVoltage
documentation: The base voltage used for all equipment within the voltage level.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VoltageLevel.Bays
documentation: The bays within this voltage level.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VoltageLevel.Substation
documentation: The substation of the voltage level.

A collection of equipment at one common system voltage forming a switchgear.
The equipment typically consists of breakers, busbars, instrumentation,
control, regulation and protection devices as well as assemblies of all
these.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ControlArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ControlArea.netInterchange
documentation:
The specified positive net interchange into the control area, i.e. positive
sign means flow into the area.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ControlArea.pTolerance
documentation:
Active power net interchange tolerance. The attribute shall be a positive
value or zero.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ControlArea.EnergyArea
documentation: The energy area that is forecast from this control area specification.

A control area is a grouping of generating units and/or loads and a cutset
of tie lines (as terminals) which may be used for a variety of purposes
including automatic generation control, power flow solution area interchange
control specification, and input to load forecasting. All generation and
load within the area defined by the terminals on the border are considered
in the area interchange control. Note that any number of overlapping control
area specifications can be superimposed on the physical model. The following
general principles apply to ControlArea:
1. The control area orientation for net interchange is positive for an
import, negative for an export.
2. The control area net interchange is determined by summing flows in Terminals.
The Terminals are identified by creating a set of TieFlow objects associated
with a ControlArea object. Each TieFlow object identifies one Terminal.
3. In a single network model, a tie between two control areas must be modelled
in both control area specifications, such that the two representations
of the tie flow sum to zero.
4. The normal orientation of Terminal flow is positive for flow into the
conducting equipment that owns the Terminal. (i.e. flow from a bus into
a device is positive.) However, the orientation of each flow in the control
area specification must align with the control area convention, i.e. import
is positive. If the orientation of the Terminal flow referenced by a TieFlow
is positive into the control area, then this is confirmed by setting TieFlow.positiveFlowIn
flag TRUE. If not, the orientation must be reversed by setting the TieFlow.positiveFlowIn
flag FALSE.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumerPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumerPhase.p
documentation:
Active power of the load. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
For voltage dependent loads the value is at rated voltage.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumerPhase.pfixed
documentation:
Active power of the load that is a fixed quantity. Load sign convention
is used, i.e. positive sign means flow out from a node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumerPhase.pfixedPct
documentation:
Fixed active power as per cent of load group fixed active power. Load sign
convention is used, i.e. positive sign means flow out from a node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumerPhase.phase
documentation:
Phase of this energy consumer component. If the energy consumer is wye
connected, the connection is from the indicated phase to the central ground
or neutral point. If the energy consumer is delta connected, the phase
indicates an energy consumer connected from the indicated phase to the
next logical non-neutral phase.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumerPhase.q
documentation:
Reactive power of the load. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive
sign means flow out from a node.
For voltage dependent loads the value is at rated voltage.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumerPhase.qfixed
documentation:
Reactive power of the load that is a fixed quantity. Load sign convention
is used, i.e. positive sign means flow out from a node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumerPhase.qfixedPct
documentation:
Fixed reactive power as per cent of load group fixed reactive power. Load
sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign means flow out from a node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumerPhase.EnergyConsumer
documentation: The energy consumer to which this phase belongs.
A single phase of an energy consumer.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySourcePhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySourcePhase.phase
documentation:
Phase of this energy source component. If the energy source wye connected,
the connection is from the indicated phase to the central ground or neutral
point. If the energy source is delta connected, the phase indicates an
energy source connected from the indicated phase to the next logical non-neutral
phase.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySourcePhase.EnergySource
documentation: The energy sourceto which the phase belongs.
Represents the single phase information of an unbalanced energy source.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Equipment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Equipment.aggregate
documentation:
The aggregate flag provides an alternative way of representing an aggregated
(equivalent) element. It is applicable in cases when the dedicated classes
for equivalent equipment do not have all of the attributes necessary to
represent the required level of detail. In case the flag is set to “true”
the single instance of equipment represents multiple pieces of equipment
that have been modelled together as an aggregate equivalent obtained by
a network reduction procedure. Examples would be power transformers or
synchronous machines operating in parallel modelled as a single aggregate
power transformer or aggregate synchronous machine.
The attribute is not used for EquivalentBranch, EquivalentShunt and EquivalentInjection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Equipment.inService
documentation:
Specifies the availability of the equipment. True means the equipment is
available for topology processing, which determines if the equipment is
energized or not. False means that the equipment is treated by network
applications as if it is not in the model.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Equipment.networkAnalysisEnabled
documentation:
The equipment is enabled to participate in network analysis. If unspecified,
the value is assumed to be true.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Equipment.normallyInService
documentation:
Specifies the availability of the equipment under normal operating conditions.
True means the equipment is available for topology processing, which determines
if the equipment is energized or not. False means that the equipment is
treated by network applications as if it is not in the model.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Equipment.AdditionalEquipmentContainer
documentation:
Additional equipment container beyond the primary equipment container.
The equipment is contained in another equipment container, but also grouped
with this equipment container.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Equipment.EquipmentContainer
documentation: Container of this equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Equipment.OperationalLimitSet
documentation: The operational limit sets associated with this equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Equipment.OperationalRestrictions
documentation: All operational restrictions for this equipment.
The parts of a power system that are physical devices, electronic or mechanical.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CompositeSwitch

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CompositeSwitch.compositeSwitchType
documentation:
An alphanumeric code that can be used as a reference to extra information
such as the description of the interlocking scheme if any.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CompositeSwitch.Switches
documentation: Switches contained in this Composite switch.

A model of a set of individual Switches normally enclosed within the same
cabinet and possibly with interlocks that restrict the combination of switch
positions. These are typically found in medium voltage distribution networks.
A CompositeSwitch could represent a Ring-Main-Unit (RMU), or pad-mounted
switchgear, with primitive internal devices such as an internal bus-bar
plus 3 or 4 internal switches each of which may individually be open or
closed. A CompositeSwitch and a set of contained Switches can also be used
to represent a multi-position switch e.g. a switch that can connect a circuit
to Ground, Open or Busbar.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ConductingEquipment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ConductingEquipment.BaseVoltage
documentation:
Base voltage of this conducting equipment. Use only when there is no voltage
level container used and only one base voltage applies. For example, not
used for transformers.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ConductingEquipment.GroundingAction
documentation: Action involving grounding operation on this conducting equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ConductingEquipment.JumpingAction
documentation: Jumper action involving jumping operation on this conducting equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ConductingEquipment.Outage
documentation: The outage that is isolated by the outage isolation equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ConductingEquipment.Terminals
documentation:
Conducting equipment have terminals that may be connected to other conducting
equipment terminals via connectivity nodes or topological nodes.

The parts of the AC power system that are designed to carry current or
that are conductively connected through terminals.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.numberOfValves
documentation: Number of valves in the converter. Used in loss calculations.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.baseS
documentation:
Base apparent power of the converter pole. The attribute shall be a positive
value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.idc
documentation:
Converter DC current, also called Id. It is converter’s state variable,
result from power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.idleLoss
documentation:
Active power loss in pole at no power transfer. It is converter’s configuration
data used in power flow. The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.maxP
documentation:
Maximum active power limit. The value is overwritten by values of VsCapabilityCurve,
if present.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.maxUdc
documentation:
The maximum voltage on the DC side at which the converter should operate.
It is converter’s configuration data used in power flow. The attribute
shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.minP
documentation:
Minimum active power limit. The value is overwritten by values of VsCapabilityCurve,
if present.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.minUdc
documentation:
The minimum voltage on the DC side at which the converter should operate.
It is converter’s configuration data used in power flow. The attribute
shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.p
documentation:
Active power at the point of common coupling. Load sign convention is used,
i.e. positive sign means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution in the case a simplified power
flow model is used.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.poleLossP
documentation:
The active power loss at a DC Pole
= idleLoss + switchingLoss*|Idc| + resitiveLoss*Idc^2.
For lossless operation Pdc=Pac.
For rectifier operation with losses Pdc=Pac-lossP.
For inverter operation with losses Pdc=Pac+lossP.
It is converter’s state variable used in power flow. The attribute shall
be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.q
documentation:
Reactive power at the point of common coupling. Load sign convention is
used, i.e. positive sign means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution in the case a simplified power
flow model is used.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.ratedUdc
documentation:
Rated converter DC voltage, also called UdN. The attribute shall be a positive
value. It is converter’s configuration data used in power flow. For instance
a bipolar HVDC link with value 200 kV has a 400kV difference between the
dc lines.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.resistiveLoss
documentation:
It is converter’s configuration data used in power flow. Refer to poleLossP.
The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.switchingLoss
documentation:
Switching losses, relative to the base apparent power 'baseS'. Refer to
poleLossP. The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.targetPpcc
documentation:
Real power injection target in AC grid, at point of common coupling. Load
sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign means flow out from a node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.targetUdc
documentation:
Target value for DC voltage magnitude. The attribute shall be a positive
value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.uc
documentation:
Line-to-line converter voltage, the voltage at the AC side of the valve.
It is converter’s state variable, result from power flow. The attribute
shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.udc
documentation:
Converter voltage at the DC side, also called Ud. It is converter’s state
variable, result from power flow. The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.valveU0
documentation:
Valve threshold voltage, also called Uvalve. Forward voltage drop when
the valve is conducting. Used in loss calculations, i.e. the switchLoss
depend on numberOfValves * valveU0.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACDCConverter.PccTerminal
documentation:
Point of common coupling terminal for this converter DC side. It is typically
the terminal on the power transformer (or switch) closest to the AC network.

A unit with valves for three phases, together with unit control equipment,
essential protective and switching devices, DC storage capacitors, phase
reactors and auxiliaries, if any, used for conversion.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CsConverter

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CsConverter.alpha
documentation:
Firing angle that determines the dc voltage at the converter dc terminal.
Typical value between 10 degrees and 18 degrees for a rectifier. It is
converter’s state variable, result from power flow. The attribute shall
be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CsConverter.gamma
documentation:
Extinction angle. It is used to limit the dc voltage at the inverter if
needed. Typical value between 17 degrees and 20 degrees for an inverter.
It is converter’s state variable, result from power flow. The attribute
shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CsConverter.maxAlpha
documentation:
Maximum firing angle. It is converter’s configuration data used in power
flow. The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CsConverter.maxGamma
documentation:
Maximum extinction angle. It is converter’s configuration data used in
power flow. The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CsConverter.maxIdc
documentation:
The maximum direct current (Id) on the DC side at which the converter should
operate. It is converter’s configuration data use in power flow. The attribute
shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CsConverter.minAlpha
documentation:
Minimum firing angle. It is converter’s configuration data used in power
flow. The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CsConverter.minGamma
documentation:
Minimum extinction angle. It is converter’s configuration data used in
power flow. The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CsConverter.minIdc
documentation:
The minimum direct current (Id) on the DC side at which the converter should
operate. It is converter’s configuration data used in power flow. The attribute
shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CsConverter.operatingMode
documentation:
Indicates whether the DC pole is operating as an inverter or as a rectifier.
It is converter’s control variable used in power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CsConverter.pPccControl
documentation: Kind of active power control.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CsConverter.ratedIdc
documentation:
Rated converter DC current, also called IdN. The attribute shall be a positive
value. It is converter’s configuration data used in power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CsConverter.targetAlpha
documentation:
Target firing angle. It is converter’s control variable used in power flow.
It is only applicable for rectifier if continuous tap changer control is
used. Allowed values are within the range minAlpha<=targetAlpha<=maxAlpha.
The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CsConverter.targetGamma
documentation:
Target extinction angle. It is converter’s control variable used in power
flow. It is only applicable for inverter if continuous tap changer control
is used. Allowed values are within the range minGamma<=targetGamma<=maxGamma.
The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CsConverter.targetIdc
documentation:
DC current target value. It is converter’s control variable used in power
flow. The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.CsConverter.CSCDynamics
documentation:
Current source converter dynamics model used to describe dynamic behaviour
of this converter.

DC side of the current source converter (CSC).
The firing angle controls the dc voltage at the converter, both for rectifier
and inverter. The difference between the dc voltages of the rectifier and
inverter determines the dc current. The extinction angle is used to limit
the dc voltage at the inverter, if needed, and is not used in active power
control. The firing angle, transformer tap position and number of connected
filters are the primary means to control a current source dc line. Higher
level controls are built on top, e.g. dc voltage, dc current and active
power. From a steady state perspective it is sufficient to specify the
wanted active power transfer (ACDCConverter.targetPpcc) and the control
functions will set the dc voltage, dc current, firing angle, transformer
tap position and number of connected filters to meet this. Therefore attributes
targetAlpha and targetGamma are not applicable in this case.
The reactive power consumed by the converter is a function of the firing
angle, transformer tap position and number of connected filter, which can
be approximated with half of the active power. The losses is a function
of the dc voltage and dc current.
The attributes minAlpha and maxAlpha define the range of firing angles
for rectifier operation between which no discrete tap changer action takes
place. The range is typically 10-18 degrees.
The attributes minGamma and maxGamma define the range of extinction angles
for inverter operation between which no discrete tap changer action takes
place. The range is typically 17-20 degrees.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsConverter

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsConverter.maxModulationIndex
documentation:
The maximum quotient between the AC converter voltage (Uc) and DC voltage
(Ud). A factor typically less than 1. It is converter’s configuration data
used in power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsConverter.targetPowerFactorPcc
documentation:
Power factor target at the AC side, at point of common coupling. The attribute
shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsConverter.targetPWMfactor
documentation:
Magnitude of pulse-modulation factor. The attribute shall be a positive
value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsConverter.delta
documentation:
Angle between VsConverter.uv and ACDCConverter.uc. It is converter’s state
variable used in power flow. The attribute shall be a positive value or
zero.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsConverter.droop
documentation:
Droop constant. The pu value is obtained as D [kV/MW] x Sb / Ubdc. The
attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsConverter.droopCompensation
documentation:
Compensation constant. Used to compensate for voltage drop when controlling
voltage at a distant bus. The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsConverter.maxValveCurrent
documentation: The maximum current through a valve. It is converter’s configuration data.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsConverter.pPccControl
documentation: Kind of control of real power and/or DC voltage.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsConverter.qPccControl
documentation: Kind of reactive power control.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsConverter.qShare
documentation:
Reactive power sharing factor among parallel converters on Uac control.
The attribute shall be a positive value or zero.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsConverter.targetPhasePcc
documentation:
Phase target at AC side, at point of common coupling. The attribute shall
be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsConverter.targetQpcc
documentation:
Reactive power injection target in AC grid, at point of common coupling.
Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign means flow out from a
node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsConverter.targetUpcc
documentation:
Voltage target in AC grid, at point of common coupling. The attribute shall
be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsConverter.uv
documentation:
Line-to-line voltage on the valve side of the converter transformer. It
is converter’s state variable, result from power flow. The attribute shall
be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsConverter.CapabilityCurve
documentation: Capability curve of this converter.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsConverter.VSCDynamics
documentation:
Voltage source converter dynamics model used to describe dynamic behaviour
of this converter.
DC side of the voltage source converter (VSC).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Clamp

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Clamp.lengthFromTerminal1
documentation:
The length to the place where the clamp is located starting from side one
of the line segment, i.e. the line segment terminal with sequence number
equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Clamp.ACLineSegment
documentation: The line segment to which the clamp is connected.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Clamp.ClampAction
documentation: The clamp action that is performed on the clamp

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Clamp.JumperAction
documentation: Action taken with this jumper.

A Clamp is a galvanic connection at a line segment where other equipment
is connected. A Clamp does not cut the line segment.
A Clamp is ConductingEquipment and has one Terminal with an associated
ConnectivityNode. Any other ConductingEquipment can be connected to the
Clamp ConnectivityNode.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Conductor

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Conductor.length
documentation: Segment length for calculating line section capabilities.

Combination of conducting material with consistent electrical characteristics,
building a single electrical system, used to carry current between points
in the power system.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegment.b0ch
documentation:
Zero sequence shunt (charging) susceptance, uniformly distributed, of the
entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegment.bch
documentation:
Positive sequence shunt (charging) susceptance, uniformly distributed,
of the entire line section. This value represents the full charging over
the full length of the line.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegment.g0ch
documentation:
Zero sequence shunt (charging) conductance, uniformly distributed, of the
entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegment.gch
documentation:
Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance, uniformly distributed,
of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegment.r
documentation: Positive sequence series resistance of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegment.r0
documentation: Zero sequence series resistance of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegment.shortCircuitEndTemperature
documentation:
Maximum permitted temperature at the end of SC for the calculation of minimum
short-circuit currents. Used for short circuit data exchange according
to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegment.x
documentation: Positive sequence series reactance of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegment.x0
documentation: Zero sequence series reactance of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegment.ACLineSegmentPhases
documentation: The line segment phases which belong to the line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegment.LineGroundingAction
documentation:
Ground action involving clamp usage (for the case when the ground is applied
along the line segment instead of at its terminals).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegment.LineJumpingAction
documentation:
Jumper action involving clamp usage (for the case when the jumper is applied
along the line segment instead of at its terminals).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegment.PerLengthImpedance
documentation: Per-length impedance of this line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ACLineSegment.WireSpacingInfo
documentation: The wire spacing information that applies to this AC line segment

A wire or combination of wires, with consistent electrical characteristics,
building a single electrical system, used to carry alternating current
between points in the power system.
For symmetrical, transposed three phase lines, it is sufficient to use
attributes of the line segment, which describe impedances and admittances
for the entire length of the segment. Additionally impedances can be computed
by using length and associated per length impedances.
The BaseVoltage at the two ends of ACLineSegments in a Line shall have
the same BaseVoltage.nominalVoltage. However, boundary lines may have slightly
different BaseVoltage.nominalVoltages and variation is allowed. Larger
voltage difference in general requires use of an equivalent branch.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.WireSegment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.WireSegment.WireSegmentPhases
documentation: The wire segment phases which belong to the wire segment.

A two terminal and power conducting device of negligible impedance and
length represented as zero impedance device that can be used to connect
auxiliary equipment to its terminals.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Connector
documentation:
A conductor, or group of conductors, with negligible impedance, that serve
to connect other conducting equipment within a single substation and are
modelled with a single logical terminal.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BusbarSection

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BusbarSection.ipMax
documentation:
Maximum allowable peak short-circuit current of busbar (Ipmax in IEC 60909-0).
Mechanical limit of the busbar in the substation itself. Used for short
circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BusbarSection.VoltageControlZone
documentation: A VoltageControlZone is controlled by a designated BusbarSection.

A conductor, or group of conductors, with negligible impedance, that serve
to connect other conducting equipment within a single substation.
Voltage measurements are typically obtained from voltage transformers that
are connected to busbar sections. A bus bar section may have many physical
terminals but for analysis is modelled with exactly one logical terminal.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Junction
documentation:
A point where one or more conducting equipments are connected with zero
resistance.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EarthFaultCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EarthFaultCompensator.r
documentation: Nominal resistance of device.

A conducting equipment used to represent a connection to ground which is
typically used to compensate earth faults. An earth fault compensator device
modelled with a single terminal implies a second terminal solidly connected
to ground. If two terminals are modelled, the ground is not assumed and
normal connection rules apply.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GroundingImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GroundingImpedance.x
documentation: Reactance of device.
A fixed impedance device used for grounding.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PetersenCoil

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PetersenCoil.mode
documentation: The mode of operation of the Petersen coil.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PetersenCoil.nominalU
documentation: The nominal voltage for which the coil is designed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PetersenCoil.offsetCurrent
documentation:
The offset current that the Petersen coil controller is operating from
the resonant point. This is normally a fixed amount for which the controller
is configured and could be positive or negative. Typically 0 to 60 A depending
on voltage and resonance conditions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PetersenCoil.positionCurrent
documentation:
The control current used to control the Petersen coil also known as the
position current. Typically in the range of 20 mA to 200 mA.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PetersenCoil.xGroundMax
documentation: The maximum reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PetersenCoil.xGroundMin
documentation: The minimum reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PetersenCoil.xGroundNominal
documentation:
The nominal reactance. This is the operating point (normally over compensation)
that is defined based on the resonance point in the healthy network condition.
The impedance is calculated based on nominal voltage divided by position
current.

A variable impedance device normally used to offset line charging during
single line faults in an ungrounded section of network.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConnection
documentation: A connection of energy generation or consumption on the power system model.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumer.customerCount
documentation: Number of individual customers represented by this demand.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumer.grounded
documentation: Used for Yn and Zn connections. True if the neutral is solidly grounded.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumer.p
documentation:
Active power of the load. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
For voltage dependent loads the value is at rated voltage.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumer.pfixed
documentation:
Active power of the load that is a fixed quantity and does not vary as
load group value varies. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumer.pfixedPct
documentation:
Fixed active power as a percentage of load group fixed active power. Used
to represent the time-varying components. Load sign convention is used,
i.e. positive sign means flow out from a node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumer.phaseConnection
documentation: The type of phase connection, such as wye or delta.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumer.q
documentation:
Reactive power of the load. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive
sign means flow out from a node.
For voltage dependent loads the value is at rated voltage.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumer.qfixed
documentation:
Reactive power of the load that is a fixed quantity and does not vary as
load group value varies. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumer.qfixedPct
documentation:
Fixed reactive power as a percentage of load group fixed reactive power.
Used to represent the time-varying components. Load sign convention is
used, i.e. positive sign means flow out from a node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumer.EnergyConsumerAction
documentation: The energy consumer action that is performed on the energy consumer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumer.EnergyConsumerPhase
documentation: The individual phase models for this energy consumer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumer.LoadDynamics
documentation: Load dynamics model used to describe dynamic behaviour of this energy consumer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumer.LoadResponse
documentation:
The load response characteristic of this load. If missing, this load is
assumed to be constant power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergyConsumer.PowerCutZone
documentation: The energy consumer is assigned to this power cut zone.

Generic user of energy - a point of consumption on the power system model.
EnergyConsumer.pfixed, .qfixed, .pfixedPct and .qfixedPct have meaning
only if there is no LoadResponseCharacteristic associated with EnergyConsumer
or if LoadResponseCharacteristic.exponentModel is set to False.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ConformLoad

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ConformLoad.LoadGroup
documentation: Group of this ConformLoad.

ConformLoad represent loads that follow a daily load change pattern where
the pattern can be used to scale the load with a system load.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonConformLoad

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonConformLoad.LoadGroup
documentation: Group of this ConformLoad.

NonConformLoad represents loads that do not follow a daily load change
pattern and whose changes are not correlated with the daily load change
pattern.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.StationSupply
documentation: Station supply with load derived from the station output.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySource

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySource.activePower
documentation:
High voltage source active injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e.
positive sign means flow out from a node.
Starting value for steady state solutions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySource.nominalVoltage
documentation: Phase-to-phase nominal voltage.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySource.pMax
documentation:
This is the maximum active power that can be produced by the source. Load
sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign means flow out from a TopologicalNode
(bus) into the conducting equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySource.pMin
documentation:
This is the minimum active power that can be produced by the source. Load
sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign means flow out from a TopologicalNode
(bus) into the conducting equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySource.r
documentation: Positive sequence Thevenin resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySource.r0
documentation: Zero sequence Thevenin resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySource.reactivePower
documentation:
High voltage source reactive injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e.
positive sign means flow out from a node.
Starting value for steady state solutions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySource.rn
documentation: Negative sequence Thevenin resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySource.voltageAngle
documentation:
Phase angle of a-phase open circuit used when voltage characteristics need
to be imposed at the node associated with the terminal of the energy source,
such as when voltages and angles from the transmission level are used as
input to the distribution network. The attribute shall be a positive value
or zero.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySource.voltageMagnitude
documentation:
Phase-to-phase open circuit voltage magnitude used when voltage characteristics
need to be imposed at the node associated with the terminal of the energy
source, such as when voltages and angles from the transmission level are
used as input to the distribution network. The attribute shall be a positive
value or zero.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySource.x
documentation: Positive sequence Thevenin reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySource.x0
documentation: Zero sequence Thevenin reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySource.xn
documentation: Negative sequence Thevenin reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySource.EnergySchedulingType
documentation: Energy Scheduling Type of an Energy Source.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySource.EnergySourceAction
documentation: Action taken with this energy source.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.EnergySource.EnergySourcePhase
documentation: The individual phase information of the energy source.

A generic equivalent for an energy supplier on a transmission or distribution
voltage level.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegulatingCondEq

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegulatingCondEq.controlEnabled
documentation:
Specifies the regulation status of the equipment. True is regulating, false
is not regulating.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegulatingCondEq.RegulatingControl
documentation: The regulating control scheme in which this equipment participates.

A type of conducting equipment that can regulate a quantity (i.e. voltage
or flow) at a specific point in the network.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection.ikSecond
documentation:
Indicates whether initial symmetrical short-circuit current and power have
been calculated according to IEC (Ik"). Used only if short circuit calculations
are done according to superposition method.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxR0ToX0Ratio
documentation:
Maximum ratio of zero sequence resistance of Network Feeder to its zero
sequence reactance (R(0)/X(0) max). Used for short circuit data exchange
according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxR1ToX1Ratio
documentation:
Maximum ratio of positive sequence resistance of Network Feeder to its
positive sequence reactance (R(1)/X(1) max). Used for short circuit data
exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxZ0ToZ1Ratio
documentation:
Maximum ratio of zero sequence impedance to its positive sequence impedance
(Z(0)/Z(1) max). Used for short circuit data exchange according to IEC
60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection.minR0ToX0Ratio
documentation:
Indicates whether initial symmetrical short-circuit current and power have
been calculated according to IEC (Ik"). Used for short circuit data exchange
according to IEC 6090.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection.minR1ToX1Ratio
documentation:
Minimum ratio of positive sequence resistance of Network Feeder to its
positive sequence reactance (R(1)/X(1) min). Used for short circuit data
exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection.minZ0ToZ1Ratio
documentation:
Minimum ratio of zero sequence impedance to its positive sequence impedance
(Z(0)/Z(1) min). Used for short circuit data exchange according to IEC
60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection.referencePriority
documentation:
Priority of unit for use as powerflow voltage phase angle reference bus
selection. 0 = don t care (default) 1 = highest priority. 2 is less than
1 and so on.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection.governorSCD
documentation:
Power Frequency Bias. This is the change in power injection divided by
the change in frequency and negated. A positive value of the power frequency
bias provides additional power injection upon a drop in frequency.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxInitialSymShCCurrent
documentation:
Maximum initial symmetrical short-circuit currents (Ik" max) in A (Ik"
= Sk"/(SQRT(3) Un)). Used for short circuit data exchange according to
IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxP
documentation: Maximum active power of the injection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxQ
documentation:
Maximum reactive power limit. It is used for modelling of infeed for load
flow exchange and not for short circuit modelling.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection.minInitialSymShCCurrent
documentation:
Minimum initial symmetrical short-circuit currents (Ik" min) in A (Ik"
= Sk"/(SQRT(3) Un)). Used for short circuit data exchange according to
IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection.minP
documentation: Minimum active power of the injection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection.minQ
documentation:
Minimum reactive power limit. It is used for modelling of infeed for load
flow exchange and not for short circuit modelling.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection.p
documentation:
Active power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for steady state solutions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection.q
documentation:
Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for steady state solutions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ExternalNetworkInjection.voltageFactor
documentation:
Voltage factor in pu, which was used to calculate short-circuit current
Ik" and power Sk". Used only if short circuit calculations are done according
to superposition method.

This class represents the external network and it is used for IEC 60909
calculations.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FrequencyConverter

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FrequencyConverter.frequency
documentation: Frequency on the AC side.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FrequencyConverter.maxP
documentation:
The maximum active power on the DC side at which the frequency converter
should operate.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FrequencyConverter.maxU
documentation:
The maximum voltage on the DC side at which the frequency converter should
operate.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FrequencyConverter.minP
documentation:
The minimum active power on the DC side at which the frequency converter
should operate.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FrequencyConverter.minU
documentation:
The minimum voltage on the DC side at which the frequency converter should
operate.

A device to convert from one frequency to another (e.g., frequency F1 to
F2) comprises a pair of FrequencyConverter instances. One converts from
F1 to DC, the other converts the DC to F2.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnection

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnection.maxIFault
documentation:
Maximum fault current this device will contribute, in per-unit of rated
current, before the converter protection will trip or bypass.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnection.maxQ
documentation:
Maximum reactive power limit. This is the maximum (nameplate) limit for
the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnection.minQ
documentation:
Minimum reactive power limit for the unit. This is the minimum (nameplate)
limit for the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnection.p
documentation:
Active power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnection.q
documentation:
Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnection.r
documentation:
Equivalent resistance (RG) of generator. RG is considered for the calculation
of all currents, except for the calculation of the peak current ip. Used
for short circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnection.r0
documentation: Zero sequence resistance of the synchronous machine.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnection.ratedS
documentation:
Nameplate apparent power rating for the unit.
The attribute shall have a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnection.ratedU
documentation:
Rated voltage (nameplate data, Ur in IEC 60909-0). It is primarily used
for short circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909.
The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnection.rn
documentation: Negative sequence Thevenin resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnection.x
documentation: Positive sequence Thevenin reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnection.x0
documentation: Zero sequence Thevenin reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnection.xn
documentation: Negative sequence Thevenin reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnection.PowerElectronicsConnectionPhase
documentation: The individual phases models for the power electronics connection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnection.PowerElectronicsUnit
documentation:
An AC network connection may have several power electronics units connecting
through it.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnection.WindTurbineType3or4Dynamics
documentation:
The wind turbine type 3 or type 4 dynamics model associated with this power
electronics connection.

A connection to the AC network for energy production or consumption that
uses power electronics rather than rotating machines.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RotatingMachine

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RotatingMachine.ratedPowerFactor
documentation:
Power factor (nameplate data). It is primarily used for short circuit data
exchange according to IEC 60909. The attribute cannot be a negative value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RotatingMachine.p
documentation:
Active power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RotatingMachine.q
documentation:
Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RotatingMachine.ratedS
documentation:
Nameplate apparent power rating for the unit.
The attribute shall have a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RotatingMachine.ratedU
documentation:
Rated voltage (nameplate data, Ur in IEC 60909-0). It is primarily used
for short circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909.
The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RotatingMachine.GeneratingUnit
documentation:
A synchronous machine may operate as a generator and as such becomes a
member of a generating unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RotatingMachine.HydroPump
documentation:
The synchronous machine drives the turbine which moves the water from a
low elevation to a higher elevation. The direction of machine rotation
for pumping may or may not be the same as for generating.
A rotating machine which may be used as a generator or motor.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.converterFedDrive
documentation:
Indicates whether the machine is a converter fed drive. Used for short
circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.iaIrRatio
documentation:
Ratio of locked-rotor current to the rated current of the motor (Ia/Ir).
Used for short circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.polePairNumber
documentation:
Number of pole pairs of stator. Used for short circuit data exchange according
to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.reversible
documentation:
Indicates for converter drive motors if the power can be reversible. Used
for short circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.rxLockedRotorRatio
documentation:
Locked rotor ratio (R/X). Used for short circuit data exchange according
to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.asynchronousMachineType
documentation: Indicates the type of Asynchronous Machine (motor or generator).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.efficiency
documentation:
Efficiency of the asynchronous machine at nominal operation as a percentage.
Indicator for converter drive motors. Used for short circuit data exchange
according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.nominalFrequency
documentation: Nameplate data indicates if the machine is 50 Hz or 60 Hz.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.nominalSpeed
documentation: Nameplate data. Depends on the slip and number of pole pairs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.ratedMechanicalPower
documentation:
Rated mechanical power (Pr in IEC 60909-0). Used for short circuit data
exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.rr1
documentation: Damper 1 winding resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.rr2
documentation: Damper 2 winding resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.tpo
documentation: Transient rotor time constant (greater than tppo).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.tppo
documentation: Sub-transient rotor time constant (greater than 0).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.xlr1
documentation: Damper 1 winding leakage reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.xlr2
documentation: Damper 2 winding leakage reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.xm
documentation: Magnetizing reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.xp
documentation: Transient reactance (unsaturated) (greater than or equal to xpp).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.xpp
documentation: Sub-transient reactance (unsaturated).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.xs
documentation: Synchronous reactance (greater than xp).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.AsynchronousMachine.AsynchronousMachineDynamics
documentation:
Asynchronous machine dynamics model used to describe dynamic behaviour
of this asynchronous machine.

A rotating machine whose shaft rotates asynchronously with the electrical
field. Also known as an induction machine with no external connection to
the rotor windings, e.g. squirrel-cage induction machine.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.coolantCondition
documentation: Temperature or pressure of coolant medium.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.earthing
documentation:
Indicates whether or not the generator is earthed. Used for short circuit
data exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.mu
documentation:
Factor to calculate the breaking current (Section 4.5.2.1 in IEC 60909-0).
Used only for single fed short circuit on a generator (Section 4.3.4.2.
in IEC 60909-0).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.referencePriority
documentation:
Priority of unit for use as powerflow voltage phase angle reference bus
selection. 0 = don t care (default) 1 = highest priority. 2 is less than
1 and so on.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.aVRToManualLag
documentation:
Time delay required when switching from Automatic Voltage Regulation (AVR)
to Manual for a lagging MVAr violation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.aVRToManualLead
documentation:
Time delay required when switching from Automatic Voltage Regulation (AVR)
to Manual for a leading MVAr violation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.baseQ
documentation:
Default base reactive power value. This value represents the initial reactive
power that can be used by any application function.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.condenserP
documentation: Active power consumed when in condenser mode operation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.coolantType
documentation: Method of cooling the machine.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.earthingStarPointR
documentation:
Generator star point earthing resistance (Re). Used for short circuit data
exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.earthingStarPointX
documentation:
Generator star point earthing reactance (Xe). Used for short circuit data
exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.ikk
documentation:
Steady-state short-circuit current (in A for the profile) of generator
with compound excitation during 3-phase short circuit.
- Ikk=0: Generator with no compound excitation.
- Ikk<>0: Generator with compound excitation.
Ikk is used to calculate the minimum steady-state short-circuit current
for generators with compound excitation.
(4.6.1.2 in IEC 60909-0:2001).
Used only for single fed short circuit on a generator. (4.3.4.2. in IEC
60909-0:2001).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.manualToAVR
documentation:
Time delay required when switching from Manual to Automatic Voltage Regulation.
This value is used in the accelerating power reference frame for powerflow
solutions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.maxQ
documentation:
Maximum reactive power limit. This is the maximum (nameplate) limit for
the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.maxU
documentation: Maximum voltage limit for the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.minQ
documentation: Minimum reactive power limit for the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.minU
documentation: Minimum voltage limit for the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.operatingMode
documentation: Current mode of operation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.qPercent
documentation:
Part of the coordinated reactive control that comes from this machine.
The attribute is used as a participation factor not necessarily summing
up to 100% for the participating devices in the control.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.r
documentation:
Equivalent resistance (RG) of generator. RG is considered for the calculation
of all currents, except for the calculation of the peak current ip. Used
for short circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.r0
documentation: Zero sequence resistance of the synchronous machine.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.r2
documentation: Negative sequence resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.satDirectSubtransX
