documentation: Direct-axis subtransient reactance saturated, also known as Xd"sat.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.satDirectSyncX
documentation:
Direct-axes saturated synchronous reactance (xdsat); reciprocal of short-circuit
ration. Used for short circuit data exchange, only for single fed short
circuit on a generator. (4.3.4.2. in IEC 60909-0:2001).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.satDirectTransX
documentation:
Saturated Direct-axis transient reactance. The attribute is primarily used
for short circuit calculations according to ANSI.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.shortCircuitRotorType
documentation:
Type of rotor, used by short circuit applications, only for single fed
short circuit according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.type
documentation: Modes that this synchronous machine can operate in.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.voltageRegulationRange
documentation:
Range of generator voltage regulation (PG in IEC 60909-0) used for calculation
of the impedance correction factor KG defined in IEC 60909-0.
This attribute is used to describe the operating voltage of the generating
unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.x0
documentation: Zero sequence reactance of the synchronous machine.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.x2
documentation: Negative sequence reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.InitialReactiveCapabilityCurve
documentation: The default reactive capability curve for use by a synchronous machine.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachine.SynchronousMachineDynamics
documentation:
Synchronous machine dynamics model used to describe dynamic behaviour of
this synchronous machine.

An electromechanical device that operates with shaft rotating synchronously
with the network. It is a single machine operating either as a generator
or synchronous condenser or pump.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensator.grounded
documentation: Used for Yn and Zn connections. True if the neutral is solidly grounded.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensator.maximumSections
documentation: The maximum number of sections that may be switched in.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensator.normalSections
documentation:
The normal number of sections switched in. The value shall be between zero
and ShuntCompensator.maximumSections.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensator.sections
documentation:
Shunt compensator sections in use. Starting value for steady state solution.
The attribute shall be a positive value or zero. Non integer values are
allowed to support continuous variables. The reasons for continuous value
are to support study cases where no discrete shunt compensators has yet
been designed, a solutions where a narrow voltage band force the sections
to oscillate or accommodate for a continuous solution as input.
For LinearShuntConpensator the value shall be between zero and ShuntCompensator.maximumSections.
At value zero the shunt compensator conductance and admittance is zero.
Linear interpolation of conductance and admittance between the previous
and next integer section is applied in case of non-integer values.
For NonlinearShuntCompensator-s shall only be set to one of the NonlinearShuntCompenstorPoint.sectionNumber.
There is no interpolation between NonlinearShuntCompenstorPoint-s.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensator.switchOnCount
documentation: The switch on count since the capacitor count was last reset or initialized.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensator.switchOnDate
documentation: The date and time when the capacitor bank was last switched on.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensator.aVRDelay
documentation:
An automatic voltage regulation delay (AVRDelay) which is the time delay
from a change in voltage to when the capacitor is allowed to change state.
This filters out temporary changes in voltage.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensator.nomU
documentation:
The voltage at which the nominal reactive power may be calculated. This
should normally be within 10% of the voltage at which the capacitor is
connected to the network.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensator.phaseConnection
documentation: The type of phase connection, such as wye or delta.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensator.voltageSensitivity
documentation:
Voltage sensitivity required for the device to regulate the bus voltage,
in voltage/reactive power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensator.ShuntCompensatorAction
documentation: The shunt compensator action that is performed on the shunt compensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensator.ShuntCompensatorPhase
documentation: The individual phases models for the shunt compensator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensator.SvShuntCompensatorSections
documentation: The state for the number of shunt compensator sections in service.

A shunt capacitor or reactor or switchable bank of shunt capacitors or
reactors. A section of a shunt compensator is an individual capacitor or
reactor. A negative value for bPerSection indicates that the compensator
is a reactor. ShuntCompensator is a single terminal device. Ground is implied.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LinearShuntCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LinearShuntCompensator.b0PerSection
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) susceptance per section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LinearShuntCompensator.bPerSection
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) susceptance per section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LinearShuntCompensator.g0PerSection
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) conductance per section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LinearShuntCompensator.gPerSection
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance per section.

A linear shunt compensator has banks or sections with equal admittance
values.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonlinearShuntCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonlinearShuntCompensator.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoints
documentation: All points of the non-linear shunt compensator.

A non linear shunt compensator has bank or section admittance values that
differ. The attributes g, b, g0 and b0 of the associated NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint
describe the total conductance and admittance of a NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint
at a section number specified by NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.sectionNumber.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.StaticVarCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.StaticVarCompensator.capacitiveRating
documentation:
Capacitive reactance at maximum capacitive reactive power. Shall always
be positive.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.StaticVarCompensator.inductiveRating
documentation:
Inductive reactance at maximum inductive reactive power. Shall always be
negative.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.StaticVarCompensator.q
documentation:
Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.StaticVarCompensator.slope
documentation:
The characteristics slope of an SVC defines how the reactive power output
changes in proportion to the difference between the regulated bus voltage
and the voltage setpoint.
The attribute shall be a positive value or zero.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.StaticVarCompensator.sVCControlMode
documentation: SVC control mode.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.StaticVarCompensator.voltageSetPoint
documentation:
The reactive power output of the SVC is proportional to the difference
between the voltage at the regulated bus and the voltage setpoint. When
the regulated bus voltage is equal to the voltage setpoint, the reactive
power output is zero.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.StaticVarCompensator.StaticVarCompensatorDynamics
documentation:
Static Var Compensator dynamics model used to describe dynamic behaviour
of this Static Var Compensator.

A facility for providing variable and controllable shunt reactive power.
The SVC typically consists of a stepdown transformer, filter, thyristor-controlled
reactor, and thyristor-switched capacitor arms.
The SVC may operate in fixed MVar output mode or in voltage control mode.
When in voltage control mode, the output of the SVC will be proportional
to the deviation of voltage at the controlled bus from the voltage setpoint.
The SVC characteristic slope defines the proportion. If the voltage at
the controlled bus is equal to the voltage setpoint, the SVC MVar output
is zero.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Ground

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Ground.GroundAction
documentation: Action taken with this ground.

A point where the system is grounded used for connecting conducting equipment
to ground. The power system model can have any number of grounds.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformer.isPartOfGeneratorUnit
documentation:
Indicates whether the machine is part of a power station unit. Used for
short circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909. It has an impact on
how the correction factors are calculated for transformers, since the transformer
is not necessarily part of a synchronous machine and generating unit. It
is not always possible to derive this information from the model. This
is why the attribute is necessary.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformer.operationalValuesConsidered
documentation:
It is used to define if the data (other attributes related to short circuit
data exchange) defines long term operational conditions or not. Used for
short circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformer.vectorGroup
documentation:
Vector group of the transformer for protective relaying, e.g., Dyn1. For
unbalanced transformers, this may not be simply determined from the constituent
winding connections and phase angle displacements.
The vectorGroup string consists of the following components in the order
listed: high voltage winding connection, mid voltage winding connection
(for three winding transformers), phase displacement clock number from
0 to 11, low voltage winding connection
phase displacement clock number from 0 to 11. The winding connections are
D (delta), Y (wye), YN (wye with neutral), Z (zigzag), ZN (zigzag with
neutral), A (auto transformer). Upper case means the high voltage, lower
case mid or low. The high voltage winding always has clock position 0 and
is not included in the vector group string. Some examples: YNy0 (two winding
wye to wye with no phase displacement), YNd11 (two winding wye to delta
with 330 degrees phase displacement), YNyn0d5 (three winding transformer
wye with neutral high voltage, wye with neutral mid voltage and no phase
displacement, delta low voltage with 150 degrees displacement).
Phase displacement is defined as the angular difference between the phasors
representing the voltages between the neutral point (real or imaginary)
and the corresponding terminals of two windings, a positive sequence voltage
system being applied to the high-voltage terminals, following each other
in alphabetical sequence if they are lettered, or in numerical sequence
if they are numbered: the phasors are assumed to rotate in a counter-clockwise
sense.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformer.beforeShCircuitHighestOperatingCurrent
documentation:
The highest operating current (Ib in IEC 60909-0) before short circuit
(depends on network configuration and relevant reliability philosophy).
It is used for calculation of the impedance correction factor KT defined
in IEC 60909-0.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformer.beforeShCircuitHighestOperatingVoltage
documentation:
The highest operating voltage (Ub in IEC 60909-0) before short circuit.
It is used for calculation of the impedance correction factor KT defined
in IEC 60909-0. This is worst case voltage on the low side winding (3.7.1
of IEC 60909:2001). Used to define operating conditions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformer.beforeShortCircuitAnglePf
documentation:
The angle of power factor before short circuit (phib in IEC 60909-0). It
is used for calculation of the impedance correction factor KT defined in
IEC 60909-0. This is the worst case power factor. Used to define operating
conditions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformer.highSideMinOperatingU
documentation:
The minimum operating voltage (uQmin in IEC 60909-0) at the high voltage
side (Q side) of the unit transformer of the power station unit. A value
well established from long-term operating experience of the system. It
is used for calculation of the impedance correction factor KG defined in
IEC 60909-0.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformer.PowerTransformerEnd
documentation: The ends of this power transformer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformer.TransformerTanks
documentation: All transformers that belong to this bank.

An electrical device consisting of two or more coupled windings, with or
without a magnetic core, for introducing mutual coupling between electric
circuits. Transformers can be used to control voltage and phase shift (active
power flow).
A power transformer may be composed of separate transformer tanks that
need not be identical.
A power transformer can be modelled with or without tanks and is intended
for use in both balanced and unbalanced representations. A power transformer
typically has two terminals, but may have one (grounding), three or more
terminals.
The inherited association ConductingEquipment.BaseVoltage should not be
used. The association from TransformerEnd to BaseVoltage should be used
instead.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SeriesCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SeriesCompensator.varistorPresent
documentation:
Describe if a metal oxide varistor (mov) for over voltage protection is
configured in parallel with the series compensator. It is used for short
circuit calculations.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SeriesCompensator.r
documentation: Positive sequence resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SeriesCompensator.r0
documentation: Zero sequence resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SeriesCompensator.varistorRatedCurrent
documentation:
The maximum current the varistor is designed to handle at specified duration.
It is used for short circuit calculations and exchanged only if SeriesCompensator.varistorPresent
is true.
The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SeriesCompensator.varistorVoltageThreshold
documentation:
The dc voltage at which the varistor starts conducting. It is used for
short circuit calculations and exchanged only if SeriesCompensator.varistorPresent
is true.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SeriesCompensator.x
documentation: Positive sequence reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SeriesCompensator.x0
documentation: Zero sequence reactance.

A Series Compensator is a series capacitor or reactor or an AC transmission
line without charging susceptance. It is a two terminal device.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Switch

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Switch.locked
documentation:
If true, the switch is locked. The resulting switch state is a combination of locked and Switch.open attributes as follows:
  • locked=true and Switch.open=true. The resulting state is open and locked;
  • locked=false and Switch.open=true. The resulting state is open;
  • locked=false and Switch.open=false. The resulting state is closed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Switch.normalOpen
documentation:
The attribute is used in cases when no Measurement for the status value
is present. If the Switch has a status measurement the Discrete.normalValue
is expected to match with the Switch.normalOpen.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Switch.open
documentation:
The attribute tells if the switch is considered open when used as input
to topology processing.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Switch.retained
documentation:
Branch is retained in the topological solution. The flow through retained
switches will normally be calculated in power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Switch.switchOnCount
documentation: The switch on count since the switch was last reset or initialized.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Switch.switchOnDate
documentation: The date and time when the switch was last switched on.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Switch.ratedCurrent
documentation:
The maximum continuous current carrying capacity in amps governed by the
device material and construction.
The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Switch.CompositeSwitch
documentation: Composite switch to which this Switch belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Switch.SwitchAction
documentation: Action changing status of this switch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Switch.SwitchPhase
documentation: The individual switch phases for the switch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Switch.SwitchSchedules
documentation: A Switch can be associated with SwitchSchedules.

A generic device designed to close, or open, or both, one or more electric
circuits. All switches are two terminal devices including grounding switches.
The ACDCTerminal.connected at the two sides of the switch shall not be
considered for assessing switch connectivity, i.e. only Switch.open, .normalOpen
and .locked are relevant.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Cut

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Cut.lengthFromTerminal1
documentation:
The length to the place where the cut is located starting from side one
of the cut line segment, i.e. the line segment Terminal with sequenceNumber
equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Cut.ACLineSegment
documentation: The line segment to which the cut is applied.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Cut.CutAction
documentation: Action taken with this cut.

A cut separates a line segment into two parts. The cut appears as a switch
inserted between these two parts and connects them together. As the cut
is normally open there is no galvanic connection between the two line segment
parts. But it is possible to close the cut to get galvanic connection.
The cut terminals are oriented towards the line segment terminals with
the same sequence number. Hence the cut terminal with sequence number equal
to 1 is oriented to the line segment's terminal with sequence number equal
to 1.
The cut terminals also act as connection points for jumpers and other equipment,
e.g. a mobile generator. To enable this, connectivity nodes are placed
at the cut terminals. Once the connectivity nodes are in place any conducting
equipment can be connected at them.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Disconnector
documentation:
A manually operated or motor operated mechanical switching device used
for changing the connections in a circuit, or for isolating a circuit or
equipment from a source of power. It is required to open or close circuits
when negligible current is broken or made.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Fuse
documentation:
An overcurrent protective device with a circuit opening fusible part that
is heated and severed by the passage of overcurrent through it. A fuse
is considered a switching device because it breaks current.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GroundDisconnector
documentation:
A manually operated or motor operated mechanical switching device used
for isolating a circuit or equipment from ground.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Jumper

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Jumper.JumperAction
documentation: Action taken with this jumper.

A short section of conductor with negligible impedance which can be manually
removed and replaced if the circuit is de-energized. Note that zero-impedance
branches can potentially be modelled by other equipment types.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ProtectedSwitch

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ProtectedSwitch.breakingCapacity
documentation:
The maximum fault current a breaking device can break safely under prescribed
conditions of use.
A ProtectedSwitch is a switching device that can be operated by ProtectionEquipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Breaker

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Breaker.inTransitTime
documentation: The transition time from open to close.

A mechanical switching device capable of making, carrying, and breaking
currents under normal circuit conditions and also making, carrying for
a specified time, and breaking currents under specified abnormal circuit
conditions e.g. those of short circuit.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DisconnectingCircuitBreaker
documentation:
A circuit breaking device including disconnecting function, eliminating
the need for separate disconnectors.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LoadBreakSwitch
documentation:
A mechanical switching device capable of making, carrying, and breaking
currents under normal operating conditions.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Recloser
documentation:
Pole-mounted fault interrupter with built-in phase and ground relays, current
transformer (CT), and supplemental controls.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Sectionaliser
documentation:
Automatic switch that will lock open to isolate a faulted section. It may,
or may not, have load breaking capability. Its primary purpose is to provide
fault sectionalising at locations where the fault current is either too
high, or too low, for proper coordination of fuses.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCConductingEquipment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCConductingEquipment.ratedUdc
documentation:
Rated DC device voltage. The attribute shall be a positive value. It is
configuration data used in power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCConductingEquipment.DCTerminals
documentation: A DC conducting equipment has DC terminals.

The parts of the DC power system that are designed to carry current or
that are conductively connected through DC terminals.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCBusbar
documentation: A busbar within a DC system.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCChopper
documentation:
Low resistance equipment used in the internal DC circuit to balance voltages.
It has typically positive and negative pole terminals and a ground.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCGround

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCGround.inductance
documentation: Inductance to ground.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCGround.r
documentation: Resistance to ground.
A ground within a DC system.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCLineSegment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCLineSegment.capacitance
documentation: Capacitance of the DC line segment. Significant for cables only.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCLineSegment.inductance
documentation:
Inductance of the DC line segment. Negligible compared with DCSeriesDevice
used for smoothing.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCLineSegment.length
documentation: Segment length for calculating line section capabilities.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCLineSegment.resistance
documentation: Resistance of the DC line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCLineSegment.PerLengthParameter
documentation: Set of per-length parameters for this line segment.

A wire or combination of wires not insulated from one another, with consistent
electrical characteristics, used to carry direct current between points
in the DC region of the power system.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCSeriesDevice

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCSeriesDevice.inductance
documentation: Inductance of the device.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCSeriesDevice.resistance
documentation: Resistance of the DC device.

A series device within the DC system, typically a reactor used for filtering
or smoothing. Needed for transient and short circuit studies.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCShunt

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCShunt.capacitance
documentation: Capacitance of the DC shunt.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCShunt.resistance
documentation: Resistance of the DC device.

A shunt device within the DC system, typically used for filtering. Needed
for transient and short circuit studies.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCSwitch
documentation: A switch within the DC system.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCBreaker
documentation: A breaker within a DC system.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCDisconnector
documentation: A disconnector within a DC system.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.longPF
documentation: Generating unit long term economic participation factor.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.normalPF
documentation:
Generating unit economic participation factor. The sum of the participation
factors across generating units does not have to sum to one. It is used
for representing distributed slack participation factor. The attribute
shall be a positive value or zero.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.penaltyFactor
documentation:
Defined as: 1 / ( 1 - Incremental Transmission Loss); with the Incremental
Transmission Loss expressed as a plus or minus value. The typical range
of penalty factors is (0.9 to 1.1).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.shortPF
documentation: Generating unit short term economic participation factor.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.tieLinePF
documentation: Generating unit economic participation factor.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.allocSpinResP
documentation:
The planned unused capacity (spinning reserve) which can be used to support
emergency load.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.autoCntrlMarginP
documentation:
The planned unused capacity which can be used to support automatic control
overruns.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.baseP
documentation:
For dispatchable units, this value represents the economic active power
basepoint, for units that are not dispatchable, this value represents the
fixed generation value. The value shall be between the operating low and
high limits.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.controlDeadband
documentation:
Unit control error deadband. When a unit's desired active power change
is less than this deadband, then no control pulses will be sent to the
unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.controlPulseHigh
documentation:
Pulse high limit which is the largest control pulse that the unit can respond
to.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.controlPulseLow
documentation:
Pulse low limit which is the smallest control pulse that the unit can respond
to.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.controlResponseRate
documentation:
Unit response rate which specifies the active power change for a control
pulse of one second in the most responsive loading level of the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.efficiency
documentation:
The efficiency of the unit in converting mechanical energy, from the prime
mover, into electrical energy.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.genControlMode
documentation: The unit control mode.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.genControlSource
documentation:
The source of controls for a generating unit. Defines the control status
of the generating unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.governorMPL
documentation: Governor motor position limit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.governorSCD
documentation:
Governor Speed Changer Droop. This is the change in generator power output
divided by the change in frequency normalized by the nominal power of the
generator and the nominal frequency and expressed in percent and negated.
A positive value of speed change droop provides additional generator output
upon a drop in frequency.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.highControlLimit
documentation: High limit for secondary (AGC) control.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.initialP
documentation:
Default initial active power which is used to store a powerflow result
for the initial active power for this unit in this network configuration.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.lowControlLimit
documentation: Low limit for secondary (AGC) control.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.lowerRampRate
documentation:
The normal maximum rate the generating unit active power output can be
lowered by control actions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.maxEconomicP
documentation:
Maximum high economic active power limit, that should not exceed the maximum
operating active power limit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.maximumAllowableSpinningReserve
documentation:
Maximum allowable spinning reserve. Spinning reserve will never be considered
greater than this value regardless of the current operating point.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.maxOperatingP
documentation:
This is the maximum operating active power limit the dispatcher can enter
for this unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.minEconomicP
documentation:
Low economic active power limit that shall be greater than or equal to
the minimum operating active power limit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.minimumOffTime
documentation: Minimum time interval between unit shutdown and startup.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.minOperatingP
documentation:
This is the minimum operating active power limit the dispatcher can enter
for this unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.modelDetail
documentation: Detail level of the generator model data.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.nominalP
documentation:
The nominal power of the generating unit. Used to give precise meaning
to percentage based attributes such as the governor speed change droop
(governorSCD attribute).
The attribute shall be a positive value equal to or less than RotatingMachine.ratedS.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.raiseRampRate
documentation:
The normal maximum rate the generating unit active power output can be
raised by control actions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.ratedGrossMaxP
documentation:
The unit's gross rated maximum capacity (book value).
The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.ratedGrossMinP
documentation:
The gross rated minimum generation level which the unit can safely operate
at while delivering power to the transmission grid.
The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.ratedNetMaxP
documentation:
The net rated maximum capacity determined by subtracting the auxiliary
power used to operate the internal plant machinery from the rated gross
maximum capacity.
The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.startupCost
documentation: The initial startup cost incurred for each start of the GeneratingUnit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.startupTime
documentation:
Time it takes to get the unit on-line, from the time that the prime mover
mechanical power is applied.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.totalEfficiency
documentation: The efficiency of the unit in converting the fuel into electrical energy.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.variableCost
documentation: The variable cost component of production per unit of ActivePower.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.ControlAreaGeneratingUnit
documentation: ControlArea specifications for this generating unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.GenUnitOpSchedule
documentation:
A generating unit may have an operating schedule, indicating the planned
operation of the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.GeneratingUnit.RotatingMachine
documentation:
A synchronous machine may operate as a generator and as such becomes a
member of a generating unit.

A single or set of synchronous machines for converting mechanical power
into alternating-current power. For example, individual machines within
a set may be defined for scheduling purposes while a single control signal
is derived for the set. In this case there would be a GeneratingUnit for
each member of the set and an additional GeneratingUnit corresponding to
the set.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.WindGeneratingUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.WindGeneratingUnit.windGenUnitType
documentation: The kind of wind generating unit.

A wind driven generating unit, connected to the grid by means of a rotating
machine. May be used to represent a single turbine or an aggregation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.HydroPump

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.HydroPump.RotatingMachine
documentation:
The synchronous machine drives the turbine which moves the water from a
low elevation to a higher elevation. The direction of machine rotation
for pumping may or may not be the same as for generating.

A synchronous motor-driven pump, typically associated with a pumped storage
plant.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsUnit.maxP
documentation:
Maximum active power limit. This is the maximum (nameplate) limit for the
unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsUnit.minP
documentation:
Minimum active power limit. This is the minimum (nameplate) limit for the
unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsUnit.PowerElectronicsConnection
documentation: A power electronics unit has a connection to the AC network.

A generating unit or battery or aggregation that connects to the AC network
using power electronics rather than rotating machines.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BatteryUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BatteryUnit.batteryState
documentation: The current state of the battery (charging, full, etc.).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BatteryUnit.ratedE
documentation:
Full energy storage capacity of the battery. The attribute shall be a positive
value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.BatteryUnit.storedE
documentation:
Amount of energy currently stored. The attribute shall be a positive value
or zero and lower than BatteryUnit.ratedE.
An electrochemical energy storage device.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhotoVoltaicUnit
documentation: A photovoltaic device or an aggregation of such devices.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsWindUnit
documentation:
A wind generating unit that connects to the AC network with power electronics
rather than rotating machines or an aggregation of such units.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerTank

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerTank.PowerTransformer
documentation: Bank this transformer belongs to.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerTank.TransformerTankEnds
documentation: All windings of this transformer.

An assembly of two or more coupled windings that transform electrical power
between voltage levels. These windings are bound on a common core and placed
in the same tank. Transformer tank can be used to model both single-phase
and 3-phase transformers.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerCutZone

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerCutZone.cutLevel1
documentation: First level (amount) of load to cut as a percentage of total zone load.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerCutZone.cutLevel2
documentation: Second level (amount) of load to cut as a percentage of total zone load.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerCutZone.EnergyConsumers
documentation: Energy consumer is assigned to the power cut zone.
An area or zone of the power system which is used for load shedding purposes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnectionPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnectionPhase.p
documentation:
Active power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow into the equipment from the network.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnectionPhase.phase
documentation:
Phase of this energy producer component. If the energy producer is wye
connected, the connection is from the indicated phase to the central ground
or neutral point. If the energy producer is delta connected, the phase
indicates an energy producer connected from the indicated phase to the
next logical non-neutral phase.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnectionPhase.q
documentation:
Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow into the equipment from the network.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerElectronicsConnectionPhase.PowerElectronicsConnection
documentation: Power electronics connection of this power electronics connection phase.
A single phase of a power electronics connection.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegulatingControl

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegulatingControl.enabled
documentation: The flag tells if regulation is enabled.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegulatingControl.maxAllowedTargetValue
documentation: Maximum allowed target value (RegulatingControl.targetValue).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegulatingControl.minAllowedTargetValue
documentation: Minimum allowed target value (RegulatingControl.targetValue).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegulatingControl.targetDeadband
documentation:
This is a deadband used with discrete control to avoid excessive update
of controls like tap changers and shunt compensator banks while regulating.
The units of those appropriate for the mode. The attribute shall be a positive
value or zero. If RegulatingControl.discrete is set to "false", the RegulatingControl.targetDeadband
is to be ignored.
Note that for instance, if the targetValue is 100 kV and the targetDeadband
is 2 kV the range is from 99 to 101 kV.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegulatingControl.targetValue
documentation:
The target value specified for case input. This value can be used for the
target value without the use of schedules. The value has the units appropriate
to the mode attribute.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegulatingControl.mode
documentation:
The regulating control mode presently available. This specification allows
for determining the kind of regulation without need for obtaining the units
from a schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegulatingControl.monitoredPhase
documentation: Phase voltage controlling this regulator, measured at regulator location.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegulatingControl.targetValueUnitMultiplier
documentation: Specify the multiplier for used for the targetValue.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegulatingControl.RegulatingCondEq
documentation: The equipment that participates in this regulating control scheme.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegulatingControl.RegulationSchedule
documentation: Schedule for this regulating control.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegulatingControl.Terminal
documentation:
The terminal associated with this regulating control. The terminal is associated
instead of a node, since the terminal could connect into either a topological
node or a connectivity node. Sometimes it is useful to model regulation
at a terminal of a bus bar object.

Specifies a set of equipment that works together to control a power system
quantity such as voltage or flow.
Remote bus voltage control is possible by specifying the controlled terminal
located at some place remote from the controlling equipment.
The specified terminal shall be associated with the connectivity node of
the controlled point. The most specific subtype of RegulatingControl shall
be used in case such equipment participate in the control, e.g. TapChangerControl
for tap changers.
For flow control, load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign means
flow out from a TopologicalNode (bus) into the conducting equipment.
The attribute minAllowedTargetValue and maxAllowedTargetValue are required
in the following cases:
- For a power generating module operated in power factor control mode to
specify maximum and minimum power factor values;
- Whenever it is necessary to have an off center target voltage for the
tap changer regulator. For instance, due to long cables to off shore wind
farms and the need to have a simpler setup at the off shore transformer
platform, the voltage is controlled from the land at the connection point
for the off shore wind farm. Since there usually is a voltage rise along
the cable, there is typical and overvoltage of up 3-4 kV compared to the
on shore station. Thus in normal operation the tap changer on the on shore
station is operated with a target set point, which is in the lower parts
of the dead band.
The attributes minAllowedTargetValue and maxAllowedTargetValue are not
related to the attribute targetDeadband and thus they are not treated as
an alternative of the targetDeadband. They are needed due to limitations
in the local substation controller. The attribute targetDeadband is used
to prevent the power flow from move the tap position in circles (hunting)
that is to be used regardless of the attributes minAllowedTargetValue and
maxAllowedTargetValue.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorControl

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorControl.branchDirect
documentation:
For VAR, amp, or power factor locally controlled shunt impedances, the
flow direction: in, out.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorControl.localOffLevel
documentation: Upper control setting.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorControl.localOnLevel
documentation: Lower control setting.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorControl.localOverride
documentation: True if the locally controlled capacitor has voltage override capability.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorControl.maxSwitchOperationCount
documentation: IdmsShuntImpedanceData.maxNumSwitchOps.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorControl.normalOpen
documentation:
True if open is normal status for a fixed capacitor bank, otherwise normal
status is closed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorControl.regBranch
documentation:
For VAR, amp, or power factor locally controlled shunt impedances, the
index of the regulation branch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorControl.regBranchEnd
cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorControl.vRegLineLine
documentation:
True if regulated voltages are measured line to line, otherwise they are
measured line to ground.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorControl.cellSize
documentation: The size of the individual units that make up the bank.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorControl.controlKind
documentation: Kind of control (if any).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorControl.highVoltageOverride
documentation:
For locally controlled shunt impedances which have a voltage override feature,
the high voltage override value. If the voltage is above this value, the
shunt impedance will be turned off regardless of the other local controller
settings.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorControl.localControlKind
documentation: Kind of local controller.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorControl.lowVoltageOverride
documentation:
For locally controlled shunt impedances which have a voltage override feature,
the low voltage override value. If the voltage is below this value, the
shunt impedance will be turned on regardless of the other local controller
settings.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorControl.regBranchKind
documentation:
(For VAR, amp, or power factor locally controlled shunt impedances) Kind
of regulation branch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorControl.sensingPhaseCode
documentation: Phases that are measured for controlling the device.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorControl.switchOperationCycle
documentation: Time interval between consecutive switching operations.
Distribution capacitor bank control settings.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChangerControl

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChangerControl.lineDropCompensation
documentation: If true, the line drop compensation is to be applied.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChangerControl.limitVoltage
documentation:
Maximum allowed regulated voltage on the PT secondary, regardless of line
drop compensation. Sometimes referred to as first-house protection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChangerControl.lineDropR
documentation: Line drop compensator resistance setting for normal (forward) power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChangerControl.lineDropX
documentation: Line drop compensator reactance setting for normal (forward) power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChangerControl.reverseLineDropR
documentation: Line drop compensator resistance setting for reverse power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChangerControl.reverseLineDropX
documentation: Line drop compensator reactance setting for reverse power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChangerControl.TapChanger
documentation: The tap changers that participates in this regulating tap control scheme.

Describes behaviour specific to tap changers, e.g. how the voltage at the
end of a line varies with the load level and compensation of the voltage
drop by tap adjustment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorPhase.maximumSections
documentation: The maximum number of sections that may be switched in for this phase.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorPhase.normalSections
documentation:
For the capacitor phase, the normal number of sections switched in. The
value shall be between zero and ShuntCompensatorPhase.maximumSections.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorPhase.sections
documentation:
Shunt compensator sections in use. Starting value for steady state solution.
The attribute shall be a positive value or zero. Non integer values are
allowed to support continuous variables. The reasons for continuous value
are to support study cases where no discrete shunt compensators has yet
been designed, a solutions where a narrow voltage band force the sections
to oscillate or accommodate for a continuous solution as input.
For LinearShuntConpensator the value shall be between zero and ShuntCompensatorPhase.maximumSections.
At value zero the shunt compensator conductance and admittance is zero.
Linear interpolation of conductance and admittance between the previous
and next integer section is applied in case of non-integer values.
For NonlinearShuntCompensator-s shall only be set to one of the NonlinearShuntCompenstorPhasePoint.sectionNumber.
There is no interpolation between NonlinearShuntCompenstorPhasePoint-s.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorPhase.phase
documentation:
Phase of this shunt compensator component. If the shunt compensator is
wye connected, the connection is from the indicated phase to the central
ground or neutral point. If the shunt compensator is delta connected, the
phase indicates a shunt compensator connected from the indicated phase
to the next logical non-neutral phase.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorPhase.ShuntCompensator
documentation: Shunt compensator of this shunt compensator phase.

Single phase of a multi-phase shunt compensator when its attributes might
be different per phase.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LinearShuntCompensatorPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LinearShuntCompensatorPhase.bPerSection
documentation:
Susceptance per section of the phase if shunt compensator is wye connected.
Susceptance per section phase to phase if shunt compensator is delta connected.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.LinearShuntCompensatorPhase.gPerSection
documentation:
Conductance per section for this phase if shunt compensator is wye connected.
Conductance per section phase to phase if shunt compensator is delta connected.

A per phase linear shunt compensator has banks or sections with equal admittance
values.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhase.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoints
documentation: All points of the non-linear shunt compensator phase.

A per phase non linear shunt compensator has bank or section admittance
values that differ. The attributes g and b of the associated NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoint
describe the total conductance and admittance of a NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoint
at a section number specified by NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoint.sectionNumber.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SwitchPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SwitchPhase.closed
documentation:
The attribute tells if the switch is considered closed when used as input
to topology processing.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SwitchPhase.normalOpen
documentation:
Used in cases when no Measurement for the status value is present. If the
SwitchPhase has a status measurement the Discrete.normalValue is expected
to match with this value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SwitchPhase.phaseSide1
documentation:
Phase of this SwitchPhase on the side with terminal sequence number equal
to 1. Should be a phase contained in that terminal’s phases attribute.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SwitchPhase.phaseSide2
documentation:
Phase of this SwitchPhase on the side with terminal sequence number equal
to 2. Should be a phase contained in that terminal’s Terminal.phases attribute.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SwitchPhase.ratedCurrent
documentation:
The maximum continuous current carrying capacity in amps governed by the
device material and construction.
The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SwitchPhase.Switch
documentation: The switch of the switch phase.

Single phase of a multi-phase switch when its attributes might be different
per phase.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChanger

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChanger.controlEnabled
documentation:
Specifies the regulation status of the equipment. True is regulating, false
is not regulating.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChanger.highStep
documentation:
Highest possible tap step position, advance from neutral.
The attribute shall be greater than lowStep.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChanger.lowStep
documentation: Lowest possible tap step position, retard from neutral.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChanger.ltcFlag
documentation: Specifies whether or not a TapChanger has load tap changing capabilities.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChanger.neutralStep
documentation:
The neutral tap step position for this winding.
The attribute shall be equal to or greater than lowStep and equal or less
than highStep.
It is the step position where the voltage is neutralU when the other terminals
of the transformer are at the ratedU. If there are other tap changers on
the transformer those taps are kept constant at their neutralStep.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChanger.normalStep
documentation:
The tap step position used in "normal" network operation for this winding.
For a "Fixed" tap changer indicates the current physical tap setting.
The attribute shall be equal to or greater than lowStep and equal to or
less than highStep.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChanger.step
documentation:
Tap changer position.
Starting step for a steady state solution. Non integer values are allowed
to support continuous tap variables. The reasons for continuous value are
to support study cases where no discrete tap changer has yet been designed,
a solution where a narrow voltage band forces the tap step to oscillate
or to accommodate for a continuous solution as input.
The attribute shall be equal to or greater than lowStep and equal to or
less than highStep.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChanger.initialDelay
documentation: For an LTC, the delay for initial tap changer operation (first step change).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChanger.neutralU
documentation:
Voltage at which the winding operates at the neutral tap setting. It is
the voltage at the terminal of the PowerTransformerEnd associated with
the tap changer when all tap changers on the transformer are at their neutralStep
position. Normally neutralU of the tap changer is the same as ratedU of
the PowerTransformerEnd, but it can differ in special cases such as when
the tapping mechanism is separate from the winding more common on lower
voltage transformers.
This attribute is not relevant for PhaseTapChangerAsymmetrical, PhaseTapChangerSymmetrical
and PhaseTapChangerLinear.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChanger.subsequentDelay
documentation:
For an LTC, the delay for subsequent tap changer operation (second and
later step changes).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChanger.SvTapStep
documentation: The tap step state associated with the tap changer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChanger.TapChangerControl
documentation: The regulating control scheme in which this tap changer participates.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChanger.TapSchedules
documentation: A TapChanger can have TapSchedules.
Mechanism for changing transformer winding tap positions.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChanger

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChanger.TransformerEnd
documentation: Transformer end to which this phase tap changer belongs.

A transformer phase shifting tap model that controls the phase angle difference
across the power transformer and potentially the active power flow through
the power transformer. This phase tap model may also impact the voltage
magnitude.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerLinear

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerLinear.stepPhaseShiftIncrement
documentation:
Phase shift per step position. A positive value indicates a positive angle
variation from the Terminal at the PowerTransformerEnd, where the TapChanger
is located, into the transformer.
The actual phase shift increment might be more accurately computed from
the symmetrical or asymmetrical models or a tap step table lookup if those
are available.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerLinear.xMax
documentation:
The reactance depends on the tap position according to a "u" shaped curve.
The maximum reactance (xMax) appears at the low and high tap positions.
Depending on the “u” curve the attribute can be either higher or lower
than PowerTransformerEnd.x.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerLinear.xMin
documentation:
The reactance depends on the tap position according to a "u" shaped curve.
The minimum reactance (xMin) appears at the mid tap position. PowerTransformerEnd.x
shall be consistent with PhaseTapChangerLinear.xMin and PhaseTapChangerNonLinear.xMin.
In case of inconsistency, PowerTransformerEnd.x shall be used.

Describes a tap changer with a linear relation between the tap step and
the phase angle difference across the transformer. This is a mathematical
model that is an approximation of a real phase tap changer.
The phase angle is computed as stepPhaseShiftIncrement times the tap position.
The voltage magnitude of both sides is the same.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerNonLinear

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerNonLinear.voltageStepIncrement
documentation:
The voltage step increment on the out of phase winding (the PowerTransformerEnd
where the TapChanger is located) specified in percent of rated voltage
of the PowerTransformerEnd. A positive value means a positive voltage variation
from the Terminal at the PowerTransformerEnd, where the TapChanger is located,
into the transformer.
When the increment is negative, the voltage decreases when the tap step
increases.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerNonLinear.xMax
documentation:
The reactance depends on the tap position according to a "u" shaped curve.
The maximum reactance (xMax) appears at the low and high tap positions.
Depending on the “u” curve the attribute can be either higher or lower
than PowerTransformerEnd.x.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerNonLinear.xMin
documentation:
The reactance depend on the tap position according to a "u" shaped curve.
The minimum reactance (xMin) appear at the mid tap position. PowerTransformerEnd.x
shall be consistent with PhaseTapChangerLinear.xMin and PhaseTapChangerNonLinear.xMin.
In case of inconsistency, PowerTransformerEnd.x shall be used.

The non-linear phase tap changer describes the non-linear behaviour of
a phase tap changer. This is a base class for the symmetrical and asymmetrical
phase tap changer models. The details of these models can be found in IEC
61970-301.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerAsymmetrical

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerAsymmetrical.windingConnectionAngle
documentation:
The phase angle between the in-phase winding and the out-of -phase winding
used for creating phase shift. The out-of-phase winding produces what is
known as the difference voltage. Setting this angle to 90 degrees is not
the same as a symmetrical transformer. The attribute can only be multiples
of 30 degrees. The allowed range is -150 degrees to 150 degrees excluding
0.

Describes the tap model for an asymmetrical phase shifting transformer
in which the difference voltage vector adds to the in-phase winding. The
out-of-phase winding is the transformer end where the tap changer is located.
The angle between the in-phase and out-of-phase windings is named the winding
connection angle. The phase shift depends on both the difference voltage
magnitude and the winding connection angle.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerSymmetrical
documentation:
Describes a symmetrical phase shifting transformer tap model in which the
voltage magnitude of both sides is the same. The difference voltage magnitude
is the base in an equal-sided triangle where the sides corresponds to the
primary and secondary voltages. The phase angle difference corresponds
to the top angle and can be expressed as twice the arctangent of half the
total difference voltage.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerTabular

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerTabular.PhaseTapChangerTable
documentation: The phase tap changer table for this phase tap changer.

Describes a tap changer with a table defining the relation between the
tap step and the phase angle difference across the transformer.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RatioTapChanger

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RatioTapChanger.stepVoltageIncrement
documentation:
Tap step increment, in per cent of rated voltage of the power transformer
end, per step position.
When the increment is negative, the voltage decreases when the tap step
increases.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RatioTapChanger.tculControlMode
documentation: Specifies the regulation control mode (voltage or reactive) of the RatioTapChanger.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RatioTapChanger.RatioTapChangerTable
documentation: The tap ratio table for this ratio tap changer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RatioTapChanger.TransformerEnd
documentation: Transformer end to which this ratio tap changer belongs.

A tap changer that changes the voltage ratio impacting the voltage magnitude
but not the phase angle across the transformer.
Angle sign convention (general): Positive value indicates a positive phase
shift from the winding where the tap is located to the other winding (for
a two-winding transformer).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VoltageControlZone

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VoltageControlZone.BusbarSection
documentation: A VoltageControlZone is controlled by a designated BusbarSection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VoltageControlZone.RegulationSchedule
documentation: A VoltageControlZone may have a voltage regulation schedule.

An area of the power system network which is defined for secondary voltage
control purposes. A voltage control zone consists of a collection of substations
with a designated bus bar section whose voltage will be controlled.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.WireSegmentPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.WireSegmentPhase.sequenceNumber
documentation:
Number designation for this wire segment phase. Each wire segment phase
within a wire segment should have a unique sequence number.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.WireSegmentPhase.phase
documentation: The phase connection of the wire at both ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.WireSegmentPhase.WireSegment
documentation: The wire segment to which the phase belongs.
Represents a single wire of an alternating current wire segment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ProductAssetModel

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ProductAssetModel.Asset
documentation: An asset of this model.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ProductAssetModel.AssetInfo
documentation: Asset information (nameplate) for this product asset model.
Asset model by a specific manufacturer.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ProtectiveActionAdjustment
documentation:
Protective actions on non-switching equipment. The operating condition
is adjusted.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RatioTapChangerTable

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RatioTapChangerTable.RatioTapChanger
documentation: The ratio tap changer of this tap ratio table.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RatioTapChangerTable.RatioTapChangerTablePoint
documentation: Points of this table.

Describes a curve for how the voltage magnitude and impedance varies with
the tap step.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RemoteControl

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RemoteControl.actuatorMaximum
documentation: The maximum set point value accepted by the remote control point.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RemoteControl.actuatorMinimum
documentation: The minimum set point value accepted by the remote control point.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RemoteControl.remoteControlled
documentation: Set to true if the actuator is remotely controlled.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RemoteControl.Control
documentation: The Control for the RemoteControl point.

Remote controls are outputs that are sent by the remote unit to actuators
in the process.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RemoteSource

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RemoteSource.deadband
documentation: The smallest change in value to be reported.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RemoteSource.sensorMaximum
documentation: The maximum value the telemetry item can return.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RemoteSource.sensorMinimum
documentation: The minimum value the telemetry item can return.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RemoteSource.scanInterval
documentation: The time interval between scans.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RemoteSource.MeasurementValue
documentation: Link to the physical telemetered point associated with this measurement.

Remote sources are state variables that are telemetered or calculated within
the remote unit.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ReportingGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ReportingGroup.BusNameMarker
documentation: The bus name markers that belong to this reporting group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ReportingGroup.PowerSystemResource
documentation: Power system resources which belong to this reporting group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ReportingGroup.ReportingSuperGroup
documentation: Reporting super group to which this reporting group belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ReportingGroup.TopologicalNode
documentation: The topological nodes that belong to the reporting group.
A reporting group is used for various ad-hoc groupings used for reporting.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ReportingSuperGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ReportingSuperGroup.ReportingGroup
documentation: Reporting groups that are grouped under this super group.
A reporting super group, groups reporting groups for a higher level report.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Season

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Season.endDate
documentation: Date season ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Season.startDate
documentation: Date season starts.
A specified time period of the year.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorAction.kind
documentation: Switching action to perform

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShuntCompensatorAction.ShuntCompensator
documentation: The shunt compensator that the shunt compensator action is performed on

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.StaticVarCompensatorDynamics

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.StaticVarCompensatorDynamics.StaticVarCompensator
documentation: Static Var Compensator to which Static Var Compensator dynamics model applies.

Static var compensator whose behaviour is described by reference to a standard
model <font color="#0f0f0f">or by definition of a user-defined model.</font>

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SubGeographicalRegion

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SubGeographicalRegion.Lines
documentation: The lines within the sub-geographical region.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SubGeographicalRegion.Region
documentation: The geographical region which this sub-geographical region is within.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SubGeographicalRegion.Substations
documentation: The substations in this sub-geographical region.
A subset of a geographical region of a power system network model.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SwitchAction

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SwitchAction.kind
documentation: Switching action to perform.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SwitchAction.OperatedSwitch
documentation: Switch that is the object of this switch action.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SwitchAction.PlannedOutage
documentation: Planned outage for whose scope this switch action applies.
Action on switch as a switching step.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachineDynamics

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SynchronousMachineDynamics.SynchronousMachine
documentation: Synchronous machine to which synchronous machine dynamics model applies.

Synchronous machine whose behaviour is described by reference to a standard model expressed in one of the following forms: - simplified (or classical), where a group of generators or motors is not modelled in detail; - detailed, in equivalent circuit form; - detailed, in time constant reactance form; or - by definition of a user-defined model. It is a common practice to represent small generators by a negative load rather than by a dynamic generator model when performing dynamics simulations. In this case, a SynchronousMachine in the static model is not represented by anything in the dynamics model, instead it is treated as an ordinary load. Parameter details:
  1. Synchronous machine parameters such as Xl, Xd, Xp etc. are actually used as inductances in the models, but are commonly referred to as reactances since, at nominal frequency, the PU values are the same. However, some references use the symbol L instead of X.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TopologicalIsland

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TopologicalIsland.AngleRefTopologicalNode
documentation:
The angle reference for the island. Normally there is one TopologicalNode
that is selected as the angle reference for each island. Other reference
schemes exist, so the association is typically optional.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TopologicalIsland.TopologicalNodes
documentation: A topological node belongs to a topological island.

An electrically connected subset of the network. Topological islands can
change as the current network state changes, e.g. due to:
- disconnect switches or breakers changing state in a SCADA/EMS.
- manual creation, change or deletion of topological nodes in a planning
tool.
Only energised TopologicalNode-s shall be part of the topological island.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TopologicalNode

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TopologicalNode.pInjection
documentation:
The active power injected into the bus at this location in addition to
injections from equipment. Positive sign means injection into the TopologicalNode
(bus).
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TopologicalNode.qInjection
documentation:
The reactive power injected into the bus at this location in addition to
injections from equipment. Positive sign means injection into the TopologicalNode
(bus).
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TopologicalNode.AngleRefTopologicalIsland
documentation:
The island for which the node is an angle reference. Normally there is
one angle reference node for each island.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TopologicalNode.BaseVoltage
documentation: The base voltage of the topological node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TopologicalNode.BusNameMarker
documentation: BusnameMarkers that may refer to a pre defined TopologicalNode.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TopologicalNode.ConnectivityNodeContainer
documentation: The connectivity node container to which the topological node belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TopologicalNode.ConnectivityNodes
documentation:
The connectivity nodes combine together to form this topological node.
May depend on the current state of switches in the network.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TopologicalNode.ReportingGroup
documentation: The reporting group to which the topological node belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TopologicalNode.Terminal
documentation:
The terminals associated with the topological node. This can be used as
an alternative to the connectivity node path to terminal, thus making it
unnecessary to model connectivity nodes in some cases. Note that if connectivity
nodes are in the model, this association would probably not be used as
an input specification.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TopologicalNode.TopologicalIsland
documentation: A topological node belongs to a topological island.

For a detailed substation model a topological node is a set of connectivity
nodes that, in the current network state, are connected together through
any type of closed switches, including jumpers. Topological nodes change
as the current network state changes (i.e., switches, breakers, etc. change
state).
For a planning model, switch statuses are not used to form topological
nodes. Instead they are manually created or deleted in a model builder
tool. Topological nodes maintained this way are also called "busses".

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerCoreAdmittance
