class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerCoreAdmittance.b
documentation: Magnetizing branch susceptance (B mag). The value can be positive or negative.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerCoreAdmittance.b0
documentation: Zero sequence magnetizing branch susceptance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerCoreAdmittance.g
documentation: Magnetizing branch conductance (G mag).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerCoreAdmittance.g0
documentation: Zero sequence magnetizing branch conductance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerCoreAdmittance.TransformerEnd
documentation: All transformer ends having this core admittance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerCoreAdmittance.TransformerEndInfo
documentation: Transformer end datasheet used to calculate this core admittance.

The transformer core admittance. Used to specify the core admittance of
a transformer in a manner that can be shared among power transformers.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerEnd

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerEnd.endNumber
documentation:
Number for this transformer end, corresponding to the end's order in the
power transformer vector group or phase angle clock number. Highest voltage
winding should be 1. Each end within a power transformer should have a
unique subsequent end number. Note the transformer end number need not
match the terminal sequence number.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerEnd.grounded
documentation: (for Yn and Zn connections) True if the neutral is solidly grounded.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerEnd.bmagSat
documentation: Core shunt magnetizing susceptance in the saturation region.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerEnd.magBaseU
documentation:
The reference voltage at which the magnetizing saturation measurements
were made.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerEnd.magSatFlux
documentation: Core magnetizing saturation curve knee flux level.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerEnd.rground
documentation:
(for Yn and Zn connections) Resistance part of neutral impedance where
'grounded' is true.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerEnd.xground
documentation:
(for Yn and Zn connections) Reactive part of neutral impedance where 'grounded'
is true.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerEnd.BaseVoltage
documentation: Base voltage of the transformer end. This is essential for PU calculation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerEnd.CoreAdmittance
documentation:
Core admittance of this transformer end, representing magnetising current
and core losses. The full values of the transformer should be supplied
for one transformer end only.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerEnd.FromMeshImpedance
documentation: All mesh impedances between this 'to' and other 'from' transformer ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerEnd.PhaseTapChanger
documentation: Phase tap changer associated with this transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerEnd.RatioTapChanger
documentation: Ratio tap changer associated with this transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerEnd.StarImpedance
documentation:
(accurate for 2- or 3-winding transformers only) Pi-model impedances of
this transformer end. By convention, for a two winding transformer, the
full values of the transformer should be entered on the high voltage end
(endNumber=1).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerEnd.Terminal
documentation: Terminal of the power transformer to which this transformer end belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerEnd.ToMeshImpedance
documentation: All mesh impedances between this 'from' and other 'to' transformer ends.

A conducting connection point of a power transformer. It corresponds to
a physical transformer winding terminal. In earlier CIM versions, the TransformerWinding
class served a similar purpose, but this class is more flexible because
it associates to terminal but is not a specialization of ConductingEquipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformerEnd

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformerEnd.phaseAngleClock
documentation:
Terminal voltage phase angle displacement where 360 degrees are represented
with clock hours. The valid values are 0 to 11. For example, for the secondary
side end of a transformer with vector group code of 'Dyn11', specify the
connection kind as wye with neutral and specify the phase angle of the
clock as 11. The clock value of the transformer end number specified as
1, is assumed to be zero. Note the transformer end number is not assumed
to be the same as the terminal sequence number.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformerEnd.b
documentation: Magnetizing branch susceptance (B mag). The value can be positive or negative.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformerEnd.b0
documentation: Zero sequence magnetizing branch susceptance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformerEnd.connectionKind
documentation: Kind of connection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformerEnd.g
documentation: Magnetizing branch conductance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformerEnd.g0
documentation: Zero sequence magnetizing branch conductance (star-model).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformerEnd.r
documentation:
Resistance (star-model) of the transformer end.
The attribute shall be equal to or greater than zero for non-equivalent
transformers.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformerEnd.r0
documentation: Zero sequence series resistance (star-model) of the transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformerEnd.ratedS
documentation:
Normal apparent power rating.
The attribute shall be a positive value. For a two-winding transformer
the values for the high and low voltage sides shall be identical.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformerEnd.ratedU
documentation:
Rated voltage: phase-phase for three-phase windings, and either phase-phase
or phase-neutral for single-phase windings.
A high voltage side, as given by TransformerEnd.endNumber, shall have a
ratedU that is greater than or equal to ratedU for the lower voltage sides.
The attribute shall be a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformerEnd.x
documentation: Positive sequence series reactance (star-model) of the transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformerEnd.x0
documentation: Zero sequence series reactance of the transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PowerTransformerEnd.PowerTransformer
documentation: The power transformer of this power transformer end.

A PowerTransformerEnd is associated with each Terminal of a PowerTransformer.
The impedance values r, r0, x, and x0 of a PowerTransformerEnd represents
a star equivalent as follows.
1) for a two Terminal PowerTransformer the high voltage (TransformerEnd.endNumber=1)
PowerTransformerEnd has non zero values on r, r0, x, and x0 while the low
voltage (TransformerEnd.endNumber=2) PowerTransformerEnd has zero values
for r, r0, x, and x0. Parameters are always provided, even if the PowerTransformerEnds
have the same rated voltage. In this case, the parameters are provided
at the PowerTransformerEnd which has TransformerEnd.endNumber equal to
1.
2) for a three Terminal PowerTransformer the three PowerTransformerEnds
represent a star equivalent with each leg in the star represented by r,
r0, x, and x0 values.
3) For a three Terminal transformer each PowerTransformerEnd shall have
g, g0, b and b0 values corresponding to the no load losses distributed
on the three PowerTransformerEnds. The total no load loss shunt impedances
may also be placed at one of the PowerTransformerEnds, preferably the end
numbered 1, having the shunt values on end 1. This is the preferred way.
4) for a PowerTransformer with more than three Terminals the PowerTransformerEnd
impedance values cannot be used. Instead use the TransformerMeshImpedance
or split the transformer into multiple PowerTransformers.
Each PowerTransformerEnd must be contained by a PowerTransformer. Because
a PowerTransformerEnd (or any other object) can not be contained by more
than one parent, a PowerTransformerEnd can not have an association to an
EquipmentContainer (Substation, VoltageLevel, etc).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerTankEnd

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerTankEnd.phases
documentation: Describes the phases carried by a conducting equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerTankEnd.TransformerTank
documentation: Transformer this winding belongs to.

Transformer tank end represents an individual winding for unbalanced models
or for transformer tanks connected into a bank (and bank is modelled with
the PowerTransformer).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerMeshImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerMeshImpedance.r
documentation: Resistance between the 'from' and the 'to' end, seen from the 'from' end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerMeshImpedance.r0
documentation:
Zero-sequence resistance between the 'from' and the 'to' end, seen from
the 'from' end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerMeshImpedance.x
documentation: Reactance between the 'from' and the 'to' end, seen from the 'from' end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerMeshImpedance.x0
documentation:
Zero-sequence reactance between the 'from' and the 'to' end, seen from
the 'from' end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerMeshImpedance.FromTransformerEnd
documentation:
From end this mesh impedance is connected to. It determines the voltage
reference.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerMeshImpedance.FromTransformerEndInfo
documentation:
'from' transformer end datasheet this mesh impedance is calculated from.
It determines the voltage reference.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerMeshImpedance.ToTransformerEnd
documentation: All transformer ends this mesh impedance is connected to.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerMeshImpedance.ToTransformerEndInfos
documentation:
All 'to' transformer end datasheets this mesh impedance for 'from' transformer
end is calculated from.

Transformer mesh impedance (Delta-model) between transformer ends.
The typical case is that this class describes the impedance between two
transformer ends pair-wise, i.e. the cardinalities at both transformer
end associations are 1. However, in cases where two or more transformer
ends are modelled the cardinalities are larger than 1.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerObservation
documentation:
Common information captured during transformer inspections and/or diagnostics.
Note that some properties may be measured through other means and therefore
have measurement values in addition to the observed values recorded here.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerStarImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerStarImpedance.r
documentation: Resistance of the transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerStarImpedance.r0
documentation: Zero sequence series resistance of the transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerStarImpedance.x
documentation: Positive sequence series reactance of the transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerStarImpedance.x0
documentation: Zero sequence series reactance of the transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerStarImpedance.TransformerEnd
documentation: All transformer ends having this star impedance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerStarImpedance.TransformerEndInfo
documentation: Transformer end datasheet used to calculate this transformer star impedance.

Transformer star impedance (Pi-model) that accurately reflects impedance
for transformers with 2 or 3 windings. For transformers with 4 or more
windings, TransformerMeshImpedance class shall be used.
For transmission networks use PowerTransformerEnd impedances (r, r0, x,
x0, b, b0, g and g0).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerTest

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerTest.basePower
documentation:
Base power at which the tests are conducted, usually equal to the rateds
of one of the involved transformer ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TransformerTest.temperature
documentation: Temperature at which the test is conducted.

Test result for transformer ends, such as short-circuit, open-circuit (excitation)
or no-load test.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NoLoadTest

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NoLoadTest.energisedEndVoltage
documentation: Voltage applied to the winding (end) during test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NoLoadTest.excitingCurrent
documentation:
Exciting current measured from a positive-sequence or single-phase excitation
test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NoLoadTest.excitingCurrentZero
documentation:
Exciting current measured from a zero-sequence open-circuit excitation
test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NoLoadTest.loss
documentation: Losses measured from a positive-sequence or single-phase excitation test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NoLoadTest.lossZero
documentation: Losses measured from a zero-sequence excitation test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NoLoadTest.EnergisedEnd
documentation: Transformer end that current is applied to in this no-load test.

No-load test results determine core admittance parameters. They include
exciting current and core loss measurements from applying voltage to one
winding. The excitation may be positive sequence or zero sequence. The
test may be repeated at different voltages to measure saturation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OpenCircuitTest

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OpenCircuitTest.energisedEndStep
documentation: Tap step number for the energised end of the test pair.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OpenCircuitTest.openEndStep
documentation: Tap step number for the open end of the test pair.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OpenCircuitTest.energisedEndVoltage
documentation: Voltage applied to the winding (end) during test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OpenCircuitTest.openEndVoltage
documentation:
Voltage measured at the open-circuited end, with the energised end set
to rated voltage and all other ends open.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OpenCircuitTest.phaseShift
documentation:
Phase shift measured at the open end with the energised end set to rated
voltage and all other ends open.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OpenCircuitTest.EnergisedEnd
documentation: Transformer end that current is applied to in this open-circuit test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OpenCircuitTest.OpenEnd
documentation:
Transformer end measured for induced voltage and angle in this open-circuit
test.

Open-circuit test results verify winding turn ratios and phase shifts.
They include induced voltage and phase shift measurements on open-circuit
windings, with voltage applied to the energised end. For three-phase windings,
the excitation can be a positive sequence (the default) or a zero sequence.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShortCircuitTest

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShortCircuitTest.energisedEndStep
documentation: Tap step number for the energised end of the test pair.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShortCircuitTest.groundedEndStep
documentation: Tap step number for the grounded end of the test pair.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShortCircuitTest.current
documentation: Short circuit current..

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShortCircuitTest.leakageImpedance
documentation:
Leakage impedance measured from a positive-sequence or single-phase short-circuit
test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShortCircuitTest.leakageImpedanceZero
documentation: Leakage impedance measured from a zero-sequence short-circuit test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShortCircuitTest.loss
documentation: Load losses from a positive-sequence or single-phase short-circuit test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShortCircuitTest.lossZero
documentation: Load losses from a zero-sequence short-circuit test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShortCircuitTest.power
documentation: Short circuit apparent power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShortCircuitTest.voltage
documentation: Short circuit voltage..

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShortCircuitTest.EnergisedEnd
documentation:
Transformer end that voltage is applied to in this short-circuit test.
The test voltage is chosen to induce rated current in the energised end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ShortCircuitTest.GroundedEnds
documentation: All ends short-circuited in this short-circuit test.

Short-circuit test results determine mesh impedance parameters. They include
load losses and leakage impedances. For three-phase windings, the excitation
can be a positive sequence (the default) or a zero sequence. There shall
be at least one grounded winding.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.UsagePointGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.UsagePointGroup.type
documentation: Type of this group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.UsagePointGroup.DemandResponsePrograms
documentation: All demand response programs this usage point group is enrolled in.

Abstraction for management of group communications within a two-way AMR
system or the data for a group of related usage points. Commands can be
issued to all of the usage points that belong to a usage point group using
a defined group address and the underlying AMR communication infrastructure.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VSCDynamics
documentation:
VSC function block whose behaviour is described by reference to a standard
model <font color="#0f0f0f">or by definition of a user-defined model.</font>

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ValueAliasSet
documentation:
Describes the translation of a set of values into a name and is intendend
to facilitate custom translations. Each ValueAliasSet has a name, description
etc. A specific Measurement may represent a discrete state like Open, Closed,
Intermediate etc. This requires a translation from the MeasurementValue.value
number to a string, e.g. 0->"Invalid", 1->"Open", 2->"Closed",
3->"Intermediate". Each ValueToAlias member in ValueAliasSet.Value describe
a mapping for one particular value to a name.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ValueToAlias

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ValueToAlias.value
documentation: The value that is mapped.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.ValueToAlias.ValueAliasSet
documentation: The ValueAliasSet having the ValueToAlias mappings.

Describes the translation of one particular value into a name, e.g. 1 as
"Open".

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.WindTurbineType3or4Dynamics
documentation:
Parent class supporting relationships to wind turbines type 3 and type
4 and wind plant including their control models.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.WindingInsulation

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.WindingInsulation.insulationPFStatus
documentation:
Status of Winding Insulation Power Factor as of statusDate: Acceptable,
Minor Deterioration or Moisture Absorption, Major Deterioration or Moisture
Absorption, Failed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.WindingInsulation.insulationResistance
documentation:
For testType, status of Winding Insulation Resistance as of statusDate.
Typical values are: Acceptable, Questionable, Failed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.WindingInsulation.leakageReactance
documentation:
As of statusDate, the leakage reactance measured at the "from" winding
with the "to" winding short-circuited and all other windings open-circuited.
Winding insulation condition as a result of a test.

The schedule used for dispatching or forecasting the values of DER monitorable

The DER curve data class provides the nominal, maximum and minimum values
for a DER Monitorable parameter for a series of time intervals defined
by the DER DispatchSchedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERMonitorableParameter.yUnitInstalledMin

The minimum value of the DERMonitorableParameter based on the installed

such as, for example, energy, power, demand.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERMonitorableParameter.yUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERMonitorableParameter.DispatchSchedule
documentation:
The schedule used for dispatching or forecasting the values of DER monitorable
parameters over time.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERMonitorableParameter.EndDeviceGroup
documentation: The DER Group having DER monitorable parameters.

To define the DER monitorable parameters that will be forecasted and dispatched
for a DER Group. Each parameter is represented as a Y Value at a point
in time or on a curve as a function of time, which is represented on the
X axis.

dispatch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DispatchSchedule.startTime

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DispatchSchedule.timeIntervalDuration
documentation: The length of time for each interval in the dispatch schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DispatchSchedule.confidence
documentation:
A value set by the system, function, algorithm or person creating a prediction
as to how accurate the prediction is.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DispatchSchedule.curveStyleKind
documentation:
Used to specify whether the values over an interval are constant (constantYValue)
or linearly interpolated (straightLineYValues)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DispatchSchedule.timeIntervalUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DispatchSchedule.DERCurveData

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DispatchSchedule.DERMonitorableParameter

Specifies the characteristics of the time schedule over which DERMonitorableParameters
are dispatched.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.IEC61970CIMVersion

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.IEC61970CIMVersion.date
documentation: Form is YYYY-MM-DD for example for January 5, 2009 it is 2009-01-05.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.IEC61970CIMVersion.version
documentation:
Form is IEC61970CIMXXvYY where XX is the major CIM package version and
the YY is the minor version. For example IEC61970CIM13v18.
This is the IEC 61970 CIM version number assigned to this UML model.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.InstanceSet

Instance of a version of a model part. This corresponds to a payload of
instance data.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.IrregularTimePoint.time

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.IrregularTimePoint.IntervalSchedule

TimePoints for a schedule where the time between the points varies.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Name

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Name.name
documentation: Any free text that name the object.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Name.IdentifiedObject
documentation: Identified object that this name designates.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Name.NameType
documentation: Type of this name.

The Name class provides the means to define any number of human readable names for an object. A name is not to be used for defining inter-object relationships. For inter-object relationships instead use the object identification 'mRID'.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NameType

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NameType.description
documentation: Description of the name type.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NameType.name
documentation: Name of the name type.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NameType.Names
documentation: All names of this type.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NameType.NameTypeAuthority
documentation: Authority responsible for managing names of this type.

Type of name. Possible values for attribute 'name' are implementation dependent
but standard profiles may specify types. An enterprise may have multiple
IT systems each having its own local name for the same object, e.g. a planning
system may have different names from an EMS. An object may also have different
names within the same IT system, e.g. localName as defined in CIM version
14. The definition from CIM14 is:
The localName is a human readable name of the object. It is a free text
name local to a node in a naming hierarchy similar to a file directory
structure. A power system related naming hierarchy may be: Substation,
VoltageLevel, Equipment etc. Children of the same parent in such a hierarchy
have names that typically are unique among them.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NameTypeAuthority

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NameTypeAuthority.description
documentation: Description of the name type authority.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NameTypeAuthority.name
documentation: Name of the name type authority.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NameTypeAuthority.NameTypes
documentation: All name types managed by this authority.

Authority responsible for creation and management of names of a given type;
typically an organization or an enterprise system.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoint.sectionNumber
documentation: The number of the section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoint.b
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) susceptance per section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoint.g
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance per section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoint.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhase
documentation: Non-linear shunt compensator phase owning this point.

A per phase non linear shunt compensator bank or section admittance value.
The number of NonlinearShuntCompenstorPhasePoint instances associated with
a NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhase shall be equal to ShuntCompensatorPhase.maximumSections.
ShuntCompensator.sections shall only be set to one of the NonlinearShuntCompenstorPhasePoint.sectionNumber.
There is no interpolation between NonlinearShuntCompenstorPhasePoint-s.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.sectionNumber
documentation: The number of the section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.b
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) susceptance per section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.b0
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) susceptance per section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.g
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance per section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.g0
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) conductance per section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.NonlinearShuntCompensator
documentation: Non-linear shunt compensator owning this point.

A non linear shunt compensator bank or section admittance value. The number
of NonlinearShuntCompenstorPoint instances associated with a NonlinearShuntCompensator
shall be equal to ShuntCompensator.maximumSections. ShuntCompensator.sections
shall only be set to one of the NonlinearShuntCompenstorPoint.sectionNumber.
There is no interpolation between NonlinearShuntCompenstorPoint-s.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperatingShare

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperatingShare.percentage
documentation:
Percentage operational ownership between the pair (power system resource
and operating participant) associated with this share. The total percentage
ownership for a power system resource should add to 100%.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperatingShare.OperatingParticipant
documentation:
The operating participant having this share with the associated power system
resource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperatingShare.PowerSystemResource
documentation: The power system resource to which the share applies.

Specifies the operations contract relationship between a power system resource
and a contract participant.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.OperatonalLimitTypeScaling
documentation:
One operational limit type scales values of another operational limit type
when under the same operational limit set. This applies to any operational
limit assigned to the target operational limit type and without other limit
dependency models.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseImpedanceData

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseImpedanceData.column
documentation:
The matrix element's column number, in the range 1 to row. Only the lower
triangle needs to be stored. This column number matches ACLineSegmentPhase.sequenceNumber.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseImpedanceData.row
documentation:
The matrix element’s row number, in the range 1 to PerLengthPhaseImpedance.conductorCount.
Only the lower triangle needs to be stored. This row number matches ACLineSegmentPhase.sequenceNumber.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseImpedanceData.b
documentation: Susceptance matrix element value, per length of unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseImpedanceData.fromPhase
documentation: Refer to the class description.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseImpedanceData.g
documentation: Conductance matrix element value, per length of unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseImpedanceData.r
documentation: Resistance matrix element value, per length of unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseImpedanceData.toPhase
documentation: Refer to the class description.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseImpedanceData.x
documentation: Reactance matrix element value, per length of unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseImpedanceData.PhaseImpedance
documentation: Conductor phase impedance to which this data belongs.

Impedance and conductance matrix element values.
The diagonal elements are described by the elements having the same toPhase
and fromPhase value and the off diagonal elements have different toPhase
and fromPhase values. The matrix can also be stored in symmetric lower
triangular format using the row and column attributes, which map to ACLineSegmentPhase.sequenceNumber.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PositionPoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PositionPoint.groupNumber
cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PositionPoint.sequenceNumber
documentation: Zero-relative sequence number of this point within a series of points.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PositionPoint.xPosition
documentation: X axis position.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PositionPoint.yPosition
documentation: Y axis position.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PositionPoint.zPosition
documentation: (if applicable) Z axis position.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PositionPoint.Location
documentation: Location described by this position point.

Set of spatial coordinates that determine a point, defined in the coordinate
system specified in 'Location.CoordinateSystem'. Use a single position
point instance to describe a point-oriented location. Use a sequence of
position points to describe a line-oriented object (physical location of
non-point oriented objects like cables or lines), or area of an object
(like a substation or a geographical zone - in this case, have first and
last position point with the same values).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Quality61850

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Quality61850.badReference
documentation: Measurement value may be incorrect due to a reference being out of calibration.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Quality61850.estimatorReplaced
documentation:
Value has been replaced by State Estimator. estimatorReplaced is not an
IEC61850 quality bit but has been put in this class for convenience.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Quality61850.failure
documentation:
This identifier indicates that a supervision function has detected an internal
or external failure, e.g. communication failure.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Quality61850.oldData
documentation:
Measurement value is old and possibly invalid, as it has not been successfully
updated during a specified time interval.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Quality61850.operatorBlocked
documentation: Measurement value is blocked and hence unavailable for transmission.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Quality61850.oscillatory
documentation:
To prevent some overload of the communication it is sensible to detect
and suppress oscillating (fast changing) binary inputs. If a signal changes
in a defined time twice in the same direction (from 0 to 1 or from 1 to
0) then oscillation is detected and the detail quality identifier "oscillatory"
is set. If it is detected a configured numbers of transient changes could
be passed by. In this time the validity status "questionable" is set. If
after this defined numbers of changes the signal is still in the oscillating
state the value shall be set either to the opposite state of the previous
stable value or to a defined default value. In this case the validity status
"questionable" is reset and "invalid" is set as long as the signal is oscillating.
If it is configured such that no transient changes should be passed by
then the validity status "invalid" is set immediately in addition to the
detail quality identifier "oscillatory" (used for status information only).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Quality61850.outOfRange
documentation: Measurement value is beyond a predefined range of value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Quality61850.overFlow
documentation:
Measurement value is beyond the capability of being represented properly.
For example, a counter value overflows from maximum count back to a value
of zero.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Quality61850.suspect
documentation:
A correlation function has detected that the value is not consistent with
other values. Typically set by a network State Estimator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Quality61850.test
documentation: Measurement value is transmitted for test purposes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Quality61850.source
documentation:
Source gives information related to the origin of a value. The value may
be acquired from the process, defaulted or substituted.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Quality61850.validity
documentation: Validity of the measurement value.

Quality flags in this class are as defined in IEC 61850, except for estimatorReplaced,
which has been included in this class for convenience.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.MeasurementValueQuality

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.MeasurementValueQuality.MeasurementValue
documentation: A MeasurementValue has a MeasurementValueQuality associated with it.

Measurement quality flags. Bits 0-10 are defined for substation automation
in IEC 61850-7-3. Bits 11-15 are reserved for future expansion by that
document. Bits 16-31 are reserved for EMS applications.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegularTimePoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegularTimePoint.sequenceNumber
documentation:
The position of the regular time point in the sequence. Note that time
points don't have to be sequential, i.e. time points may be omitted. The
actual time for a RegularTimePoint is computed by multiplying the associated
regular interval schedule's time step with the regular time point sequence
number and adding the associated schedules start time. To specify values
for the start time, use sequence number 0. The sequence number cannot be
negative.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegularTimePoint.value1
documentation:
The first value at the time. The meaning of the value is defined by the
derived type of the associated schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegularTimePoint.value2
documentation:
The second value at the time. The meaning of the value is defined by the
derived type of the associated schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RegularTimePoint.IntervalSchedule
documentation: Regular interval schedule containing this time point.

Time point for a schedule where the time between the consecutive points
is constant.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SvShuntCompensatorSections

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SvShuntCompensatorSections.sections
documentation:
The number of sections in service as a continuous variable. The attribute
shall be a positive value or zero. To get integer value scale with ShuntCompensator.bPerSection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SvShuntCompensatorSections.phase
documentation:
The terminal phase at which the connection is applied. If missing, the
injection is assumed to be balanced among non-neutral phases.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SvShuntCompensatorSections.ShuntCompensator
documentation: The shunt compensator for which the state applies.
State variable for the number of sections in service for a shunt compensator.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SvTapStep

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SvTapStep.position
documentation:
The floating point tap position. This is not the tap ratio, but rather
the tap step position as defined by the related tap changer model and normally
is constrained to be within the range of minimum and maximum tap positions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.SvTapStep.TapChanger
documentation: The tap changer associated with the tap step state.
State variable for transformer tap step.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChangerTablePoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChangerTablePoint.ratio
documentation:
The voltage at the tap step divided by rated voltage of the transformer
end having the tap changer. Hence this is a value close to one.
For example, if the ratio at step 1 is 1.01, and the rated voltage of the
transformer end is 110kV, then the voltage obtained by setting the tap
changer to step 1 to is 111.1kV.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChangerTablePoint.step
documentation: The tap step.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChangerTablePoint.b
documentation:
The magnetizing branch susceptance deviation as a percentage of nominal
value. The actual susceptance is calculated as follows:
calculated magnetizing susceptance = b(nominal) * (1 + b(from this class)/100).
The b(nominal) is defined as the static magnetizing susceptance on the
associated power transformer end or ends. This model assumes the star impedance
(pi model) form.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChangerTablePoint.g
documentation:
The magnetizing branch conductance deviation as a percentage of nominal
value. The actual conductance is calculated as follows:
calculated magnetizing conductance = g(nominal) * (1 + g(from this class)/100).
The g(nominal) is defined as the static magnetizing conductance on the
associated power transformer end or ends. This model assumes the star impedance
(pi model) form.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChangerTablePoint.r
documentation:
The resistance deviation as a percentage of nominal value. The actual reactance
is calculated as follows:
calculated resistance = r(nominal) * (1 + r(from this class)/100). The
r(nominal) is defined as the static resistance on the associated power
transformer end or ends. This model assumes the star impedance (pi model)
form.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.TapChangerTablePoint.x
documentation:
The series reactance deviation as a percentage of nominal value. The actual
reactance is calculated as follows:
calculated reactance = x(nominal) * (1 + x(from this class)/100). The x(nominal)
is defined as the static series reactance on the associated power transformer
end or ends. This model assumes the star impedance (pi model) form.
Describes each tap step in the tabular curve.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerTablePoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerTablePoint.angle
documentation:
The angle difference in degrees. A positive value indicates a positive
angle variation from the Terminal at the PowerTransformerEnd, where the
TapChanger is located, into the transformer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseTapChangerTablePoint.PhaseTapChangerTable
documentation: The table of this point.
Describes each tap step in the phase tap changer tabular curve.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RatioTapChangerTablePoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.RatioTapChangerTablePoint.RatioTapChangerTable

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCPolarityKind

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCPolarityKind.middle
documentation:
Middle pole. The converter terminal is the midpoint in a bipolar or symmetric
monopole configuration. The midpoint can be grounded and/or have a metallic
return.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCPolarityKind.negative
documentation:
Negative pole. The converter terminal is intended to operate at a negative
voltage relative the midpoint or positive terminal.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DCPolarityKind.positive
documentation:
Positive pole. The converter terminal is intended to operate at a positive
voltage relative the midpoint or negative terminal.
Polarity for DC circuits.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERParameterKind.apparentPower

The combination of reactive power and active power, and is the product
of voltage and current without reference to the phase angle.

documentation: An indication of whether the ramp rate associated with a curve is negative.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERParameterKind.highFilterBiDirectionalRegulation
documentation: AC voltage regulation of high frequency

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERParameterKind.highFilterDownRegulation
documentation: High Pass Frequency Regulation downwards.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERParameterKind.highFilterUpRegulation
documentation: High Pass Frequency Regulation upwards.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERParameterKind.increasingRampRate
documentation: An indication of whether the ramp rate associated with a curve is positive.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERParameterKind.lowFilterBiDirectionalRegulation
documentation: Used to filter out the sinosuidal disturbances to the grid.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERParameterKind.lowFilterDownRegulation
documentation: Low Pass Frequency Regulation downwards.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERParameterKind.lowFilterUpRegulation
documentation: Low Pass Frequency Regulation upwards.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERParameterKind.reactivePower
documentation: The measure of reactance, measured in Vars.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERParameterKind.voltage
documentation:
The measure of difference in potential between two points, measured in
Volts.

Specifies the DER parameters related to the unit of power, ramp rate and
regulation flows.

documentation: Ampere seconds (A·s).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.Btu
documentation: Energy, British Thermal Unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.Hz
documentation: Frequency in Hertz (1/s).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.Q
documentation: Quantity power, Q.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.Qh
documentation: Quantity energy, Qh.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.V
documentation: Electric potential in Volt (W/A).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.VA
documentation: Apparent power in Volt Ampere (See also real power and reactive power.)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.VAh
documentation: Apparent energy in Volt Ampere hours.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.VAr
documentation:
Reactive power in Volt Ampere reactive. The “reactive” or “imaginary” component
of electrical power (VIsin(phi)). (See also real power and apparent power).
Note: Different meter designs use different methods to arrive at their
results. Some meters may compute reactive power as an arithmetic value,
while others compute the value vectorially. The data consumer should determine
the method in use and the suitability of the measurement for the intended
purpose.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.VArh
documentation: Reactive energy in Volt Ampere reactive hours.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.VPerVA
documentation:
Power factor, PF, the ratio of the active power to the apparent power.
Note: The sign convention used for power factor will differ between IEC
meters and EEI (ANSI) meters. It is assumed that the data consumers understand
the type of meter being used and agree on the sign convention in use at
any given utility.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.VPerVAr
documentation:
Power factor, PF, the ratio of the active power to the apparent power.
Note: The sign convention used for power factor will differ between IEC
meters and EEI (ANSI) meters. It is assumed that the data consumers understand
the type of meter being used and agree on the sign convention in use at
any given utility.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.Vh
documentation: Volt-hour, Volt hours.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.Vs
documentation: Volt second (Ws/A).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.W
documentation:
Real power in Watt (J/s). Electrical power may have real and reactive components.
The real portion of electrical power (I²R or VIcos(phi)), is expressed
in Watts. (See also apparent power and reactive power.)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.WPerA
documentation: Active power per current flow, watt per Ampere.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.WPers
documentation: Ramp rate in Watt per second.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.Wh
documentation: Real energy in Watt hours.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.deg
documentation: Plane angle in degrees.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.degC
documentation:
Relative temperature in degrees Celsius.
In the SI unit system the symbol is ºC. Electric charge is measured in
coulomb that has the unit symbol C. To distinguish degree Celsius form
coulomb the symbol used in the UML is degC. Reason for not using ºC is
the special character º is difficult to manage in software.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.h
documentation: Time, hour = 60 min = 3600 s.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.min
documentation: Time, minute = 60 s.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.ohm
documentation: Electric resistance in ohm (V/A).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.ohmPerm
documentation: Electric resistance per length in ohm per metre ((V/A)/m).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.ohmm
documentation: resistivity, Ohm metre, (rho).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.onePerHz
documentation: Reciprocal of frequency (1/Hz).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.s
documentation: Time in seconds.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.DERUnitSymbol.therm
documentation: Energy, Therm.

The units defined for usage in DER related contexts. This class is a subset
plus possible additions of the UnitSymbol Class in the CIM Domain Package.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FlowDirectionKind

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FlowDirectionKind.forward
documentation: "Delivered," or "Imported" as defined 61968-2. Forward Active Energy is a positive kWh value as one would naturally expect to find as energy is supplied by the utility and consumed at the service. Forward Reactive Energy is a positive VArh value as one would naturally expect to find in the presence of inductive loading. In polyphase metering, the forward energy register is incremented when the sum of the phase energies is greater than zero:

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FlowDirectionKind.lagging
documentation:
Typically used to describe that a power factor is lagging the reference
value.
Note 1: When used to describe VA, “lagging” describes a form of measurement
where reactive power is considered in all four quadrants, but real power
is considered only in quadrants I and IV.
Note 2: When used to describe power factor, the term “Lagging” implies
that the PF is negative. The term “lagging” in this case takes the place
of the negative sign. If a signed PF value is to be passed by the data
producer, then the direction of flow enumeration zero (none) should be
used in order to avoid the possibility of creating an expression that employs
a double negative. The data consumer should be able to tell from the sign
of the data if the PF is leading or lagging. This principle is analogous
to the concept that “Reverse” energy is an implied negative value, and
to publish a negative reverse value would be ambiguous.
Note 3: Lagging power factors typically indicate inductive loading.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FlowDirectionKind.leading
documentation:
Typically used to describe that a power factor is leading the reference
value.
Note: Leading power factors typically indicate capacitive loading.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FlowDirectionKind.net
documentation:
|Forward| - |Reverse|, See 61968-2.
Note: In some systems, the value passed as a “net” value could become negative.
In other systems the value passed as a “net” value is always a positive
number, and rolls-over and rolls-under as needed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FlowDirectionKind.none
documentation: Not Applicable (N/A)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FlowDirectionKind.q1minusQ4
documentation: Q1 minus Q4

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FlowDirectionKind.q1plusQ2
documentation: Reactive positive quadrants. (The term “lagging” is preferred.)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FlowDirectionKind.q1plusQ3
documentation: Quadrants 1 and 3

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FlowDirectionKind.q1plusQ4

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FlowDirectionKind.reverse
documentation: Reverse Active Energy is equivalent to "Received," or "Exported" as defined in 61968-2. Reverse Active Energy is a positive kWh value as one would expect to find when energy is backfed by the service onto the utility network. Reverse Reactive Energy is a positive VArh value as one would expect to find in the presence of capacitive loading and a leading Power Factor. In polyphase metering, the reverse energy register is incremented when the sum of the phase energies is less than zero: Note: The value passed as a reverse value is always a positive value. It is understood by the label “reverse” that it represents negative flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FlowDirectionKind.total
documentation: |Forward| + |Reverse|, See 61968-2. The sum of the commodity in all quadrants Q1+Q2+Q3+Q4. In polyphase metering, the total energy register is incremented when the absolute value of the sum of the phase energies is greater than zero:

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.FlowDirectionKind.totalByPhase
documentation: In polyphase metering, the total by phase energy register is incremented when the sum of the absolute values of the phase energies is greater than zero: In single phase metering, the formulas for “Total” and “Total by phase” collapse to the same expression. For communication purposes however, the “Total” enumeration should be used with single phase meter data.

Kind of flow direction for reading/measured values proper to some commodities
such as, for example, energy, power, demand.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseCode.s2
documentation: Secondary phase 2.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.PhaseCode.s2N
documentation: Secondary phase 2 and neutral.

An unordered enumeration of phase identifiers. Allows designation of phases
for both transmission and distribution equipment, circuits and loads. The
enumeration, by itself, does not describe how the phases are connected
together or connected to ground. Ground is not explicitly denoted as a
phase.
Residential and small commercial loads are often served from single-phase,
or split-phase, secondary circuits. For the example of s12N, phases 1 and
2 refer to hot wires that are 180 degrees out of phase, while N refers
to the neutral wire. Through single-phase transformer connections, these
secondary circuits may be served from one or two of the primary phases
A, B, and C. For three-phase loads, use the A, B, C phase codes instead
of s12N.
The integer values are from IEC 61968-9 to support revenue metering applications.

documentation: Giga 10**9.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.UnitMultiplier.k

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.UnitMultiplier.micro

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.UnitMultiplier.none
documentation: No multiplier or equivalently multiply by 1.

The unit multipliers defined for the CIM. When applied to unit symbols,
the unit symbol is treated as a derived unit. Regardless of the contents
of the unit symbol text, the unit symbol shall be treated as if it were
a single-character unit symbol. Unit symbols should not contain multipliers,
and it should be left to the multiplier to define the multiple for an entire
data type.
For example, if a unit symbol is "m2Pers" and the multiplier is "k", then
the value is k(m**2/s), and the multiplier applies to the entire final
value, not to any individual part of the value. This can be conceptualized
by substituting a derived unit symbol for the unit type. If one imagines
that the symbol "Þ" represents the derived unit "m2Pers", then applying
the multiplier "k" can be conceptualized simply as "kÞ".
For example, the SI unit for mass is "kg" and not "g". If the unit symbol
is defined as "kg", then the multiplier is applied to "kg" as a whole and
does not replace the "k" in front of the "g". In this case, the multiplier
of "m" would be used with the unit symbol of "kg" to represent one gram.
As a text string, this violates the instructions in IEC 80000-1. However,
because the unit symbol in CIM is treated as a derived unit instead of
as an SI unit, it makes more sense to conceptualize the "kg" as if it were
replaced by one of the proposed replacements for the SI mass symbol. If
one imagines that the "kg" were replaced by a symbol "Þ", then it is easier
to conceptualize the multiplier "m" as creating the proper unit "mÞ", and
not the forbidden unit "mkg".

The derived units defined for usage in the CIM. In some cases, the derived
unit is equal to an SI unit. Whenever possible, the standard derived symbol
is used instead of the formula for the derived unit. For example, the unit
symbol Farad is defined as "F" instead of "CPerV". In cases where a standard
symbol does not exist for a derived unit, the formula for the unit is used
as the unit symbol. For example, density does not have a standard symbol
and so it is represented as "kgPerm3". With the exception of the "kg",
which is an SI unit, the unit symbols do not contain multipliers and therefore
represent the base derived unit to which a multiplier can be applied as
a whole.
Every unit symbol is treated as an unparseable text as if it were a single-letter
symbol. The meaning of each unit symbol is defined by the accompanying
descriptive text and not by the text contents of the unit symbol.
To allow the widest possible range of serializations without requiring
special character handling, several substitutions are made which deviate
from the format described in IEC 80000-1. The division symbol "/" is replaced
by the letters "Per". Exponents are written in plain text after the unit
as "m3" instead of being formatted as "m" with a superscript of 3 or introducing
a symbol as in "m^3". The degree symbol "°" is replaced with the letters
"deg". Any clarification of the meaning for a substitution is included
in the description for the unit symbol.
Non-SI units are included in list of unit symbols to allow sources of data
to be correctly labelled with their non-SI units (for example, a GPS sensor
that is reporting numbers that represent feet instead of meters). This
allows software to use the unit symbol information correctly convert and
scale the raw data of those sources into SI-based units.
The integer values are used for harmonization with IEC 61850.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Validity

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Validity.GOOD
documentation:
The value is marked good if no abnormal condition of the acquisition function
or the information source is detected.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Validity.INVALID
documentation:
The value is marked invalid when a supervision function recognises abnormal
conditions of the acquisition function or the information source (missing
or non-operating updating devices). The value is not defined under this
condition. The mark invalid is used to indicate to the client that the
value may be incorrect and shall not be used.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.Validity.QUESTIONABLE
documentation:
The value is marked questionable if a supervision function detects an abnormal
behaviour, however the value could still be valid. The client is responsible
for determining whether or not values marked "questionable" should be used.
Validity for MeasurementValue.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsPpccControlKind

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsPpccControlKind.pPcc
documentation:
Control is real power at point of common coupling. The target value is
provided by ACDCConverter.targetPpcc.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsPpccControlKind.pPccAndUdcDroop
documentation:
Control is active power at point of common coupling and local DC voltage,
with the droop. Target values are provided by ACDCConverter.targetPpcc,
ACDCConverter.targetUdc and VsConverter.droop.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsPpccControlKind.pPccAndUdcDroopPilot
documentation:
Control is active power at point of common coupling and the pilot DC voltage,
with the droop. The mode is used for Multi Terminal High Voltage DC (MTDC)
systems where multiple HVDC Substations are connected to the HVDC transmission
lines. The pilot voltage is then used to coordinate the control the DC
voltage across the HVDC substations. Targets are provided by ACDCConverter.targetPpcc,
ACDCConverter.targetUdc and VsConverter.droop.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsPpccControlKind.pPccAndUdcDroopWithCompensation
documentation:
Control is active power at point of common coupling and compensated DC
voltage, with the droop. Compensation factor is the resistance, as an approximation
of the DC voltage of a common (real or virtual) node in the DC network.
Targets are provided by ACDCConverter.targetPpcc, ACDCConverter.targetUdc,
VsConverter.droop and VsConverter.droopCompensation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsPpccControlKind.phasePcc
documentation: Control is phase at point of common coupling. Target is provided by VsConverter.targetPhasePcc.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsPpccControlKind.udc
documentation: Control is DC voltage with target value provided by ACDCConverter.targetUdc.

Types applicable to the control of real power and/or DC voltage by voltage
source converter.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsQpccControlKind

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsQpccControlKind.powerFactorPcc
documentation:
Control is power factor at point of common coupling. Target is provided
by VsConverter.targetPowerFactorPcc.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsQpccControlKind.pulseWidthModulation
documentation:
No explicit control. Pulse-modulation factor is directly set in magnitude
(VsConverter.targetPWMfactor) and phase (VsConverter.targetPhasePcc).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsQpccControlKind.reactivePcc
documentation:
Control is reactive power at point of common coupling. Target is provided
by VsConverter.targetQpcc.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cim17v40.cim17v40.VsQpccControlKind.voltagePcc
documentation: Control is voltage at point of common coupling. Target is provided by VsConverter.targetUpcc.

Kind of reactive power control at point of common coupling for a voltage
source converter.

classes.name: cimgraph.databases.blazegraph.blazegraph.BlazegraphConnection

methods.name: cimgraph.databases.blazegraph.blazegraph.BlazegraphConnection.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a new BlazegraphConnection.

Args:
    connection_params (ConnectionInterface): The connection parameters containing CIM profile, namespace, etc.

methods.name: cimgraph.databases.blazegraph.blazegraph.BlazegraphConnection.connect
documentation: Establish a connection to the Blazegraph endpoint.

methods.name: cimgraph.databases.blazegraph.blazegraph.BlazegraphConnection.disconnect
documentation: Disconnect from the Blazegraph endpoint.

methods.name: cimgraph.databases.blazegraph.blazegraph.BlazegraphConnection.execute
documentation:
Execute a given SPARQL query on the Blazegraph endpoint.

Args:
    query_message (str): The SPARQL query to be executed.

Returns:
    query_output (QueryResponse): The response from the query execution.

methods.name: cimgraph.databases.blazegraph.blazegraph.BlazegraphConnection.update
documentation:
Execute SPARQL update on the Blazegraph endpoint.

Args:
    update_message (str): The SPARQL query to be executed.

Returns:
    output (str): The response from the update statement.

methods.name: cimgraph.databases.blazegraph.blazegraph.BlazegraphConnection.get_object
documentation:
Retrieve an object from the Blazegraph database using its mRID.

Args:
    mrid (str): The mRID of the object to be retrieved.
    graph (dict, optional): The graph database to store the fetched object. Defaults to {}.

Returns:
    object: The retrieved object.

methods.name: cimgraph.databases.blazegraph.blazegraph.BlazegraphConnection.create_new_graph
documentation:
Create a new graph structure for a CIM EquipmentContainer object.
The method uses a SPARQL query to obtain all terminals in the graph,
along with all nodes and conducting equipment associated with each
terminal. This forms the baseline knowledge graph for the GraphModel.
If a graph is specified, the new objects will be added to the existing
graph. Otherwise, a new graph will be created from scratch.

Args:
    container (object): The container object for which the graph is created.
    graph (dict, optional): Graph of CIM objects, grouped by class and UUID.

Returns:
    dict: Graph consisting of types and UUID mapped to object instances.

methods.name: cimgraph.databases.blazegraph.blazegraph.BlazegraphConnection.parse_node_query
documentation:
Parse the results of a node query to update a graph structure.

Args:
    graph (dict): Graph to be updated.
    query_output (dict): Query output to be parsed.

Returns:
    dict: Updated graph structure.

modules.name: cimgraph.databases.gridappsd
documentation:
This module provides functionality to interact with the GridAPPSD platform.

It attempts to import the GridAPPSD package and sets an environment variable
'CIMGRAPH_HAS_GRIDAPPSD' to '1' and HAS_GRIDAPPSD flag set to True, if the import is
successful. If the import fails, it sets a flag 'HAS_GRIDAPPSD' to False.

The module also provides a function 'get_topology_response' which sends a
request to the GridAPPSD platform to get the topology of a specific feeder.

Functions:
----------
get_topology_response(feeder_mrid: str, gapps: GridAPPSD) -> Dict:
    Sends a request to the GridAPPSD platform to get the topology of a specific feeder.

    Parameters:
    -----------
    feeder_mrid : str
        The mRID of the feeder for which the topology is requested.
    gapps : GridAPPSD
        An instance of the GridAPPSD class.

    Returns:
    --------
    dict
        A dictionary containing the response from the GridAPPSD platform.

methods.name: cimgraph.databases.gridappsd.gridappsd.GridappsdConnection.create_new_graph
documentation:
Create a new graph structure for a CIM EquipmentContainer object.
The method uses a SPARQL query to obtain all terminals in the graph,
along with all nodes and conducting equipment associated with each
terminal. This forms the baseline knowledge graph for the GraphModel.
If a graph is specified, the new objects will be added to the existing
graph. Otherwise, a new graph will be created from scratch.

Args:
    container (object): The container object for which the graph is created.
    graph (dict, optional): Graph of CIM objects, grouped by class and UUID.

Returns:
    dict: Graph consisting of types and UUID mapped to object instances.

methods.name: cimgraph.databases.fileparsers.xml_parser.XMLFile.parse_nodes
documentation: async def create_new_graph_async(self):
for f in asyncio.as_completed([
asyncio.to_thread(self.parse_nodes(element))
for element in self.root]):
await f
for f in asyncio.as_completed([
asyncio.to_thread(self.parse_edges(element))
for element in self.root]):
await f
@time_func

def jsonld_to_obj(cim:cim, json_ld:jsonld|str[jsonld]) -> object:
if type(json_ld) == str:
json_ld = json.loads(json_ld)
elif type(json_ld) == dict:
pass
else:
raise TypeError('json_ld input must be string or dict')
