classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.IdentifiedObject

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.IdentifiedObject.mRID
documentation:
Master resource identifier issued by a model authority. The mRID is unique
within an exchange context. Global uniqueness is easily achieved by using
a UUID, as specified in RFC 4122, for the mRID. The use of UUID is strongly
recommended.
For CIMXML data files in RDF syntax conforming to IEC 61970-552 Edition
1, the mRID is mapped to rdf:ID or rdf:about attributes that identify CIM
object elements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.IdentifiedObject.aliasName
documentation:
The aliasName is free text human readable name of the object alternative
to IdentifiedObject.name. It may be non unique and may not correlate to
a naming hierarchy.
The attribute aliasName is retained because of backwards compatibility
between CIM relases. It is however recommended to replace aliasName with
the Name class as aliasName is planned for retirement at a future time.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.IdentifiedObject.description
documentation:
The description is a free human readable text describing or naming the
object. It may be non unique and may not correlate to a naming hierarchy.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.IdentifiedObject.name
documentation:
The name is any free human readable and possibly non unique text naming
the object.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.IdentifiedObject.Names
documentation: All names of this identified object.

This is a root class to provide common identification for all classes needing
identification and naming attributes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACDCTerminal

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACDCTerminal.connected
documentation:
The connected status is related to a bus-branch model and the topological
node to terminal relation. True implies the terminal is connected to the
related topological node and false implies it is not.
In a bus-branch model, the connected status is used to tell if equipment
is disconnected without having to change the connectivity described by
the topological node to terminal relation. A valid case is that conducting
equipment can be connected in one end and open in the other. In particular
for an AC line segment, where the reactive line charging can be significant,
this is a relevant case.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACDCTerminal.sequenceNumber
documentation:
The orientation of the terminal connections for a multiple terminal conducting
equipment. The sequence numbering starts with 1 and additional terminals
should follow in increasing order. The first terminal is the "starting
point" for a two terminal branch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACDCTerminal.BusNameMarker
documentation: The bus name marker used to name the bus (topological node).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACDCTerminal.Measurements
documentation:
Measurements associated with this terminal defining where the measurement
is placed in the network topology. It may be used, for instance, to capture
the sensor position, such as a voltage transformer (PT) at a busbar or
a current transformer (CT) at the bar between a breaker and an isolator.

An electrical connection point (AC or DC) to a piece of conducting equipment.
Terminals are connected at physical connection points called connectivity
nodes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Terminal

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Terminal.BoundedSchedulingArea
documentation: The SubSchedulingArea bounded by the specific Terminal

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Terminal.ConductingEquipment
documentation:
The conducting equipment of the terminal. Conducting equipment have terminals
that may be connected to other conducting equipment terminals via connectivity
nodes or topological nodes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Terminal.ConnectivityNode
documentation: The connectivity node to which this terminal connects with zero impedance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Terminal.HasFirstMutualCoupling
documentation: Mutual couplings associated with the branch as the first branch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Terminal.HasSecondMutualCoupling
documentation: Mutual couplings with the branch associated as the first branch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Terminal.NormalHeadFeeder
documentation:
The feeder that this terminal normally feeds. Only specifed for the terminals
at head of feeders.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Terminal.RegulatingControl
documentation: The controls regulating this terminal.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Terminal.TopologicalNode
documentation:
The topological node associated with the terminal. This can be used as
an alternative to the connectivity node path to topological node, thus
making it unneccesary to model connectivity nodes in some cases. Note that
the if connectivity nodes are in the model, this association would probably
not be used as an input specification.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Terminal.TransformerEnd
documentation: All transformer ends connected at this terminal.

An AC electrical connection point to a piece of conducting equipment. Terminals
are connected at physical connection points called connectivity nodes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Asset

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Asset.AssetContainer
documentation: Container of this asset.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Asset.AssetInfo
documentation: Data applicable to this asset.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Asset.Location
documentation: Location of this asset.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Asset.OrganisationRoles
documentation: All roles an organisation plays for this asset.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Asset.PowerSystemResources
documentation:
All power system resources used to electrically model this asset. For example,
transformer asset is electrically modelled with a transformer and its windings
and tap changer.

Tangible resource of the utility, including power system equipment, various
end devices, cabinets, buildings, etc. For electrical network equipment,
the role of the asset is defined through PowerSystemResource and its subclasses,
defined mainly in the Wires model (refer to IEC61970-301 and model package
IEC61970::Wires). Asset description places emphasis on the physical characteristics
of the equipment fulfilling that role.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AssetContainer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AssetContainer.Assets
documentation: All assets within this container asset.

Asset that is aggregation of other assets such as conductors, transformers,
switchgear, land, fences, buildings, equipment, vehicles, etc.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DuctBank

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DuctBank.circuitCount
documentation:
Number of circuits in duct bank. Refer to associations between a duct (ConductorAsset)
and an ACLineSegment to understand which circuits are in which ducts.

A duct contains individual wires in the layout as specified with associated
wire spacing instances; number of them gives the number of conductors in
this duct.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EndDevice

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EndDevice.amrSystem
documentation:
Automated meter reading (AMR) or other communication system responsible
for communications to this end device.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EndDevice.installCode
documentation: Installation code.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EndDevice.isPan
documentation: If true, this is a premises area network (PAN) device.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EndDevice.isVirtual
documentation:
If true, there is no physical device. As an example, a virtual meter can
be defined to aggregate the consumption for two or more physical meters.
Otherwise, this is a physical hardware device.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EndDevice.timeZoneOffset
documentation: Time zone offset relative to GMT for the location of this end device.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EndDevice.Customer
documentation: Customer owning this end device.

Asset container that performs one or more end device functions. One type
of end device is a meter which can perform metering, load management, connect/disconnect,
accounting functions, etc. Some end devices, such as ones monitoring and
controlling air conditioners, refrigerators, pool pumps may be connected
to a meter. All end devices may have communication capability defined by
the associated communication function(s). An end device may be owned by
a consumer, a service provider, utility or otherwise.
There may be a related end device function that identifies a sensor or
control point within a metering application or communications systems (e.g.,
water, gas, electricity).
Some devices may use an optical port that conforms to the ANSI C12.18 standard
for communications.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Structure
documentation:
Construction holding assets such as conductors, transformers, switchgear,
etc. Where applicable, number of conductors can be derived from the number
of associated wire spacing instances.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Bushing

- as attributes of an asset instance (installed or in stock)
- as attributes of an asset model (product by a manufacturer)
- as attributes of a type asset (generic type of an asset as used in designs/extension
planning).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.endNumber
documentation:
Number for this transformer end, corresponding to the end's order in the
PowerTransformer.vectorGroup attribute. Highest voltage winding should
be 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.phaseAngleClock
documentation:
Winding phase angle where 360 degrees are represented with clock hours,
so the valid values are {0, ..., 11}. For example, to express the second
winding in code 'Dyn11', set attributes as follows: 'endNumber'=2, 'connectionKind'
= Yn and 'phaseAngleClock' = 11.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.emergencyS
documentation:
Apparent power that the winding can carry under emergency conditions (also
called long-term emergency power).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.insulationU
documentation: Basic insulation level voltage rating.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.r
documentation: DC resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.ratedS
documentation: Normal apparent power rating.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.ratedU
documentation:
Rated voltage: phase-phase for three-phase windings, and either phase-phase
or phase-neutral for single-phase windings.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.shortTermS
documentation:
Apparent power that this winding can carry for a short period of time (in
emergency).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.connectionKind
documentation: Kind of connection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.CoreAdmittance
documentation:
Core admittance calculated from this transformer end datasheet, representing
magnetising current and core losses. The full values of the transformer
should be supplied for one transformer end info only.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.EnergisedEndNoLoadTests
documentation: All no-load test measurements in which this transformer end was energised.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.EnergisedEndOpenCircuitTests
documentation: All open-circuit test measurements in which this transformer end was excited.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.EnergisedEndShortCircuitTests
documentation: All short-circuit test measurements in which this transformer end was energised.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.FromMeshImpedances
documentation: All mesh impedances between this 'to' and other 'from' transformer ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.GroundedEndShortCircuitTests
documentation: All short-circuit test measurements in which this transformer end was short-circuited.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.OpenEndOpenCircuitTests
documentation:
All open-circuit test measurements in which this transformer end was not
excited.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.ToMeshImpedances
documentation: All mesh impedances between this 'from' and other 'to' transformer ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.TransformerStarImpedance
documentation: Transformer star impedance calculated from this transformer end datasheet.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEndInfo.TransformerTankInfo
documentation: Transformer tank data that this end description is part of.
Transformer end data.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerTankInfo.PowerTransformerInfo

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerTankInfo.TransformerEndInfos
documentation: Data for all the ends described by this transformer tank data.
Set of transformer tank data, from an equipment library.

A WireAssemblyInfo is used to compute the PerLengthParameter data in the
Wires package

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireInfo

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireInfo.coreStrandCount
documentation: (if used) Number of strands in the steel core.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireInfo.insulated
documentation: True if conductor is insulated.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireInfo.sizeDescription
documentation: Describes the wire gauge or cross section (e.g., 4/0, #2, 336.5).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireInfo.strandCount
documentation: Number of strands in the conductor.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireInfo.coreRadius
documentation: (if there is a different core material) Radius of the central core.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireInfo.gmr
documentation:
Geometric mean radius. If we replace the conductor by a thin walled tube
of radius GMR, then its reactance is identical to the reactance of the
actual conductor.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireInfo.insulationMaterial
documentation: (if insulated conductor) Material used for insulation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireInfo.insulationThickness
documentation: (if insulated conductor) Thickness of the insulation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireInfo.material
documentation: Conductor material.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireInfo.rAC25
documentation: AC resistance per unit length of the conductor at 25 °C.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireInfo.rAC50
documentation: AC resistance per unit length of the conductor at 50 °C.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireInfo.rAC75
documentation: AC resistance per unit length of the conductor at 75 °C.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireInfo.radius
documentation: Outside radius of the wire.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireInfo.ratedCurrent
documentation: Current carrying capacity of the wire under stated thermal conditions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireInfo.rDC20
documentation: DC resistance per unit length of the conductor at 20 °C.

Wire data that can be specified per line segment phase, or for the line
segment as a whole in case its phases all have the same wire characteristics.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CableInfo.diameterOverScreen

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CableInfo.outerJacketKind

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CableInfo.shieldMaterial
documentation: Material of the shield.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ConcentricNeutralCableInfo.neutralStrandRDC20

Concentric neutral cable data.

Percentage of the tape shield width that overlaps in each wrap, typically
10% to 25%.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapeShieldCableInfo.tapeThickness

documentation: If true, this spacing data describes a cable.

documentation:
Number of wire sub-conductors in the symmetrical bundle (typically between
1 and 4).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireSpacingInfo.phaseWireSpacing
documentation: Distance between wire sub-conductors in a symmetrical bundle.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireSpacingInfo.usage
documentation: Usage of the associated wires.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WireSpacingInfo.WirePositions
documentation: All positions of single wires (phase or neutral) making the conductor.

Wire spacing data that associates multiple wire positions with the line
segment, and allows to calculate line segment impedances. Number of phases
can be derived from the number of associated wire positions whose phase
is not neutral.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BaseFrequency

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BaseFrequency.frequency
documentation: The base frequency.

The class describe a base frequency for a power system network. In case
of multiple power networks with different frequencies, e.g. 50 or 60 Hertz
each network will have it's own base frequency class. Hence it is assumed
that power system objects having different base frequencies appear in separate
documents where each document has a single base frequency instance.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BasePower

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BasePower.basePower
documentation: Value used as base power.
The BasePower class defines the base power used in the per unit calculations.

documentation: The power system resource's base voltage.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BaseVoltage.ConductingEquipment
documentation:
All conducting equipment with this base voltage. Use only when there is
no voltage level container used and only one base voltage applies. For
example, not used for transformers.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BaseVoltage.TopologicalNode

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BaseVoltage.TransformerEnds

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BaseVoltage.VoltageLevel
documentation: The voltage levels having this base voltage.
Defines a system base voltage which is referenced.

The time for the first time point. The value can be a time of day, not
a specific date.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BasicIntervalSchedule.value1Multiplier

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BasicIntervalSchedule.value1Unit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BasicIntervalSchedule.value2Multiplier

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BasicIntervalSchedule.value2Unit

Schedule of values at points in time.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.IrregularIntervalSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.IrregularIntervalSchedule.TimePoints

The schedule has time points where the time between them varies.

The time for the last time point. The value can be a time of day, not a
specific date.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegularIntervalSchedule.timeStep
documentation:
The time between each pair of subsequent regular time points in sequence
order.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegularIntervalSchedule.TimePoints
documentation: The regular interval time point data values that define this schedule.
The schedule has time points where the time between them is constant.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.GenUnitOpSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.GenUnitOpSchedule.GeneratingUnit
documentation:
A generating unit may have an operating schedule, indicating the planned
operation of the unit.

The generating unit's Operator-approved current operating schedule (or
plan), typically produced with the aid of unit commitment type analyses.
The X-axis represents absolute time. The Y1-axis represents the status
(0=off-line and unavailable: 1=available: 2=must run: 3=must run at fixed
power value: etc.). The Y2-axis represents the must run fixed power value
where required.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SeasonDayTypeSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SeasonDayTypeSchedule.DayType
documentation: DayType for the Schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SeasonDayTypeSchedule.Season
documentation: Season for the Schedule.

A time schedule covering a 24 hour period, with curve data for a specific
type of season and day.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ConformLoadSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ConformLoadSchedule.ConformLoadGroup
documentation: The ConformLoadGroup where the ConformLoadSchedule belongs.

A curve of load versus time (X-axis) showing the active power values (Y1-axis)
and reactive power (Y2-axis) for each unit of the period covered. This
curve represents a typical pattern of load over the time period for a given
day type and season.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NonConformLoadSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NonConformLoadSchedule.NonConformLoadGroup
documentation: The NonConformLoadGroup where the NonConformLoadSchedule belongs.

An active power (Y1-axis) and reactive power (Y2-axis) schedule (curves)
versus time (X-axis) for non-conforming loads, e.g., large industrial load
or power station service (where modeled).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegulationSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegulationSchedule.RegulatingControl
documentation: Regulating controls that have this Schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegulationSchedule.VoltageControlZones
documentation: A VoltageControlZone may have a voltage regulation schedule.

A pre-established pattern over time for a controlled variable, e.g., busbar
voltage.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SwitchSchedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SwitchSchedule.Switch

A schedule of switch positions. If RegularTimePoint.value1 is 0, the switch
is open. If 1, the switch is closed.

A pre-established pattern over time for a tap step.

documentation: Monitor the active power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BranchGroup.monitorReactivePower
documentation: Monitor the reactive power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BranchGroup.maximumActivePower
documentation: The maximum active power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BranchGroup.maximumReactivePower
documentation: The maximum reactive power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BranchGroup.minimumActivePower
documentation: The minimum active power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BranchGroup.minimumReactivePower

A group of branch terminals whose directed flow summation is to be monitored.
A branch group need not form a cutset of the network.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BusNameMarker

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BusNameMarker.priority
documentation:
Priority of bus name marker for use as topology bus name. Use 0 for don
t care. Use 1 for highest priority. Use 2 as priority is less than 1 and
so on.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BusNameMarker.ReportingGroup
documentation: The reporting group to which this bus name marker belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BusNameMarker.Terminal
documentation: The terminals associated with this bus name marker.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BusNameMarker.TopologicalNode
documentation:
A user defined topological node that was originally defined in a planning
model not yet having topology described by ConnectivityNodes. Once ConnectivityNodes
has been created they may linked to user defined ToplogicalNdes using BusNameMarkers.

Used to apply user standard names to topology buses. Typically used for
"bus/branch" case generation. Associated with one or more terminals that
are normally connected with the bus name. The associated terminals are
normally connected by non-retained switches. For a ring bus station configuration,
all busbar terminals in the ring are typically associated. For a breaker
and a half scheme, both busbars would normally be associated. For a ring
bus, all busbars would normally be associated. For a "straight" busbar
configuration, normally only the main terminal at the busbar would be associated.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CatalogAssetType

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CatalogAssetType.stockItem
documentation: True if item is a stock item (default).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CatalogAssetType.estimatedUnitCost
documentation:
Estimated unit cost (or cost per unit length) of this type of asset. It
does not include labor to install, construct or configure it.
assets that may be used for planning, work or design purposes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ConnectivityNode

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ConnectivityNode.ConnectivityNodeContainer
documentation: Container of this connectivity node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ConnectivityNode.Terminals
documentation: Terminals interconnected with zero impedance at a this connectivity node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ConnectivityNode.TopologicalNode
documentation:
The topological node to which this connectivity node is assigned. May depend
on the current state of switches in the network.

Connectivity nodes are points where terminals of AC conducting equipment
are connected together with zero impedance.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CoordinateSystem

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CoordinateSystem.crsUrn
documentation:
A Uniform Resource Name (URN) for the coordinate reference system (crs)
used to define 'Location.PositionPoints'.
An example would be the European Petroleum Survey Group (EPSG) code for
a coordinate reference system, defined in URN under the Open Geospatial
Consortium (OGC) namespace as: urn:ogc:def:uom:EPSG::XXXX, where XXXX is
an EPSG code (a full list of codes can be found at the EPSG Registry web
site http://www.epsg-registry.org/). To define the coordinate system as
being WGS84 (latitude, longitude) using an EPSG OGC, this attribute would
be urn:ogc:def:uom:EPSG::4236.
A profile should limit this code to a set of allowed URNs agreed to by
all sending and receiving parties.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CoordinateSystem.Locations
documentation: All locations described with position points in this coordinate system.
Coordinate reference system.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Curve.xMultiplier
documentation: Multiplier for X-axis.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Curve.xUnit

documentation: Multiplier for Y1-axis.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Curve.y1Unit
documentation: The Y1-axis units of measure.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Curve.y2Multiplier
documentation: Multiplier for Y2-axis.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Curve.y2Unit
documentation: The Y2-axis units of measure.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Curve.y3Multiplier
documentation: Multiplier for Y3-axis.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Curve.y3Unit
documentation: The Y3-axis units of measure.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Curve.CurveDatas
documentation: The point data values that define this curve.

A multi-purpose curve or functional relationship between an independent
variable (X-axis) and dependent (Y-axis) variables.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.GrossToNetActivePowerCurve

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.GrossToNetActivePowerCurve.GeneratingUnit
documentation:
A generating unit may have a gross active power to net active power curve,
describing the losses and auxiliary power requirements of the unit.

Relationship between the generating unit's gross active power output on
the X-axis (measured at the terminals of the machine(s)) and the generating
unit's net active power output on the Y-axis (based on utility-defined
measurements at the power station). Station service loads, when modeled,
should be treated as non-conforming bus loads. There may be more than one
curve, depending on the auxiliary equipment that is in service.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ReactiveCapabilityCurve

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ReactiveCapabilityCurve.coolantTemperature
documentation:
The machine's coolant temperature (e.g., ambient air or stator circulating
water).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ReactiveCapabilityCurve.hydrogenPressure
documentation: The hydrogen coolant pressure

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ReactiveCapabilityCurve.SynchronousMachines
documentation: Synchronous machines using this curve.

Reactive power rating envelope versus the synchronous machine's active
power, in both the generating and motoring modes. For each active power
value there is a corresponding high and low reactive power limit value.
Typically there will be a separate curve for each coolant condition, such
as hydrogen pressure. The Y1 axis values represent reactive minimum and
the Y2 axis values represent reactive maximum.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Customer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Customer.locale
documentation: Locale designating language to use in communications with this customer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Customer.pucNumber
documentation: (if applicable) Public utilities commission (PUC) identification number.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Customer.specialNeed
documentation:
True if customer organisation has special service needs such as life support,
hospitals, etc.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Customer.vip
documentation:
(use 'priority' instead) True if this is an important customer. Importance
is for matters different than those in 'specialNeed' attribute.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Customer.kind
documentation: Kind of customer.
Organisation receiving services from service supplier.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CustomerAgreement

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CustomerAgreement.isPrePay
documentation: If true, the customer is a pre-pay customer for the specified service.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CustomerAgreement.loadMgmt
documentation: Load management code.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CustomerAgreement.shutOffDateTime
documentation: Final date and time the service will be billed to the previous customer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CustomerAgreement.Customer
documentation: Customer for this agreement.

Agreement between the customer and the service supplier to pay for service
at a specific service location. It records certain billing information
about the type of service provided at the service location and is used
during charge creation to determine the type of service.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DayType
documentation:
Group of similar days. For example it could be used to represent weekdays,
weekend, or holidays.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DemandResponseProgram

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DemandResponseProgram.type
documentation:
Type of demand response program; examples are CPP (critical-peak pricing),
RTP (real-time pricing), DLC (direct load control), DBP (demand bidding
program), BIP (base interruptible program). Note that possible types change
a lot and it would be impossible to enumerate them all.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DemandResponseProgram.CustomerAgreements
documentation:
All customer agreements through which the customer is enrolled in this
demand response program.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DemandResponseProgram.EndDeviceGroups
documentation: All groups of end devices enrolled in this demand response program.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DemandResponseProgram.UsagePointGroups
documentation: All usage point groups enrolled in this demand response program.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DemandResponseProgram.validityInterval
documentation: Interval within which the program is valid.
Demand response program.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EndDeviceControl
documentation:
Instructs an end device (or an end device group) to perform a specified
action.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EndDeviceGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EndDeviceGroup.type
documentation: Type of this group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EndDeviceGroup.DemandResponsePrograms
documentation: All demand response programs this group of end devices is enrolled in.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EndDeviceGroup.EndDeviceControls
documentation: All end device controls sending commands to this end device group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EndDeviceGroup.EndDevices
documentation: All end devices this end device group refers to.

Abstraction for management of group communications within a two-way AMR
system or the data for a group of related end devices. Commands can be
issued to all of the end devices that belong to the group using a defined
group address and the underlying AMR communication infrastructure. A DERGroup
and a PANDeviceGroup is an EndDeviceGroup.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyArea
documentation:
Describes an area having energy production or consumption. Specializations
are intended to support the load allocation function as typically required
in energy management systems or planning studies to allocate hypothesized
load levels to individual load points for power flow analysis. Often the
energy area can be linked to both measured and forecast load levels.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LoadArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LoadArea.SubLoadAreas
documentation: The SubLoadAreas in the LoadArea.

The class is the root or first level in a hierarchical structure for grouping
of loads for the purpose of load flow load scaling.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SubLoadArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SubLoadArea.LoadArea
documentation: The LoadArea where the SubLoadArea belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SubLoadArea.LoadGroups
documentation: The Loadgroups in the SubLoadArea.

The class is the second level in a hierarchical structure for grouping
of loads for the purpose of load flow load scaling.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConnectionProfile

and WindGen power

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConnectionProfile.dssDuty
documentation:
Reference to OpenDSS Duty Cycle curve, for Load, Storage, PVSystem, Generator,
and WindGen power

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConnectionProfile.dssLoadCvrCurve

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConnectionProfile.dssLoadGrowth

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConnectionProfile.dssPVTDaily

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConnectionProfile.dssPVTDuty
documentation: Reference to OpenDSS Duty Cycle curve, for PVSystem temperature

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConnectionProfile.dssPVTYearly
documentation: Reference to OpenDSS Yearly curve, for PVSystem temperature

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConnectionProfile.dssSpectrum
documentation:
Reference to OpenDSS harmonic current Spectrum, for Load, Storage, PVSystem,
Generator, and WindGen power

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConnectionProfile.dssYearly
documentation:
Reference to OpenDSS Yearly curve, for Load, Storage, PVSystem, Generator,
and WindGen power

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConnectionProfile.gldPlayer
documentation:
GridLAB-D Player for base_power attributes on Load and Triplex_Load objects,
and P_Out for Battery objects. Netlisted as player.value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConnectionProfile.gldSchedule
documentation:
GridLAB-D schedule for base_power attributes on Load and Triplex_Load objects,
and P_Out attributes on Battery objects.

Optional references to shapes for OpenDSS, and players or schedules for
GridLAB-D. See attribute documentation for applicability. The shapes, players,
and schedules are not maintained in CIM, i.e., they must be made available
to the simulator from an external source.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.GeographicalRegion

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.GeographicalRegion.Regions
documentation: All sub-geograhpical regions within this geographical region.
A geographical region of a power system network model.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.House
documentation:
In GridLAB-D, a single-family residence with building envelope represented
by the equivalent thermal parameter (ETP) model, heating, ventilating and
air conditioning (HVAC), other appliances, lights and plug loads. In power
flow, these house loads aggregate into ZIP loads. These house parameters
are the minimal set required to consistently initialize or repeat a GridLAB-D
simulation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.IOPoint
documentation:
The class describe a measurement or control value. The purpose is to enable
having attributes and associations common for measurement and control.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Control

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Control.controlType
documentation:
Specifies the type of Control, e.g. BreakerOn/Off, GeneratorVoltageSetPoint,
TieLineFlow etc. The ControlType.name shall be unique among all specified
types and describe the type.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Control.operationInProgress
documentation:
Indicates that a client is currently sending control commands that has
not completed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Control.timeStamp
documentation: The last time a control output was sent.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Control.unitMultiplier
documentation: The unit multiplier of the controlled quantity.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Control.unitSymbol
documentation: The unit of measure of the controlled quantity.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Control.PowerSystemResource
documentation: Regulating device governed by this control output.

Control is used for supervisory/device control. It represents control outputs
that are used to change the state in a process, e.g. close or open breaker,
a set point value or a raise lower command.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AccumulatorReset

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AccumulatorReset.AccumulatorValue
documentation: The accumulator value that is reset by the command.
This command reset the counter value to zero.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AnalogControl

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AnalogControl.maxValue
documentation:
Normal value range maximum for any of the Control.value. Used for scaling,
e.g. in bar graphs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AnalogControl.minValue
documentation:
Normal value range minimum for any of the Control.value. Used for scaling,
e.g. in bar graphs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AnalogControl.AnalogValue
documentation: The MeasurementValue that is controlled.
An analog control used for supervisory control.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RaiseLowerCommand

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RaiseLowerCommand.ValueAliasSet
documentation: The ValueAliasSet used for translation of a Control value to a name.

An analog control that increase or decrease a set point value with pulses.
Unless otherwise specified, one pulse moves the set point by one.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SetPoint.normalValue
documentation: Normal value for Control.value e.g. used for percentage scaling.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SetPoint.value

An analog control that issue a set point value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Command.normalValue

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Command.value
documentation: The value representing the actuator output.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Command.DiscreteValue
documentation: The MeasurementValue that is controlled.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Command.ValueAliasSet
documentation: The ValueAliasSet used for translation of a Control value to a name.
A Command is a discrete control used for supervisory control.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MeasurementValue

documentation: The time when the value was last updated

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MeasurementValue.sensorAccuracy
documentation:
The limit, expressed as a percentage of the sensor maximum, that errors
will not exceed when the sensor is used under reference conditions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MeasurementValue.MeasurementValueQuality
documentation: A MeasurementValue has a MeasurementValueQuality associated with it.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MeasurementValue.MeasurementValueSource
documentation:
A reference to the type of source that updates the MeasurementValue, e.g.
SCADA, CCLink, manual, etc. User conventions for the names of sources are
contained in the introduction to IEC 61970-301.

The current state for a measurement. A state value is an instance of a
measurement from a specific source. Measurements can be associated with
many state values, each representing a different source for the measurement.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AccumulatorValue

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AccumulatorValue.value
documentation: The value to supervise. The value is positive.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AccumulatorValue.Accumulator
documentation: Measurement to which this value is connected.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AccumulatorValue.AccumulatorReset
documentation: The command that reset the accumulator value.
AccumulatorValue represents an accumulated (counted) MeasurementValue.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AnalogValue

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AnalogValue.value
documentation: The value to supervise.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AnalogValue.Analog
documentation: Measurement to which this value is connected.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AnalogValue.AnalogControl
documentation: The Control variable associated with the MeasurementValue.
AnalogValue represents an analog MeasurementValue.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DiscreteValue

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DiscreteValue.value
documentation: The value to supervise.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DiscreteValue.Command
documentation: The Control variable associated with the MeasurementValue.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DiscreteValue.Discrete
documentation: Measurement to which this value is connected.
DiscreteValue represents a discrete MeasurementValue.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.StringMeasurementValue

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.StringMeasurementValue.value
documentation: The value to supervise.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.StringMeasurementValue.StringMeasurement
documentation: Measurement to which this value is connected.
StringMeasurementValue represents a measurement value of type string.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Limit
documentation:
Specifies one limit value for a Measurement. A Measurement typically has
several limits that are kept together by the LimitSet class. The actual
meaning and use of a Limit instance (i.e., if it is an alarm or warning
limit or if it is a high or low limit) is not captured in the Limit class.
However the name of a Limit instance may indicate both meaning and use.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AccumulatorLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AccumulatorLimit.value
documentation: The value to supervise against. The value is positive.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AccumulatorLimit.LimitSet
documentation: The set of limits.
Limit values for Accumulator measurements.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AnalogLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AnalogLimit.value
documentation: The value to supervise against.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AnalogLimit.LimitSet
documentation: The set of limits.
Limit values for Analog measurements.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LimitSet

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LimitSet.isPercentageLimits
documentation:
Tells if the limit values are in percentage of normalValue or the specified
Unit for Measurements and Controls.

Specifies a set of Limits that are associated with a Measurement. A Measurement
may have several LimitSets corresponding to seasonal or other changing
conditions. The condition is captured in the name and description attributes.
The same LimitSet may be used for several Measurements. In particular percentage
limits are used this way.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AccumulatorLimitSet

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AccumulatorLimitSet.Limits
documentation: The limit values used for supervision of Measurements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AccumulatorLimitSet.Measurements
documentation: The Measurements using the LimitSet.

An AccumulatorLimitSet specifies a set of Limits that are associated with
an Accumulator measurement.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AnalogLimitSet

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AnalogLimitSet.Limits
documentation: The limit values used for supervision of Measurements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AnalogLimitSet.Measurements
documentation: The Measurements using the LimitSet.

An AnalogLimitSet specifies a set of Limits that are associated with an
Analog measurement.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LoadGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LoadGroup.SubLoadArea
documentation: The SubLoadArea where the Loadgroup belongs.

The class is the third level in a hierarchical structure for grouping of
loads for the purpose of load flow load scaling.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ConformLoadGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ConformLoadGroup.ConformLoadSchedules
documentation: The ConformLoadSchedules in the ConformLoadGroup.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ConformLoadGroup.EnergyConsumers
documentation: Conform loads assigned to this ConformLoadGroup.
A group of loads conforming to an allocation pattern.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NonConformLoadGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NonConformLoadGroup.EnergyConsumers
documentation: Conform loads assigned to this ConformLoadGroup.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NonConformLoadGroup.NonConformLoadSchedules
documentation: The NonConformLoadSchedules in the NonConformLoadGroup.
Loads that do not follow a daily and seasonal load variation pattern.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LoadResponseCharacteristic

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LoadResponseCharacteristic.exponentModel
documentation:
Indicates the exponential voltage dependency model is to be used. If false,
the coefficient model is to be used.
The exponential voltage dependency model consist of the attributes
- pVoltageExponent
- qVoltageExponent.
The coefficient model consist of the attributes
- pConstantImpedance
- pConstantCurrent
- pConstantPower
- qConstantImpedance
- qConstantCurrent
- qConstantPower.
The sum of pConstantImpedance, pConstantCurrent and pConstantPower shall
equal 1.
The sum of qConstantImpedance, qConstantCurrent and qConstantPower shall
equal 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LoadResponseCharacteristic.pConstantCurrent
documentation: Portion of active power load modeled as constant current.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LoadResponseCharacteristic.pConstantImpedance
documentation: Portion of active power load modeled as constant impedance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LoadResponseCharacteristic.pConstantPower
documentation: Portion of active power load modeled as constant power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LoadResponseCharacteristic.pVoltageExponent
documentation: Exponent of per unit voltage effecting real power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LoadResponseCharacteristic.qConstantCurrent
documentation: Portion of reactive power load modeled as constant current.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LoadResponseCharacteristic.qConstantImpedance
documentation: Portion of reactive power load modeled as constant impedance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LoadResponseCharacteristic.qConstantPower
documentation: Portion of reactive power load modeled as constant power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LoadResponseCharacteristic.qVoltageExponent
documentation: Exponent of per unit voltage effecting reactive power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LoadResponseCharacteristic.EnergyConsumer
documentation: The set of loads that have the response characteristics.

Models the characteristic response of the load demand due to changes in
system conditions such as voltage and frequency. This is not related to
demand response.
If LoadResponseCharacteristic.exponentModel is True, the voltage exponents
are specified and used as to calculate:
Active power component = Pnominal * (Voltage/cim:BaseVoltage.nominalVoltage)
** cim:LoadResponseCharacteristic.pVoltageExponent
Reactive power component = Qnominal * (Voltage/cim:BaseVoltage.nominalVoltage)**
cim:LoadResponseCharacteristic.qVoltageExponent
Where * means "multiply" and ** is "raised to power of".

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Location

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Location.direction
documentation:
(if applicable) Direction that allows field crews to quickly find a given
asset. For a given location, such as a street address, this is the relative
direction in which to find the asset. For example, a streetlight may be
located at the 'NW' (northwest) corner of the customer's site, or a usage
point may be located on the second floor of an apartment building.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Location.geoInfoReference
documentation:
(if applicable) Reference to geographical information source, often external
to the utility.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Location.type
documentation:
Classification by utility's corporate standards and practices, relative
to the location itself (e.g., geographical, functional accounting, etc.,
not a given property that happens to exist at that location).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Location.Assets
documentation: All assets at this location.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Location.CoordinateSystem
documentation: Coordinate system used to describe position points of this location.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Location.PositionPoints
documentation:
Sequence of position points describing this location, expressed in coordinate
system 'Location.CoordinateSystem'.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Location.PowerSystemResources
documentation: All power system resources at this location.

The place, scene, or point of something where someone or something has
been, is, and/or will be at a given moment in time. It can be defined with
one or more postition points (coordinates) in a given coordinate system.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Measurement

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Measurement.measurementType
documentation:
Specifies the type of measurement. For example, this specifies if the measurement
represents an indoor temperature, outdoor temperature, bus voltage, line
flow, etc.
When the measurementType is set to "Specialization", the type of Measurement
is defined in more detail by the specialized class which inherits from
Measurement.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Measurement.phases
documentation:
Indicates to which phases the measurement applies and avoids the need to
use 'measurementType' to also encode phase information (which would explode
the types). The phase information in Measurement, along with 'measurementType'
and 'phases' uniquely defines a Measurement for a device, based on normal
network phase. Their meaning will not change when the computed energizing
phasing is changed due to jumpers or other reasons.
If the attribute is missing three phases (ABC) shall be assumed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Measurement.PowerSystemResource
documentation: The power system resource that contains the measurement.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Measurement.Terminal
documentation: One or more measurements may be associated with a terminal in the network.

A Measurement represents any measured, calculated or non-measured non-calculated
quantity. Any piece of equipment may contain Measurements, e.g. a substation
may have temperature measurements and door open indications, a transformer
may have oil temperature and tank pressure measurements, a bay may contain
a number of power flow measurements and a Breaker may contain a switch
status measurement.
The PSR - Measurement association is intended to capture this use of Measurement
and is included in the naming hierarchy based on EquipmentContainer. The
naming hierarchy typically has Measurements as leafs, e.g. Substation-VoltageLevel-Bay-Switch-Measurement.
Some Measurements represent quantities related to a particular sensor location
in the network, e.g. a voltage transformer (PT) at a busbar or a current
transformer (CT) at the bar between a breaker and an isolator. The sensing
position is not captured in the PSR - Measurement association. Instead
it is captured by the Measurement - Terminal association that is used to
define the sensing location in the network topology. The location is defined
by the connection of the Terminal to ConductingEquipment.
If both a Terminal and PSR are associated, and the PSR is of type ConductingEquipment,
the associated Terminal should belong to that ConductingEquipment instance.
When the sensor location is needed both Measurement-PSR and Measurement-Terminal
are used. The Measurement-Terminal association is never used alone.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Accumulator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Accumulator.maxValue
documentation:
Normal value range maximum for any of the MeasurementValue.values. Used
for scaling, e.g. in bar graphs or of telemetered raw values.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Accumulator.AccumulatorValues
documentation: The values connected to this measurement.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Accumulator.LimitSets
documentation: A measurement may have zero or more limit ranges defined for it.

Accumulator represents an accumulated (counted) Measurement, e.g. an energy
value.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Analog

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Analog.maxValue
documentation:
Normal value range maximum for any of the MeasurementValue.values. Used
for scaling, e.g. in bar graphs or of telemetered raw values.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Analog.minValue
documentation:
Normal value range minimum for any of the MeasurementValue.values. Used
for scaling, e.g. in bar graphs or of telemetered raw values.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Analog.normalValue
documentation: Normal measurement value, e.g., used for percentage calculations.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Analog.positiveFlowIn
documentation:
If true then this measurement is an active power, reactive power or current
with the convention that a positive value measured at the Terminal means
power is flowing into the related PowerSystemResource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Analog.AnalogValues
documentation: The values connected to this measurement.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Analog.LimitSets
documentation: A measurement may have zero or more limit ranges defined for it.
Analog represents an analog Measurement.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Discrete

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Discrete.maxValue
documentation:
Normal value range maximum for any of the MeasurementValue.values. Used
for scaling, e.g. in bar graphs or of telemetered raw values.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Discrete.minValue
documentation:
Normal value range minimum for any of the MeasurementValue.values. Used
for scaling, e.g. in bar graphs or of telemetered raw values.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Discrete.normalValue
documentation: Normal measurement value, e.g., used for percentage calculations.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Discrete.DiscreteValues
documentation: The values connected to this measurement.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Discrete.ValueAliasSet
documentation:
The ValueAliasSet used for translation of a MeasurementValue.value to a
name.

Discrete represents a discrete Measurement, i.e. a Measurement representing
discrete values, e.g. a Breaker position.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.StringMeasurement

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.StringMeasurement.StringMeasurementValues
documentation: The values connected to this measurement.
StringMeasurement represents a measurement with values of type string.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MeasurementValueSource

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MeasurementValueSource.MeasurementValues
documentation: The MeasurementValues updated by the source.

MeasurementValueSource describes the alternative sources updating a MeasurementValue.
User conventions for how to use the MeasurementValueSource attributes are
described in the introduction to IEC 61970-301.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MutualCoupling

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MutualCoupling.b0ch
documentation:
Zero sequence mutual coupling shunt (charging) susceptance, uniformly distributed,
of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MutualCoupling.distance11
documentation:
Distance to the start of the coupled region from the first line's terminal
having sequence number equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MutualCoupling.distance12
documentation:
Distance to the end of the coupled region from the first line's terminal
with sequence number equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MutualCoupling.distance21
documentation:
Distance to the start of coupled region from the second line's terminal
with sequence number equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MutualCoupling.distance22
documentation:
Distance to the end of coupled region from the second line's terminal with
sequence number equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MutualCoupling.g0ch
documentation:
Zero sequence mutual coupling shunt (charging) conductance, uniformly distributed,
of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MutualCoupling.r0
documentation: Zero sequence branch-to-branch mutual impedance coupling, resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MutualCoupling.x0
documentation: Zero sequence branch-to-branch mutual impedance coupling, reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MutualCoupling.First_Terminal
documentation:
The starting terminal for the calculation of distances along the first
branch of the mutual coupling. Normally MutualCoupling would only be used
for terminals of AC line segments. The first and second terminals of a
mutual coupling should point to different AC line segments.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MutualCoupling.Second_Terminal
documentation:
The starting terminal for the calculation of distances along the second
branch of the mutual coupling.
This class represents the zero sequence line mutual coupling.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OperatingParticipant

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OperatingParticipant.OperatingShare
documentation:
The operating shares of this operating participant. An operating participant
can be resused for any number of power system resources.

An operator of multiple power system resource objects. Note multple operating
participants may operate the same power system resource object. This can
be used for modeling jointly owned units where each owner operates as a
contractual share.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OperationalLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OperationalLimit.OperationalLimitSet
documentation: The limit set to which the limit values belong.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OperationalLimit.OperationalLimitType
documentation: The limit type associated with this limit.

A value associated with a specific kind of limit.
The sub class value attribute shall be positive.
The sub class value attribute is inversely proportional to OperationalLimitType.acceptableDuration
(acceptableDuration for short). A pair of value_x and acceptableDuration_x
are related to each other as follows:
if value_1 > value_2 > value_3 >... then
acceptableDuration_1 < acceptableDuration_2 < acceptableDuration_3
< ...
A value_x with direction="high" shall be greater than a value_y with direction="low".

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ActivePowerLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ActivePowerLimit.normalValue
documentation: The normal value of active power limit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ActivePowerLimit.value
documentation: Value of active power limit.
Limit on active power flow.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ApparentPowerLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ApparentPowerLimit.normalValue
documentation: The normal apparent power limit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ApparentPowerLimit.value
documentation: The apparent power limit.
Apparent power limit.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CurrentLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CurrentLimit.normalValue
documentation: The normal value for limit on current flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CurrentLimit.value
documentation: Limit on current flow.
Operational limit on current.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.VoltageLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.VoltageLimit.normalValue
documentation:
The normal limit on voltage. High or low limit nature of the limit depends
upon the properties of the operational limit type.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.VoltageLimit.value
documentation:
Limit on voltage. High or low limit nature of the limit depends upon the
properties of the operational limit type.
Operational limit applied to voltage.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OperationalLimitSet

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OperationalLimitSet.Equipment
documentation: The equipment to which the limit set applies.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OperationalLimitSet.OperationalLimitValue
documentation: Values of equipment limits.

A set of limits associated with equipment. Sets of limits might apply to
a specific temperature, or season for example. A set of limits may contain
different severities of limit levels that would apply to the same equipment.
The set may contain limits of different types such as apparent power and
current limits or high and low voltage limits that are logically applied
together as a set.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OperationalLimitType

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OperationalLimitType.acceptableDuration
documentation:
The nominal acceptable duration of the limit. Limits are commonly expressed
in terms of the a time limit for which the limit is normally acceptable.
The actual acceptable duration of a specific limit may depend on other
local factors such as temperature or wind speed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OperationalLimitType.direction
documentation: The direction of the limit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OperationalLimitType.OperationalLimit
documentation: The operational limits associated with this type of limit.
The operational meaning of a category of limits.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PSRType

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PSRType.PowerSystemResources
documentation: Power system resources classified with this power system resource type.

Classifying instances of the same class, e.g. overhead and underground
ACLineSegments. This classification mechanism is intended to provide flexibility
outside the scope of this standard, i.e. provide customisation that is
non standard.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PerLengthLineParameter

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PerLengthLineParameter.WireAssemblyInfo
documentation:
A WireAssemblyInfo is used to compute the PerLengthParameter data in the
Wires package
Common type for per-length electrical catalogues describing line parameters.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PerLengthImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PerLengthImpedance.ACLineSegments
documentation: All line segments described by this per-length impedance.
Common type for per-length impedance electrical catalogues.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PerLengthPhaseImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PerLengthPhaseImpedance.conductorCount
documentation:
Number of phase, neutral, and other wires retained. Constrains the number
of matrix elements and the phase codes that can be used with this matrix.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PerLengthPhaseImpedance.PhaseImpedanceData
documentation: All data that belong to this conductor phase impedance.

Impedance and admittance parameters per unit length for n-wire unbalanced
lines, in matrix form.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PerLengthSequenceImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.b0ch
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) susceptance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.bch
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) susceptance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.g0ch
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) conductance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.gch
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.r
documentation: Positive sequence series resistance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.r0
documentation: Zero sequence series resistance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.x
documentation: Positive sequence series reactance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.x0
documentation: Zero sequence series reactance, per unit of length.

Sequence impedance and admittance parameters per unit length, for transposed
lines of 1, 2, or 3 phases. For 1-phase lines, define x=x0=xself. For 2-phase
lines, define x=xs-xm and x0=xs+xm.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChangerTable

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChangerTable.PhaseTapChangerTablePoint
documentation: The points of this table.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChangerTable.PhaseTapChangerTabular
documentation: The phase tap changers to which this phase tap table applies.

Describes a tabular curve for how the phase angle difference and impedance
varies with the tap step.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerSystemResource

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerSystemResource.AssetDatasheet
documentation: Datasheet information for this power system resource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerSystemResource.Assets
documentation:
All assets represented by this power system resource. For example, multiple
conductor assets are electrically modelled as a single AC line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerSystemResource.Controls
documentation:
The controller outputs used to actually govern a regulating device, e.g.
the magnetization of a synchronous machine or capacitor bank breaker actuator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerSystemResource.Location
documentation: Location of this power system resource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerSystemResource.Measurements
documentation: The measurements associated with this power system resource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerSystemResource.PSRType
documentation: Custom classification for this power system resource.

A power system resource can be an item of equipment such as a switch, an
equipment container containing many individual items of equipment such
as a substation, or an organisational entity such as sub-control area.
Power system resources can have measurements associated.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACLineSegmentPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACLineSegmentPhase.sequenceNumber
documentation:
Number designation for this line segment phase. Each line segment phase
within a line segment should have a unique sequence number. This is useful
for unbalanced modeling to bind the mathematical model (PhaseImpedanceData
of PerLengthPhaseImpedance) with the connectivity model (this class) and
the physical model (WirePosition, WirePhaseInfo) without tight coupling.
Multiple circuits on the same pole, tower or right-of-way can be included
with unique sequence numbers for the phases, and identical sequence numbers
for any shared neutrals.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACLineSegmentPhase.phase
documentation: The phase connection of the wire at both ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACLineSegmentPhase.ACLineSegment
documentation: The line segment to which the phase belongs.
Represents a single wire of an alternating current line segment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ConnectivityNodeContainer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ConnectivityNodeContainer.ConnectivityNodes
documentation: Connectivity nodes which belong to this connectivity node container.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ConnectivityNodeContainer.TopologicalNode
documentation: The topological nodes which belong to this connectivity node container.

A base class for all objects that may contain connectivity nodes or topological
nodes.
