classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EquipmentContainer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EquipmentContainer.AdditionalGroupedEquipment
documentation:
The additonal contained equipment. The equipment belong to the equipment
container. The equipment is contained in another equipment container, but
also grouped with this equipment container. Examples include when a switch
contained in a substation is also desired to be grouped with a line contianer
or when a switch is included in a secondary substation and also grouped
in a feeder.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EquipmentContainer.Equipments
documentation: Contained equipment.
A modeling construct to provide a root class for containing equipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Bay

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Bay.bayEnergyMeasFlag
documentation: Indicates the presence/absence of energy measurements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Bay.bayPowerMeasFlag
documentation: Indicates the presence/absence of active/reactive power measurements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Bay.breakerConfiguration
documentation: Breaker configuration.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Bay.busBarConfiguration
documentation: Bus bar configuration.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Bay.Substation
documentation: Substation containing the bay.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Bay.VoltageLevel
documentation: The voltage level containing this bay.

A collection of power system resources (within a given substation) including
conducting equipment, protection relays, measurements, and telemetry. A
bay typically represents a physical grouping related to modularization
of equipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Feeder

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Feeder.DistributionArea
documentation: The DistributionArea to which the feeder belongs

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Feeder.FeederArea
documentation: The FeederArea (which contains Equipment not contained in

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Feeder.NamingSecondarySubstation
documentation:
The secondary substations that are normally energized from the feeder.
Used for naming purposes. Should be consistent with the other associations
for energizing terminal specification and the feeder energization specification.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Feeder.NormalEnergizedSubstation
documentation: The substations that are normally energized by the feeder.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Feeder.NormalEnergizingSubstation
documentation:
The substation that nominally energizes the feeder. Also used for naming
purposes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Feeder.NormalHeadTerminal
documentation: The normal head terminal or terminals of the feeder.

A collection of equipment for organizational purposes, used for grouping
distribution resources.
The organization a feeder does not necessarily reflect connectivity or
current operation state.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Line

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Line.Region
documentation: The sub-geographical region of the line.

Contains equipment beyond a substation belonging to a power transmission
line.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Plant
documentation: A Plant is a collection of equipment for purposes of generation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Substation

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Substation.Bays
documentation: Bays contained in the substation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Substation.NamingFeeder
documentation:
The primary feeder that normally energizes the secondary substation. Used
for naming purposes. Either this association or the substation to subgeographical
region should be used for hiearchical containment specification.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Substation.NormalEnergizedFeeder
documentation: The normal energized feeders of the substation. Also used for naming purposes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Substation.NormalEnergizingFeeder
documentation:
The feeders that potentially energize the downstream substation. Should
be consistent with the associations that describe the naming hiearchy.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Substation.Region
documentation: The SubGeographicalRegion containing the substation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Substation.VoltageLevels
documentation: The voltage levels within this substation.

A collection of equipment for purposes other than generation or utilization,
through which electric energy in bulk is passed for the purposes of switching
or modifying its characteristics.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.VoltageLevel

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.VoltageLevel.highVoltageLimit
documentation: The bus bar's high voltage limit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.VoltageLevel.lowVoltageLimit
documentation: The bus bar's low voltage limit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.VoltageLevel.BaseVoltage
documentation: The base voltage used for all equipment within the voltage level.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.VoltageLevel.Bays
documentation: The bays within this voltage level.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.VoltageLevel.Substation
documentation: The substation of the voltage level.

A collection of equipment at one common system voltage forming a switchgear.
The equipment typically consist of breakers, busbars, instrumentation,
control, regulation and protection devices as well as assemblies of all
these.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConsumerPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConsumerPhase.p
documentation:
Active power of the load. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
For voltage dependent loads the value is at rated voltage.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConsumerPhase.phase
documentation:
Phase of this energy consumer component. If the energy consumer is wye
connected, the connection is from the indicated phase to the central ground
or neutral point. If the energy consumer is delta connected, the phase
indicates an energy consumer connected from the indicated phase to the
next logical non-neutral phase.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConsumerPhase.q
documentation:
Reactive power of the load. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive
sign means flow out from a node.
For voltage dependent loads the value is at rated voltage.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConsumerPhase.EnergyConsumer
documentation: The energy consumer to which this phase belongs.
A single phase of an energy consumer.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergySourcePhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergySourcePhase.phase
documentation:
Phase of this energy source component. If the energy source wye connected,
the connection is from the indicated phase to the central ground or neutral
point. If the energy source is delta connected, the phase indicates an
energy source connected from the indicated phase to the next logical non-neutral
phase.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergySourcePhase.EnergySource
documentation: The energy sourceto which the phase belongs.
Represents the single phase information of an unbalanced energy source.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Equipment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Equipment.aggregate
documentation:
The single instance of equipment represents multiple pieces of equipment
that have been modeled together as an aggregate. Examples would be power
transformers or synchronous machines operating in parallel modeled as a
single aggregate power transformer or aggregate synchronous machine. This
is not to be used to indicate equipment that is part of a group of interdependent
equipment produced by a network production program.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Equipment.inService
documentation: If true, the equipment is in service.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Equipment.networkAnalysisEnabled
documentation:
The equipment is enabled to participate in network analysis. If unspecified,
the value is assumed to be true.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Equipment.normallyInService
documentation: If true, the equipment is normally in service.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Equipment.AdditionalEquipmentContainer
documentation:
Additional equipment container beyond the primary equipment container.
The equipment is contained in another equipment container, but also grouped
with this equipment container.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Equipment.EquipmentContainer
documentation: Container of this equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Equipment.OperationalLimitSet
documentation: The operational limit sets associated with this equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Equipment.SubSchedulingArea
documentation: The SubSchedulingArea in which the equipment is contained and controlled.
The parts of a power system that are physical devices, electronic or mechanical.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CompositeSwitch

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CompositeSwitch.compositeSwitchType
documentation:
An alphanumeric code that can be used as a reference to extra information
such as the description of the interlocking scheme if any.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CompositeSwitch.Switches
documentation: Switches contained in this Composite switch.

A model of a set of individual Switches normally enclosed within the same
cabinet and possibly with interlocks that restrict the combination of switch
positions. These are typically found in medium voltage distribution networks.
A CompositeSwitch could represent a Ring-Main-Unit (RMU), or pad-mounted
switchgear, with primitive internal devices such as an internal bus-bar
plus 3 or 4 internal switches each of which may individually be open or
closed. A CompositeSwitch and a set of contained Switches can also be used
to represent a multi-position switch e.g. a switch that can connect a circuit
to Ground, Open or Busbar.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ConductingEquipment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ConductingEquipment.BaseVoltage
documentation:
Base voltage of this conducting equipment. Use only when there is no voltage
level container used and only one base voltage applies. For example, not
used for transformers.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ConductingEquipment.Terminals
documentation:
Conducting equipment have terminals that may be connected to other conducting
equipment terminals via connectivity nodes or topological nodes.

The parts of the AC power system that are designed to carry current or
that are conductively connected through terminals.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Clamp

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Clamp.lengthFromTerminal1
documentation:
The length to the place where the clamp is located starting from side one
of the line segment, i.e. the line segment terminal with sequence number
equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Clamp.ACLineSegment
documentation: The line segment to which the clamp is connected.

A Clamp is a galvanic connection at a line segment where other equipment
is connected. A Clamp does not cut the line segment.
A Clamp is ConductingEquipment and has one Terminal with an associated
ConnectivityNode. Any other ConductingEquipment can be connected to the
Clamp ConnectivityNode.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Conductor

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Conductor.length
documentation: Segment length for calculating line section capabilities

Combination of conducting material with consistent electrical characteristics,
building a single electrical system, used to carry current between points
in the power system.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACLineSegment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACLineSegment.b0ch
documentation:
Zero sequence shunt (charging) susceptance, uniformly distributed, of the
entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACLineSegment.bch
documentation:
Positive sequence shunt (charging) susceptance, uniformly distributed,
of the entire line section. This value represents the full charging over
the full length of the line.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACLineSegment.g0ch
documentation:
Zero sequence shunt (charging) conductance, uniformly distributed, of the
entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACLineSegment.gch
documentation:
Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance, uniformly distributed,
of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACLineSegment.r
documentation: Positive sequence series resistance of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACLineSegment.r0
documentation: Zero sequence series resistance of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACLineSegment.shortCircuitEndTemperature
documentation:
Maximum permitted temperature at the end of SC for the calculation of minimum
short-circuit currents. Used for short circuit data exchange according
to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACLineSegment.x
documentation: Positive sequence series reactance of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACLineSegment.x0
documentation: Zero sequence series reactance of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACLineSegment.ACLineSegmentPhases
documentation: The line segment phases which belong to the line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ACLineSegment.PerLengthImpedance
documentation: Per-length impedance of this line segment.

A wire or combination of wires, with consistent electrical characteristics,
building a single electrical system, used to carry alternating current
between points in the power system.
For symmetrical, transposed 3ph lines, it is sufficient to use attributes
of the line segment, which describe impedances and admittances for the
entire length of the segment. Additionally impedances can be computed by
using length and associated per length impedances.
The BaseVoltage at the two ends of ACLineSegments in a Line shall have
the same BaseVoltage.nominalVoltage. However, boundary lines may have slightly
different BaseVoltage.nominalVoltages and variation is allowed. Larger
voltage difference in general requires use of an equivalent branch.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Connector
documentation:
A conductor, or group of conductors, with negligible impedance, that serve
to connect other conducting equipment within a single substation and are
modelled with a single logical terminal.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BusbarSection

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BusbarSection.ipMax
documentation:
Maximum allowable peak short-circuit current of busbar (Ipmax in the IEC
60909-0).
Mechanical limit of the busbar in the substation itself. Used for short
circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BusbarSection.VoltageControlZone
documentation: A VoltageControlZone is controlled by a designated BusbarSection.

A conductor, or group of conductors, with negligible impedance, that serve
to connect other conducting equipment within a single substation.
Voltage measurements are typically obtained from VoltageTransformers that
are connected to busbar sections. A bus bar section may have many physical
terminals but for analysis is modelled with exactly one logical terminal.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Junction
documentation:
A point where one or more conducting equipments are connected with zero
resistance.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EarthFaultCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EarthFaultCompensator.r
documentation: Nominal resistance of device.

A conducting equipment used to represent a connection to ground which is
typically used to compensate earth faults.. An earth fault compensator
device modeled with a single terminal implies a second terminal solidly
connected to ground. If two terminals are modeled, the ground is not assumed
and normal connection rules apply.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.GroundingImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.GroundingImpedance.x
documentation: Reactance of device.
A fixed impedance device used for grounding.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PetersenCoil

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PetersenCoil.nominalU
documentation: The nominal voltage for which the coil is designed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PetersenCoil.offsetCurrent
documentation:
The offset current that the Petersen coil controller is operating from
the resonant point. This is normally a fixed amount for which the controller
is configured and could be positive or negative. Typically 0 to 60 Amperes
depending on voltage and resonance conditions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PetersenCoil.positionCurrent
documentation:
The control current used to control the Petersen coil also known as the
position current. Typically in the range of 20-200mA.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PetersenCoil.xGroundMax
documentation: The maximum reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PetersenCoil.xGroundMin
documentation: The minimum reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PetersenCoil.xGroundNominal
documentation:
The nominal reactance. This is the operating point (normally over compensation)
that is defined based on the resonance point in the healthy network condition.
The impedance is calculated based on nominal voltage divided by position
current.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PetersenCoil.mode
documentation: The mode of operation of the Petersen coil.

A tunable impedance device normally used to offset line charging during
single line faults in an ungrounded section of network.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConsumer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConsumer.customerCount
documentation: Number of individual customers represented by this demand.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConsumer.grounded
documentation: Used for Yn and Zn connections. True if the neutral is solidly grounded.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConsumer.p
documentation:
Active power of the load. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
For voltage dependent loads the value is at rated voltage.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConsumer.phaseConnection
documentation: The type of phase connection, such as wye or delta.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConsumer.q
documentation:
Reactive power of the load. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive
sign means flow out from a node.
For voltage dependent loads the value is at rated voltage.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConsumer.EnergyConsumerPhase
documentation: The individual phase models for this energy consumer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConsumer.LoadResponse
documentation:
The load response characteristic of this load. If missing, this load is
assumed to be constant power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergyConsumer.PowerCutZone
documentation: The energy consumer is assigned to this power cut zone.
Generic user of energy - a point of consumption on the power system model.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ConformLoad

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ConformLoad.LoadGroup
documentation: Group of this ConformLoad.

ConformLoad represent loads that follow a daily load change pattern where
the pattern can be used to scale the load with a system load.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NonConformLoad

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NonConformLoad.LoadGroup
documentation: Group of this ConformLoad.

NonConformLoad represent loads that do not follow a daily load change pattern
and changes are not correlated with the daily load change pattern.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.StationSupply
documentation: Station supply with load derived from the station output.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergySource

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergySource.nominalVoltage
documentation: Phase-to-phase nominal voltage.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergySource.r
documentation: Positive sequence Thevenin resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergySource.r0
documentation: Zero sequence Thevenin resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergySource.voltageAngle
documentation: Phase angle of a-phase open circuit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergySource.voltageMagnitude
documentation: Phase-to-phase open circuit voltage magnitude.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergySource.x
documentation: Positive sequence Thevenin reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergySource.x0
documentation: Zero sequence Thevenin reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.EnergySource.EnergySourcePhase
documentation: The individual phase information of the energy source.

A generic equivalent for an energy supplier on a transmission or distribution
voltage level.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegulatingCondEq

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegulatingCondEq.controlEnabled
documentation:
Specifies the regulation status of the equipment. True is regulating, false
is not regulating.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegulatingCondEq.RegulatingControl
documentation: The regulating control scheme in which this equipment participates.

A type of conducting equipment that can regulate a quantity (i.e. voltage
or flow) at a specific point in the network.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection.ikSecond
documentation:
Indicates whether initial symmetrical short-circuit current and power have
been calculated according to IEC (Ik").

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxR0ToX0Ratio
documentation:
Maximum ratio of zero sequence resistance of Network Feeder to its zero
sequence reactance (R(0)/X(0) max). Used for short circuit data exchange
according to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxR1ToX1Ratio
documentation:
Maximum ratio of positive sequence resistance of Network Feeder to its
positive sequence reactance (R(1)/X(1) max). Used for short circuit data
exchange according to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxZ0ToZ1Ratio
documentation:
Maximum ratio of zero sequence impedance to its positive sequence impedance
(Z(0)/Z(1) max). Used for short circuit data exchange according to IEC
60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection.minR0ToX0Ratio
documentation:
Indicates whether initial symmetrical short-circuit current and power have
been calculated according to IEC (Ik"). Used for short circuit data exchange
according to IEC 6090

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection.minR1ToX1Ratio
documentation:
Minimum ratio of positive sequence resistance of Network Feeder to its
positive sequence reactance (R(1)/X(1) min). Used for short circuit data
exchange according to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection.minZ0ToZ1Ratio
documentation:
Minimum ratio of zero sequence impedance to its positive sequence impedance
(Z(0)/Z(1) min). Used for short circuit data exchange according to IEC
60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection.referencePriority
documentation:
Priority of unit for use as powerflow voltage phase angle reference bus
selection. 0 = don t care (default) 1 = highest priority. 2 is less than
1 and so on.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection.governorSCD
documentation:
Power Frequency Bias. This is the change in power injection divided by
the change in frequency and negated. A positive value of the power frequency
bias provides additional power injection upon a drop in frequency.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxInitialSymShCCurrent
documentation:
Maximum initial symmetrical short-circuit currents (Ik" max) in A (Ik"
= Sk"/(SQRT(3) Un)). Used for short circuit data exchange according to
IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxP
documentation: Maximum active power of the injection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxQ
documentation:
Not for short circuit modelling; It is used for modelling of infeed for
load flow exchange. If maxQ and minQ are not used ReactiveCapabilityCurve
can be used

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection.minInitialSymShCCurrent
documentation:
Minimum initial symmetrical short-circuit currents (Ik" min) in A (Ik"
= Sk"/(SQRT(3) Un)). Used for short circuit data exchange according to
IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection.minP
documentation: Minimum active power of the injection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection.minQ
documentation:
Not for short circuit modelling; It is used for modelling of infeed for
load flow exchange. If maxQ and minQ are not used ReactiveCapabilityCurve
can be used

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection.p
documentation:
Active power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for steady state solutions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection.q
documentation:
Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for steady state solutions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ExternalNetworkInjection.voltageFactor
documentation:
Voltage factor in pu, which was used to calculate short-circuit current
Ik" and power Sk".
This class represents external network and it is used for IEC 60909 calculations.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FrequencyConverter

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FrequencyConverter.frequency
documentation: Frequency on the AC side.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FrequencyConverter.maxP
documentation:
The maximum active power on the DC side at which the frequence converter
should operate.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FrequencyConverter.maxU
documentation:
The maximum voltage on the DC side at which the frequency converter should
operate.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FrequencyConverter.minP
documentation:
The minimum active power on the DC side at which the frequence converter
should operate.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FrequencyConverter.minU
documentation:
The minimum voltage on the DC side at which the frequency converter should
operate.

A device to convert from one frequency to another (e.g., frequency F1 to
F2) comprises a pair of FrequencyConverter instances. One converts from
F1 to DC, the other converts the DC to F2.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerElectronicsConnection

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerElectronicsConnection.maxIFault
documentation:
Maximum fault current this device will contribute, in per-unit of rated
current, before the converter protection will trip or bypass.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerElectronicsConnection.maxQ
documentation:
Maximum reactive power limit. This is the maximum (nameplate) limit for
the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerElectronicsConnection.minQ
documentation:
Minimum reactive power limit for the unit. This is the minimum (nameplate)
limit for the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerElectronicsConnection.p
documentation:
Active power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerElectronicsConnection.q
documentation:
Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerElectronicsConnection.ratedS
documentation:
Nameplate apparent power rating for the unit.
The attribute shall have a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerElectronicsConnection.ratedU
documentation:
Rated voltage (nameplate data, Ur in IEC 60909-0). It is primarily used
for short circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerElectronicsConnection.DERDynamics
documentation: DER dynamics model associated with this power electronics connection model.

A connection to the AC network for energy production or consumption that
uses power electronics rather than rotating machines.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RotatingMachine

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RotatingMachine.ratedPowerFactor
documentation:
Power factor (nameplate data). It is primarily used for short circuit data
exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RotatingMachine.p
documentation:
Active power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RotatingMachine.q
documentation:
Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RotatingMachine.ratedS
documentation:
Nameplate apparent power rating for the unit.
The attribute shall have a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RotatingMachine.ratedU
documentation:
Rated voltage (nameplate data, Ur in IEC 60909-0). It is primarily used
for short circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RotatingMachine.GeneratingUnit
documentation:
A synchronous machine may operate as a generator and as such becomes a
member of a generating unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RotatingMachine.HydroPump
documentation:
The synchronous machine drives the turbine which moves the water from a
low elevation to a higher elevation. The direction of machine rotation
for pumping may or may not be the same as for generating.
A rotating machine which may be used as a generator or motor.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AsynchronousMachine

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AsynchronousMachine.converterFedDrive
documentation:
Indicates whether the machine is a converter fed drive. Used for short
circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AsynchronousMachine.iaIrRatio
documentation:
Ratio of locked-rotor current to the rated current of the motor (Ia/Ir).
Used for short circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AsynchronousMachine.polePairNumber
documentation:
Number of pole pairs of stator. Used for short circuit data exchange according
to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AsynchronousMachine.reversible
documentation:
Indicates for converter drive motors if the power can be reversible. Used
for short circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AsynchronousMachine.rxLockedRotorRatio
documentation:
Locked rotor ratio (R/X). Used for short circuit data exchange according
to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AsynchronousMachine.asynchronousMachineType
documentation: Indicates the type of Asynchronous Machine (motor or generator).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AsynchronousMachine.efficiency
documentation:
Efficiency of the asynchronous machine at nominal operation in percent.
Indicator for converter drive motors. Used for short circuit data exchange
according to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AsynchronousMachine.nominalFrequency
documentation: Nameplate data indicates if the machine is 50 or 60 Hz.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AsynchronousMachine.nominalSpeed
documentation: Nameplate data. Depends on the slip and number of pole pairs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AsynchronousMachine.ratedMechanicalPower
documentation:
Rated mechanical power (Pr in the IEC 60909-0). Used for short circuit
data exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AsynchronousMachine.DERDynamics
documentation: DER dynamics model associated with this asynchronous machine model.

A rotating machine whose shaft rotates asynchronously with the electrical
field. Also known as an induction machine with no external connection to
the rotor windings, e.g squirrel-cage induction machine.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SynchronousMachine

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SynchronousMachine.ikk
documentation:
Steady-state short-circuit current (in A for the profile) of generator
with compound excitation during 3-phase short circuit.
- Ikk=0: Generator with no compound excitation.
- Ikk?0: Generator with compound excitation.
Ikk is used to calculate the minimum steady-state short-circuit current
for generators with compound excitation
(Section 4.6.1.2 in the IEC 60909-0)
Used only for single fed short circuit on a generator. (Section 4.3.4.2.
in the IEC 60909-0)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SynchronousMachine.maxQ
documentation:
Maximum reactive power limit. This is the maximum (nameplate) limit for
the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SynchronousMachine.minQ
documentation: Minimum reactive power limit for the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SynchronousMachine.operatingMode
documentation: Current mode of operation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SynchronousMachine.type
documentation: Modes that this synchronous machine can operate in.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SynchronousMachine.DERDynamics
documentation: DER dynamics model associated with this synchronous machine model.

An electromechanical device that operates with shaft rotating synchronously
with the network. It is a single machine operating either as a generator
or synchronous condenser or pump.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShuntCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShuntCompensator.grounded
documentation: Used for Yn and Zn connections. True if the neutral is solidly grounded.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShuntCompensator.maximumSections
documentation: The maximum number of sections that may be switched in.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShuntCompensator.normalSections
documentation: The normal number of sections switched in.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShuntCompensator.sections
documentation:
Shunt compensator sections in use.
Starting value for steady state solution. Non integer values are allowed
to support continuous variables. The reasons for continuous value are to
support study cases where no discrete shunt compensators has yet been designed,
a solutions where a narrow voltage band force the sections to oscillate
or accommodate for a continuous solution as input.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShuntCompensator.aVRDelay
documentation:
Time delay required for the device to be connected or disconnected by automatic
voltage regulation (AVR).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShuntCompensator.nomU
documentation:
The voltage at which the nominal reactive power may be calculated. This
should normally be within 10% of the voltage at which the capacitor is
connected to the network.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShuntCompensator.phaseConnection
documentation: The type of phase connection, such as wye or delta.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShuntCompensator.ShuntCompensatorPhase
documentation: The individual phases models for the shunt compensator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShuntCompensator.SvShuntCompensatorSections
documentation: The state for the number of shunt compensator sections in service.

A shunt capacitor or reactor or switchable bank of shunt capacitors or
reactors. A section of a shunt compensator is an individual capacitor or
reactor. A negative value for reactivePerSection indicates that the compensator
is a reactor. ShuntCompensator is a single terminal device. Ground is implied.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LinearShuntCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LinearShuntCompensator.b0PerSection
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) susceptance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LinearShuntCompensator.bPerSection
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) susceptance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LinearShuntCompensator.g0PerSection
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) conductance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LinearShuntCompensator.gPerSection
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance per section

A linear shunt compensator has banks or sections with equal admittance
values.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NonlinearShuntCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NonlinearShuntCompensator.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoints
documentation: All points of the non-linear shunt compensator.

A non linear shunt compensator has bank or section admittance values that
differs.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.StaticVarCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.StaticVarCompensator.capacitiveRating
documentation: Maximum available capacitive reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.StaticVarCompensator.inductiveRating
documentation: Maximum available inductive reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.StaticVarCompensator.q
documentation:
Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.StaticVarCompensator.slope
documentation:
The characteristics slope of an SVC defines how the reactive power output
changes in proportion to the difference between the regulated bus voltage
and the voltage setpoint.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.StaticVarCompensator.sVCControlMode
documentation: SVC control mode.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.StaticVarCompensator.voltageSetPoint
documentation:
The reactive power output of the SVC is proportional to the difference
between the voltage at the regulated bus and the voltage setpoint. When
the regulated bus voltage is equal to the voltage setpoint, the reactive
power output is zero.

A facility for providing variable and controllable shunt reactive power.
The SVC typically consists of a stepdown transformer, filter, thyristor-controlled
reactor, and thyristor-switched capacitor arms.
The SVC may operate in fixed MVar output mode or in voltage control mode.
When in voltage control mode, the output of the SVC will be proportional
to the deviation of voltage at the controlled bus from the voltage setpoint.
The SVC characteristic slope defines the proportion. If the voltage at
the controlled bus is equal to the voltage setpoint, the SVC MVar output
is zero.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Ground
documentation:
A point where the system is grounded used for connecting conducting equipment
to ground. The power system model can have any number of grounds.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerTransformer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerTransformer.vectorGroup
documentation:
Vector group of the transformer for protective relaying, e.g., Dyn1. For
unbalanced transformers, this may not be simply determined from the constituent
winding connections and phase angle dispacements.
The vectorGroup string consists of the following components in the order
listed: high voltage winding connection, mid voltage winding connection
(for three winding transformers), phase displacement clock number from
0 to 11, low voltage winding connection
phase displacement clock number from 0 to 11. The winding connections are
D (delta), Y (wye), YN (wye with neutral), Z (zigzag), ZN (zigzag with
neutral), A (auto transformer). Upper case means the high voltage, lower
case mid or low. The high voltage winding always has clock postion 0 and
is not included in the vector group string. Some examples: YNy0 (two winding
wye to wye with no phase displacement), YNd11 (two winding wye to delta
with 330 degrees phase displacement), YNyn0d5 (three winding transformer
wye with neutral high voltgage, wye with neutral mid voltgage and no phase
displacement, delta low voltage with 150 degrees displacement).
Phase displacement is defined as the angular difference between the phasors
representing the voltages between the neutral point (real or imaginary)
and the corresponding terminals of two windings, a positive sequence voltage
system being applied to the high-voltage terminals, following each other
in alphabetical sequence if they are lettered, or in numerical sequence
if they are numbered: the phasors are assumed to rotate in a counter-clockwise
sense.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerTransformer.PowerTransformerEnd
documentation: The ends of this power transformer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerTransformer.TransformerTanks
documentation: All transformers that belong to this bank.

An electrical device consisting of two or more coupled windings, with or
without a magnetic core, for introducing mutual coupling between electric
circuits. Transformers can be used to control voltage and phase shift (active
power flow).
A power transformer may be composed of separate transformer tanks that
need not be identical.
A power transformer can be modeled with or without tanks and is intended
for use in both balanced and unbalanced representations. A power transformer
typically has two terminals, but may have one (grounding), three or more
terminals.
The inherited association ConductingEquipment.BaseVoltage should not be
used. The association from TransformerEnd to BaseVoltage should be used
instead.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SeriesCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SeriesCompensator.varistorPresent
documentation:
Describe if a metal oxide varistor (mov) for over voltage protection is
configured at the series compensator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SeriesCompensator.r
documentation: Positive sequence resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SeriesCompensator.r0
documentation: Zero sequence resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SeriesCompensator.varistorRatedCurrent
documentation: The maximum current the varistor is designed to handle at specified duration.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SeriesCompensator.varistorVoltageThreshold
documentation: The dc voltage at which the varistor start conducting.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SeriesCompensator.x
documentation: Positive sequence reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SeriesCompensator.x0
documentation: Zero sequence reactance.

A Series Compensator is a series capacitor or reactor or an AC transmission
line without charging susceptance. It is a two terminal device.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Switch

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Switch.normalOpen
documentation:
The attribute is used in cases when no Measurement for the status value
is present. If the Switch has a status measurement the Discrete.normalValue
is expected to match with the Switch.normalOpen.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Switch.open
documentation:
The attribute tells if the switch is considered open when used as input
to topology processing.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Switch.retained
documentation:
Branch is retained in a bus branch model. The flow through retained switches
will normally be calculated in power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Switch.ratedCurrent
documentation:
The maximum continuous current carrying capacity in amps governed by the
device material and construction.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Switch.CompositeSwitch
documentation: Composite switch to which this Switch belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Switch.SwitchPhase
documentation: The individual switch phases for the switch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Switch.SwitchSchedules
documentation: A Switch can be associated with SwitchSchedules.

A generic device designed to close, or open, or both, one or more electric
circuits. All switches are two terminal devices including grounding switches.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Cut

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Cut.lengthFromTerminal1
documentation:
The length to the place where the cut is located starting from side one
of the cut line segment, i.e. the line segment Terminal with sequenceNumber
equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Cut.ACLineSegment
documentation: The line segment to which the cut is applied.

A cut separates a line segment into two parts. The cut appears as a switch
inserted between these two parts and connects them together. As the cut
is normally open there is no galvanic connection between the two line segment
parts. But it is possible to close the cut to get galvanic connection.
The cut terminals are oriented towards the line segment terminals with
the same sequence number. Hence the cut terminal with sequence number equal
to 1 is oriented to the line segment's terminal with sequence number equal
to 1.
The cut terminals also act as connection points for jumpers and other equipment,
e.g. a mobile generator. To enable this, connectivity nodes are placed
at the cut terminals. Once the connectivity nodes are in place any conducting
equipment can be connected at them.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Disconnector
documentation:
A manually operated or motor operated mechanical switching device used
for changing the connections in a circuit, or for isolating a circuit or
equipment from a source of power. It is required to open or close circuits
when negligible current is broken or made.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Fuse
documentation:
An overcurrent protective device with a circuit opening fusible part that
is heated and severed by the passage of overcurrent through it. A fuse
is considered a switching device because it breaks current.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.GroundDisconnector
documentation:
A manually operated or motor operated mechanical switching device used
for isolating a circuit or equipment from ground.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Jumper
documentation:
A short section of conductor with negligible impedance which can be manually
removed and replaced if the circuit is de-energized. Note that zero-impedance
branches can potentially be modeled by other equipment types.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ProtectedSwitch

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ProtectedSwitch.breakingCapacity
documentation:
The maximum fault current a breaking device can break safely under prescribed
conditions of use.
A ProtectedSwitch is a switching device that can be operated by ProtectionEquipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Breaker

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Breaker.inTransitTime
documentation: The transition time from open to close.

A mechanical switching device capable of making, carrying, and breaking
currents under normal circuit conditions and also making, carrying for
a specified time, and breaking currents under specified abnormal circuit
conditions e.g. those of short circuit.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LoadBreakSwitch
documentation:
A mechanical switching device capable of making, carrying, and breaking
currents under normal operating conditions.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Recloser
documentation:
Pole-mounted fault interrupter with built-in phase and ground relays, current
transformer (CT), and supplemental controls.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Sectionaliser
documentation:
Automatic switch that will lock open to isolate a faulted section. It may,
or may not, have load breaking capability. Its primary purpose is to provide
fault sectionalising at locations where the fault current is either too
high, or too low, for proper coordination of fuses.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.GeneratingUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.GeneratingUnit.maxOperatingP
documentation:
This is the maximum operating active power limit the dispatcher can enter
for this unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.GeneratingUnit.minOperatingP
documentation:
This is the minimum operating active power limit the dispatcher can enter
for this unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.GeneratingUnit.GrossToNetActivePowerCurves
documentation:
A generating unit may have a gross active power to net active power curve,
describing the losses and auxiliary power requirements of the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.GeneratingUnit.RotatingMachine
documentation:
A synchronous machine may operate as a generator and as such becomes a
member of a generating unit.

A single or set of synchronous machines for converting mechanical power
into alternating-current power. For example, individual machines within
a set may be defined for scheduling purposes while a single control signal
is derived for the set. In this case there would be a GeneratingUnit for
each member of the set and an additional GeneratingUnit corresponding to
the set.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SolarGeneratingUnit
documentation:
A solar thermal generating unit, connected to the grid by means of a rotating
machine. This class does not represent photovoltaic (PV) generation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WindGeneratingUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WindGeneratingUnit.windGenUnitType
documentation: The kind of wind generating unit

A wind driven generating unit, connected to the grid by means of a rotating
machine. May be used to represent a single turbine or an aggregation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.HydroPump

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.HydroPump.RotatingMachine
documentation:
The synchronous machine drives the turbine which moves the water from a
low elevation to a higher elevation. The direction of machine rotation
for pumping may or may not be the same as for generating.

A synchronous motor-driven pump, typically associated with a pumped storage
plant.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerElectronicsUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerElectronicsUnit.maxP
documentation:
Maximum active power limit. This is the maximum (nameplate) limit for the
unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerElectronicsUnit.minP
documentation:
Minimum active power limit. This is the minimum (nameplate) limit for the
unit.

A generating unit or battery or aggregation that connects to the AC network
using power electronics rather than rotating machines.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BatteryUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BatteryUnit.ratedE
documentation: full energy storage capacity of the battery

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BatteryUnit.storedE
documentation: amount of energy currently stored; no more than ratedE

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BatteryUnit.batteryState
documentation: indicates whether the battery is charging, discharging or idle
An electrochemical energy storage device

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhotovoltaicUnit
documentation: A photovoltaic device or an aggregation of such devices

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerElectronicsWindUnit
documentation:
A wind generating unit that connects to the AC network with power electronics
rather than rotating machines or an aggregation of such units.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerTank

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerTank.PowerTransformer
documentation: Bank this transformer belongs to.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerTank.TransformerTankEnds
documentation: All windings of this transformer.

An assembly of two or more coupled windings that transform electrical power
between voltage levels. These windings are bound on a common core and place
in the same tank. Transformer tank can be used to model both single-phase
and 3-phase transformers.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerCutZone

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerCutZone.cutLevel1
documentation: First level (amount) of load to cut as a percentage of total zone load.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerCutZone.cutLevel2
documentation: Second level (amount) of load to cut as a percentage of total zone load.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerCutZone.EnergyConsumers
documentation: Energy consumer is assigned to the power cut zone.
An area or zone of the power system which is used for load shedding purposes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerElectronicsConnectionPhase.p
documentation:
Active power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow into the equipment from the network.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerElectronicsConnectionPhase.q
documentation:
Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow into the equipment from the network.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerElectronicsConnectionPhase.phase
documentation:
Phase of this energy producer component. If the energy producer is wye
connected, the connection is from the indicated phase to the central ground
or neutral point. If the energy producer is delta connected, the phase
indicates an energy producer connected from the indicated phase to the
next logical non-neutral phase.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegulatingControl

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegulatingControl.discrete
documentation:
The regulation is performed in a discrete mode. This applies to equipment
with discrete controls, e.g. tap changers and shunt compensators.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegulatingControl.enabled
documentation: The flag tells if regulation is enabled.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegulatingControl.targetDeadband
documentation:
This is a deadband used with discrete control to avoid excessive update
of controls like tap changers and shunt compensator banks while regulating.
The units of those appropriate for the mode.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegulatingControl.targetValue
documentation:
The target value specified for case input. This value can be used for the
target value without the use of schedules. The value has the units appropriate
to the mode attribute.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegulatingControl.mode
documentation:
The regulating control mode presently available. This specification allows
for determining the kind of regulation without need for obtaining the units
from a schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegulatingControl.monitoredPhase
documentation: Phase voltage controlling this regulator, measured at regulator location.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegulatingControl.RegulatingCondEq
documentation: The equipment that participates in this regulating control scheme.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegulatingControl.RegulationSchedule
documentation: Schedule for this Regulating regulating control.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegulatingControl.Terminal
documentation:
The terminal associated with this regulating control. The terminal is associated
instead of a node, since the terminal could connect into either a topological
node (bus in bus-branch model) or a connectivity node (detailed switch
model). Sometimes it is useful to model regulation at a terminal of a bus
bar object since the bus bar can be present in both a bus-branch model
or a model with switch detail.

Specifies a set of equipment that works together to control a power system
quantity such as voltage or flow.
Remote bus voltage control is possible by specifying the controlled terminal
located at some place remote from the controlling equipment.
In case multiple equipment, possibly of different types, control same terminal
there must be only one RegulatingControl at that terminal. The most specific
subtype of RegulatingControl shall be used in case such equipment participate
in the control, e.g. TapChangerControl for tap changers.
For flow control load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign means
flow out from a TopologicalNode (bus) into the conducting equipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChangerControl

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChangerControl.lineDropCompensation
documentation: If true, the line drop compensation is to be applied.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChangerControl.lineDropR
documentation: Line drop compensator resistance setting for normal (forward) power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChangerControl.lineDropX
documentation: Line drop compensator reactance setting for normal (forward) power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChangerControl.maxLimitVoltage
documentation:
Maximum allowed regulated voltage on the PT secondary, regardless of line
drop compensation. Sometimes referred to as first-house protection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChangerControl.reverseLineDropR
documentation: Line drop compensator resistance setting for reverse power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChangerControl.reverseLineDropX
documentation: Line drop compensator reactance setting for reverse power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChangerControl.TapChanger
documentation: The tap changers that participates in this regulating tap control scheme.

Describes behavior specific to tap changers, e.g. how the voltage at the
end of a line varies with the load level and compensation of the voltage
drop by tap adjustment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SubSchedulingArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SubSchedulingArea.BoundaryTerminals
documentation: The set of terminals that define the persistent boundaries of the SubSchedulingArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SubSchedulingArea.ContainedEquipment
documentation: The Equipment contained within the SubSchedulingArea.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SubSchedulingArea.SinkConfiguration
documentation: Set of possible configurations for the sink area.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SubSchedulingArea.SourceConfiguration
documentation: The set of possible configurations for the source area

A persistent connectivity-based containment of ConductingEquipment objects
with clearly-defined electrical boundaries forming a local power system
with one or more points of common coupling. Each piece of ConductingEquipment
can be associated with one ResourceContainer. The boundaries of the ResourceContainer
are specified through the Terminals of equipment forming the boundary (such
as a Recloser or PowerTransformer)

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DistributionArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DistributionArea.Feeders
documentation:
Feeders within the service territory of the DistributionArea of a particular
Distribution System Operator (DSO)

A persistent connectivity-based containment of medium-voltage and high-voltage
distribution ConductingEquipment with clearly defined electrical boundaries
based on electrical connectivity of a distribution substation or multiple
substations.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FeederArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FeederArea.Feeder
documentation:
The Feeder (which contains the ConnectivityNode and all Equipment) associated
with the FeeederArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FeederArea.SwitchAreas
documentation: The set of SwitchArea normally energized by the FeederArea

A persistent connectivity-based containment of medium-voltage distribution
ConductingEquipment with clearly defined electrical boundaries based on
electrical connectivity of a distribution feeder.
The FeederArea contains all medium voltage equipment not contained in a
SwitchArea or Substation / Bay. It also includes all Sectionalisers, Reclosers,
and all other poletop and pad-mounted switchgear that form the boundary
of a SwitchArea.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SecondaryArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SecondaryArea.primaryPhase
documentation:
Used to represent the ABC phase to which the secondary split-phase transformer
is connected in North American systems. For secondary areas served by a
center-tap transformer, the phase connection of equipment will generally
be SinglePhaseKind.s1 or SinglePhaseKind.s2, and it is not readily apparent
what phase serves the loads at the medium voltage level.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SecondaryArea.SwitchArea
documentation: The SwitchArea that normally energizes the SecondaryArea

A persistent connectivity-based containment of low-voltage distribution
ConductingEquipment with clearly defined electrical boundaries formed by
one or more PowerTransformer objects.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SwitchArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SwitchArea.FeederArea
documentation: The FeederArea that normally energizes the SwitchArea

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SwitchArea.SecondaryAreas
documentation: The set of SecondAreas normally energized by the SwitchArea

A persistent connectivity-based containment of medium-voltage distribution
ConductingEquipment with clearly defined electrical boundaries formed by
one or more Switch objects.
The SwitchArea contains all conductors, fuses, poletop equipment, and vault
equipment. It also contains all secondary service transformers not contained
in a SecondarySubstation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Microgrid
documentation:
A persistent connectivity-based containment of distribution ConductingEquipment
that 1) has clearly-defined electrical boundaries formed by one or more
point of common coupling Switch objects and 2) that acts as a single controllable
entity which can be operated in grid-connected or islanded mode.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShuntCompensatorPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShuntCompensatorPhase.maximumSections
documentation: The maximum number of sections that may be switched in for this phase.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShuntCompensatorPhase.normalSections
documentation: For the capacitor phase, the normal number of sections switched in.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShuntCompensatorPhase.sections
documentation:
Number of sections in use for this phase, when controlled independently
from the other phases. If not provided, may default to the parent ShuntCompensator.sections
value (see ShuntCompensator documentation for more details).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShuntCompensatorPhase.phase
documentation:
Phase of this shunt compensator component. If the shunt compensator is
wye connected, the connection is from the indicated phase to the central
ground or neutral point. If the shunt compensator is delta connected, the
phase indicates a shunt compensator connected from the indicated phase
to the next logical non-neutral phase.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShuntCompensatorPhase.ShuntCompensator
documentation: Shunt compensator of this shunt compensator phase.

Single phase of a multi-phase shunt compensator when its attributes might
be different per phase.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LinearShuntCompensatorPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LinearShuntCompensatorPhase.bPerSection
documentation:
Susceptance per section of the phase if shunt compensator is wye connected.
Susceptance per section phase to phase if shunt compensator is delta connected.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.LinearShuntCompensatorPhase.gPerSection
documentation:
Conductance per section for this phase if shunt compensator is wye connected.
Conductance per section phase to phase if shunt compensator is delta connected.

A per phase linear shunt compensator has banks or sections with equal admittance
values.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhase.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoints
documentation: All points of the non-linear shunt compensator phase.

A per phase non linear shunt compensator has bank or section admittance
values that differs.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SwitchPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SwitchPhase.closed
documentation:
The attribute tells if the switch is considered closed when used as input
to topology processing.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SwitchPhase.normalOpen
documentation:
Used in cases when no Measurement for the status value is present. If the
SwitchPhase has a status measurement the Discrete.normalValue is expected
to match with this value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SwitchPhase.ratedCurrent
documentation: Rated current through this phase, if different from the others.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SwitchPhase.phaseSide1
documentation:
Phase of this SwitchPhase on the side with terminal sequence number equal
1. Should be a phase contained in that terminal’s phases attribute.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SwitchPhase.phaseSide2
documentation:
Phase of this SwitchPhase on the side with terminal sequence number equal
2. Should be a phase contained in that terminal’s Terminal.phases
attribute.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SwitchPhase.Switch
documentation: The switch of the switch phase.

Single phase of a multi-phase switch when its attributes might be different
per phase.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChanger

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChanger.controlEnabled
documentation:
Specifies the regulation status of the equipment. True is regulating, false
is not regulating.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChanger.highStep
documentation:
Highest possible tap step position, advance from neutral.
The attribute shall be greater than lowStep.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChanger.lowStep
documentation: Lowest possible tap step position, retard from neutral

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChanger.ltcFlag
documentation: Specifies whether or not a TapChanger has load tap changing capabilities.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChanger.neutralStep
documentation:
The neutral tap step position for this winding.
The attribute shall be equal or greater than lowStep and equal or less
than highStep.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChanger.normalStep
documentation:
The tap step position used in "normal" network operation for this winding.
For a "Fixed" tap changer indicates the current physical tap setting.
The attribute shall be equal or greater than lowStep and equal or less
than highStep.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChanger.step
documentation:
Tap changer position.
Starting step for a steady state solution. Non integer values are allowed
to support continuous tap variables. The reasons for continuous value are
to support study cases where no discrete tap changers has yet been designed,
a solutions where a narrow voltage band force the tap step to oscillate
or accommodate for a continuous solution as input.
The attribute shall be equal or greater than lowStep and equal or less
than highStep.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChanger.initialDelay
documentation: For an LTC, the delay for initial tap changer operation (first step change)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChanger.neutralU
documentation: Voltage at which the winding operates at the neutral tap setting.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChanger.subsequentDelay
documentation:
For an LTC, the delay for subsequent tap changer operation (second and
later step changes)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChanger.SvTapStep
documentation: The tap step state associated with the tap changer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChanger.TapChangerControl
documentation: The regulating control scheme in which this tap changer participates.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChanger.TapSchedules
documentation: A TapChanger can have TapSchedules.
Mechanism for changing transformer winding tap positions.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChanger

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChanger.TransformerEnd
documentation: Transformer end to which this phase tap changer belongs.

A transformer phase shifting tap model that controls the phase angle difference
across the power transformer and potentially the active power flow through
the power transformer. This phase tap model may also impact the voltage
magnitude.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChangerLinear

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChangerLinear.stepPhaseShiftIncrement
documentation:
Phase shift per step position. A positive value indicates a positive phase
shift from the winding where the tap is located to the other winding (for
a two-winding transformer).
The actual phase shift increment might be more accurately computed from
the symmetrical or asymmetrical models or a tap step table lookup if those
are available.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChangerLinear.xMax
documentation:
The reactance depend on the tap position according to a "u" shaped curve.
The maximum reactance (xMax) appear at the low and high tap positions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChangerLinear.xMin
documentation:
The reactance depend on the tap position according to a "u" shaped curve.
The minimum reactance (xMin) appear at the mid tap position.

Describes a tap changer with a linear relation between the tap step and
the phase angle difference across the transformer. This is a mathematical
model that is an approximation of a real phase tap changer.
The phase angle is computed as stepPhaseShitfIncrement times the tap position.
The secondary side voltage magnitude is the same as at the primary side.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChangerNonLinear

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChangerNonLinear.voltageStepIncrement
documentation:
