The voltage step increment on the out of phase winding specified in percent
of neutral voltage of the tap changer.
When the increment is negative, the voltage decreases when the tap step
increases.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChangerNonLinear.xMax
documentation:
The reactance depend on the tap position according to a "u" shaped curve.
The maximum reactance (xMax) appear at the low and high tap positions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChangerNonLinear.xMin
documentation:
The reactance depend on the tap position according to a "u" shaped curve.
The minimum reactance (xMin) appear at the mid tap position.

The non-linear phase tap changer describes the non-linear behavior of a
phase tap changer. This is a base class for the symmetrical and asymmetrical
phase tap changer models. The details of these models can be found in the
IEC 61970-301 document.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChangerAsymmetrical

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChangerAsymmetrical.windingConnectionAngle
documentation:
The phase angle between the in-phase winding and the out-of -phase winding
used for creating phase shift. The out-of-phase winding produces what is
known as the difference voltage. Setting this angle to 90 degrees is not
the same as a symmetrical transformer.

Describes the tap model for an asymmetrical phase shifting transformer
in which the difference voltage vector adds to the primary side voltage.
The angle between the primary side voltage and the difference voltage is
named the winding connection angle. The phase shift depends on both the
difference voltage magnitude and the winding connection angle.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChangerSymmetrical
documentation:
Describes a symmetrical phase shifting transformer tap model in which the
secondary side voltage magnitude is the same as at the primary side. The
difference voltage magnitude is the base in an equal-sided triangle where
the sides corresponds to the primary and secondary voltages. The phase
angle difference corresponds to the top angle and can be expressed as twice
the arctangent of half the total difference voltage.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChangerTabular.PhaseTapChangerTable
documentation: The phase tap changer table for this phase tap changer.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RatioTapChanger

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RatioTapChanger.stepVoltageIncrement
documentation:
Tap step increment, in per cent of neutral voltage, per step position.
When the increment is negative, the voltage decreases when the tap step
increases.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RatioTapChanger.RatioTapChangerTable
documentation: The tap ratio table for this ratio tap changer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RatioTapChanger.TransformerEnd
documentation: Transformer end to which this ratio tap changer belongs.

A tap changer that changes the voltage ratio impacting the voltage magnitude
but not the phase angle across the transformer.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.VoltageControlZone

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.VoltageControlZone.BusbarSection
documentation: A VoltageControlZone is controlled by a designated BusbarSection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.VoltageControlZone.RegulationSchedule
documentation: A VoltageControlZone may have a voltage regulation schedule.

An area of the power system network which is defined for secondary voltage
control purposes. A voltage control zone consists of a collection of substations
with a designated bus bar section whose voltage will be controlled.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ProductAssetModel

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ProductAssetModel.Asset
documentation: An asset of this model.
Asset model by a specific manufacturer.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RatioTapChangerTable

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RatioTapChangerTable.RatioTapChanger
documentation: The ratio tap changer of this tap ratio table.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RatioTapChangerTable.RatioTapChangerTablePoint
documentation: Points of this table.

Describes a curve for how the voltage magnitude and impedance varies with
the tap step.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ReportingGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ReportingGroup.BusNameMarker
documentation: The bus name markers that belong to this reporting group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ReportingGroup.PowerSystemResource
documentation: Power system resources which belong to this reporting group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ReportingGroup.ReportingSuperGroup
documentation: Reporting super group to which this reporting group belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ReportingGroup.TopologicalNode
documentation: The topological nodes that belong to the reporting group.
A reporting group is used for various ad-hoc groupings used for reporting.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ReportingSuperGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ReportingSuperGroup.ReportingGroup
documentation: Reporting groups that are grouped under this super group.
A reporting super group, groups reporting groups for a higher level report.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Season

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Season.endDate
documentation: Date season ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Season.startDate
documentation: Date season starts.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Season.SeasonDayTypeSchedules
documentation: Schedules that use this Season.
A specified time period of the year.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SubGeographicalRegion

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SubGeographicalRegion.Lines
documentation: The lines within the sub-geographical region.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SubGeographicalRegion.Region
documentation: The geographical region to which this sub-geographical region is within.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SubGeographicalRegion.Substations
documentation: The substations in this sub-geographical region.
A subset of a geographical region of a power system network model.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ThermostatController

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ThermostatController.aggregatorName
documentation:
name of a market aggregator that collects bid curves for a higher-level
market

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ThermostatController.rampHigh
documentation: slope of high-temperature bidding curve, $/degreeC

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ThermostatController.rampLow
documentation: slope of low-temperature bidding curve, $/degreeC

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ThermostatController.baseSetpoint
documentation:
user's desired thermostat setpoint, including the effects of pre-programmed
schedule

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ThermostatController.priceCap
documentation:
maximum price per kwh that the controller will bid, regardless of the market's
price cap

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ThermostatController.rangeHigh
documentation: maximum postive offset to the thermostat setpoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ThermostatController.rangeLow
documentation: maximum negative offset to the thermostat setpoint
a price-responsive or bidding smart thermostat

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TopologicalIsland

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TopologicalIsland.AngleRefTopologicalNode
documentation:
The angle reference for the island. Normally there is one TopologicalNode
that is selected as the angle reference for each island. Other reference
schemes exist, so the association is typically optional.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TopologicalIsland.TopologicalNodes
documentation: A topological node belongs to a topological island.

An electrically connected subset of the network. Topological islands can
change as the current network state changes: e.g. due to
- disconnect switches or breakers change state in a SCADA/EMS
- manual creation, change or deletion of topological nodes in a planning
tool.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TopologicalNode

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TopologicalNode.pInjection
documentation:
The active power injected into the bus at this location in addition to
injections from equipment. Positive sign means injection into the TopologicalNode
(bus).
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TopologicalNode.qInjection
documentation:
The reactive power injected into the bus at this location in addition to
injections from equipment. Positive sign means injection into the TopologicalNode
(bus).
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TopologicalNode.AngleRefTopologicalIsland
documentation:
The island for which the node is an angle reference. Normally there is
one angle reference node for each island.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TopologicalNode.BaseVoltage
documentation: The base voltage of the topologocial node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TopologicalNode.BusNameMarker
documentation: BusnameMarkers that may refer to a pre defined TopologicalNode.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TopologicalNode.ConnectivityNodeContainer
documentation: The connectivity node container to which the toplogical node belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TopologicalNode.ConnectivityNodes
documentation:
The connectivity nodes combine together to form this topological node.
May depend on the current state of switches in the network.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TopologicalNode.ReportingGroup
documentation: The reporting group to which the topological node belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TopologicalNode.Terminal
documentation:
The terminals associated with the topological node. This can be used as
an alternative to the connectivity node path to terminal, thus making it
unneccesary to model connectivity nodes in some cases. Note that if connectivity
nodes are in the model, this association would probably not be used as
an input specification.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TopologicalNode.TopologicalIsland
documentation: A topological node belongs to a topological island.

For a detailed substation model a topological node is a set of connectivity
nodes that, in the current network state, are connected together through
any type of closed switches, including jumpers. Topological nodes change
as the current network state changes (i.e., switches, breakers, etc. change
state).
For a planning model, switch statuses are not used to form topological
nodes. Instead they are manually created or deleted in a model builder
tool. Topological nodes maintained this way are also called "busses".

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerCoreAdmittance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerCoreAdmittance.b
documentation: Magnetizing branch susceptance (B mag). The value can be positive or negative.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerCoreAdmittance.b0
documentation: Zero sequence magnetizing branch susceptance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerCoreAdmittance.g
documentation: Magnetizing branch conductance (G mag).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerCoreAdmittance.g0
documentation: Zero sequence magnetizing branch conductance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerCoreAdmittance.TransformerEnd
documentation: All transformer ends having this core admittance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerCoreAdmittance.TransformerEndInfo
documentation: Transformer end datasheet used to calculate this core admittance.

The transformer core admittance. Used to specify the core admittance of
a transformer in a manner that can be shared among power transformers.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEnd

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEnd.endNumber
documentation:
Number for this transformer end, corresponding to the end's order in the
power transformer vector group or phase angle clock number. Highest voltage
winding should be 1. Each end within a power transformer should have a
unique subsequent end number. Note the transformer end number need not
match the terminal sequence number.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEnd.grounded
documentation: (for Yn and Zn connections) True if the neutral is solidly grounded.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEnd.rground
documentation:
(for Yn and Zn connections) Resistance part of neutral impedance where
'grounded' is true.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEnd.xground
documentation:
(for Yn and Zn connections) Reactive part of neutral impedance where 'grounded'
is true.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEnd.BaseVoltage
documentation: Base voltage of the transformer end. This is essential for PU calculation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEnd.CoreAdmittance
documentation:
Core admittance of this transformer end, representing magnetising current
and core losses. The full values of the transformer should be supplied
for one transformer end only.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEnd.FromMeshImpedance
documentation: All mesh impedances between this 'to' and other 'from' transformer ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEnd.PhaseTapChanger
documentation: Phase tap changer associated with this transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEnd.RatioTapChanger
documentation: Ratio tap changer associated with this transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEnd.StarImpedance
documentation:
(accurate for 2- or 3-winding transformers only) Pi-model impedances of
this transformer end. By convention, for a two winding transformer, the
full values of the transformer should be entered on the high voltage end
(endNumber=1).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEnd.Terminal
documentation: Terminal of the power transformer to which this transformer end belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerEnd.ToMeshImpedance
documentation: All mesh impedances between this 'from' and other 'to' transformer ends.

A conducting connection point of a power transformer. It corresponds to
a physical transformer winding terminal. In earlier CIM versions, the TransformerWinding
class served a similar purpose, but this class is more flexible because
it associates to terminal but is not a specialization of ConductingEquipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerTransformerEnd

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerTransformerEnd.phaseAngleClock
documentation:
Terminal voltage phase angle displacement where 360 degrees are represented
with clock hours. The valid values are 0 to 11. For example, for the secondary
side end of a transformer with vector group code of 'Dyn11', specify the
connection kind as wye with neutral and specify the phase angle of the
clock as 11. The clock value of the transformer end number specified as
1, is assumed to be zero. Note the transformer end number is not assumed
to be the same as the terminal sequence number.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerTransformerEnd.connectionKind
documentation: Kind of connection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerTransformerEnd.r
documentation:
Resistance (star-model) of the transformer end.
The attribute shall be equal or greater than zero for non-equivalent transformers.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerTransformerEnd.ratedS
documentation:
Normal apparent power rating.
The attribute shall be a positive value. For a two-winding transformer
the values for the high and low voltage sides shall be identical.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerTransformerEnd.ratedU
documentation:
Rated voltage: phase-phase for three-phase windings, and either phase-phase
or phase-neutral for single-phase windings.
A high voltage side, as given by TransformerEnd.endNumber, shall have a
ratedU that is greater or equal than ratedU for the lower voltage sides.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PowerTransformerEnd.PowerTransformer
documentation: The power transformer of this power transformer end.

A PowerTransformerEnd is associated with each Terminal of a PowerTransformer.
The impedance values r, r0, x, and x0 of a PowerTransformerEnd represents
a star equivalent as follows
1) for a two Terminal PowerTransformer the high voltage (TransformerEnd.endNumber=1)
PowerTransformerEnd has non zero values on r, r0, x, and x0 while the low
voltage (TransformerEnd.endNumber=0) PowerTransformerEnd has zero values
for r, r0, x, and x0.
2) for a three Terminal PowerTransformer the three PowerTransformerEnds
represents a star equivalent with each leg in the star represented by r,
r0, x, and x0 values.
3) For a three Terminal transformer each PowerTransformerEnd shall have
g, g0, b and b0 values corresponding the no load losses distributed on
the three PowerTransformerEnds. The total no load loss shunt impedances
may also be placed at one of the PowerTransformerEnds, preferably the end
numbered 1, having the shunt values on end 1 is the preferred way.
4) for a PowerTransformer with more than three Terminals the PowerTransformerEnd
impedance values cannot be used. Instead use the TransformerMeshImpedance
or split the transformer into multiple PowerTransformers.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerTankEnd

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerTankEnd.orderedPhases
documentation:
Identifies the phases present and the order of their connection on this
winding (end) of the transformer. In some use cases, such as open-wye,
open-delta transformers and single-phase, center-tap secondary transformers,
the order of phase connection is important, so the OrderedPhaseCodeKind
enumeration is used instead of PhaseCode.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerTankEnd.TransformerTank
documentation: Transformer this winding belongs to.

Transformer tank end represents an individual winding for unbalanced models
or for transformer tanks connected into a bank (and bank is modelled with
the PowerTransformer).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerMeshImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerMeshImpedance.r
documentation: Resistance between the 'from' and the 'to' end, seen from the 'from' end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerMeshImpedance.r0
documentation:
Zero-sequence resistance between the 'from' and the 'to' end, seen from
the 'from' end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerMeshImpedance.x
documentation: Reactance between the 'from' and the 'to' end, seen from the 'from' end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerMeshImpedance.x0
documentation:
Zero-sequence reactance between the 'from' and the 'to' end, seen from
the 'from' end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerMeshImpedance.FromTransformerEnd
documentation:
From end this mesh impedance is connected to. It determines the voltage
reference.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerMeshImpedance.FromTransformerEndInfo
documentation:
'from' transformer end datasheet this mesh impedance is calculated from.
It determines the voltage reference.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerMeshImpedance.ToTransformerEnd
documentation: All transformer ends this mesh impedance is connected to.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerMeshImpedance.ToTransformerEndInfos
documentation:
All 'to' transformer end datasheets this mesh impedance for 'from' transformer
end is calculated from.

Transformer mesh impedance (Delta-model) between transformer ends.
The typical case is that this class describes the impedance between two
transformer ends pair-wise, i.e. the cardinalities at both tranformer end
associations are 1. But in cases where two or more transformer ends are
modeled the cardinalities are larger than 1.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerStarImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerStarImpedance.r
documentation: Resistance of the transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerStarImpedance.x
documentation: Positive sequence series reactance of the transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerStarImpedance.x0
documentation: Zero sequence series reactance of the transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerStarImpedance.TransformerEnd
documentation: All transformer ends having this star impedance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerStarImpedance.TransformerEndInfo
documentation: Transformer end datasheet used to calculate this transformer star impedance.

Transformer star impedance (Pi-model) that accurately reflects impedance
for transformers with 2 or 3 windings. For transformers with 4 or more
windings, you must use TransformerMeshImpedance class.
For transmission networks use PowerTransformerEnd impedances (r, r0, x,
x0, b, b0, g and g0).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerTest

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerTest.basePower
documentation:
Base power at which the tests are conducted, usually equal to the rateds
of one of the involved transformer ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TransformerTest.temperature
documentation: Temperature at which the test is conducted.

Test result for transformer ends, such as short-circuit, open-circuit (excitation)
or no-load test.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NoLoadTest

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NoLoadTest.energisedEndVoltage
documentation: Voltage applied to the winding (end) during test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NoLoadTest.excitingCurrent
documentation:
Exciting current measured from a positive-sequence or single-phase excitation
test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NoLoadTest.excitingCurrentZero
documentation:
Exciting current measured from a zero-sequence open-circuit excitation
test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NoLoadTest.loss
documentation: Losses measured from a positive-sequence or single-phase excitation test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NoLoadTest.lossZero
documentation: Losses measured from a zero-sequence excitation test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NoLoadTest.EnergisedEnd
documentation: Transformer end that current is applied to in this no-load test.

No-load test results determine core admittance parameters. They include
exciting current and core loss measurements from applying voltage to one
winding. The excitation may be positive sequence or zero sequence. The
test may be repeated at different voltages to measure saturation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OpenCircuitTest

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OpenCircuitTest.energisedEndStep
documentation: Tap step number for the energised end of the test pair.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OpenCircuitTest.openEndStep
documentation: Tap step number for the open end of the test pair.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OpenCircuitTest.energisedEndVoltage
documentation: Voltage applied to the winding (end) during test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OpenCircuitTest.openEndVoltage
documentation:
Voltage measured at the open-circuited end, with the energised end set
to rated voltage and all other ends open.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OpenCircuitTest.phaseShift
documentation:
Phase shift measured at the open end with the energised end set to rated
voltage and all other ends open.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OpenCircuitTest.EnergisedEnd
documentation: Transformer end that current is applied to in this open-circuit test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OpenCircuitTest.OpenEnd
documentation:
Transformer end measured for induced voltage and angle in this open-circuit
test.

Open-circuit test results verify winding turn ratios and phase shifts.
They include induced voltage and phase shift measurements on open-circuit
windings, with voltage applied to the energised end. For three-phase windings,
the excitation can be a positive sequence (the default) or a zero sequence.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShortCircuitTest

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShortCircuitTest.energisedEndStep
documentation: Tap step number for the energised end of the test pair.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShortCircuitTest.groundedEndStep
documentation: Tap step number for the grounded end of the test pair.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShortCircuitTest.leakageImpedance
documentation:
Leakage impedance measured from a positive-sequence or single-phase short-circuit
test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShortCircuitTest.leakageImpedanceZero
documentation: Leakage impedance measured from a zero-sequence short-circuit test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShortCircuitTest.loss
documentation: Load losses from a positive-sequence or single-phase short-circuit test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShortCircuitTest.lossZero
documentation: Load losses from a zero-sequence short-circuit test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShortCircuitTest.EnergisedEnd
documentation:
Transformer end that voltage is applied to in this short-circuit test.
The test voltage is chosen to induce rated current in the energised end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ShortCircuitTest.GroundedEnds
documentation: All ends short-circuited in this short-circuit test.

Short-circuit test results determine mesh impedance parameters. They include
load losses and leakage impedances. For three-phase windings, the excitation
can be a positive sequence (the default) or a zero sequence. There shall
be at least one grounded winding.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.UsagePointGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.UsagePointGroup.type
documentation: Type of this group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.UsagePointGroup.DemandResponsePrograms
documentation: All demand response programs this usage point group is enrolled in.

Abstraction for management of group communications within a two-way AMR
system or the data for a group of related usage points. Commands can be
issued to all of the usage points that belong to a usage point group using
a defined group address and the underlying AMR communication infrastructure.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ValueAliasSet
documentation:
Describes the translation of a set of values into a name and is intendend
to facilitate cusom translations. Each ValueAliasSet has a name, description
etc. A specific Measurement may represent a discrete state like Open, Closed,
Intermediate etc. This requires a translation from the MeasurementValue.value
number to a string, e.g. 0->"Invalid", 1->"Open", 2->"Closed",
3->"Intermediate". Each ValueToAlias member in ValueAliasSet.Value describe
a mapping for one particular value to a name.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ValueToAlias

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ValueToAlias.value
documentation: The value that is mapped.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.ValueToAlias.ValueAliasSet
documentation: The ValueAliasSet having the ValueToAlias mappings.

Describes the translation of one particular value into a name, e.g. 1 as
"Open".

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WirePosition

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WirePosition.sequenceNumber
documentation:
Numbering for wires on a WireSpacingInfo. Neutrals should be numbered last.
Multiple circuits on the same pole, tower or right-of-way can be included
with unique sequence numbers for the phases, and identical sequence numbers
for any shared neutrals.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WirePosition.xCoord
documentation:
Signed horizontal distance from the wire at this position to a common reference
point.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WirePosition.yCoord
documentation:
Signed vertical distance from the wire at this position: above ground (positive
value) or burial depth below ground (negative value).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.WirePosition.WireSpacingInfo
documentation: Wire spacing data this wire position belongs to.

Identification, spacing and configuration of the wires of a conductor with
respect to a structure.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AreaConfiguration

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AreaConfiguration.priority
documentation:
Value 0 means ignore priority. 1 means the highest priority, 2 is the second
highest priority.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AreaConfiguration.EnergizedArea
documentation: The sink area being energized by the source area.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AreaConfiguration.EnergizingArea
documentation: The source area which is energizing the sink area

Alternate configurations for abnormal feeder switching conditions. The
distribution feeder can be segmented into source and sink SubSchedulingArea
to represent upstream and downstream sections relative to the head terminal.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AssetOrganisationRole

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.AssetOrganisationRole.Assets
documentation: All assets for this organisation role.
Role an organisation plays with respect to asset.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BranchGroupTerminal

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BranchGroupTerminal.positiveFlowIn
documentation:
The flow into the terminal is summed if set true. The flow out of the terminanl
is summed if set false.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BranchGroupTerminal.BranchGroup
documentation: The branch group to which the directed branch group terminals belong.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.BranchGroupTerminal.Terminal
documentation: The terminal to be summed.
A specific directed terminal flow for a branch group.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CurveData

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CurveData.xvalue
documentation: The data value of the X-axis variable, depending on the X-axis units.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.CurveData.y1value
documentation: The data value of the first Y-axis variable, depending on the Y-axis units.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Name

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Name.name
documentation: Any free text that name the object.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Name.IdentifiedObject
documentation: Identified object that this name designates.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Name.NameType
documentation: Type of this name.

The Name class provides the means to define any number of human readable names for an object. A name is not to be used for defining inter-object relationships. For inter-object relationships instead use the object identification 'mRID'.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NameType

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NameType.description
documentation: Description of the name type.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NameType.name
documentation: Name of the name type.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NameType.Names
documentation: All names of this type.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.NameType.NameTypeAuthority
documentation: Authority responsible for managing names of this type.

Type of name. Possible values for attribute 'name' are implementation dependent
but standard profiles may specify types. An enterprise may have multiple
IT systems each having its own local name for the same object, e.g. a planning
system may have different names from an EMS. An object may also have different
names within the same IT system, e.g. localName as defined in CIM version
14. The definition from CIM14 is:
The localName is a human readable name of the object. It is a free text
name local to a node in a naming hierarchy similar to a file directory
structure. A power system related naming hierarchy may be: Substation,
VoltageLevel, Equipment etc. Children of the same parent in such a hierarchy
have names that typically are unique among them.

typically an organization or an enterprise system.

Percentage operational ownership between the pair (power system resource
and operatging participant) associated with this share. The total percentage

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseImpedanceData

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseImpedanceData.column
documentation:
This matrix element's column number, in the range 1 to row. Only the lower
triangle needs to be stored. Neutrals should be numbered last. Multiple
circuits on the same pole, tower or right-of-way can be included with unique
sequence numbers for the phases, and identical sequence numbers for any
shared neutrals. This solumn number matches ACLineSegmentPhase.sequenceNumber,
WirePosition.sequenceNumber and WirePhaseInfo.sequenceNumber as applicable..

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseImpedanceData.row
documentation:
This matrix element's row number, in the range 1 to PerLengthPhaseImpedance.conductorCount.
Only the lower triangle needs to be stored. Neutrals should be numbered
last. Multiple circuits on the same pole, tower or right-of-way can be
included with unique sequence numbers for the phases, and identical sequence
numbers for any shared neutrals. This row number matches ACLineSegmentPhase.sequenceNumber,
WirePosition.sequenceNumber and WirePhaseInfo.sequenceNumber as applicable..

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseImpedanceData.b
documentation: Susceptance matrix element value, per length of unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseImpedanceData.g
documentation: Conductance matrix element value, per length of unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseImpedanceData.r
documentation: Resistance matrix element value, per length of unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseImpedanceData.x
documentation: Reactance matrix element value, per length of unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseImpedanceData.PhaseImpedance
documentation: Conductor phase impedance to which this data belongs.

Impedance and conductance matrix element values.
The diagonal elements are described by the elements having the same toPhase
and fromPhase value and the off diagonal elements have different toPhase
and fromPhase values. The matrix can also be stored in symmetric lower
triangular format using the row and column attributes, which map to ACLineSegmentPhase.sequenceNumber.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PositionPoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PositionPoint.groupNumber
cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PositionPoint.sequenceNumber
documentation: Zero-relative sequence number of this point within a series of points.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PositionPoint.xPosition
documentation: X axis position.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PositionPoint.yPosition
documentation: Y axis position.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PositionPoint.zPosition
documentation: (if applicable) Z axis position.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PositionPoint.Location
documentation: Location described by this position point.

Set of spatial coordinates that determine a point, defined in the coordinate
system specified in 'Location.CoordinateSystem'. Use a single position
point instance to desribe a point-oriented location. Use a sequence of
position points to describe a line-oriented object (physical location of
non-point oriented objects like cables or lines), or area of an object
(like a substation or a geographical zone - in this case, have first and
last position point with the same values).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Quality61850

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Quality61850.badReference
documentation: Measurement value may be incorrect due to a reference being out of calibration.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Quality61850.estimatorReplaced
documentation:
Value has been replaced by State Estimator. estimatorReplaced is not an
IEC61850 quality bit but has been put in this class for convenience.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Quality61850.failure
documentation:
This identifier indicates that a supervision function has detected an internal
or external failure, e.g. communication failure.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Quality61850.oldData
documentation:
Measurement value is old and possibly invalid, as it has not been successfully
updated during a specified time interval.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Quality61850.operatorBlocked
documentation: Measurement value is blocked and hence unavailable for transmission.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Quality61850.oscillatory
documentation:
To prevent some overload of the communication it is sensible to detect
and suppress oscillating (fast changing) binary inputs. If a signal changes
in a defined time (tosc) twice in the same direction (from 0 to 1 or from
1 to 0) then oscillation is detected and the detail quality identifier
"oscillatory" is set. If it is detected a configured numbers of transient
changes could be passed by. In this time the validity status "questionable"
is set. If after this defined numbers of changes the signal is still in
the oscillating state the value shall be set either to the opposite state
of the previous stable value or to a defined default value. In this case
the validity status "questionable" is reset and "invalid" is set as long
as the signal is oscillating. If it is configured such that no transient
changes should be passed by then the validity status "invalid" is set immediately
in addition to the detail quality identifier "oscillatory" (used for status
information only).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Quality61850.outOfRange
documentation: Measurement value is beyond a predefined range of value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Quality61850.overFlow
documentation:
Measurement value is beyond the capability of being represented properly.
For example, a counter value overflows from maximum count back to a value
of zero.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Quality61850.suspect
documentation:
A correlation function has detected that the value is not consitent with
other values. Typically set by a network State Estimator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Quality61850.test
documentation: Measurement value is transmitted for test purposes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Quality61850.source
documentation:
Source gives information related to the origin of a value. The value may
be acquired from the process, defaulted or substituted.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Quality61850.validity
documentation: Validity of the measurement value.

Quality flags in this class are as defined in IEC 61850, except for estimatorReplaced,
which has been included in this class for convenience.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MeasurementValueQuality

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.MeasurementValueQuality.MeasurementValue
documentation: A MeasurementValue has a MeasurementValueQuality associated with it.

Measurement quality flags. Bits 0-10 are defined for substation automation
in draft IEC 61850 part 7-3. Bits 11-15 are reserved for future expansion
by that document. Bits 16-31 are reserved for EMS applications.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegularTimePoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegularTimePoint.sequenceNumber
documentation:
The position of the regular time point in the sequence. Note that time
points don't have to be sequential, i.e. time points may be omitted. The
actual time for a RegularTimePoint is computed by multiplying the associated
regular interval schedule's time step with the regular time point sequence
number and adding the associated schedules start time.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegularTimePoint.value1
documentation:
The first value at the time. The meaning of the value is defined by the
derived type of the associated schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegularTimePoint.value2
documentation:
The second value at the time. The meaning of the value is defined by the
derived type of the associated schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.RegularTimePoint.IntervalSchedule
documentation: Regular interval schedule containing this time point.

Time point for a schedule where the time between the consecutive points
is constant.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SvShuntCompensatorSections

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SvShuntCompensatorSections.sections
documentation:
The number of sections in service as a continous variable. To get integer
value scale with ShuntCompensator.bPerSection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SvShuntCompensatorSections.phase
documentation:
The terminal phase at which the connection is applied. If missing, the
injection is assumed to be balanced among non-neutral phases.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SvShuntCompensatorSections.ShuntCompensator
documentation: The shunt compensator for which the state applies.
State variable for the number of sections in service for a shunt compensator.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SvTapStep

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SvTapStep.position
documentation:
The floating point tap position. This is not the tap ratio, but rather
the tap step position as defined by the related tap changer model and normally
is constrained to be within the range of minimum and maximum tap positions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.SvTapStep.TapChanger
documentation: The tap changer associated with the tap step state.
State variable for transformer tap step.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChangerTablePoint.ratio
documentation:
The voltage at the tap step divided by rated voltage of the transformer
end having the tap changer. Hence this is a value close to one.
For example, if the ratio at step 1 is 1.01, and the rated voltage of the
transformer end is 110kV, then the voltage obtained by setting the tap
changer to step 1 to is 111.1kV.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChangerTablePoint.step
documentation: The tap step.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChangerTablePoint.b
documentation:
The magnetizing branch susceptance deviation in percent of nominal value.
The actual susceptance is calculated as follows:
calculated magnetizing susceptance = b(nominal) * (1 + b(from this class)/100).
The b(nominal) is defined as the static magnetizing susceptance on the
associated power transformer end or ends. This model assumes the star impedance
(pi model) form.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChangerTablePoint.g
documentation:
The magnetizing branch conductance deviation in percent of nominal value.
The actual conductance is calculated as follows:
calculated magnetizing conductance = g(nominal) * (1 + g(from this class)/100).
The g(nominal) is defined as the static magnetizing conductance on the
associated power transformer end or ends. This model assumes the star impedance
(pi model) form.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChangerTablePoint.r
documentation:
The resistance deviation in percent of nominal value. The actual reactance
is calculated as follows:
calculated resistance = r(nominal) * (1 + r(from this class)/100). The
r(nominal) is defined as the static resistance on the associated power
transformer end or ends. This model assumes the star impedance (pi model)
form.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.TapChangerTablePoint.x
documentation:
The series reactance deviation in percent of nominal value. The actual
reactance is calculated as follows:
calculated reactance = x(nominal) * (1 + x(from this class)/100). The x(nominal)
is defined as the static series reactance on the associated power transformer
end or ends. This model assumes the star impedance (pi model) form.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChangerTablePoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChangerTablePoint.angle

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseTapChangerTablePoint.PhaseTapChangerTable
documentation: The table of this point.
Describes each tap step in the phase tap changer tabular curve.

documentation: Quantity power, Q.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.Qh
documentation: Quantity energy, Qh.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.V
documentation: Electric potential in Volt (W/A).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.VA
documentation: Apparent power in Volt Ampere (See also real power and reactive power.)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.VAh
documentation: Apparent energy in Volt Ampere hours.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.VAr
documentation:
Reactive power in Volt Ampere reactive. The “reactive” or “imaginary” component
of electrical power (VIsin(phi)). (See also real power and apparent power).
Note: Different meter designs use different methods to arrive at their
results. Some meters may compute reactive power as an arithmetic value,
while others compute the value vectorially. The data consumer should determine
the method in use and the suitability of the measurement for the intended
purpose.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.VArh
documentation: Reactive energy in Volt Ampere reactive hours.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.VPerVA
documentation:
Power factor, PF, the ratio of the active power to the apparent power.
Note: The sign convention used for power factor will differ between IEC
meters and EEI (ANSI) meters. It is assumed that the data consumers understand
the type of meter being used and agree on the sign convention in use at
any given utility.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.VPerVAr
documentation:
Power factor, PF, the ratio of the active power to the apparent power.
Note: The sign convention used for power factor will differ between IEC
meters and EEI (ANSI) meters. It is assumed that the data consumers understand
the type of meter being used and agree on the sign convention in use at
any given utility.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.Vh
documentation: Volt-hour, Volt hours.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.Vs
documentation: Volt second (Ws/A).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.W
documentation:
Real power in Watt (J/s). Electrical power may have real and reactive components.
The real portion of electrical power (I²R or VIcos(phi)), is expressed
in Watts. (See also apparent power and reactive power.)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.WPerA
documentation: Active power per current flow, watt per Ampere.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.WPers
documentation: Ramp rate in Watt per second.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.Wh
documentation: Real energy in Watt hours.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.deg
documentation: Plane angle in degrees.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.degC
documentation:
Relative temperature in degrees Celsius.
In the SI unit system the symbol is ºC. Electric charge is measured in
coulomb that has the unit symbol C. To distinguish degree Celsius form
coulomb the symbol used in the UML is degC. Reason for not using ºC is
the special character º is difficult to manage in software.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.h
documentation: Time, hour = 60 min = 3600 s.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.min
documentation: Time, minute = 60 s.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.ohm
documentation: Electric resistance in ohm (V/A).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.ohmPerm
documentation: Electric resistance per length in ohm per metre ((V/A)/m).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.ohmm
documentation: resistivity, Ohm metre, (rho).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.onePerHz
documentation: Reciprocal of frequency (1/Hz).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.DERUnitSymbol.s

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.forward
documentation:
"Delivered," or "Imported" as defined 61968-2.
Forward Active Energy is a positive kWh value as one would naturally expect
to find as energy is supplied by the utility and consumed at the service.
Forward Reactive Energy is a positive VArh value as one would naturally
expect to find in the presence of inductive loading.
In polyphase metering, the forward energy register is incremented when
the sum of the phase energies is greater than zero:
<img src="HTS_1.PNG" width="209" height="16" border="0" alt="graphic"/>

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.lagging
documentation:
Typically used to describe that a power factor is lagging the reference
value.
Note 1: When used to describe VA, “lagging” describes a form of measurement
where reactive power is considered in all four quadrants, but real power
is considered only in quadrants I and IV.
Note 2: When used to describe power factor, the term “Lagging” implies
that the PF is negative. The term “lagging” in this case takes the place
of the negative sign. If a signed PF value is to be passed by the data
producer, then the direction of flow enumeration zero (none) should be
used in order to avoid the possibility of creating an expression that employs
a double negative. The data consumer should be able to tell from the sign
of the data if the PF is leading or lagging. This principle is analogous
to the concept that “Reverse” energy is an implied negative value, and
to publish a negative reverse value would be ambiguous.
Note 3: Lagging power factors typically indicate inductive loading.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.leading
documentation:
Typically used to describe that a power factor is leading the reference
value.
Note: Leading power factors typically indicate capacitive loading.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.net
documentation:
|Forward| - |Reverse|, See 61968-2.
Note: In some systems, the value passed as a “net” value could become negative.
In other systems the value passed as a “net” value is always a positive
number, and rolls-over and rolls-under as needed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.none
documentation: Not Applicable (N/A)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.q1minusQ4
documentation: Q1 minus Q4

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.q1plusQ2
documentation: Reactive positive quadrants. (The term “lagging” is preferred.)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.q1plusQ3
documentation: Quadrants 1 and 3

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.q1plusQ4
documentation: Quadrants 1 and 4 usually represent forward active energy

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.q2minusQ3
documentation: Q2 minus Q3

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.q2plusQ3
documentation: Quadrants 2 and 3 usually represent reverse active energy

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.q2plusQ4
documentation: Quadrants 2 and 4

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.q3minusQ2
documentation: Q3 minus Q2

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.q3plusQ4
documentation: Reactive negative quadrants. (The term “leading” is preferred.)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.quadrant1
documentation: Q1 only

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.quadrant2
documentation: Q2 only

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.quadrant3
documentation: Q3 only

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.quadrant4
documentation: Q4 only

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.reverse
documentation:
Reverse Active Energy is equivalent to "Received," or "Exported" as defined
in 61968-2.
Reverse Active Energy is a positive kWh value as one would expect to find
when energy is backfed by the service onto the utility network.
Reverse Reactive Energy is a positive VArh value as one would expect to
find in the presence of capacitive loading and a leading Power Factor.
In polyphase metering, the reverse energy register is incremented when
the sum of the phase energies is less than zero:
<img src="HTS_1.PNG" width="209" height="16" border="0" alt="graphic"/>
Note: The value passed as a reverse value is always a positive value. It
is understood by the label “reverse” that it represents negative flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.total
documentation:
|Forward| + |Reverse|, See 61968-2.
The sum of the commodity in all quadrants Q1+Q2+Q3+Q4.
In polyphase metering, the total energy register is incremented when the
absolute value of the sum of the phase energies is greater than zero:
<img src="HTS_1.PNG" width="217" height="16" border="0" alt="graphic"/>

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.FlowDirectionKind.totalByPhase
documentation:
In polyphase metering, the total by phase energy register is incremented
when the sum of the absolute values of the phase energies is greater than
zero:
<img src="HTS_1.PNG" width="234" height="16" border="0" alt="graphic"/>
In single phase metering, the formulas for “Total” and “Total by phase”
collapse to the same expression. For communication purposes however, the
“Total” enumeration should be used with single phase meter data.

Kind of flow direction for reading/measured values proper to some commodities
such as, for example, energy, power, demand.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OperationalLimitDirectionKind

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OperationalLimitDirectionKind.absoluteValue
documentation:
An absoluteValue limit means that a monitored absolute value above the
limit value is a violation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OperationalLimitDirectionKind.high
documentation:
High means that a monitored value above the limit value is a violation.
If applied to a terminal flow, the positive direction is into the terminal.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OperationalLimitDirectionKind.low
documentation:
Low means a monitored value below the limit is a violation. If applied
to a terminal flow, the positive direction is into the terminal.
The direction attribute describes the side of a limit that is a violation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.OrderedPhaseCodeKind
documentation:
In some use cases, the ordering of phases is important. The PhaseCode class
does not represent order, but this class addresses such use cases. When
two or more phases are present, the individual phases may occur in any
order, but the neutral must always occur last. When only one phase and
the neutral is present, that phase and the neutral may be re-ordered.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseCode.s1
documentation: Secondary phase 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseCode.s12
documentation: Secondary phase 1 and 2.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseCode.s12N
documentation: Secondary phases 1, 2, and neutral.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseCode.s1N
documentation: Secondary phase 1 and neutral.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseCode.s2
documentation: Secondary phase 2.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.PhaseCode.s2N
documentation: Secondary phase 2 and neutral.

An unordered enumeration of phase identifiers. Allows designation of phases
for both transmission and distribution equipment, circuits and loads. The
enumeration, by itself, does not describe how the phases are connected
together or connected to ground. Ground is not explicitly denoted as a
phase.
Residential and small commercial loads are often served from single-phase,
or split-phase, secondary circuits. For example of s12N, phases 1 and 2
refer to hot wires that are 180 degrees out of phase, while N refers to
the neutral wire. Through single-phase transformer connections, these secondary
circuits may be served from one or two of the primary phases A, B, and
C. For three-phase loads, use the A, B, C phase codes instead of s12N.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.UnitMultiplier.none
documentation: No multiplier or equivalently multiply by 1.

The unit multipliers defined for the CIM. When applied to unit symbols,
the unit symbol is treated as a derived unit. Regardless of the contents
of the unit symbol text, the unit symbol shall be treated as if it were
a single-character unit symbol. Unit symbols should not contain multipliers,
and it should be left to the multiplier to define the multiple for an entire
data type.
For example, if a unit symbol is "A2Perh" and the multiplier is "k", then
the value is k(A^2/h), and the multiplier applies to the entire final value,
not to any individual part of the value. This can be conceptualized by
substituting a derived unit symbol for the unit type. If one imagines that
the symbol "Þ" represents the derived unit "A2Perh", then applying the
multiplier "k" can be conceptualized simply as "kÞ".
For example, the SI unit for mass is "kg" and not "g". If the unit symbol
is defined as "kg", then the multiplier is applied to "kg" as a whole and
does not replace the "k" in front of the "g". In this case, the multiplier
of "m" would be used with the unit symbol of "kg" to represent one gram.
As a text string, this violates the instructions in IEC 80000-1. However,
because the unit symbol in CIM is treated as a derived unit instead of
as an SI unit, it makes more sense to conceptualize the "kg" as if it were
replaced by one of the proposed replacements for the SI mass symbol. If
one imagines that the "kg" were replaced by a symbol "Þ", then it is easier
to conceptualize the multiplier "m" as creating the proper unit "mÞ", and
not the forbidden unit "mkg".

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.UnitSymbol.tonne
documentation: mass, “tonne” or “metric ton” (1000 kg = 1 Mg).

The derived units defined for usage in the CIM. In some cases, the derived
unit is equal to an SI unit. Whenever possible, the standard derived symbol
is used instead of the formula for the derived unit. For example, the unit
symbol Farad is defined as "F" instead of "CPerV". In cases where a standard
symbol does not exist for a derived unit, the formula for the unit is used
as the unit symbol. For example, density does not have a standard symbol
and so it is represented as "kgPerm3". With the exception of the "kg",
which is an SI unit, the unit symbols do not contain multipliers and therefore
represent the base derived unit to which a multiplier can be applied as
a whole.
Every unit symbol is treated as an unparseable text as if it were a single-letter
symbol. The meaning of each unit symbol is defined by the accompanying
descriptive text and not by the text contents of the unit symbol.
To allow the widest possible range of serializations without requiring
special character handling, several substitutions are made which deviate
from the format described in IEC 80000-1. The division symbol "/" is replaced
by the letters"Per". Exponents are written in plain text after the unit
as "m3" instead of being formatted as in "m<sup>3</sup>" or introducing
a symbol as in "m^3". The degree symbol "°" is replaced with the letters
"deg". Any clarification of the meaning for a substitution is included
in the description for the unit symbol.
Non-SI units are included in list of unit symbols to allow sources of data
to be correctly labeled with their non-SI units (for example, a GPS sensor
that is reporting numbers that represent feet instead of meters). This
allows software to use the unit symbol information correctly convert and
scale the raw data of those sources into SI-based units.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Validity

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Validity.GOOD
documentation:
The value is marked good if no abnormal condition of the acquisition function
or the information source is detected.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Validity.INVALID
documentation:
The value is marked invalid when a supervision function recognises abnormal
conditions of the acquisition function or the information source (missing
or non-operating updating devices). The value is not defined under this
condition. The mark invalid is used to indicate to the client that the
value may be incorrect and shall not be used.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.cimhub_2023.cimhub_2023.Validity.QUESTIONABLE
documentation:
The value is marked questionable if a supervision function detects an abnormal
behaviour, however the value could still be valid. The client is responsible
for determining whether or not values marked "questionable" should be used.
Validity for MeasurementValue.

class is discouraged if a more specific class can be used to specify the
x and y axis values along with their specific data types.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DispatchablePowerCapability.maxReactivePower
documentation:
Product of RMS value of the voltage and the RMS value of the quadrature

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DispatchablePowerCapability.minActivePower

Product of RMS value of the voltage and the RMS value of the in-phase component

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DispatchablePowerCapability.minApparentPower

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DispatchablePowerCapability.minReactivePower
documentation:
Product of RMS value of the voltage and the RMS value of the quadrature

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EndDeviceAction.durationIndefinite

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EndDeviceAction.startDateTime
documentation: Start date and time for action of this control.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EndDeviceAction.duration

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EndDeviceAction.EndDeviceControl
documentation: End device control issuing this end device action.

Action/command performed by an end device on a device other than the end
device.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EndDeviceEventDetail

documentation: Name.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EndDeviceEventDetail.EndDeviceEvent
documentation: End device owning this detail.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EndDeviceEventDetail.value
documentation: Value, including unit information.
Name-value pair, specific to end device events.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnvironmentalLocationType

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnvironmentalLocationType.kind
documentation:
The kind of location. Typical values might be center, extent, primary,
secondary, etc.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnvironmentalLocationType.EnvironmentalAlert
documentation: Environmental alert applying to location of this type.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnvironmentalLocationType.EnvironmentalPhenomenon
documentation: Environmental phenomenon for which this location is of relevance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnvironmentalLocationType.Location
documentation: Location of this instance of ths kind of environmental location.

Kind of environmental location. Used when an environmental alert or phenomenon
has multiple locations associated with it.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.FieldDispatchStep

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.FieldDispatchStep.occurredDateTime
documentation: The date and time at which the dispatch status occurred.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.FieldDispatchStep.remarks
documentation: freeform comments related to the dispatch to perform field work.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.FieldDispatchStep.sequenceNumber
documentation:
The sequence number of the field dispatch step within the field dispatch
history. Begins with 1 and increments up.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.FieldDispatchStep.dispatchStatus
documentation:
The status of one or more crews dispatched to perform field work at one
or more work sites
Details of the step in the field dispatch history.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IEC61970CIMVersion

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IEC61970CIMVersion.date
documentation: Form is YYYY-MM-DD for example for January 5, 2009 it is 2009-01-05.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IEC61970CIMVersion.version
documentation:
Form is IEC61970CIMXXvYY where XX is the major CIM package version and
the YY is the minor version. For example IEC61970CIM13v18.
This is the IEC 61970 CIM version number assigned to this UML model.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IdentifiedObject

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IdentifiedObject.mRID
documentation:
Master resource identifier issued by a model authority. The mRID is unique
within an exchange context. Global uniqueness is easily achieved by using
a UUID, as specified in RFC 4122, for the mRID. The use of UUID is strongly
recommended.
For CIMXML data files in RDF syntax conforming to IEC 61970-552 Edition
1, the mRID is mapped to rdf:ID or rdf:about attributes that identify CIM
object elements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IdentifiedObject.aliasName
documentation:
The aliasName is free text human readable name of the object alternative
to IdentifiedObject.name. It may be non unique and may not correlate to
a naming hierarchy.
The attribute aliasName is retained because of backwards compatibility
between CIM relases. It is however recommended to replace aliasName with
the Name class as aliasName is planned for retirement at a future time.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IdentifiedObject.description
documentation:
The description is a free human readable text describing or naming the
object. It may be non unique and may not correlate to a naming hierarchy.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IdentifiedObject.name
documentation:
The name is any free human readable and possibly non unique text naming
the object.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IdentifiedObject.Names
documentation: All names of this identified object.

This is a root class to provide common identification for all classes needing
identification and naming attributes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACDCTerminal

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACDCTerminal.connected
documentation:
The connected status is related to a bus-branch model and the topological
node to terminal relation. True implies the terminal is connected to the
related topological node and false implies it is not.
In a bus-branch model, the connected status is used to tell if equipment
is disconnected without having to change the connectivity described by
the topological node to terminal relation. A valid case is that conducting
equipment can be connected in one end and open in the other. In particular
for an AC line segment, where the reactive line charging can be significant,
this is a relevant case.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACDCTerminal.sequenceNumber
documentation:
The orientation of the terminal connections for a multiple terminal conducting
equipment. The sequence numbering starts with 1 and additional terminals
should follow in increasing order. The first terminal is the "starting
point" for a two terminal branch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACDCTerminal.BusNameMarker
documentation: The bus name marker used to name the bus (topological node).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACDCTerminal.Measurements
documentation:
Measurements associated with this terminal defining where the measurement
is placed in the network topology. It may be used, for instance, to capture
the sensor position, such as a voltage transformer (PT) at a busbar or
a current transformer (CT) at the bar between a breaker and an isolator.

An electrical connection point (AC or DC) to a piece of conducting equipment.
Terminals are connected at physical connection points called connectivity
nodes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Terminal

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Terminal.BranchGroupTerminal
documentation: The directed branch group terminals for which this terminal is monitored.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Terminal.ConductingEquipment
documentation:
The conducting equipment of the terminal. Conducting equipment have terminals
that may be connected to other conducting equipment terminals via connectivity
nodes or topological nodes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Terminal.ConnectivityNode
documentation: The connectivity node to which this terminal connects with zero impedance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Terminal.EquipmentFaults
documentation: The equipment faults at this terminal.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Terminal.HasFirstMutualCoupling
documentation: Mutual couplings associated with the branch as the first branch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Terminal.HasSecondMutualCoupling
documentation: Mutual couplings with the branch associated as the first branch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Terminal.NormalHeadFeeder
documentation:
The feeder that this terminal normally feeds. Only specifed for the terminals
at head of feeders.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Terminal.RegulatingControl
documentation: The controls regulating this terminal.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Terminal.SvPowerFlow
documentation: The power flow state variable associated with the terminal.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Terminal.TopologicalNode
documentation:
The topological node associated with the terminal. This can be used as
an alternative to the connectivity node path to topological node, thus
making it unneccesary to model connectivity nodes in some cases. Note that
the if connectivity nodes are in the model, this association would probably
not be used as an input specification.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Terminal.TransformerEnd
documentation: All transformer ends connected at this terminal.

An AC electrical connection point to a piece of conducting equipment. Terminals
are connected at physical connection points called connectivity nodes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ActivityRecord

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ActivityRecord.createdDateTime

Date and time this activity record has been created (different from the

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ActivityRecord.severity

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ActivityRecord.type

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ActivityRecord.Assets

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ActivityRecord.Author
documentation: Author of this activity record.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ActivityRecord.status
documentation: Information on consequence of event resulting in this activity record.

Records activity for an entity at a point in time; activity may be for
an event that has already occurred or for a planned activity.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AssetHealthEvent

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AssetHealthEvent.actionTimeline

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConfigurationEvent.remark
