class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Command.normalValue
documentation: Normal value for Control.value e.g. used for percentage scaling.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Command.value
documentation: The value representing the actuator output.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Command.DiscreteValue
documentation: The MeasurementValue that is controlled.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Command.ValueAliasSet
documentation: The ValueAliasSet used for translation of a Control value to a name.
A Command is a discrete control used for supervisory control.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.MeasurementValue

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.MeasurementValue.timeStamp
documentation: The time when the value was last updated

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.MeasurementValue.sensorAccuracy
documentation:
The limit, expressed as a percentage of the sensor maximum, that errors
will not exceed when the sensor is used under reference conditions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.MeasurementValue.MeasurementValueQuality
documentation: A MeasurementValue has a MeasurementValueQuality associated with it.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.MeasurementValue.MeasurementValueSource
documentation:
A reference to the type of source that updates the MeasurementValue, e.g.
SCADA, CCLink, manual, etc. User conventions for the names of sources are
contained in the introduction to IEC 61970-301.

The current state for a measurement. A state value is an instance of a
measurement from a specific source. Measurements can be associated with
many state values, each representing a different source for the measurement.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulatorValue

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulatorValue.value
documentation: The value to supervise. The value is positive.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulatorValue.Accumulator
documentation: Measurement to which this value is connected.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulatorValue.AccumulatorReset
documentation: The command that reset the accumulator value.
AccumulatorValue represents an accumulated (counted) MeasurementValue.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AnalogValue

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AnalogValue.value
documentation: The value to supervise.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AnalogValue.Analog
documentation: Measurement to which this value is connected.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AnalogValue.AnalogControl
documentation: The Control variable associated with the MeasurementValue.
AnalogValue represents an analog MeasurementValue.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DiscreteValue

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DiscreteValue.value
documentation: The value to supervise.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DiscreteValue.Command
documentation: The Control variable associated with the MeasurementValue.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DiscreteValue.Discrete
documentation: Measurement to which this value is connected.
DiscreteValue represents a discrete MeasurementValue.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IntervalReading

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IntervalReading.IntervalBlocks
documentation: All blocks containing this interval reading.

Data captured at regular intervals of time. Interval data could be captured
as incremental data, absolute data, or relative data. The source for the
data is usually a tariff quantity or an engineering quantity. Data is typically
captured in time-tagged, uniform, fixed-length intervals of 5 min, 10 min,
15 min, 30 min, or 60 min.
Note: Interval Data is sometimes also called "Interval Data Readings" (IDR).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reading

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reading.reason
documentation: Reason for this reading being taken.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reading.MeterReadings
documentation: All meter readings (sets of values) containing this reading value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reading.ReadingType
documentation: Type information for this reading value.

Specific value measured by a meter or other asset, or calculated by a system.
Each Reading is associated with a specific ReadingType.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.StringMeasurementValue

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.StringMeasurementValue.value
documentation: The value to supervise.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.StringMeasurementValue.StringMeasurement
documentation: Measurement to which this value is connected.
StringMeasurementValue represents a measurement value of type string.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Limit
documentation:
Specifies one limit value for a Measurement. A Measurement typically has
several limits that are kept together by the LimitSet class. The actual
meaning and use of a Limit instance (i.e., if it is an alarm or warning
limit or if it is a high or low limit) is not captured in the Limit class.
However the name of a Limit instance may indicate both meaning and use.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulatorLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulatorLimit.value
documentation: The value to supervise against. The value is positive.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulatorLimit.LimitSet
documentation: The set of limits.
Limit values for Accumulator measurements.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AnalogLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AnalogLimit.value
documentation: The value to supervise against.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AnalogLimit.LimitSet
documentation: The set of limits.
Limit values for Analog measurements.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LimitSet

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LimitSet.isPercentageLimits
documentation:
Tells if the limit values are in percentage of normalValue or the specified
Unit for Measurements and Controls.

Specifies a set of Limits that are associated with a Measurement. A Measurement
may have several LimitSets corresponding to seasonal or other changing
conditions. The condition is captured in the name and description attributes.
The same LimitSet may be used for several Measurements. In particular percentage
limits are used this way.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulatorLimitSet

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulatorLimitSet.Limits
documentation: The limit values used for supervision of Measurements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulatorLimitSet.Measurements
documentation: The Measurements using the LimitSet.

An AccumulatorLimitSet specifies a set of Limits that are associated with
an Accumulator measurement.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AnalogLimitSet

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AnalogLimitSet.Limits
documentation: The limit values used for supervision of Measurements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AnalogLimitSet.Measurements
documentation: The Measurements using the LimitSet.

An AnalogLimitSet specifies a set of Limits that are associated with an
Analog measurement.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LoadGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LoadGroup.SubLoadArea
documentation: The SubLoadArea where the Loadgroup belongs.

The class is the third level in a hierarchical structure for grouping of
loads for the purpose of load flow load scaling.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConformLoadGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConformLoadGroup.ConformLoadSchedules
documentation: The ConformLoadSchedules in the ConformLoadGroup.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConformLoadGroup.EnergyConsumers
documentation: Conform loads assigned to this ConformLoadGroup.
A group of loads conforming to an allocation pattern.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonConformLoadGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonConformLoadGroup.EnergyConsumers
documentation: Conform loads assigned to this ConformLoadGroup.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonConformLoadGroup.NonConformLoadSchedules
documentation: The NonConformLoadSchedules in the NonConformLoadGroup.
Loads that do not follow a daily and seasonal load variation pattern.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LoadResponseCharacteristic

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LoadResponseCharacteristic.exponentModel
documentation:
Indicates the exponential voltage dependency model is to be used. If false,
the coefficient model is to be used.
The exponential voltage dependency model consist of the attributes
- pVoltageExponent
- qVoltageExponent.
The coefficient model consist of the attributes
- pConstantImpedance
- pConstantCurrent
- pConstantPower
- qConstantImpedance
- qConstantCurrent
- qConstantPower.
The sum of pConstantImpedance, pConstantCurrent and pConstantPower shall
equal 1.
The sum of qConstantImpedance, qConstantCurrent and qConstantPower shall
equal 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LoadResponseCharacteristic.pConstantCurrent
documentation: Portion of active power load modeled as constant current.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LoadResponseCharacteristic.pConstantImpedance
documentation: Portion of active power load modeled as constant impedance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LoadResponseCharacteristic.pConstantPower
documentation: Portion of active power load modeled as constant power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LoadResponseCharacteristic.pVoltageExponent
documentation: Exponent of per unit voltage effecting real power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LoadResponseCharacteristic.qConstantCurrent
documentation: Portion of reactive power load modeled as constant current.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LoadResponseCharacteristic.qConstantImpedance
documentation: Portion of reactive power load modeled as constant impedance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LoadResponseCharacteristic.qConstantPower
documentation: Portion of reactive power load modeled as constant power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LoadResponseCharacteristic.qVoltageExponent
documentation: Exponent of per unit voltage effecting reactive power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LoadResponseCharacteristic.EnergyConsumer
documentation: The set of loads that have the response characteristics.

Models the characteristic response of the load demand due to changes in
system conditions such as voltage and frequency. This is not related to
demand response.
If LoadResponseCharacteristic.exponentModel is True, the voltage exponents
are specified and used as to calculate:
Active power component = Pnominal * (Voltage/cim:BaseVoltage.nominalVoltage)
** cim:LoadResponseCharacteristic.pVoltageExponent
Reactive power component = Qnominal * (Voltage/cim:BaseVoltage.nominalVoltage)**
cim:LoadResponseCharacteristic.qVoltageExponent
Where * means "multiply" and ** is "raised to power of".

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Location

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Location.direction
documentation:
(if applicable) Direction that allows field crews to quickly find a given
asset. For a given location, such as a street address, this is the relative
direction in which to find the asset. For example, a streetlight may be

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Location.PowerSystemResources
documentation: All power system resources at this location.

Secondary address of the location. For example, PO Box address may have

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Location.status

been, is, and/or will be at a given moment in time. It can be defined with
one or more postition points (coordinates) in a given coordinate system.

Method for the service person to access this usage point location. For
example, a description of where to obtain a key if the facility is unmanned

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePointLocation.remark
documentation: Remarks about this location.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePointLocation.siteAccessProblem
documentation:
Problems previously encountered when visiting or performing work at this
location. Examples include: bad dog, violent customer, verbally abusive
occupant, obstructions, safety hazards, etc.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePointLocation.UsagePoints
documentation: All usage points at this location.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Measurement

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Measurement.measurementType
documentation:
Specifies the type of measurement. For example, this specifies if the measurement
represents an indoor temperature, outdoor temperature, bus voltage, line
flow, etc.
When the measurementType is set to "Specialization", the type of Measurement
is defined in more detail by the specialized class which inherits from
Measurement.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Measurement.phases
documentation:
Indicates to which phases the measurement applies and avoids the need to
use 'measurementType' to also encode phase information (which would explode
the types). The phase information in Measurement, along with 'measurementType'
and 'phases' uniquely defines a Measurement for a device, based on normal
network phase. Their meaning will not change when the computed energizing
phasing is changed due to jumpers or other reasons.
If the attribute is missing three phases (ABC) shall be assumed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Measurement.PowerSystemResource
documentation: The power system resource that contains the measurement.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Measurement.Procedures
documentation: Measurements are specified in types of documents, such as procedures.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Measurement.Terminal
documentation: One or more measurements may be associated with a terminal in the network.

A Measurement represents any measured, calculated or non-measured non-calculated
quantity. Any piece of equipment may contain Measurements, e.g. a substation
may have temperature measurements and door open indications, a transformer
may have oil temperature and tank pressure measurements, a bay may contain
a number of power flow measurements and a Breaker may contain a switch
status measurement.
The PSR - Measurement association is intended to capture this use of Measurement
and is included in the naming hierarchy based on EquipmentContainer. The
naming hierarchy typically has Measurements as leafs, e.g. Substation-VoltageLevel-Bay-Switch-Measurement.
Some Measurements represent quantities related to a particular sensor location
in the network, e.g. a voltage transformer (PT) at a busbar or a current
transformer (CT) at the bar between a breaker and an isolator. The sensing
position is not captured in the PSR - Measurement association. Instead
it is captured by the Measurement - Terminal association that is used to
define the sensing location in the network topology. The location is defined
by the connection of the Terminal to ConductingEquipment.
If both a Terminal and PSR are associated, and the PSR is of type ConductingEquipment,
the associated Terminal should belong to that ConductingEquipment instance.
When the sensor location is needed both Measurement-PSR and Measurement-Terminal
are used. The Measurement-Terminal association is never used alone.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Accumulator

Accumulator represents an accumulated (counted) Measurement, e.g. an energy
value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.MutualCoupling.distance22

Zero sequence mutual coupling shunt (charging) conductance, uniformly distributed,
of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.MutualCoupling.x0

The starting terminal for the calculation of distances along the first

hurricane or tornado or flood, or example).

The operating shares of this operating participant. An operating participant
can be resused for any number of power system resources.

An operator of multiple power system resource objects. Note multple operating
participants may operate the same power system resource object. This can
be used for modeling jointly owned units where each owner operates as a
contractual share.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.OperationalLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.OperationalLimit.OperationalLimitSet
documentation: The limit set to which the limit values belong.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.OperationalLimit.OperationalLimitType
documentation: The limit type associated with this limit.

A value associated with a specific kind of limit.
The sub class value attribute shall be positive.
The sub class value attribute is inversely proportional to OperationalLimitType.acceptableDuration
(acceptableDuration for short). A pair of value_x and acceptableDuration_x
are related to each other as follows:
if value_1 > value_2 > value_3 >... then
acceptableDuration_1 < acceptableDuration_2 < acceptableDuration_3
< ...
A value_x with direction="high" shall be greater than a value_y with direction="low".

The set may contain limits of different types such as apparent power and
current limits or high and low voltage limits that are logically applied

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.OperationalLimitType.direction

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.OperationalLimitType.OperationalLimit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.b0ch
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) susceptance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.bch
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) susceptance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.g0ch
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) conductance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.gch
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.r
documentation: Positive sequence series resistance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.r0
documentation: Zero sequence series resistance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.x
documentation: Positive sequence series reactance, per unit of length.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PerLengthSequenceImpedance.x0
documentation: Zero sequence series reactance, per unit of length.

Sequence impedance and admittance parameters per unit length, for transposed
lines of 1, 2, or 3 phases. For 1-phase lines, define x=x0=xself. For 2-phase
lines, define x=xs-xm and x0=xs+xm.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseImpedanceData

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseImpedanceData.column
documentation:
This matrix element's column number, in the range 1 to row. Only the lower
triangle needs to be stored. Neutrals should be numbered last. Multiple
circuits on the same pole, tower or right-of-way can be included with unique
sequence numbers for the phases, and identical sequence numbers for any
shared neutrals. This solumn number matches ACLineSegmentPhase.sequenceNumber,
WirePosition.sequenceNumber and WirePhaseInfo.sequenceNumber as applicable..

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseImpedanceData.row
documentation:
This matrix element's row number, in the range 1 to PerLengthPhaseImpedance.conductorCount.
Only the lower triangle needs to be stored. Neutrals should be numbered
last. Multiple circuits on the same pole, tower or right-of-way can be
included with unique sequence numbers for the phases, and identical sequence
numbers for any shared neutrals. This row number matches ACLineSegmentPhase.sequenceNumber,
WirePosition.sequenceNumber and WirePhaseInfo.sequenceNumber as applicable..

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseImpedanceData.b
documentation: Susceptance matrix element value, per length of unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseImpedanceData.g
documentation: Conductance matrix element value, per length of unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseImpedanceData.r
documentation: Resistance matrix element value, per length of unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseImpedanceData.x
documentation: Reactance matrix element value, per length of unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseImpedanceData.PhaseImpedance
documentation: Conductor phase impedance to which this data belongs.

Impedance and conductance matrix element values.
The diagonal elements are described by the elements having the same toPhase
and fromPhase value and the off diagonal elements have different toPhase
and fromPhase values. The matrix can also be stored in symmetric lower
triangular format using the row and column attributes, which map to ACLineSegmentPhase.sequenceNumber.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChangerTable

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChangerTable.PhaseTapChangerTablePoint
documentation: The points of this table.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChangerTable.PhaseTapChangerTabular
documentation: The phase tap changers to which this phase tap table applies.

Describes a tabular curve for how the phase angle difference and impedance
varies with the tap step.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhenomenonClassification
documentation: A pre-defined phenomenon classification as defined by a particular authority.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerSystemResource

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerSystemResource.AssetDatasheet
documentation: Datasheet information for this power system resource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerSystemResource.Assets
documentation:
All assets represented by this power system resource. For example, multiple
conductor assets are electrically modelled as a single AC line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerSystemResource.Controls
documentation:
The controller outputs used to actually govern a regulating device, e.g.
the magnetization of a synchronous machine or capacitor bank breaker actuator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerSystemResource.Location
documentation: Location of this power system resource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerSystemResource.Measurements
documentation: The measurements associated with this power system resource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerSystemResource.OperatingShare
documentation: The operating shares of this power system resource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerSystemResource.PSRType
documentation: Custom classification for this power system resource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerSystemResource.ReportingGroup
documentation: Reporting groups to which this power system resource belongs.

A power system resource can be an item of equipment such as a switch, an
equipment container containing many individual items of equipment such
as a substation, or an organisational entity such as sub-control area.
Power system resources can have measurements associated.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegmentPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegmentPhase.sequenceNumber
documentation:
Number designation for this line segment phase. Each line segment phase
within a line segment should have a unique sequence number. This is useful
for unbalanced modeling to bind the mathematical model (PhaseImpedanceData
of PerLengthPhaseImpedance) with the connectivity model (this class) and
the physical model (WirePosition, WirePhaseInfo) without tight coupling.
Multiple circuits on the same pole, tower or right-of-way can be included
with unique sequence numbers for the phases, and identical sequence numbers
for any shared neutrals.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegmentPhase.phase
documentation: The phase connection of the wire at both ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegmentPhase.ACLineSegment
documentation: The line segment to which the phase belongs.
Represents a single wire of an alternating current line segment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AirCompressor

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AirCompressor.airCompressorRating
documentation: Rating of the CAES air compressor.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AirCompressor.CAESPlant
documentation: An air compressor may be a member of a compressed air energy storage plant.

Combustion turbine air compressor which is an integral part of a compressed
air energy storage (CAES) plant.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.CAESPlant.energyStorageCapacity
documentation: The rated energy storage capacity.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.CAESPlant.ratedCapacityP
documentation: The CAES plant's gross rated generating capacity.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.CAESPlant.AirCompressor
documentation: An air compressor may be a member of a compressed air energy storage plant.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.CAESPlant.ThermalGeneratingUnit
documentation:
A thermal generating unit may be a member of a compressed air energy storage
plant.
Compressed air energy storage plant.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.CogenerationPlant

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.CogenerationPlant.cogenHPSendoutRating
documentation: The high pressure steam sendout.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.CogenerationPlant.cogenHPSteamRating
documentation: The high pressure steam rating.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.CogenerationPlant.cogenLPSendoutRating
documentation: The low pressure steam sendout.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.CogenerationPlant.cogenLPSteamRating
documentation: The low pressure steam rating.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.CogenerationPlant.ratedP
documentation: The rated output active power of the cogeneration plant.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.CogenerationPlant.SteamSendoutSchedule
documentation: A cogeneration plant has a steam sendout schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.CogenerationPlant.ThermalGeneratingUnits
documentation: A thermal generating unit may be a member of a cogeneration plant.

A set of thermal generating units for the production of electrical energy
and process steam (usually from the output of the steam turbines). The
steam sendout is typically used for industrial purposes or for municipal
heating and cooling.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.CombinedCyclePlant

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.CombinedCyclePlant.combCyclePlantRating
documentation: The combined cycle plant's active power output rating.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.CombinedCyclePlant.ThermalGeneratingUnits
documentation: A thermal generating unit may be a member of a combined cycle plant.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConnectivityNodeContainer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConnectivityNodeContainer.ConnectivityNodes
documentation: Connectivity nodes which belong to this connectivity node container.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConnectivityNodeContainer.TopologicalNode
documentation: The topological nodes which belong to this connectivity node container.

A base class for all objects that may contain connectivity nodes or topological
nodes.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EquipmentContainer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EquipmentContainer.AdditionalGroupedEquipment
documentation:
The additonal contained equipment. The equipment belong to the equipment
container. The equipment is contained in another equipment container, but
also grouped with this equipment container. Examples include when a switch
contained in a substation is also desired to be grouped with a line contianer
or when a switch is included in a secondary substation and also grouped
in a feeder.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EquipmentContainer.Equipments
documentation: Contained equipment.
A modeling construct to provide a root class for containing equipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Bay

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Bay.bayEnergyMeasFlag
documentation: Indicates the presence/absence of energy measurements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Bay.bayPowerMeasFlag
documentation: Indicates the presence/absence of active/reactive power measurements.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Bay.breakerConfiguration
documentation: Breaker configuration.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Bay.busBarConfiguration
documentation: Bus bar configuration.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Bay.Substation
documentation: Substation containing the bay.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Bay.VoltageLevel
documentation: The voltage level containing this bay.

A collection of power system resources (within a given substation) including
conducting equipment, protection relays, measurements, and telemetry. A
bay typically represents a physical grouping related to modularization
of equipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Feeder

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Feeder.NamingSecondarySubstation
documentation:
The secondary substations that are normally energized from the feeder.
Used for naming purposes. Should be consistent with the other associations
for energizing terminal specification and the feeder energization specification.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Feeder.NormalEnergizedSubstation
documentation: The substations that are normally energized by the feeder.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Feeder.NormalEnergizingSubstation
documentation:
The substation that nominally energizes the feeder. Also used for naming
purposes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Feeder.NormalHeadTerminal
documentation: The normal head terminal or terminals of the feeder.

A collection of equipment for organizational purposes, used for grouping
distribution resources.
The organization a feeder does not necessarily reflect connectivity or
current operation state.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Line

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Line.Region
documentation: The sub-geographical region of the line.

Contains equipment beyond a substation belonging to a power transmission
line.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Plant
documentation: A Plant is a collection of equipment for purposes of generation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Substation

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Substation.Bays
documentation: Bays contained in the substation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Substation.NamingFeeder
documentation:
The primary feeder that normally energizes the secondary substation. Used
for naming purposes. Either this association or the substation to subgeographical
region should be used for hiearchical containment specification.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Substation.NormalEnergizedFeeder
documentation: The normal energized feeders of the substation. Also used for naming purposes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Substation.NormalEnergizingFeeder
documentation:
The feeders that potentially energize the downstream substation. Should
be consistent with the associations that describe the naming hiearchy.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Substation.Region
documentation: The SubGeographicalRegion containing the substation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Substation.VoltageLevels
documentation: The voltage levels within this substation.

A collection of equipment for purposes other than generation or utilization,
through which electric energy in bulk is passed for the purposes of switching
or modifying its characteristics.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.VoltageLevel

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.VoltageLevel.highVoltageLimit
documentation: The bus bar's high voltage limit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.VoltageLevel.lowVoltageLimit
documentation: The bus bar's low voltage limit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.VoltageLevel.BaseVoltage
documentation: The base voltage used for all equipment within the voltage level.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.VoltageLevel.Bays
documentation: The bays within this voltage level.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.VoltageLevel.Substation
documentation: The substation of the voltage level.

A collection of equipment at one common system voltage forming a switchgear.
The equipment typically consist of breakers, busbars, instrumentation,
control, regulation and protection devices as well as assemblies of all
these.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergyConsumerPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergyConsumerPhase.p
documentation:
Active power of the load. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
For voltage dependent loads the value is at rated voltage.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergyConsumerPhase.phase
documentation:
Phase of this energy consumer component. If the energy consumer is wye
connected, the connection is from the indicated phase to the central ground
or neutral point. If the energy consumer is delta connected, the phase
indicates an energy consumer connected from the indicated phase to the
next logical non-neutral phase.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergyConsumerPhase.q
documentation:
Reactive power of the load. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive
sign means flow out from a node.
For voltage dependent loads the value is at rated voltage.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergyConsumerPhase.EnergyConsumer
documentation: The energy consumer to which this phase belongs.
A single phase of an energy consumer.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergySourcePhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergySourcePhase.phase
documentation:
Phase of this energy source component. If the energy source wye connected,
the connection is from the indicated phase to the central ground or neutral
point. If the energy source is delta connected, the phase indicates an
energy source connected from the indicated phase to the next logical non-neutral
phase.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergySourcePhase.EnergySource
documentation: The energy sourceto which the phase belongs.
Represents the single phase information of an unbalanced energy source.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Equipment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Equipment.aggregate
documentation:
The single instance of equipment represents multiple pieces of equipment
that have been modeled together as an aggregate. Examples would be power
transformers or synchronous machines operating in parallel modeled as a
single aggregate power transformer or aggregate synchronous machine. This
is not to be used to indicate equipment that is part of a group of interdependent
equipment produced by a network production program.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Equipment.inService
documentation: If true, the equipment is in service.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Equipment.networkAnalysisEnabled
documentation:
The equipment is enabled to participate in network analysis. If unspecified,
the value is assumed to be true.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Equipment.normallyInService
documentation: If true, the equipment is normally in service.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Equipment.AdditionalEquipmentContainer
documentation:
Additional equipment container beyond the primary equipment container.
The equipment is contained in another equipment container, but also grouped
with this equipment container.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Equipment.EquipmentContainer
documentation: Container of this equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Equipment.Faults
documentation: All faults on this equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Equipment.OperationalLimitSet
documentation: The operational limit sets associated with this equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Equipment.UsagePoints
documentation: All usage points connected to the electrical grid through this equipment.
The parts of a power system that are physical devices, electronic or mechanical.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.CompositeSwitch

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.CompositeSwitch.compositeSwitchType
documentation:
An alphanumeric code that can be used as a reference to extra information
such as the description of the interlocking scheme if any.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.CompositeSwitch.Switches
documentation: Switches contained in this Composite switch.

A model of a set of individual Switches normally enclosed within the same
cabinet and possibly with interlocks that restrict the combination of switch
positions. These are typically found in medium voltage distribution networks.
A CompositeSwitch could represent a Ring-Main-Unit (RMU), or pad-mounted
switchgear, with primitive internal devices such as an internal bus-bar
plus 3 or 4 internal switches each of which may individually be open or
closed. A CompositeSwitch and a set of contained Switches can also be used
to represent a multi-position switch e.g. a switch that can connect a circuit
to Ground, Open or Busbar.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.controlEnabled

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.converterFedDrive

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.grounded
documentation: Used for Yn and Zn connections. True if the neutral is solidly grounded.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.grounded
documentation: Used for Yn and Zn connections. True if the neutral is solidly grounded.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.open

The attribute tells if the switch is considered open when used as input
to topology processing.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.polePairNumber

Number of pole pairs of stator. Used for short circuit data exchange according

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.ratedPowerFactor
documentation:
Power factor (nameplate data). It is primarily used for short circuit data

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.referencePriority
documentation:
Priority of unit for use as powerflow voltage phase angle reference bus
selection. 0 = don t care (default) 1 = highest priority. 2 is less than

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.retained

Branch is retained in a bus branch model. The flow through retained switches

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.reversible
documentation:
Indicates for converter drive motors if the power can be reversible. Used

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.rxLockedRotorRatio
documentation:
Locked rotor ratio (R/X). Used for short circuit data exchange according

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.sections
documentation:
Shunt compensator sections in use.
Starting value for steady state solution. Non integer values are allowed
to support continuous variables. The reasons for continuous value are to
support study cases where no discrete shunt compensators has yet been designed,
a solutions where a narrow voltage band force the sections to oscillate
or accommodate for a continuous solution as input.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.varistorPresent
documentation:
Describe if a metal oxide varistor (mov) for over voltage protection is
configured at the series compensator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.vectorGroup
documentation:
Vector group of the transformer for protective relaying, e.g., Dyn1. For
unbalanced transformers, this may not be simply determined from the constituent
winding connections and phase angle dispacements.
The vectorGroup string consists of the following components in the order
listed: high voltage winding connection, mid voltage winding connection
(for three winding transformers), phase displacement clock number from
0 to 11, low voltage winding connection
phase displacement clock number from 0 to 11. The winding connections are
D (delta), Y (wye), YN (wye with neutral), Z (zigzag), ZN (zigzag with
neutral), A (auto transformer). Upper case means the high voltage, lower
case mid or low. The high voltage winding always has clock postion 0 and
is not included in the vector group string. Some examples: YNy0 (two winding
wye to wye with no phase displacement), YNd11 (two winding wye to delta
with 330 degrees phase displacement), YNyn0d5 (three winding transformer
wye with neutral high voltgage, wye with neutral mid voltgage and no phase
displacement, delta low voltage with 150 degrees displacement).
Phase displacement is defined as the angular difference between the phasors
representing the voltages between the neutral point (real or imaginary)
and the corresponding terminals of two windings, a positive sequence voltage
system being applied to the high-voltage terminals, following each other
in alphabetical sequence if they are lettered, or in numerical sequence
if they are numbered: the phasors are assumed to rotate in a counter-clockwise
sense.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.asynchronousMachineType
documentation: Indicates the type of Asynchronous Machine (motor or generator).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.aVRDelay

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.bPerSection

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.capacitiveRating

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.gch

Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance, uniformly distributed,

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.governorSCD
documentation:
Power Frequency Bias. This is the change in power injection divided by

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.gPerSection

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.ikk
documentation:
Steady-state short-circuit current (in A for the profile) of generator
with compound excitation during 3-phase short circuit.
- Ikk=0: Generator with no compound excitation.
- Ikk?0: Generator with compound excitation.
Ikk is used to calculate the minimum steady-state short-circuit current
for generators with compound excitation
(Section 4.6.1.2 in the IEC 60909-0)
Used only for single fed short circuit on a generator. (Section 4.3.4.2.
in the IEC 60909-0)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.inductiveRating

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.inTransitTime

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.ipMax

Maximum allowable peak short-circuit current of busbar (Ipmax in the IEC
60909-0).
Mechanical limit of the busbar in the substation itself. Used for short

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.length

Starting value for steady state solutions.

Active power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.p

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.q

Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.q

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.q

Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.voltageAngle
documentation: Phase angle of a-phase open circuit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.voltageFactor
documentation:
Voltage factor in pu, which was used to calculate short-circuit current
Ik" and power Sk".

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.voltageMagnitude
documentation: Phase-to-phase open circuit voltage magnitude.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.voltageSetPoint
documentation:
The reactive power output of the SVC is proportional to the difference
between the voltage at the regulated bus and the voltage setpoint. When
the regulated bus voltage is equal to the voltage setpoint, the reactive
power output is zero.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.x
documentation: Positive sequence Thevenin reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.x
documentation: Positive sequence series reactance of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.x
documentation: Reactance of device.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.x
documentation: Positive sequence reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.x0
documentation: Zero sequence reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.x0
documentation: Zero sequence series reactance of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.x0
documentation: Zero sequence Thevenin reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.xGroundMax
documentation: The maximum reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.xGroundMin
documentation: The minimum reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.xGroundNominal
documentation:
The nominal reactance. This is the operating point (normally over compensation)
that is defined based on the resonance point in the healthy network condition.
The impedance is calculated based on nominal voltage divided by position
current.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.ACLineSegment
documentation: The line segment to which the clamp is connected.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.ACLineSegment
documentation: The line segment to which the cut is applied.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.ACLineSegmentPhases
documentation: The line segment phases which belong to the line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.BaseVoltage
documentation:
Base voltage of this conducting equipment. Use only when there is no voltage
level container used and only one base voltage applies. For example, not
used for transformers.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.Clamp
documentation: The clamps connected to the line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.CompositeSwitch
documentation: Composite switch to which this Switch belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.Cut
documentation: Cuts applied to the line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.EnergyConsumerPhase
documentation: The individual phase models for this energy consumer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.EnergySourcePhase
documentation: The individual phase information of the energy source.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.GeneratingUnit
documentation:
A synchronous machine may operate as a generator and as such becomes a
member of a generating unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.HydroPump
documentation:
The synchronous machine drives the turbine which moves the water from a
low elevation to a higher elevation. The direction of machine rotation
for pumping may or may not be the same as for generating.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.InitialReactiveCapabilityCurve
documentation: The default reactive capability curve for use by a synchronous machine.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.LineFaults
documentation: The line faults of the line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.LoadGroup
documentation: Group of this ConformLoad.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.LoadGroup
documentation: Group of this ConformLoad.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.LoadResponse
documentation:
The load response characteristic of this load. If missing, this load is
assumed to be constant power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoints
documentation: All points of the non-linear shunt compensator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.PerLengthImpedance
documentation: Per-length impedance of this line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.PowerCutZone
documentation: The energy consumer is assigned to this power cut zone.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.PowerTransformerEnd
documentation: The ends of this power transformer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.ReactiveCapabilityCurves
documentation: All available reactive capability curves for this synchronous machine.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.RegulatingControl
documentation: The regulating control scheme in which this equipment participates.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.ShuntCompensatorPhase
documentation: The individual phases models for the shunt compensator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.SvShuntCompensatorSections
documentation: The state for the number of shunt compensator sections in service.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.SvStatus
documentation: The status state variable associated with this conducting equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.SvSwitch
documentation: The switch state associated with the switch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.SwitchPhase
documentation: The individual switch phases for the switch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.SwitchSchedules
documentation: A Switch can be associated with SwitchSchedules.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.Terminals
documentation:
Conducting equipment have terminals that may be connected to other conducting
equipment terminals via connectivity nodes or topological nodes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.TransformerTanks
documentation: All transformers that belong to this bank.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConductingEquipment.VoltageControlZone
documentation: A VoltageControlZone is controlled by a designated BusbarSection.

The parts of the AC power system that are designed to carry current or
that are conductively connected through terminals.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Clamp

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Clamp.lengthFromTerminal1
documentation:
The length to the place where the clamp is located starting from side one
of the line segment, i.e. the line segment terminal with sequence number
equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Clamp.ACLineSegment
documentation: The line segment to which the clamp is connected.

A Clamp is a galvanic connection at a line segment where other equipment
is connected. A Clamp does not cut the line segment.
A Clamp is ConductingEquipment and has one Terminal with an associated
ConnectivityNode. Any other ConductingEquipment can be connected to the
Clamp ConnectivityNode.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Conductor

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Conductor.length
documentation: Segment length for calculating line section capabilities

Combination of conducting material with consistent electrical characteristics,
building a single electrical system, used to carry current between points
in the power system.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegment

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegment.b0ch
documentation:
Zero sequence shunt (charging) susceptance, uniformly distributed, of the
entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegment.bch
documentation:
Positive sequence shunt (charging) susceptance, uniformly distributed,
of the entire line section. This value represents the full charging over
the full length of the line.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegment.g0ch
documentation:
Zero sequence shunt (charging) conductance, uniformly distributed, of the
entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegment.gch
documentation:
Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance, uniformly distributed,
of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegment.r
documentation: Positive sequence series resistance of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegment.r0
documentation: Zero sequence series resistance of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegment.shortCircuitEndTemperature
documentation:
Maximum permitted temperature at the end of SC for the calculation of minimum
short-circuit currents. Used for short circuit data exchange according
to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegment.x
documentation: Positive sequence series reactance of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegment.x0
documentation: Zero sequence series reactance of the entire line section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegment.ACLineSegmentPhases
documentation: The line segment phases which belong to the line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegment.Clamp
documentation: The clamps connected to the line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegment.Cut
documentation: Cuts applied to the line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegment.LineFaults
documentation: The line faults of the line segment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ACLineSegment.PerLengthImpedance
documentation: Per-length impedance of this line segment.

A wire or combination of wires, with consistent electrical characteristics,
building a single electrical system, used to carry alternating current
between points in the power system.
For symmetrical, transposed 3ph lines, it is sufficient to use attributes
of the line segment, which describe impedances and admittances for the
entire length of the segment. Additionally impedances can be computed by
using length and associated per length impedances.
The BaseVoltage at the two ends of ACLineSegments in a Line shall have
the same BaseVoltage.nominalVoltage. However, boundary lines may have slightly
different BaseVoltage.nominalVoltages and variation is allowed. Larger
voltage difference in general requires use of an equivalent branch.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Connector
documentation:
A conductor, or group of conductors, with negligible impedance, that serve
to connect other conducting equipment within a single substation and are
modelled with a single logical terminal.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.BusbarSection

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.BusbarSection.ipMax
documentation:
Maximum allowable peak short-circuit current of busbar (Ipmax in the IEC
60909-0).
Mechanical limit of the busbar in the substation itself. Used for short
circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.BusbarSection.VoltageControlZone
documentation: A VoltageControlZone is controlled by a designated BusbarSection.

A conductor, or group of conductors, with negligible impedance, that serve
to connect other conducting equipment within a single substation.
Voltage measurements are typically obtained from VoltageTransformers that
are connected to busbar sections. A bus bar section may have many physical
terminals but for analysis is modelled with exactly one logical terminal.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Junction
documentation:
A point where one or more conducting equipments are connected with zero
resistance.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EarthFaultCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EarthFaultCompensator.r
documentation: Nominal resistance of device.

A conducting equipment used to represent a connection to ground which is
typically used to compensate earth faults.. An earth fault compensator
device modeled with a single terminal implies a second terminal solidly
connected to ground. If two terminals are modeled, the ground is not assumed
and normal connection rules apply.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.GroundingImpedance
documentation: A fixed impedance device used for grounding.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PetersenCoil

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PetersenCoil.mode
documentation: The mode of operation of the Petersen coil.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PetersenCoil.nominalU
documentation: The nominal voltage for which the coil is designed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PetersenCoil.offsetCurrent
documentation:
The offset current that the Petersen coil controller is operating from
the resonant point. This is normally a fixed amount for which the controller
is configured and could be positive or negative. Typically 0 to 60 Amperes
depending on voltage and resonance conditions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PetersenCoil.positionCurrent
documentation:
The control current used to control the Petersen coil also known as the
position current. Typically in the range of 20-200mA.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PetersenCoil.xGroundMax
documentation: The maximum reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PetersenCoil.xGroundMin
documentation: The minimum reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PetersenCoil.xGroundNominal
documentation:
The nominal reactance. This is the operating point (normally over compensation)
that is defined based on the resonance point in the healthy network condition.
The impedance is calculated based on nominal voltage divided by position
current.

A tunable impedance device normally used to offset line charging during
single line faults in an ungrounded section of network.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergyConsumer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergyConsumer.customerCount
documentation: Number of individual customers represented by this demand.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergyConsumer.grounded
documentation: Used for Yn and Zn connections. True if the neutral is solidly grounded.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergyConsumer.p
documentation:
Active power of the load. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
For voltage dependent loads the value is at rated voltage.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergyConsumer.phaseConnection
documentation: The type of phase connection, such as wye or delta.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergyConsumer.q
documentation:
Reactive power of the load. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive
sign means flow out from a node.
For voltage dependent loads the value is at rated voltage.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergyConsumer.EnergyConsumerPhase
documentation: The individual phase models for this energy consumer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergyConsumer.LoadResponse
documentation:
The load response characteristic of this load. If missing, this load is
assumed to be constant power.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergyConsumer.PowerCutZone
documentation: The energy consumer is assigned to this power cut zone.
Generic user of energy - a point of consumption on the power system model.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ConformLoad
documentation:
ConformLoad represent loads that follow a daily load change pattern where
the pattern can be used to scale the load with a system load.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonConformLoad

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonConformLoad.LoadGroup
documentation: Group of this ConformLoad.

NonConformLoad represent loads that do not follow a daily load change pattern
and changes are not correlated with the daily load change pattern.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.StationSupply
documentation: Station supply with load derived from the station output.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergySource

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergySource.nominalVoltage
documentation: Phase-to-phase nominal voltage.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergySource.r
documentation: Positive sequence Thevenin resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergySource.r0
documentation: Zero sequence Thevenin resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergySource.voltageAngle
documentation: Phase angle of a-phase open circuit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergySource.voltageMagnitude
documentation: Phase-to-phase open circuit voltage magnitude.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergySource.x
documentation: Positive sequence Thevenin reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergySource.x0
documentation: Zero sequence Thevenin reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.EnergySource.EnergySourcePhase
documentation: The individual phase information of the energy source.

A generic equivalent for an energy supplier on a transmission or distribution
voltage level.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RegulatingCondEq

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RegulatingCondEq.controlEnabled
documentation:
Specifies the regulation status of the equipment. True is regulating, false
is not regulating.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RegulatingCondEq.RegulatingControl
documentation: The regulating control scheme in which this equipment participates.

A type of conducting equipment that can regulate a quantity (i.e. voltage
or flow) at a specific point in the network.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection.ikSecond
documentation:
Indicates whether initial symmetrical short-circuit current and power have
been calculated according to IEC (Ik").

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxR0ToX0Ratio
documentation:
Maximum ratio of zero sequence resistance of Network Feeder to its zero
sequence reactance (R(0)/X(0) max). Used for short circuit data exchange
according to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxR1ToX1Ratio
documentation:
Maximum ratio of positive sequence resistance of Network Feeder to its
positive sequence reactance (R(1)/X(1) max). Used for short circuit data
exchange according to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxZ0ToZ1Ratio
documentation:
Maximum ratio of zero sequence impedance to its positive sequence impedance
(Z(0)/Z(1) max). Used for short circuit data exchange according to IEC
60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection.minR0ToX0Ratio
documentation:
Indicates whether initial symmetrical short-circuit current and power have
been calculated according to IEC (Ik"). Used for short circuit data exchange
according to IEC 6090

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection.minR1ToX1Ratio
documentation:
Minimum ratio of positive sequence resistance of Network Feeder to its
positive sequence reactance (R(1)/X(1) min). Used for short circuit data
exchange according to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection.minZ0ToZ1Ratio
documentation:
Minimum ratio of zero sequence impedance to its positive sequence impedance
(Z(0)/Z(1) min). Used for short circuit data exchange according to IEC
60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection.referencePriority
documentation:
Priority of unit for use as powerflow voltage phase angle reference bus
selection. 0 = don t care (default) 1 = highest priority. 2 is less than
1 and so on.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection.governorSCD
documentation:
Power Frequency Bias. This is the change in power injection divided by
the change in frequency and negated. A positive value of the power frequency
bias provides additional power injection upon a drop in frequency.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxInitialSymShCCurrent
documentation:
Maximum initial symmetrical short-circuit currents (Ik" max) in A (Ik"
= Sk"/(SQRT(3) Un)). Used for short circuit data exchange according to
IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxP
documentation: Maximum active power of the injection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection.maxQ
documentation:
Not for short circuit modelling; It is used for modelling of infeed for
load flow exchange. If maxQ and minQ are not used ReactiveCapabilityCurve
can be used

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection.minInitialSymShCCurrent
documentation:
Minimum initial symmetrical short-circuit currents (Ik" min) in A (Ik"
= Sk"/(SQRT(3) Un)). Used for short circuit data exchange according to
IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection.minP
documentation: Minimum active power of the injection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection.minQ
documentation:
Not for short circuit modelling; It is used for modelling of infeed for
load flow exchange. If maxQ and minQ are not used ReactiveCapabilityCurve
can be used

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection.p
documentation:
Active power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for steady state solutions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection.q
documentation:
Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for steady state solutions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ExternalNetworkInjection.voltageFactor
documentation:
Voltage factor in pu, which was used to calculate short-circuit current
Ik" and power Sk".
This class represents external network and it is used for IEC 60909 calculations.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.FrequencyConverter

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.FrequencyConverter.frequency
documentation: Frequency on the AC side.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.FrequencyConverter.maxP
documentation:
The maximum active power on the DC side at which the frequence converter
should operate.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.FrequencyConverter.maxU
documentation:
The maximum voltage on the DC side at which the frequency converter should
operate.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.FrequencyConverter.minP
documentation:
The minimum active power on the DC side at which the frequence converter
should operate.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.FrequencyConverter.minU
documentation:
The minimum voltage on the DC side at which the frequency converter should
operate.

A device to convert from one frequency to another (e.g., frequency F1 to
F2) comprises a pair of FrequencyConverter instances. One converts from
F1 to DC, the other converts the DC to F2.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerElectronicsConnection

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerElectronicsConnection.maxIFault
documentation:
Maximum fault current this device will contribute, in per-unit of rated
current, before the converter protection will trip or bypass.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerElectronicsConnection.maxQ
documentation:
Maximum reactive power limit. This is the maximum (nameplate) limit for
the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerElectronicsConnection.minQ
documentation:
Minimum reactive power limit for the unit. This is the minimum (nameplate)
limit for the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerElectronicsConnection.p
documentation:
Active power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerElectronicsConnection.q
documentation:
Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerElectronicsConnection.ratedS
documentation:
Nameplate apparent power rating for the unit.
The attribute shall have a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerElectronicsConnection.ratedU
documentation:
Rated voltage (nameplate data, Ur in IEC 60909-0). It is primarily used
for short circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909.

A connection to the AC network for energy production or consumption that
uses power electronics rather than rotating machines.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RotatingMachine

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RotatingMachine.ratedPowerFactor
documentation:
Power factor (nameplate data). It is primarily used for short circuit data
exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RotatingMachine.p
documentation:
Active power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RotatingMachine.q
documentation:
Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RotatingMachine.ratedS
documentation:
Nameplate apparent power rating for the unit.
The attribute shall have a positive value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RotatingMachine.ratedU
documentation:
Rated voltage (nameplate data, Ur in IEC 60909-0). It is primarily used
for short circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RotatingMachine.GeneratingUnit
documentation:
A synchronous machine may operate as a generator and as such becomes a
member of a generating unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RotatingMachine.HydroPump
documentation:
The synchronous machine drives the turbine which moves the water from a
low elevation to a higher elevation. The direction of machine rotation
for pumping may or may not be the same as for generating.
A rotating machine which may be used as a generator or motor.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AsynchronousMachine

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AsynchronousMachine.converterFedDrive
documentation:
Indicates whether the machine is a converter fed drive. Used for short
circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AsynchronousMachine.iaIrRatio
documentation:
Ratio of locked-rotor current to the rated current of the motor (Ia/Ir).
Used for short circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AsynchronousMachine.polePairNumber
documentation:
Number of pole pairs of stator. Used for short circuit data exchange according
to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AsynchronousMachine.reversible
documentation:
Indicates for converter drive motors if the power can be reversible. Used
for short circuit data exchange according to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AsynchronousMachine.rxLockedRotorRatio
documentation:
Locked rotor ratio (R/X). Used for short circuit data exchange according
to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AsynchronousMachine.asynchronousMachineType
documentation: Indicates the type of Asynchronous Machine (motor or generator).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AsynchronousMachine.efficiency
documentation:
