Efficiency of the asynchronous machine at nominal operation in percent.
Indicator for converter drive motors. Used for short circuit data exchange
according to IEC 60909

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AsynchronousMachine.nominalFrequency
documentation: Nameplate data indicates if the machine is 50 or 60 Hz.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AsynchronousMachine.nominalSpeed
documentation: Nameplate data. Depends on the slip and number of pole pairs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AsynchronousMachine.ratedMechanicalPower
documentation:
Rated mechanical power (Pr in the IEC 60909-0). Used for short circuit
data exchange according to IEC 60909.

A rotating machine whose shaft rotates asynchronously with the electrical
field. Also known as an induction machine with no external connection to
the rotor windings, e.g squirrel-cage induction machine.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SynchronousMachine

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SynchronousMachine.ikk
documentation:
Steady-state short-circuit current (in A for the profile) of generator
with compound excitation during 3-phase short circuit.
- Ikk=0: Generator with no compound excitation.
- Ikk?0: Generator with compound excitation.
Ikk is used to calculate the minimum steady-state short-circuit current
for generators with compound excitation
(Section 4.6.1.2 in the IEC 60909-0)
Used only for single fed short circuit on a generator. (Section 4.3.4.2.
in the IEC 60909-0)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SynchronousMachine.maxQ
documentation:
Maximum reactive power limit. This is the maximum (nameplate) limit for
the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SynchronousMachine.minQ
documentation: Minimum reactive power limit for the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SynchronousMachine.operatingMode
documentation: Current mode of operation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SynchronousMachine.type
documentation: Modes that this synchronous machine can operate in.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SynchronousMachine.ReactiveCapabilityCurves
documentation: All available reactive capability curves for this synchronous machine.

An electromechanical device that operates with shaft rotating synchronously
with the network. It is a single machine operating either as a generator
or synchronous condenser or pump.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShuntCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShuntCompensator.grounded
documentation: Used for Yn and Zn connections. True if the neutral is solidly grounded.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShuntCompensator.maximumSections
documentation: The maximum number of sections that may be switched in.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShuntCompensator.normalSections
documentation: The normal number of sections switched in.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShuntCompensator.sections
documentation:
Shunt compensator sections in use.
Starting value for steady state solution. Non integer values are allowed
to support continuous variables. The reasons for continuous value are to
support study cases where no discrete shunt compensators has yet been designed,
a solutions where a narrow voltage band force the sections to oscillate
or accommodate for a continuous solution as input.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShuntCompensator.aVRDelay
documentation:
Time delay required for the device to be connected or disconnected by automatic
voltage regulation (AVR).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShuntCompensator.nomU
documentation:
The voltage at which the nominal reactive power may be calculated. This
should normally be within 10% of the voltage at which the capacitor is
connected to the network.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShuntCompensator.phaseConnection
documentation: The type of phase connection, such as wye or delta.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShuntCompensator.ShuntCompensatorPhase
documentation: The individual phases models for the shunt compensator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShuntCompensator.SvShuntCompensatorSections
documentation: The state for the number of shunt compensator sections in service.

A shunt capacitor or reactor or switchable bank of shunt capacitors or
reactors. A section of a shunt compensator is an individual capacitor or
reactor. A negative value for reactivePerSection indicates that the compensator
is a reactor. ShuntCompensator is a single terminal device. Ground is implied.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LinearShuntCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LinearShuntCompensator.b0PerSection
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) susceptance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LinearShuntCompensator.bPerSection
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) susceptance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LinearShuntCompensator.g0PerSection
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) conductance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LinearShuntCompensator.gPerSection
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance per section

A linear shunt compensator has banks or sections with equal admittance
values.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonlinearShuntCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonlinearShuntCompensator.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoints
documentation: All points of the non-linear shunt compensator.

A non linear shunt compensator has bank or section admittance values that
differs.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.StaticVarCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.StaticVarCompensator.capacitiveRating
documentation: Maximum available capacitive reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.StaticVarCompensator.inductiveRating
documentation: Maximum available inductive reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.StaticVarCompensator.q
documentation:
Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a node.
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.StaticVarCompensator.slope
documentation:
The characteristics slope of an SVC defines how the reactive power output
changes in proportion to the difference between the regulated bus voltage
and the voltage setpoint.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.StaticVarCompensator.sVCControlMode
documentation: SVC control mode.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.StaticVarCompensator.voltageSetPoint
documentation:
The reactive power output of the SVC is proportional to the difference
between the voltage at the regulated bus and the voltage setpoint. When
the regulated bus voltage is equal to the voltage setpoint, the reactive
power output is zero.

A facility for providing variable and controllable shunt reactive power.
The SVC typically consists of a stepdown transformer, filter, thyristor-controlled
reactor, and thyristor-switched capacitor arms.
The SVC may operate in fixed MVar output mode or in voltage control mode.
When in voltage control mode, the output of the SVC will be proportional
to the deviation of voltage at the controlled bus from the voltage setpoint.
The SVC characteristic slope defines the proportion. If the voltage at
the controlled bus is equal to the voltage setpoint, the SVC MVar output
is zero.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Ground
documentation:
A point where the system is grounded used for connecting conducting equipment
to ground. The power system model can have any number of grounds.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerTransformer

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerTransformer.vectorGroup
documentation:
Vector group of the transformer for protective relaying, e.g., Dyn1. For
unbalanced transformers, this may not be simply determined from the constituent
winding connections and phase angle dispacements.
The vectorGroup string consists of the following components in the order
listed: high voltage winding connection, mid voltage winding connection
(for three winding transformers), phase displacement clock number from
0 to 11, low voltage winding connection
phase displacement clock number from 0 to 11. The winding connections are
D (delta), Y (wye), YN (wye with neutral), Z (zigzag), ZN (zigzag with
neutral), A (auto transformer). Upper case means the high voltage, lower
case mid or low. The high voltage winding always has clock postion 0 and
is not included in the vector group string. Some examples: YNy0 (two winding
wye to wye with no phase displacement), YNd11 (two winding wye to delta
with 330 degrees phase displacement), YNyn0d5 (three winding transformer
wye with neutral high voltgage, wye with neutral mid voltgage and no phase
displacement, delta low voltage with 150 degrees displacement).
Phase displacement is defined as the angular difference between the phasors
representing the voltages between the neutral point (real or imaginary)
and the corresponding terminals of two windings, a positive sequence voltage
system being applied to the high-voltage terminals, following each other
in alphabetical sequence if they are lettered, or in numerical sequence
if they are numbered: the phasors are assumed to rotate in a counter-clockwise
sense.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerTransformer.PowerTransformerEnd
documentation: The ends of this power transformer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerTransformer.TransformerTanks
documentation: All transformers that belong to this bank.

An electrical device consisting of two or more coupled windings, with or
without a magnetic core, for introducing mutual coupling between electric
circuits. Transformers can be used to control voltage and phase shift (active
power flow).
A power transformer may be composed of separate transformer tanks that
need not be identical.
A power transformer can be modeled with or without tanks and is intended
for use in both balanced and unbalanced representations. A power transformer
typically has two terminals, but may have one (grounding), three or more
terminals.
The inherited association ConductingEquipment.BaseVoltage should not be
used. The association from TransformerEnd to BaseVoltage should be used
instead.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SeriesCompensator

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SeriesCompensator.varistorPresent
documentation:
Describe if a metal oxide varistor (mov) for over voltage protection is
configured at the series compensator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SeriesCompensator.r
documentation: Positive sequence resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SeriesCompensator.r0
documentation: Zero sequence resistance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SeriesCompensator.varistorRatedCurrent
documentation: The maximum current the varistor is designed to handle at specified duration.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SeriesCompensator.varistorVoltageThreshold
documentation: The dc voltage at which the varistor start conducting.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SeriesCompensator.x
documentation: Positive sequence reactance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SeriesCompensator.x0
documentation: Zero sequence reactance.

A Series Compensator is a series capacitor or reactor or an AC transmission
line without charging susceptance. It is a two terminal device.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Switch

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Switch.normalOpen
documentation:
The attribute is used in cases when no Measurement for the status value
is present. If the Switch has a status measurement the Discrete.normalValue
is expected to match with the Switch.normalOpen.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Switch.open
documentation:
The attribute tells if the switch is considered open when used as input
to topology processing.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Switch.retained
documentation:
Branch is retained in a bus branch model. The flow through retained switches
will normally be calculated in power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Switch.ratedCurrent
documentation:
The maximum continuous current carrying capacity in amps governed by the
device material and construction.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Switch.CompositeSwitch
documentation: Composite switch to which this Switch belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Switch.SvSwitch
documentation: The switch state associated with the switch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Switch.SwitchPhase
documentation: The individual switch phases for the switch.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Switch.SwitchSchedules
documentation: A Switch can be associated with SwitchSchedules.

A generic device designed to close, or open, or both, one or more electric
circuits. All switches are two terminal devices including grounding switches.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Cut

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Cut.lengthFromTerminal1
documentation:
The length to the place where the cut is located starting from side one
of the cut line segment, i.e. the line segment Terminal with sequenceNumber
equal to 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Cut.ACLineSegment
documentation: The line segment to which the cut is applied.

A cut separates a line segment into two parts. The cut appears as a switch
inserted between these two parts and connects them together. As the cut
is normally open there is no galvanic connection between the two line segment
parts. But it is possible to close the cut to get galvanic connection.
The cut terminals are oriented towards the line segment terminals with
the same sequence number. Hence the cut terminal with sequence number equal
to 1 is oriented to the line segment's terminal with sequence number equal
to 1.
The cut terminals also act as connection points for jumpers and other equipment,
e.g. a mobile generator. To enable this, connectivity nodes are placed
at the cut terminals. Once the connectivity nodes are in place any conducting
equipment can be connected at them.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Disconnector
documentation:
A manually operated or motor operated mechanical switching device used
for changing the connections in a circuit, or for isolating a circuit or
equipment from a source of power. It is required to open or close circuits
when negligible current is broken or made.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Fuse
documentation:
An overcurrent protective device with a circuit opening fusible part that
is heated and severed by the passage of overcurrent through it. A fuse
is considered a switching device because it breaks current.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.GroundDisconnector
documentation:
A manually operated or motor operated mechanical switching device used
for isolating a circuit or equipment from ground.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Jumper
documentation:
A short section of conductor with negligible impedance which can be manually
removed and replaced if the circuit is de-energized. Note that zero-impedance
branches can potentially be modeled by other equipment types.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ProtectedSwitch

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ProtectedSwitch.breakingCapacity
documentation:
The maximum fault current a breaking device can break safely under prescribed
conditions of use.
A ProtectedSwitch is a switching device that can be operated by ProtectionEquipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Breaker

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Breaker.inTransitTime
documentation: The transition time from open to close.

A mechanical switching device capable of making, carrying, and breaking
currents under normal circuit conditions and also making, carrying for
a specified time, and breaking currents under specified abnormal circuit
conditions e.g. those of short circuit.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LoadBreakSwitch
documentation:
A mechanical switching device capable of making, carrying, and breaking
currents under normal operating conditions.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Recloser
documentation:
Pole-mounted fault interrupter with built-in phase and ground relays, current
transformer (CT), and supplemental controls.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Sectionaliser
documentation:
Automatic switch that will lock open to isolate a faulted section. It may,
or may not, have load breaking capability. Its primary purpose is to provide
fault sectionalising at locations where the fault current is either too
high, or too low, for proper coordination of fuses.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.GeneratingUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.GeneratingUnit.maxOperatingP
documentation:
This is the maximum operating active power limit the dispatcher can enter
for this unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.GeneratingUnit.minOperatingP
documentation:
This is the minimum operating active power limit the dispatcher can enter
for this unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.GeneratingUnit.GenUnitOpCostCurves
documentation:
A generating unit may have one or more cost curves, depending upon fuel
mixture and fuel cost.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.GeneratingUnit.GenUnitOpSchedule
documentation:
A generating unit may have an operating schedule, indicating the planned
operation of the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.GeneratingUnit.GrossToNetActivePowerCurves
documentation:
A generating unit may have a gross active power to net active power curve,
describing the losses and auxiliary power requirements of the unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.GeneratingUnit.RotatingMachine
documentation:
A synchronous machine may operate as a generator and as such becomes a
member of a generating unit.

A single or set of synchronous machines for converting mechanical power
into alternating-current power. For example, individual machines within
a set may be defined for scheduling purposes while a single control signal
is derived for the set. In this case there would be a GeneratingUnit for
each member of the set and an additional GeneratingUnit corresponding to
the set.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroGeneratingUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroGeneratingUnit.energyConversionCapability
documentation: Energy conversion capability for generating.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroGeneratingUnit.hydroUnitWaterCost
documentation: The equivalent cost of water that drives the hydro turbine.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroGeneratingUnit.HydroGeneratingEfficiencyCurves
documentation: A hydro generating unit has an efficiency curve.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroGeneratingUnit.HydroPowerPlant
documentation: The hydro generating unit belongs to a hydro power plant.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroGeneratingUnit.PenstockLossCurve
documentation: A hydro generating unit has a penstock loss curve.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroGeneratingUnit.TailbayLossCurve
documentation: A hydro generating unit has a tailbay loss curve.

A generating unit whose prime mover is a hydraulic turbine (e.g., Francis,
Pelton, Kaplan).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NuclearGeneratingUnit
documentation: A nuclear generating unit.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SolarGeneratingUnit
documentation:
A solar thermal generating unit, connected to the grid by means of a rotating
machine. This class does not represent photovoltaic (PV) generation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ThermalGeneratingUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ThermalGeneratingUnit.oMCost
documentation: Operating and maintenance cost for the thermal unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ThermalGeneratingUnit.CAESPlant
documentation:
A thermal generating unit may be a member of a compressed air energy storage
plant.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ThermalGeneratingUnit.CogenerationPlant
documentation: A thermal generating unit may be a member of a cogeneration plant.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ThermalGeneratingUnit.CombinedCyclePlant
documentation: A thermal generating unit may be a member of a combined cycle plant.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ThermalGeneratingUnit.EmissionCurves
documentation: A thermal generating unit may have one or more emission curves.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ThermalGeneratingUnit.EmmissionAccounts
documentation: A thermal generating unit may have one or more emission allowance accounts.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ThermalGeneratingUnit.FossilFuels
documentation: A thermal generating unit may have one or more fossil fuels.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ThermalGeneratingUnit.FuelAllocationSchedules
documentation: A thermal generating unit may have one or more fuel allocation schedules.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ThermalGeneratingUnit.HeatInputCurve
documentation: A thermal generating unit may have a heat input curve.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ThermalGeneratingUnit.HeatRateCurve
documentation: A thermal generating unit may have a heat rate curve.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ThermalGeneratingUnit.IncrementalHeatRateCurve
documentation: A thermal generating unit may have an incremental heat rate curve.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ThermalGeneratingUnit.ShutdownCurve
documentation: A thermal generating unit may have a shutdown curve.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ThermalGeneratingUnit.StartupModel
documentation: A thermal generating unit may have a startup model.

A generating unit whose prime mover could be a steam turbine, combustion
turbine, or diesel engine.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.WindGeneratingUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.WindGeneratingUnit.windGenUnitType
documentation: The kind of wind generating unit

A wind driven generating unit, connected to the grid by means of a rotating
machine. May be used to represent a single turbine or an aggregation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPump

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPump.pumpDischAtMaxHead
documentation: The pumping discharge under maximum head conditions, usually at full gate.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPump.pumpDischAtMinHead
documentation: The pumping discharge under minimum head conditions, usually at full gate.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPump.pumpPowerAtMaxHead
documentation: The pumping power under maximum head conditions, usually at full gate.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPump.pumpPowerAtMinHead
documentation: The pumping power under minimum head conditions, usually at full gate.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPump.HydroPowerPlant
documentation:
The hydro pump may be a member of a pumped storage plant or a pump for
distributing water.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPump.HydroPumpOpSchedule
documentation:
The hydro pump has a pumping schedule over time, indicating when pumping
is to occur.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPump.RotatingMachine
documentation:
The synchronous machine drives the turbine which moves the water from a
low elevation to a higher elevation. The direction of machine rotation
for pumping may or may not be the same as for generating.

A synchronous motor-driven pump, typically associated with a pumped storage
plant.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerElectronicsUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerElectronicsUnit.maxP
documentation:
Maximum active power limit. This is the maximum (nameplate) limit for the
unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerElectronicsUnit.minP
documentation:
Minimum active power limit. This is the minimum (nameplate) limit for the
unit.

A generating unit or battery or aggregation that connects to the AC network
using power electronics rather than rotating machines.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.BatteryUnit

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.BatteryUnit.batteryState
documentation: indicates whether the battery is charging, discharging or idle

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.BatteryUnit.ratedE
documentation: full energy storage capacity of the battery

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.BatteryUnit.storedE
documentation: amount of energy currently stored; no more than ratedE
An electrochemical energy storage device

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhotovoltaicUnit
documentation: A photovoltaic device or an aggregation of such devices

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerElectronicsWindUnit
documentation:
A wind generating unit that connects to the AC network with power electronics
rather than rotating machines or an aggregation of such units.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerTank

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerTank.PowerTransformer
documentation: Bank this transformer belongs to.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerTank.TransformerTankEnds
documentation: All windings of this transformer.

An assembly of two or more coupled windings that transform electrical power
between voltage levels. These windings are bound on a common core and place
in the same tank. Transformer tank can be used to model both single-phase
and 3-phase transformers.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPowerPlant

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPowerPlant.penstockType
documentation: Type and configuration of hydro plant penstock(s).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPowerPlant.surgeTankCode
documentation:
A code describing the type (or absence) of surge tank that is associated
with the hydro power plant.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPowerPlant.dischargeTravelDelay
documentation: Water travel delay from tailbay to next downstream hydro power station.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPowerPlant.genRatedP
documentation: The hydro plant's generating rating active power for rated head conditions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPowerPlant.hydroPlantStorageType
documentation: The type of hydro power plant water storage.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPowerPlant.plantDischargeCapacity
documentation: Total plant discharge capacity.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPowerPlant.plantRatedHead
documentation: The plant's rated gross head.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPowerPlant.pumpRatedP
documentation: The hydro plant's pumping rating active power for rated head conditions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPowerPlant.surgeTankCrestLevel
documentation: The level at which the surge tank spills.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPowerPlant.GenSourcePumpDischargeReservoir
documentation:
Generators are supplied water from or pumps discharge water to an upstream
reservoir.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPowerPlant.HydroGeneratingUnits
documentation: The hydro generating unit belongs to a hydro power plant.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPowerPlant.HydroPumps
documentation:
The hydro pump may be a member of a pumped storage plant or a pump for
distributing water.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.HydroPowerPlant.Reservoir
documentation:
Generators discharge water to or pumps are supplied water from a downstream
reservoir.

A hydro power station which can generate or pump. When generating, the
generator turbines receive water from an upper reservoir. When pumping,
the pumps receive their water from a lower reservoir.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerCutZone

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerCutZone.cutLevel1
documentation: First level (amount) of load to cut as a percentage of total zone load.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerCutZone.cutLevel2
documentation: Second level (amount) of load to cut as a percentage of total zone load.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerCutZone.EnergyConsumers
documentation: Energy consumer is assigned to the power cut zone.
An area or zone of the power system which is used for load shedding purposes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerElectronicsConnectionPhase.p
documentation:
Active power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow into the equipment from the network.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerElectronicsConnectionPhase.phase
documentation:
Phase of this energy producer component. If the energy producer is wye
connected, the connection is from the indicated phase to the central ground
or neutral point. If the energy producer is delta connected, the phase
indicates an energy producer connected from the indicated phase to the
next logical non-neutral phase.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerElectronicsConnectionPhase.q
documentation:
Reactive power injection. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow into the equipment from the network.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RegulatingControl

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RegulatingControl.discrete
documentation:
The regulation is performed in a discrete mode. This applies to equipment
with discrete controls, e.g. tap changers and shunt compensators.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RegulatingControl.enabled
documentation: The flag tells if regulation is enabled.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RegulatingControl.targetDeadband
documentation:
This is a deadband used with discrete control to avoid excessive update
of controls like tap changers and shunt compensator banks while regulating.
The units of those appropriate for the mode.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RegulatingControl.targetValue
documentation:
The target value specified for case input. This value can be used for the
target value without the use of schedules. The value has the units appropriate
to the mode attribute.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RegulatingControl.mode
documentation:
The regulating control mode presently available. This specification allows
for determining the kind of regulation without need for obtaining the units
from a schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RegulatingControl.monitoredPhase
documentation: Phase voltage controlling this regulator, measured at regulator location.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RegulatingControl.RegulatingCondEq
documentation: The equipment that participates in this regulating control scheme.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RegulatingControl.RegulationSchedule
documentation: Schedule for this Regulating regulating control.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RegulatingControl.Terminal
documentation:
The terminal associated with this regulating control. The terminal is associated
instead of a node, since the terminal could connect into either a topological
node (bus in bus-branch model) or a connectivity node (detailed switch
model). Sometimes it is useful to model regulation at a terminal of a bus
bar object since the bus bar can be present in both a bus-branch model
or a model with switch detail.

Specifies a set of equipment that works together to control a power system
quantity such as voltage or flow.
Remote bus voltage control is possible by specifying the controlled terminal
located at some place remote from the controlling equipment.
In case multiple equipment, possibly of different types, control same terminal
there must be only one RegulatingControl at that terminal. The most specific
subtype of RegulatingControl shall be used in case such equipment participate
in the control, e.g. TapChangerControl for tap changers.
For flow control load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign means
flow out from a TopologicalNode (bus) into the conducting equipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChangerControl

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChangerControl.lineDropCompensation
documentation: If true, the line drop compensation is to be applied.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChangerControl.limitVoltage
documentation:
Maximum allowed regulated voltage on the PT secondary, regardless of line
drop compensation. Sometimes referred to as first-house protection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChangerControl.lineDropR
documentation: Line drop compensator resistance setting for normal (forward) power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChangerControl.lineDropX
documentation: Line drop compensator reactance setting for normal (forward) power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChangerControl.reverseLineDropR
documentation: Line drop compensator resistance setting for reverse power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChangerControl.reverseLineDropX
documentation: Line drop compensator reactance setting for reverse power flow.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChangerControl.TapChanger
documentation: The tap changers that participates in this regulating tap control scheme.

Describes behavior specific to tap changers, e.g. how the voltage at the
end of a line varies with the load level and compensation of the voltage
drop by tap adjustment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir.energyStorageRating
documentation: The reservoir's energy storage rating in energy for given head conditions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir.riverOutletWorks
documentation: River outlet works for riparian right releases or other purposes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir.spillwayCapacity
documentation: The flow capacity of the spillway in cubic meters per second.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir.spillWayGateType
documentation: Type of spillway gate, including parameters.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir.activeStorageCapacity
documentation:
Storage volume between the full supply level and the normal minimum operating
level.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir.fullSupplyLevel
documentation:
Full supply level, above which water will spill. This can be the spillway
crest level or the top of closed gates.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir.grossCapacity
documentation: Total capacity of reservoir.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir.normalMinOperateLevel
documentation: Normal minimum operating level below which the penstocks will draw air.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir.spillTravelDelay
documentation: The spillway water travel delay to the next downstream reservoir.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir.spillwayCrestLength
documentation: The length of the spillway crest.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir.spillwayCrestLevel
documentation: Spillway crest level above which water will spill.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir.HydroPowerPlants
documentation:
Generators discharge water to or pumps are supplied water from a downstream
reservoir.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir.InflowForecasts
documentation: A reservoir may have a "natural" inflow forecast.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir.LevelVsVolumeCurves
documentation: A reservoir may have a level versus volume relationship.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir.SpillsFromReservoir
documentation: A reservoir may spill into a downstream reservoir.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir.SpillsIntoReservoirs
documentation: A reservoir may spill into a downstream reservoir.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir.TargetLevelSchedule
documentation: A reservoir may have a water level target schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Reservoir.UpstreamFromHydroPowerPlants
documentation:
Generators are supplied water from or pumps discharge water to an upstream
reservoir.

A water storage facility within a hydro system, including: ponds, lakes,
lagoons, and rivers. The storage is usually behind some type of dam.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShuntCompensatorPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShuntCompensatorPhase.maximumSections
documentation: The maximum number of sections that may be switched in for this phase.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShuntCompensatorPhase.normalSections
documentation: For the capacitor phase, the normal number of sections switched in.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShuntCompensatorPhase.sections
documentation:
Number of sections in use for this phase, when controlled independently
from the other phases. If not provided, may default to the parent ShuntCompensator.sections
value (see ShuntCompensator documentation for more details).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShuntCompensatorPhase.phase
documentation:
Phase of this shunt compensator component. If the shunt compensator is
wye connected, the connection is from the indicated phase to the central
ground or neutral point. If the shunt compensator is delta connected, the
phase indicates a shunt compensator connected from the indicated phase
to the next logical non-neutral phase.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShuntCompensatorPhase.ShuntCompensator
documentation: Shunt compensator of this shunt compensator phase.

Single phase of a multi-phase shunt compensator when its attributes might
be different per phase.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LinearShuntCompensatorPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LinearShuntCompensatorPhase.bPerSection
documentation:
Susceptance per section of the phase if shunt compensator is wye connected.
Susceptance per section phase to phase if shunt compensator is delta connected.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.LinearShuntCompensatorPhase.gPerSection
documentation:
Conductance per section for this phase if shunt compensator is wye connected.
Conductance per section phase to phase if shunt compensator is delta connected.

A per phase linear shunt compensator has banks or sections with equal admittance
values.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhase.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoints
documentation: All points of the non-linear shunt compensator phase.

A per phase non linear shunt compensator has bank or section admittance
values that differs.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SwitchPhase

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SwitchPhase.closed
documentation:
The attribute tells if the switch is considered closed when used as input
to topology processing.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SwitchPhase.normalOpen
documentation:
Used in cases when no Measurement for the status value is present. If the
SwitchPhase has a status measurement the Discrete.normalValue is expected
to match with this value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SwitchPhase.phaseSide1
documentation:
Phase of this SwitchPhase on the side with terminal sequence number equal
1. Should be a phase contained in that terminal’s phases attribute.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SwitchPhase.phaseSide2
documentation:
Phase of this SwitchPhase on the side with terminal sequence number equal
2. Should be a phase contained in that terminal’s Terminal.phases
attribute.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SwitchPhase.ratedCurrent
documentation: Rated current through this phase, if different from the others.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SwitchPhase.Switch
documentation: The switch of the switch phase.

Single phase of a multi-phase switch when its attributes might be different
per phase.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChanger

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChanger.controlEnabled
documentation:
Specifies the regulation status of the equipment. True is regulating, false
is not regulating.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChanger.highStep
documentation:
Highest possible tap step position, advance from neutral.
The attribute shall be greater than lowStep.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChanger.lowStep
documentation: Lowest possible tap step position, retard from neutral

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChanger.ltcFlag
documentation: Specifies whether or not a TapChanger has load tap changing capabilities.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChanger.neutralStep
documentation:
The neutral tap step position for this winding.
The attribute shall be equal or greater than lowStep and equal or less
than highStep.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChanger.normalStep
documentation:
The tap step position used in "normal" network operation for this winding.
For a "Fixed" tap changer indicates the current physical tap setting.
The attribute shall be equal or greater than lowStep and equal or less
than highStep.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChanger.step
documentation:
Tap changer position.
Starting step for a steady state solution. Non integer values are allowed
to support continuous tap variables. The reasons for continuous value are
to support study cases where no discrete tap changers has yet been designed,
a solutions where a narrow voltage band force the tap step to oscillate
or accommodate for a continuous solution as input.
The attribute shall be equal or greater than lowStep and equal or less
than highStep.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChanger.initialDelay
documentation: For an LTC, the delay for initial tap changer operation (first step change)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChanger.neutralU
documentation: Voltage at which the winding operates at the neutral tap setting.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChanger.subsequentDelay
documentation:
For an LTC, the delay for subsequent tap changer operation (second and
later step changes)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChanger.SvTapStep
documentation: The tap step state associated with the tap changer.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChanger.TapChangerControl
documentation: The regulating control scheme in which this tap changer participates.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChanger.TapSchedules
documentation: A TapChanger can have TapSchedules.
Mechanism for changing transformer winding tap positions.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChanger

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChanger.TransformerEnd
documentation: Transformer end to which this phase tap changer belongs.

A transformer phase shifting tap model that controls the phase angle difference
across the power transformer and potentially the active power flow through
the power transformer. This phase tap model may also impact the voltage
magnitude.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChangerLinear

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChangerLinear.stepPhaseShiftIncrement
documentation:
Phase shift per step position. A positive value indicates a positive phase
shift from the winding where the tap is located to the other winding (for
a two-winding transformer).
The actual phase shift increment might be more accurately computed from
the symmetrical or asymmetrical models or a tap step table lookup if those
are available.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChangerLinear.xMax
documentation:
The reactance depend on the tap position according to a "u" shaped curve.
The maximum reactance (xMax) appear at the low and high tap positions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChangerLinear.xMin
documentation:
The reactance depend on the tap position according to a "u" shaped curve.
The minimum reactance (xMin) appear at the mid tap position.

Describes a tap changer with a linear relation between the tap step and
the phase angle difference across the transformer. This is a mathematical
model that is an approximation of a real phase tap changer.
The phase angle is computed as stepPhaseShitfIncrement times the tap position.
The secondary side voltage magnitude is the same as at the primary side.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChangerNonLinear

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChangerNonLinear.voltageStepIncrement
documentation:
The voltage step increment on the out of phase winding specified in percent
of neutral voltage of the tap changer.
When the increment is negative, the voltage decreases when the tap step
increases.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChangerNonLinear.xMax
documentation:
The reactance depend on the tap position according to a "u" shaped curve.
The maximum reactance (xMax) appear at the low and high tap positions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChangerNonLinear.xMin
documentation:
The reactance depend on the tap position according to a "u" shaped curve.
The minimum reactance (xMin) appear at the mid tap position.

The non-linear phase tap changer describes the non-linear behavior of a
phase tap changer. This is a base class for the symmetrical and asymmetrical
phase tap changer models. The details of these models can be found in the
IEC 61970-301 document.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChangerAsymmetrical

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChangerAsymmetrical.windingConnectionAngle
documentation:
The phase angle between the in-phase winding and the out-of -phase winding
used for creating phase shift. The out-of-phase winding produces what is
known as the difference voltage. Setting this angle to 90 degrees is not
the same as a symmetrical transformer.

Describes the tap model for an asymmetrical phase shifting transformer
in which the difference voltage vector adds to the primary side voltage.
The angle between the primary side voltage and the difference voltage is
named the winding connection angle. The phase shift depends on both the
difference voltage magnitude and the winding connection angle.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChangerSymmetrical
documentation:
Describes a symmetrical phase shifting transformer tap model in which the
secondary side voltage magnitude is the same as at the primary side. The
difference voltage magnitude is the base in an equal-sided triangle where
the sides corresponds to the primary and secondary voltages. The phase
angle difference corresponds to the top angle and can be expressed as twice
the arctangent of half the total difference voltage.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChangerTabular.PhaseTapChangerTable
documentation: The phase tap changer table for this phase tap changer.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.consumptionTier
documentation:
In case of common flat-rate pricing for power, in which all purchases are
at a given rate, 'consumptionTier'=0. Otherwise, the value indicates the
consumption tier, which can be used in conjunction with TOU or CPP pricing.
Consumption tier pricing refers to the method of billing in which a certain
"block" of energy is purchased/sold at one price, after which the next
block of energy is purchased at another price, and so on, all throughout
a defined period. At the start of the defined period, consumption is initially
zero, and any usage is measured against the first consumption tier ('consumptionTier'=1).
If this block of energy is consumed before the end of the period, energy
consumption moves to be reconed against the second consumption tier ('consumptionTier'=2),
and so on. At the end of the defined period, the consumption accumulator
is reset, and usage within the 'consumptionTier'=1 restarts.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.cpp
documentation:
Critical peak period (CPP) bucket the reading value is attributed to. Value
0 means not applicable. Even though CPP is usually considered a specialised
form of time of use 'tou', this attribute is defined explicitly for flexibility.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.tou
documentation:
Time of use (TOU) bucket the reading value is attributed to. Value 0 means
not applicable.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.accumulation
documentation:
Accumulation behaviour of a reading over time, usually 'measuringPeriod',
to be used with individual endpoints (as opposed to 'macroPeriod' and 'aggregate'
that are used to describe aggregations of data from individual endpoints).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.aggregate
documentation:
Salient attribute of the reading data aggregated from individual endpoints.
This is mainly used to define a mathematical operation carried out over
'macroPeriod', but may also be used to describe an attribute of the data
when the 'macroPeriod' is not defined.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.commodity
documentation: Commodity being measured.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.currency
documentation: Metering-specific currency.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.flowDirection
documentation:
Flow direction for a reading where the direction of flow of the commodity
is important (for electricity measurements this includes current, energy,
power, and demand).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.macroPeriod
documentation:
Time period of interest that reflects how the reading is viewed or captured
over a long period of time.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.measurementKind
documentation:
Identifies "what" is being measured, as refinement of 'commodity'. When
combined with 'unit', it provides detail to the unit of measure. For example,
'energy' with a unit of measure of 'kWh' indicates to the user that active
energy is being measured, while with 'kVAh' or 'kVArh', it indicates apparent
energy and reactive energy, respectively. 'power' can be combined in a
similar way with various power units of measure: Distortion power ('distortionVoltAmperes')
with 'kVA' is different from 'power' with 'kVA'.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.measuringPeriod
documentation:
Time attribute inherent or fundamental to the reading value (as opposed
to 'macroPeriod' that supplies an "adjective" to describe aspects of a
time period with regard to the measurement). It refers to the way the value
was originally measured and not to the frequency at which it is reported
or presented. For example, an hourly interval of consumption data would
have value 'hourly' as an attribute. However in the case of an hourly sampled
voltage value, the meterReadings schema would carry the 'hourly' interval
size information.
It is common for meters to report demand in a form that is measured over
the course of a portion of an hour, while enterprise applications however
commonly assume the demand (in kW or kVAr) normalised to 1 hour. The system
that receives readings directly from the meter therefore shall perform
this transformation before publishing readings for use by the other enterprise
systems. The scalar used is chosen based on the block size (not any sub-interval
size).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.multiplier
documentation: Metering-specific multiplier.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.phases
documentation: Metering-specific phase code.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.unit
documentation: Metering-specific unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.argument
documentation:
Argument used to introduce numbers into the unit of measure description
where they are needed (e.g., 4 where the measure needs an argument such
as CEMI(n=4)). Most arguments used in practice however will be integers
(i.e., 'denominator'=1).
Value 0 in 'numerator' and 'denominator' means not applicable.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.Channel
documentation: Channel reporting/collecting register values with this type information.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.interharmonic
documentation:
Indication of a "harmonic" or "interharmonic" basis for the measurement.
Value 0 in 'numerator' and 'denominator' means not applicable.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.IntervalBlocks
documentation: All blocks containing interval reading values with this type information.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.MetrologyRequirements
documentation: All metrology requirements that require this reading type to be collected.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.PendingCalculation
documentation: Pending calculation that produced this reading type.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReadingType.Readings
documentation: All reading values with this type information.

Detailed description for a type of a reading value. Values in attributes
allow for the creation of recommended codes to be used for identifying
reading value types as follows: <macroPeriod>.<aggregate>.<measuringPeriod>.<accumulation>.<flowDirection>.<commodity>.<measurementKind>.<interharmonic.numerator>.<interharmonic.denominator>.<argument.numerator>.<argument.denominator>.<tou>.<cpp>.<consumptionTier>.<phases>.<multiplier>.<unit>.<currency>.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Register
documentation:
A device that indicates or records units of the commodity or other quantity
measured.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReportingGroup.PowerSystemResource

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReportingGroup.ReportingSuperGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ServiceCategory.PricingStructures

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ServiceCategory.UsagePoints

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ServiceMultiplier

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.StartupModel.minimumDownTime

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.StartupModel.minimumRunTime

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.StartupModel.riskFactorCost

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.StartupModel.startupCost

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.StartupModel.stbyAuxP

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ThermostatController.rampHigh

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ThermostatController.rampLow

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ThermostatController.priceCap

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ThermostatController.rangeLow
