classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TopologicalIsland

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TopologicalIsland.AngleRefTopologicalNode
documentation:
The angle reference for the island. Normally there is one TopologicalNode
that is selected as the angle reference for each island. Other reference
schemes exist, so the association is typically optional.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TopologicalIsland.TopologicalNodes
documentation: A topological node belongs to a topological island.

An electrically connected subset of the network. Topological islands can
change as the current network state changes: e.g. due to
- disconnect switches or breakers change state in a SCADA/EMS
- manual creation, change or deletion of topological nodes in a planning
tool.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TopologicalNode

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TopologicalNode.pInjection
documentation:
The active power injected into the bus at this location in addition to
injections from equipment. Positive sign means injection into the TopologicalNode
(bus).
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TopologicalNode.qInjection
documentation:
The reactive power injected into the bus at this location in addition to
injections from equipment. Positive sign means injection into the TopologicalNode
(bus).
Starting value for a steady state solution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TopologicalNode.AngleRefTopologicalIsland
documentation:
The island for which the node is an angle reference. Normally there is
one angle reference node for each island.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TopologicalNode.BaseVoltage
documentation: The base voltage of the topologocial node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TopologicalNode.BusNameMarker
documentation: BusnameMarkers that may refer to a pre defined TopologicalNode.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TopologicalNode.ConnectivityNodeContainer
documentation: The connectivity node container to which the toplogical node belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TopologicalNode.ConnectivityNodes
documentation:
The connectivity nodes combine together to form this topological node.
May depend on the current state of switches in the network.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TopologicalNode.ReportingGroup
documentation: The reporting group to which the topological node belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TopologicalNode.SvInjection
documentation: The injection flows state variables associated with the topological node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TopologicalNode.SvVoltage
documentation: The state voltage associated with the topological node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TopologicalNode.Terminal
documentation:
The terminals associated with the topological node. This can be used as
an alternative to the connectivity node path to terminal, thus making it
unneccesary to model connectivity nodes in some cases. Note that if connectivity
nodes are in the model, this association would probably not be used as
an input specification.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TopologicalNode.TopologicalIsland
documentation: A topological node belongs to a topological island.

For a detailed substation model a topological node is a set of connectivity
nodes that, in the current network state, are connected together through
any type of closed switches, including jumpers. Topological nodes change
as the current network state changes (i.e., switches, breakers, etc. change
state).
For a planning model, switch statuses are not used to form topological
nodes. Instead they are manually created or deleted in a model builder
tool. Topological nodes maintained this way are also called "busses".

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerCoreAdmittance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerCoreAdmittance.b
documentation: Magnetizing branch susceptance (B mag). The value can be positive or negative.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerCoreAdmittance.b0
documentation: Zero sequence magnetizing branch susceptance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerCoreAdmittance.g
documentation: Magnetizing branch conductance (G mag).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerCoreAdmittance.g0
documentation: Zero sequence magnetizing branch conductance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerCoreAdmittance.TransformerEnd
documentation: All transformer ends having this core admittance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerCoreAdmittance.TransformerEndInfo
documentation: Transformer end datasheet used to calculate this core admittance.

The transformer core admittance. Used to specify the core admittance of
a transformer in a manner that can be shared among power transformers.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerEnd

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerEnd.endNumber
documentation:
Number for this transformer end, corresponding to the end's order in the
power transformer vector group or phase angle clock number. Highest voltage
winding should be 1. Each end within a power transformer should have a
unique subsequent end number. Note the transformer end number need not
match the terminal sequence number.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerEnd.grounded
documentation: (for Yn and Zn connections) True if the neutral is solidly grounded.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerEnd.rground
documentation:
(for Yn and Zn connections) Resistance part of neutral impedance where
'grounded' is true.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerEnd.xground
documentation:
(for Yn and Zn connections) Reactive part of neutral impedance where 'grounded'
is true.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerEnd.BaseVoltage
documentation: Base voltage of the transformer end. This is essential for PU calculation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerEnd.CoreAdmittance
documentation:
Core admittance of this transformer end, representing magnetising current
and core losses. The full values of the transformer should be supplied
for one transformer end only.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerEnd.FromMeshImpedance
documentation: All mesh impedances between this 'to' and other 'from' transformer ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerEnd.PhaseTapChanger
documentation: Phase tap changer associated with this transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerEnd.RatioTapChanger
documentation: Ratio tap changer associated with this transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerEnd.StarImpedance
documentation:
(accurate for 2- or 3-winding transformers only) Pi-model impedances of
this transformer end. By convention, for a two winding transformer, the
full values of the transformer should be entered on the high voltage end
(endNumber=1).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerEnd.Terminal
documentation: Terminal of the power transformer to which this transformer end belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerEnd.ToMeshImpedance
documentation: All mesh impedances between this 'from' and other 'to' transformer ends.

A conducting connection point of a power transformer. It corresponds to
a physical transformer winding terminal. In earlier CIM versions, the TransformerWinding
class served a similar purpose, but this class is more flexible because
it associates to terminal but is not a specialization of ConductingEquipment.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerTransformerEnd

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerTransformerEnd.phaseAngleClock
documentation:
Terminal voltage phase angle displacement where 360 degrees are represented
with clock hours. The valid values are 0 to 11. For example, for the secondary
side end of a transformer with vector group code of 'Dyn11', specify the
connection kind as wye with neutral and specify the phase angle of the
clock as 11. The clock value of the transformer end number specified as
1, is assumed to be zero. Note the transformer end number is not assumed
to be the same as the terminal sequence number.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerTransformerEnd.connectionKind
documentation: Kind of connection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerTransformerEnd.r
documentation:
Resistance (star-model) of the transformer end.
The attribute shall be equal or greater than zero for non-equivalent transformers.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerTransformerEnd.ratedS
documentation:
Normal apparent power rating.
The attribute shall be a positive value. For a two-winding transformer
the values for the high and low voltage sides shall be identical.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerTransformerEnd.ratedU
documentation:
Rated voltage: phase-phase for three-phase windings, and either phase-phase
or phase-neutral for single-phase windings.
A high voltage side, as given by TransformerEnd.endNumber, shall have a
ratedU that is greater or equal than ratedU for the lower voltage sides.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PowerTransformerEnd.PowerTransformer
documentation: The power transformer of this power transformer end.

A PowerTransformerEnd is associated with each Terminal of a PowerTransformer.
The impedance values r, r0, x, and x0 of a PowerTransformerEnd represents
a star equivalent as follows
1) for a two Terminal PowerTransformer the high voltage (TransformerEnd.endNumber=1)
PowerTransformerEnd has non zero values on r, r0, x, and x0 while the low
voltage (TransformerEnd.endNumber=0) PowerTransformerEnd has zero values
for r, r0, x, and x0.
2) for a three Terminal PowerTransformer the three PowerTransformerEnds
represents a star equivalent with each leg in the star represented by r,
r0, x, and x0 values.
3) For a three Terminal transformer each PowerTransformerEnd shall have
g, g0, b and b0 values corresponding the no load losses distributed on
the three PowerTransformerEnds. The total no load loss shunt impedances
may also be placed at one of the PowerTransformerEnds, preferably the end
numbered 1, having the shunt values on end 1 is the preferred way.
4) for a PowerTransformer with more than three Terminals the PowerTransformerEnd
impedance values cannot be used. Instead use the TransformerMeshImpedance
or split the transformer into multiple PowerTransformers.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerTankEnd

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerTankEnd.phases
documentation: Describes the phases carried by a conducting equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerTankEnd.TransformerTank
documentation: Transformer this winding belongs to.

Transformer tank end represents an individual winding for unbalanced models
or for transformer tanks connected into a bank (and bank is modelled with
the PowerTransformer).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerMeshImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerMeshImpedance.r
documentation: Resistance between the 'from' and the 'to' end, seen from the 'from' end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerMeshImpedance.r0
documentation:
Zero-sequence resistance between the 'from' and the 'to' end, seen from
the 'from' end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerMeshImpedance.x
documentation: Reactance between the 'from' and the 'to' end, seen from the 'from' end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerMeshImpedance.x0
documentation:
Zero-sequence reactance between the 'from' and the 'to' end, seen from
the 'from' end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerMeshImpedance.FromTransformerEnd
documentation:
From end this mesh impedance is connected to. It determines the voltage
reference.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerMeshImpedance.FromTransformerEndInfo
documentation:
'from' transformer end datasheet this mesh impedance is calculated from.
It determines the voltage reference.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerMeshImpedance.ToTransformerEnd
documentation: All transformer ends this mesh impedance is connected to.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerMeshImpedance.ToTransformerEndInfos
documentation:
All 'to' transformer end datasheets this mesh impedance for 'from' transformer
end is calculated from.

Transformer mesh impedance (Delta-model) between transformer ends.
The typical case is that this class describes the impedance between two
transformer ends pair-wise, i.e. the cardinalities at both tranformer end
associations are 1. But in cases where two or more transformer ends are
modeled the cardinalities are larger than 1.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerStarImpedance

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerStarImpedance.r
documentation: Resistance of the transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerStarImpedance.r0
documentation: Zero sequence series resistance of the transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerStarImpedance.x
documentation: Positive sequence series reactance of the transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerStarImpedance.x0
documentation: Zero sequence series reactance of the transformer end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerStarImpedance.TransformerEnd
documentation: All transformer ends having this star impedance.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerStarImpedance.TransformerEndInfo
documentation: Transformer end datasheet used to calculate this transformer star impedance.

Transformer star impedance (Pi-model) that accurately reflects impedance
for transformers with 2 or 3 windings. For transformers with 4 or more
windings, you must use TransformerMeshImpedance class.
For transmission networks use PowerTransformerEnd impedances (r, r0, x,
x0, b, b0, g and g0).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerTest

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerTest.basePower
documentation:
Base power at which the tests are conducted, usually equal to the rateds
of one of the involved transformer ends.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TransformerTest.temperature
documentation: Temperature at which the test is conducted.

Test result for transformer ends, such as short-circuit, open-circuit (excitation)
or no-load test.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NoLoadTest

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NoLoadTest.energisedEndVoltage
documentation: Voltage applied to the winding (end) during test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NoLoadTest.excitingCurrent
documentation:
Exciting current measured from a positive-sequence or single-phase excitation
test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NoLoadTest.excitingCurrentZero
documentation:
Exciting current measured from a zero-sequence open-circuit excitation
test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NoLoadTest.loss
documentation: Losses measured from a positive-sequence or single-phase excitation test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NoLoadTest.lossZero
documentation: Losses measured from a zero-sequence excitation test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NoLoadTest.EnergisedEnd
documentation: Transformer end that current is applied to in this no-load test.

No-load test results determine core admittance parameters. They include
exciting current and core loss measurements from applying voltage to one
winding. The excitation may be positive sequence or zero sequence. The
test may be repeated at different voltages to measure saturation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.OpenCircuitTest

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.OpenCircuitTest.energisedEndStep
documentation: Tap step number for the energised end of the test pair.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.OpenCircuitTest.openEndStep
documentation: Tap step number for the open end of the test pair.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.OpenCircuitTest.energisedEndVoltage
documentation: Voltage applied to the winding (end) during test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.OpenCircuitTest.openEndVoltage
documentation:
Voltage measured at the open-circuited end, with the energised end set
to rated voltage and all other ends open.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.OpenCircuitTest.phaseShift
documentation:
Phase shift measured at the open end with the energised end set to rated
voltage and all other ends open.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.OpenCircuitTest.EnergisedEnd
documentation: Transformer end that current is applied to in this open-circuit test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.OpenCircuitTest.OpenEnd
documentation:
Transformer end measured for induced voltage and angle in this open-circuit
test.

Open-circuit test results verify winding turn ratios and phase shifts.
They include induced voltage and phase shift measurements on open-circuit
windings, with voltage applied to the energised end. For three-phase windings,
the excitation can be a positive sequence (the default) or a zero sequence.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShortCircuitTest

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShortCircuitTest.energisedEndStep
documentation: Tap step number for the energised end of the test pair.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShortCircuitTest.groundedEndStep
documentation: Tap step number for the grounded end of the test pair.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShortCircuitTest.leakageImpedance
documentation:
Leakage impedance measured from a positive-sequence or single-phase short-circuit
test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShortCircuitTest.leakageImpedanceZero
documentation: Leakage impedance measured from a zero-sequence short-circuit test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShortCircuitTest.loss
documentation: Load losses from a positive-sequence or single-phase short-circuit test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShortCircuitTest.lossZero
documentation: Load losses from a zero-sequence short-circuit test.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShortCircuitTest.EnergisedEnd
documentation:
Transformer end that voltage is applied to in this short-circuit test.
The test voltage is chosen to induce rated current in the energised end.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ShortCircuitTest.GroundedEnds
documentation: All ends short-circuited in this short-circuit test.

Short-circuit test results determine mesh impedance parameters. They include
load losses and leakage impedances. For three-phase windings, the excitation
can be a positive sequence (the default) or a zero sequence. There shall
be at least one grounded winding.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.checkBilling
documentation:
True if as a result of an inspection or otherwise, there is a reason to
suspect that a previous billing may have been performed with erroneous
data. Value should be reset once this potential discrepancy has been resolved.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.grounded
documentation: True if grounded.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.isSdp
documentation:
If true, this usage point is a service delivery point, i.e., a usage point
where the ownership of the service changes hands.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.isVirtual
documentation:
If true, this usage point is virtual, i.e., no physical location exists
in the network where a meter could be located to collect the meter readings.
For example, one may define a virtual usage point to serve as an aggregation
of usage for all of a company's premises distributed widely across the
distribution territory. Otherwise, the usage point is physical, i.e., there
is a logical point in the network where a meter could be located to collect
meter readings.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.minimalUsageExpected
documentation:
If true, minimal or zero usage is expected at this usage point for situations
such as premises vacancy, logical or physical disconnect. It is used for
readings validation and estimation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.outageRegion
documentation: Outage region in which this usage point is located.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.readCycle
documentation:
Cycle day on which the meter for this usage point will normally be read.
Usually correlated with the billing cycle.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.readRoute
documentation:
Identifier of the route to which this usage point is assigned for purposes
of meter reading. Typically used to configure hand held meter reading systems
prior to collection of reads.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.serviceDeliveryRemark
documentation:
Remarks about this usage point, for example the reason for it being rated
with a non-nominal priority.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.servicePriority
documentation:
Priority of service for this usage point. Note that usage points at the
same service location can have different priorities.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.amiBillingReady
documentation:
Tracks the lifecycle of the metering installation at a usage point with
respect to readiness for billing via advanced metering infrastructure reads.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.connectionState
documentation: State of the usage point with respect to connection to the network.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.estimatedLoad
documentation: Estimated load.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.nominalServiceVoltage
documentation: Nominal service voltage.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.phaseCode
documentation:
Phase code. Number of wires and specific nominal phases can be deduced
from enumeration literal values. For example, ABCN is three-phase, four-wire,
s12n (splitSecondary12N) is single-phase, three-wire, and s1n and s2n are
single-phase, two-wire.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.ratedCurrent
documentation: Current flow that this usage point is configured to deliver.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.ratedPower
documentation: Active power that this usage point is configured to deliver.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.ConfigurationEvents
documentation: All configuration events created for this usage point.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.CustomerAgreement
documentation: Customer agreement regulating this service delivery point.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.EndDeviceControls
documentation: All end device controls sending commands to this usage point.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.EndDeviceEvents
documentation: All end device events reported for this usage point.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.EndDevices
documentation: All end devices at this usage point.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.Equipments
documentation: All equipment connecting this usage point to the electrical grid.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.MeterReadings
documentation: All meter readings obtained from this usage point.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.MeterServiceWorkTasks
documentation: All meter service work tasks at this usage point.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.MetrologyRequirements
documentation: All metrology requirements for this usage point.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.PricingStructures
documentation:
All pricing structures applicable to this service delivery point (with
prepayment meter running as a stand-alone device, with no CustomerAgreement
or Customer).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.ServiceCategory
documentation: Service category delivered by this usage point.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.ServiceLocation
documentation: Service location where the service delivered by this usage point is consumed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.ServiceMultipliers
documentation: All multipliers applied at this usage point.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.UsagePointGroups
documentation: All groups to which this usage point belongs.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePoint.UsagePointLocation
documentation: Location of this usage point.

Logical or physical point in the network to which readings or events may
be attributed. Used at the place where a physical or virtual meter may
be located; however, it is not required that a meter be present.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePointGroup

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePointGroup.type
documentation: Type of this group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePointGroup.DemandResponsePrograms
documentation: All demand response programs this usage point group is enrolled in.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePointGroup.EndDeviceControls
documentation: All end device controls sending commands to this usage point group.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UsagePointGroup.UsagePoints
documentation: All usage points in this group.

Abstraction for management of group communications within a two-way AMR
system or the data for a group of related usage points. Commands can be
issued to all of the usage points that belong to a usage point group using
a defined group address and the underlying AMR communication infrastructure.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ValueAliasSet

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ValueAliasSet.Commands
documentation: The Commands using the set for translation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ValueAliasSet.Discretes
documentation: The Measurements using the set for translation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ValueAliasSet.RaiseLowerCommands
documentation: The Commands using the set for translation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ValueAliasSet.Values
documentation: The ValueToAlias mappings included in the set.

Describes the translation of a set of values into a name and is intendend
to facilitate cusom translations. Each ValueAliasSet has a name, description
etc. A specific Measurement may represent a discrete state like Open, Closed,
Intermediate etc. This requires a translation from the MeasurementValue.value
number to a string, e.g. 0->"Invalid", 1->"Open", 2->"Closed",
3->"Intermediate". Each ValueToAlias member in ValueAliasSet.Value describe
a mapping for one particular value to a name.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ValueToAlias

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ValueToAlias.value
documentation: The value that is mapped.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ValueToAlias.ValueAliasSet
documentation: The ValueAliasSet having the ValueToAlias mappings.

Describes the translation of one particular value into a name, e.g. 1 as
"Open".

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.WirePosition

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.WirePosition.sequenceNumber
documentation:
Numbering for wires on a WireSpacingInfo. Neutrals should be numbered last.
Multiple circuits on the same pole, tower or right-of-way can be included
with unique sequence numbers for the phases, and identical sequence numbers
for any shared neutrals.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.WirePosition.xCoord
documentation:
Signed horizontal distance from the wire at this position to a common reference
point.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.WirePosition.yCoord
documentation:
Signed vertical distance from the wire at this position: above ground (positive
value) or burial depth below ground (negative value).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.WirePosition.WireSpacingInfo
documentation: Wire spacing data this wire position belongs to.

Identification, spacing and configuration of the wires of a conductor with
respect to a structure.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IntervalBlock

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IntervalBlock.IntervalReadings
documentation: Interval reading contained in this block.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IntervalBlock.MeterReading
documentation: Meter reading containing this interval block.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IntervalBlock.PendingCalculation
documentation:
Pending calculation to apply to interval reading values contained by this
block (after which the resulting reading type is different than the original
because it reflects the conversion result).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IntervalBlock.ReadingType
documentation: Type information for interval reading values contained in this block.

Time sequence of readings of the same reading type. Contained interval
readings may need conversion through the application of an offset and a
scalar defined in associated pending.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IrregularTimePoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IrregularTimePoint.value1
documentation:
The first value at the time. The meaning of the value is defined by the
derived type of the associated schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IrregularTimePoint.value2
documentation:
The second value at the time. The meaning of the value is defined by the
derived type of the associated schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IrregularTimePoint.time
documentation: The time is relative to the schedule starting time.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.IrregularTimePoint.IntervalSchedule
documentation: An IrregularTimePoint belongs to an IrregularIntervalSchedule.
TimePoints for a schedule where the time between the points varies.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.MeterWorkTask

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.MeterWorkTask.Meter
documentation: Meter on which this non-replacement work task is performed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.MeterWorkTask.OldMeter
documentation: Old meter replaced by this work task.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.MeterWorkTask.UsagePoint
documentation: Usage point to which this meter service work task applies.
Work task involving meters.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Name

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Name.name
documentation: Any free text that name the object.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Name.IdentifiedObject
documentation: Identified object that this name designates.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Name.NameType
documentation: Type of this name.

The Name class provides the means to define any number of human readable names for an object. A name is not to be used for defining inter-object relationships. For inter-object relationships instead use the object identification 'mRID'.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NameType

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NameType.description
documentation: Description of the name type.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NameType.name
documentation: Name of the name type.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NameType.Names
documentation: All names of this type.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NameType.NameTypeAuthority
documentation: Authority responsible for managing names of this type.

Type of name. Possible values for attribute 'name' are implementation dependent
but standard profiles may specify types. An enterprise may have multiple
IT systems each having its own local name for the same object, e.g. a planning
system may have different names from an EMS. An object may also have different
names within the same IT system, e.g. localName as defined in CIM version
14. The definition from CIM14 is:
The localName is a human readable name of the object. It is a free text
name local to a node in a naming hierarchy similar to a file directory
structure. A power system related naming hierarchy may be: Substation,
VoltageLevel, Equipment etc. Children of the same parent in such a hierarchy
have names that typically are unique among them.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NameTypeAuthority

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NameTypeAuthority.description
documentation: Description of the name type authority.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NameTypeAuthority.name
documentation: Name of the name type authority.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NameTypeAuthority.NameTypes
documentation: All name types managed by this authority.

Authority responsible for creation and management of names of a given type;
typically an organization or an enterprise system.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoint.sectionNumber
documentation: The number of the section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoint.b
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) susceptance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoint.g
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhasePoint.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPhase
documentation: Non-linear shunt compensator phase owning this point.
A per phase non linear shunt compensator bank or section admittance value.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.sectionNumber
documentation: The number of the section.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.b
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) susceptance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.b0
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) susceptance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.g
documentation: Positive sequence shunt (charging) conductance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.g0
documentation: Zero sequence shunt (charging) conductance per section

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.NonlinearShuntCompensatorPoint.NonlinearShuntCompensator
documentation: Non-linear shunt compensator owning this point.
A non linear shunt compensator bank or section admittance value.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.OperatingShare

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.OperatingShare.percentage
documentation:
Percentage operational ownership between the pair (power system resource
and operatging participant) associated with this share. The total percentage
ownership for a power system resource should add to 100%.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.OperatingShare.OperatingParticipant
documentation:
The operating participant having this share with the associated power system
resource.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.OperatingShare.PowerSystemResource
documentation: The power system resource to which the share applies.

Specifies the operations contract relationship between a power system resource
and a contract participant.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PendingCalculation

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PendingCalculation.multiplyBeforeAdd
documentation: Whether scalars should be applied before adding the 'offset'.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PendingCalculation.offset
documentation: (if applicable) Offset to be added as well as multiplication using scalars.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PendingCalculation.scalarDenominator
documentation:
(if scalar is rational number) When 'IntervalReading.value' is multiplied
by 'scalarNumerator' and divided by this value, it causes a unit of measure
conversion to occur, resulting in the 'ReadingType.unit'.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PendingCalculation.scalarFloat
documentation:
(if scalar is floating number) When multiplied with 'IntervalReading.value',
it causes a unit of measure conversion to occur, according to the 'ReadingType.unit'.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PendingCalculation.scalarNumerator
documentation:
(if scalar is integer or rational number) When the scalar is a simple integer,
and this attribute is presented alone and multiplied with 'IntervalReading.value',
it causes a unit of measure conversion to occur, resulting in the 'ReadingType.unit'.
It is never used in conjunction with 'scalarFloat', only with 'scalarDenominator'.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PendingCalculation.IntervalBlocks
documentation:
All blocks of interval reading values to which this pending conversion
applies.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PendingCalculation.ReadingType
documentation: Reading type resulting from this pending conversion.

When present, a scalar conversion that needs to be applied to every IntervalReading.value
contained in IntervalBlock. This conversion results in a new associated
ReadingType, reflecting the true dimensions of IntervalReading values after
the conversion.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PositionPoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PositionPoint.groupNumber
cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PositionPoint.sequenceNumber
documentation: Zero-relative sequence number of this point within a series of points.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PositionPoint.xPosition
documentation: X axis position.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PositionPoint.yPosition
documentation: Y axis position.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PositionPoint.zPosition
documentation: (if applicable) Z axis position.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PositionPoint.Location
documentation: Location described by this position point.

Set of spatial coordinates that determine a point, defined in the coordinate
system specified in 'Location.CoordinateSystem'. Use a single position
point instance to desribe a point-oriented location. Use a sequence of
position points to describe a line-oriented object (physical location of
non-point oriented objects like cables or lines), or area of an object
(like a substation or a geographical zone - in this case, have first and
last position point with the same values).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Quality61850

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Quality61850.badReference
documentation: Measurement value may be incorrect due to a reference being out of calibration.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Quality61850.estimatorReplaced
documentation:
Value has been replaced by State Estimator. estimatorReplaced is not an
IEC61850 quality bit but has been put in this class for convenience.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Quality61850.failure
documentation:
This identifier indicates that a supervision function has detected an internal
or external failure, e.g. communication failure.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Quality61850.oldData
documentation:
Measurement value is old and possibly invalid, as it has not been successfully
updated during a specified time interval.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Quality61850.operatorBlocked
documentation: Measurement value is blocked and hence unavailable for transmission.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Quality61850.oscillatory
documentation:
To prevent some overload of the communication it is sensible to detect
and suppress oscillating (fast changing) binary inputs. If a signal changes
in a defined time (tosc) twice in the same direction (from 0 to 1 or from
1 to 0) then oscillation is detected and the detail quality identifier
"oscillatory" is set. If it is detected a configured numbers of transient
changes could be passed by. In this time the validity status "questionable"
is set. If after this defined numbers of changes the signal is still in
the oscillating state the value shall be set either to the opposite state
of the previous stable value or to a defined default value. In this case
the validity status "questionable" is reset and "invalid" is set as long
as the signal is oscillating. If it is configured such that no transient
changes should be passed by then the validity status "invalid" is set immediately
in addition to the detail quality identifier "oscillatory" (used for status
information only).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Quality61850.outOfRange
documentation: Measurement value is beyond a predefined range of value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Quality61850.overFlow
documentation:
Measurement value is beyond the capability of being represented properly.
For example, a counter value overflows from maximum count back to a value
of zero.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Quality61850.suspect
documentation:
A correlation function has detected that the value is not consitent with
other values. Typically set by a network State Estimator.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Quality61850.test
documentation: Measurement value is transmitted for test purposes.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Quality61850.source
documentation:
Source gives information related to the origin of a value. The value may
be acquired from the process, defaulted or substituted.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.Quality61850.validity
documentation: Validity of the measurement value.

Quality flags in this class are as defined in IEC 61850, except for estimatorReplaced,
which has been included in this class for convenience.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.MeasurementValueQuality

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.MeasurementValueQuality.MeasurementValue
documentation: A MeasurementValue has a MeasurementValueQuality associated with it.

Measurement quality flags. Bits 0-10 are defined for substation automation
in draft IEC 61850 part 7-3. Bits 11-15 are reserved for future expansion
by that document. Bits 16-31 are reserved for EMS applications.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RegularTimePoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RegularTimePoint.sequenceNumber
documentation:
The position of the regular time point in the sequence. Note that time
points don't have to be sequential, i.e. time points may be omitted. The
actual time for a RegularTimePoint is computed by multiplying the associated
regular interval schedule's time step with the regular time point sequence
number and adding the associated schedules start time.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RegularTimePoint.value1
documentation:
The first value at the time. The meaning of the value is defined by the
derived type of the associated schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RegularTimePoint.value2
documentation:
The second value at the time. The meaning of the value is defined by the
derived type of the associated schedule.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.RegularTimePoint.IntervalSchedule
documentation: Regular interval schedule containing this time point.

Time point for a schedule where the time between the consecutive points
is constant.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReportingCapability

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReportingCapability.reportingIntervalPeriod
documentation: Number of units of time making up reporting period.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReportingCapability.reportingIntervalType
documentation: Unit of time in which reporting period is expressed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReportingCapability.reportingMethod
documentation: Indicates how the weather station reports observations.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ReportingCapability.EnvironmentalAnalog
documentation: One of the environmental value sets expressing one of the reporting capabilities.

<font color="#0f0f0f">Definition of one set of reporting capabilities for
this monitoring station. The associated EnvironmentalValueSets describe
the maximum range of possible environmental values the station is capable
of returning via relationships with non-populated AnalogValue child instances.
This attribute is intended primarily to assist a utility in managing its
stations. </font>

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ScheduledEventData

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ScheduledEventData.estimatedWindow
documentation:
Estimated date and time for activity execution (with earliest possibility
of activity initiation and latest possibility of activity completion).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ScheduledEventData.requestedWindow
documentation: Requested date and time interval for activity execution.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ScheduledEventData.ScheduledEvents
documentation: All scheduled events with this specification.

Schedule parameters for an activity that is to occur, is occurring, or
has completed.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ServiceLocation

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ServiceLocation.accessMethod
documentation:
Method for the service person to access this service location. For example,
a description of where to obtain a key if the facility is unmanned and
secured.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ServiceLocation.needsInspection
documentation:
True if inspection is needed of facilities at this service location. This
could be requested by a customer, due to suspected tampering, environmental
concerns (e.g., a fire in the vicinity), or to correct incompatible data.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ServiceLocation.siteAccessProblem
documentation:
Problems previously encountered when visiting or performing work on this
location. Examples include: bad dog, violent customer, verbally abusive
occupant, obstructions, safety hazards, etc.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ServiceLocation.CustomerAgreements
documentation: All customer agreements regulating this service location.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ServiceLocation.EndDevices
documentation: All end devices that measure the service delivered to this service location.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.ServiceLocation.UsagePoints
documentation:
All usage points delivering service (of the same type) to this service
location.
A real estate location, commonly referred to as premises.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.StateVariable
documentation: An abstract class for state variables.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvInjection

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvInjection.phase
documentation:
The terminal phase at which the connection is applied. If missing, the
injection is assumed to be balanced among non-neutral phases.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvInjection.pInjection
documentation:
The active power mismatch between calculated injection and initial injection.
Positive sign means injection into the TopologicalNode (bus).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvInjection.qInjection
documentation:
The reactive power mismatch between calculated injection and initial injection.
Positive sign means injection into the TopologicalNode (bus).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvInjection.TopologicalNode
documentation: The topological node associated with the flow injection state variable.

The SvInjection is reporting the calculated bus injection minus the sum
of the terminal flows. The terminal flow is positive out from the bus (load
sign convention) and bus injection has positive flow into the bus. SvInjection
may have the remainder after state estimation or slack after power flow
calculation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvPowerFlow

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvPowerFlow.p
documentation:
The active power flow. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a TopologicalNode (bus) into the conducting equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvPowerFlow.phase
documentation:
The individual phase of the flow. If unspecified, then assumed to be balanced
among phases.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvPowerFlow.q
documentation:
The reactive power flow. Load sign convention is used, i.e. positive sign
means flow out from a TopologicalNode (bus) into the conducting equipment.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvPowerFlow.Terminal
documentation: The terminal associated with the power flow state variable.

State variable for power flow. Load convention is used for flow direction.
This means flow out from the TopologicalNode into the equipment is positive.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvShuntCompensatorSections

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvShuntCompensatorSections.sections
documentation:
The number of sections in service as a continous variable. To get integer
value scale with ShuntCompensator.bPerSection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvShuntCompensatorSections.phase
documentation:
The terminal phase at which the connection is applied. If missing, the
injection is assumed to be balanced among non-neutral phases.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvShuntCompensatorSections.ShuntCompensator
documentation: The shunt compensator for which the state applies.
State variable for the number of sections in service for a shunt compensator.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvStatus

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvStatus.inService
documentation: The in service status as a result of topology processing.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvStatus.phase
documentation:
The individual phase status. If the attribute is unspecified, then three
phase model is assumed.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvStatus.ConductingEquipment
documentation: The conducting equipment associated with the status state variable.
State variable for status.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvSwitch

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvSwitch.open
documentation: The attribute tells if the computed state of the switch is considered open.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvSwitch.phase
documentation:
The terminal phase at which the connection is applied. If missing, the
injection is assumed to be balanced among non-neutral phases.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvSwitch.Switch
documentation: The switch associated with the switch state.
State variable for switch.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvTapStep

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvTapStep.position
documentation:
The floating point tap position. This is not the tap ratio, but rather
the tap step position as defined by the related tap changer model and normally
is constrained to be within the range of minimum and maximum tap positions.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvTapStep.TapChanger
documentation: The tap changer associated with the tap step state.
State variable for transformer tap step.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvVoltage

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvVoltage.angle
documentation:
The voltage angle of the topological node complex voltage with respect
to system reference.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvVoltage.phase
documentation:
If specified the voltage is the line to ground voltage of the individual
phase. If unspecified, then the voltage is assumed balanced.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvVoltage.v
documentation: The voltage magnitude at the topological node.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.SvVoltage.TopologicalNode
documentation: The topological node associated with the voltage state.
State variable for voltage.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChangerTablePoint.ratio
documentation:
The voltage at the tap step divided by rated voltage of the transformer
end having the tap changer. Hence this is a value close to one.
For example, if the ratio at step 1 is 1.01, and the rated voltage of the
transformer end is 110kV, then the voltage obtained by setting the tap
changer to step 1 to is 111.1kV.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChangerTablePoint.step
documentation: The tap step.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChangerTablePoint.b
documentation:
The magnetizing branch susceptance deviation in percent of nominal value.
The actual susceptance is calculated as follows:
calculated magnetizing susceptance = b(nominal) * (1 + b(from this class)/100).
The b(nominal) is defined as the static magnetizing susceptance on the
associated power transformer end or ends. This model assumes the star impedance
(pi model) form.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChangerTablePoint.g
documentation:
The magnetizing branch conductance deviation in percent of nominal value.
The actual conductance is calculated as follows:
calculated magnetizing conductance = g(nominal) * (1 + g(from this class)/100).
The g(nominal) is defined as the static magnetizing conductance on the
associated power transformer end or ends. This model assumes the star impedance
(pi model) form.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChangerTablePoint.r
documentation:
The resistance deviation in percent of nominal value. The actual reactance
is calculated as follows:
calculated resistance = r(nominal) * (1 + r(from this class)/100). The
r(nominal) is defined as the static resistance on the associated power
transformer end or ends. This model assumes the star impedance (pi model)
form.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.TapChangerTablePoint.x
documentation:
The series reactance deviation in percent of nominal value. The actual
reactance is calculated as follows:
calculated reactance = x(nominal) * (1 + x(from this class)/100). The x(nominal)
is defined as the static series reactance on the associated power transformer
end or ends. This model assumes the star impedance (pi model) form.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChangerTablePoint

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseTapChangerTablePoint.angle
documentation:
The angle difference in degrees. A positive value indicates a positive
phase shift from the winding where the tap is located to the other winding
(for a two-winding transformer).

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulationKind

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulationKind.boundedQuantity
documentation:
A time-independent cumulative quantity much like a 'bulkQuantity' or a
'latchingQuantity', except that the accumulation stops at the maximum or
minimum values. When the maximum is reached, any additional positive accumulation
is discarded, but negative accumulation may be accepted (thus lowering
the counter.) Likewise, when the negative bound is reached, any additional
negative accumulation is discarded, but positive accumulation is accepted
(thus increasing the counter.)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulationKind.bulkQuantity
documentation:
A value from a register which represents the bulk quantity of a commodity.
This quantity is computed as the integral of the commodity usage rate.
This value is typically used as the basis for the dial reading at the meter,
and as a result, will roll over upon reaching a maximum dial value.
Note 1: With the metering system, the roll-over behaviour typically implies
a roll-under behavior so that the value presented is always a positive
value (e.g. unsigned integer or positive decimal.) However, when communicating
data between enterprise applications a negative value might occur in a
case such as net metering.
Note 2: A 'bulkQuantity' refers primarily to the dial reading and not the
consumption over a specific period of time.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulationKind.continuousCumulative
documentation:
The sum of the previous billing period values and the present period value.
Note: 'continuousCumulative' is commonly used in conjunction with 'demand',
and it would represent the cumulative sum of the previous billing period
maximum demand values (as occurring with each demand reset) summed with
the present period maximum demand value (which has yet to be reset.)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulationKind.cumulative
documentation:
The sum of the previous billing period values.
Note: 'cumulative' is commonly used in conjunction with “demand.” Each
demand reset causes the maximum demand value for the present billing period
(since the last demand reset) to accumulate as an accumulative total of
all maximum demands. So instead of 'zeroing' the demand register, a demand
reset has the effect of adding the present maximum demand to this accumulating
total.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulationKind.deltaData
documentation:
The difference between the value at the end of the prescribed interval
and the beginning of the interval. This is used for incremental interval
data.
Note: One common application would be for load profile data, another use
might be to report the number of events within an interval (such as the
number of equipment energisations within the specified period of time.)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulationKind.indicating
documentation:
As if a needle is swung out on the meter face to a value to indicate the
current value.
Note: An 'indicating' value is typically measured over hundreds of milliseconds
or greater, or may imply a “pusher” mechanism to capture a value. Compare
this to 'instantaneous' which is measured over a shorter period of time.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulationKind.instantaneous
documentation:
Typically measured over the fastest period of time allowed by the definition
of the metric (usually milliseconds or tens of milliseconds.)
Note: 'instantaneous' was moved to attribute #3 in Ed.2 of IEC 61968-9,
from attribute #1 in Ed.1 of IEC 61968-9.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulationKind.latchingQuantity
documentation:
When this description is applied to a metered value, it implies that the
value is a time-independent cumulative quantity much like a 'bulkQuantity',
except that it latches upon the maximum value upon reaching that value.
Any additional accumulation (positive or negative) is discarded until a
reset occurs.
Note: A 'latchingQuantity' may also occur in the downward direction – upon
reaching a minimum value. The terms 'maximum' or 'minimum' (for 'aggregate')
will usually be included when this type of accumulation behaviour is present.
When this description is applied to an encoded value (UOM= 'Code'), it
implies that the value has one or more bits which are latching. The condition
that caused the bit to be set may have long since evaporated.
In either case, the timestamp that accompanies the value may not coincide
with the moment the value was initially set.
In both cases a system will need to perform an operation to clear the latched
value.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulationKind.none
documentation: Not applicable, or implied by the unit of measure.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulationKind.summation
documentation:
A form of accumulation which is selective with respect to time.
Note : 'summation' could be considered a specialisation of 'bulkQuantity'
as it selectively accumulates pulses over a timing pattern (while 'bulkQuantity'
accumulates pulses all of the time).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.AccumulationKind.timeDelay
documentation:
A form of computation which introduces a time delay characteristic to the
data value.

Kind of accumulation behaviour for read / measured values from individual
end points.

known as linear interpolation.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.A
documentation: Current in Ampere.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.Ah
documentation: Ampere-hours, Ampere-hours.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.As
documentation: Ampere seconds (A·s).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.Btu
documentation: Energy, British Thermal Unit.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.Hz
documentation: Frequency in Hertz (1/s).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.Q
documentation: Quantity power, Q.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.Qh
documentation: Quantity energy, Qh.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.V
documentation: Electric potential in Volt (W/A).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.VA
documentation: Apparent power in Volt Ampere (See also real power and reactive power.)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.VAh
documentation: Apparent energy in Volt Ampere hours.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.VAr
documentation:
Reactive power in Volt Ampere reactive. The “reactive” or “imaginary” component
of electrical power (VIsin(phi)). (See also real power and apparent power).
Note: Different meter designs use different methods to arrive at their
results. Some meters may compute reactive power as an arithmetic value,
while others compute the value vectorially. The data consumer should determine
the method in use and the suitability of the measurement for the intended
purpose.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.VArh
documentation: Reactive energy in Volt Ampere reactive hours.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.VPerVA
documentation:
Power factor, PF, the ratio of the active power to the apparent power.
Note: The sign convention used for power factor will differ between IEC
meters and EEI (ANSI) meters. It is assumed that the data consumers understand
the type of meter being used and agree on the sign convention in use at
any given utility.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.VPerVAr
documentation:
Power factor, PF, the ratio of the active power to the apparent power.
Note: The sign convention used for power factor will differ between IEC
meters and EEI (ANSI) meters. It is assumed that the data consumers understand
the type of meter being used and agree on the sign convention in use at
any given utility.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.Vh
documentation: Volt-hour, Volt hours.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.Vs
documentation: Volt second (Ws/A).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.W
documentation:
Real power in Watt (J/s). Electrical power may have real and reactive components.
The real portion of electrical power (I²R or VIcos(phi)), is expressed
in Watts. (See also apparent power and reactive power.)

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.WPerA
documentation: Active power per current flow, watt per Ampere.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.WPers
documentation: Ramp rate in Watt per second.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.Wh
documentation: Real energy in Watt hours.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.deg
documentation: Plane angle in degrees.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.degC
documentation:
Relative temperature in degrees Celsius.
In the SI unit system the symbol is ºC. Electric charge is measured in
coulomb that has the unit symbol C. To distinguish degree Celsius form
coulomb the symbol used in the UML is degC. Reason for not using ºC is
the special character º is difficult to manage in software.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.h
documentation: Time, hour = 60 min = 3600 s.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.min
documentation: Time, minute = 60 s.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.ohm
documentation: Electric resistance in ohm (V/A).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.ohmPerm
documentation: Electric resistance per length in ohm per metre ((V/A)/m).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.ohmm
documentation: resistivity, Ohm metre, (rho).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.onePerHz
documentation: Reciprocal of frequency (1/Hz).

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.s
documentation: Time in seconds.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.DERUnitSymbol.therm
documentation: Energy, Therm.
The units defined for usage in the CIM.

to a terminal flow, the positive direction is into the terminal.
The direction attribute describes the side of a limit that is a violation.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseCode.s1
documentation: Secondary phase 1.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseCode.s12
documentation: Secondary phase 1 and 2.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseCode.s12N
documentation: Secondary phases 1, 2, and neutral.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseCode.s1N
documentation: Secondary phase 1 and neutral.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseCode.s2
documentation: Secondary phase 2.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseCode.s2N
documentation: Secondary phase 2 and neutral.

An unordered enumeration of phase identifiers. Allows designation of phases
for both transmission and distribution equipment, circuits and loads. The
enumeration, by itself, does not describe how the phases are connected
together or connected to ground. Ground is not explicitly denoted as a
phase.
Residential and small commercial loads are often served from single-phase,
or split-phase, secondary circuits. For example of s12N, phases 1 and 2
refer to hot wires that are 180 degrees out of phase, while N refers to
the neutral wire. Through single-phase transformer connections, these secondary
circuits may be served from one or two of the primary phases A, B, and
C. For three-phase loads, use the A, B, C phase codes instead of s12N.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseConnectedFaultKind

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseConnectedFaultKind.lineOpen
documentation:
The fault is when the conductor path is broken between two terminals. Additional
coexisting faults may be required if the broken conductor also causes connections
to grounds or other lines or phases.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseConnectedFaultKind.lineToGround
documentation:
The fault connects the indicated phases to ground. The line to line fault
impedance is not used and assumed infinite. The full ground impedance is
connected between each phase specified in the fault and ground, but not
between the phases.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseConnectedFaultKind.lineToLine
documentation:
The fault connects the specified phases together without a connection to
ground. The ground impedance of this fault is ignored. The line to line
impedance is connected between each of the phases specified in the fault.
For example three times for a three phase fault, one time for a two phase
fault. A single phase fault should not be specified.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseConnectedFaultKind.lineToLineToGround
documentation:
The fault connects the indicated phases to ground and to each other. The
line to line impedance is connected between each of the phases specified
in the fault in a full mesh. For example three times for a three phase
fault, one time for a two phase fault. A single phase fault should not
be specified. The full ground impedance is connected between each phase
specified in the fault and ground.
The type of fault connection among phases.

classes.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseShuntConnectionKind

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseShuntConnectionKind.D
documentation: Delta connection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseShuntConnectionKind.G
documentation:
Ground connection; use when explicit connection to ground needs to be expressed

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseShuntConnectionKind.I
documentation: Independent winding, for single-phase connections.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseShuntConnectionKind.Y
documentation: Wye connection.

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.PhaseShuntConnectionKind.Yn
documentation: Wye, with neutral brought out for grounding.

The configuration of phase connections for a single terminal device such

class_variables.name: cimgraph.data_profile.rc4_2021.rc4_2021.UnitMultiplier.none
documentation: No multiplier or equivalently multiply by 1.

The unit multipliers defined for the CIM. When applied to unit symbols,
the unit symbol is treated as a derived unit. Regardless of the contents
of the unit symbol text, the unit symbol shall be treated as if it were
a single-character unit symbol. Unit symbols should not contain multipliers,
and it should be left to the multiplier to define the multiple for an entire
data type.
For example, if a unit symbol is "A2Perh" and the multiplier is "k", then
the value is k(A^2/h), and the multiplier applies to the entire final value,
not to any individual part of the value. This can be conceptualized by
substituting a derived unit symbol for the unit type. If one imagines that
the symbol "Þ" represents the derived unit "A2Perh", then applying the
multiplier "k" can be conceptualized simply as "kÞ".
For example, the SI unit for mass is "kg" and not "g". If the unit symbol
is defined as "kg", then the multiplier is applied to "kg" as a whole and
does not replace the "k" in front of the "g". In this case, the multiplier
of "m" would be used with the unit symbol of "kg" to represent one gram.
As a text string, this violates the instructions in IEC 80000-1. However,
because the unit symbol in CIM is treated as a derived unit instead of
as an SI unit, it makes more sense to conceptualize the "kg" as if it were
replaced by one of the proposed replacements for the SI mass symbol. If
one imagines that the "kg" were replaced by a symbol "Þ", then it is easier
to conceptualize the multiplier "m" as creating the proper unit "mÞ", and
not the forbidden unit "mkg".
